18_1_1.NKJV: There was a man in the land of Uz, whose name was Job; and that man was blameless and upright, and one who feared God and shunned evil.
18_1_1.KJV: There was a man in the land of Uz, whose name was Job; and that man was perfect and upright, and one that feared God, and eschewed evil.
18_1_1.ESV: There was a man in the land of Uz whose name was Job, and that man was blameless and upright, one who feared God and turned away from evil.
18_1_1.NASB: There was a man in the land of Uz whose name was Job; and that man was blameless, upright, fearing God and turning away from evil.
18_1_1.NLT: There once was a man named Job who lived in the land of Uz. He was blameless — a man of complete integrity. He feared God and stayed away from evil.
18_1_1.WEB: There was a man in the land of Uz, whose name was Job. That man was blameless and upright, and one who feared God, and turned away from evil.
18_1_1.CSB17: There was a man in the country of Uz named Job. He was a man of complete integrity, who feared God and turned away from evil.
18_1_1.AMP: There was a man in the land of Uz whose name was Job; and that man was blameless and upright, and one who feared God [with reverence] and abstained from and turned away from evil [because he honored God].
18_1_1.MSG: Job was a man who lived in Uz. He was honest inside and out, a man of his word, who was totally devoted to God and hated evil with a passion.
18_1_1.NIV: PrologueIn the land of Uz there lived a man whose name was Job. This man was blameless and upright; he feared God and shunned evil.
18_1_1.NRSVCE: There was once a man in the land of Uz whose name was Job. That man was blameless and upright, one who feared God and turned away from evil.

18_1_2.NIV: He had seven sons and three daughters,
18_1_2.NRSVCE: There were born to him seven sons and three daughters.

18_1_3.NKJV: Also, his possessions were seven thousand sheep, three thousand camels, five hundred yoke of oxen, five hundred female donkeys, and a very large household, so that this man was the greatest of all the people of the East.
18_1_3.KJV: His substance also was seven thousand sheep, and three thousand camels, and five hundred yoke of oxen, and five hundred she asses, and a very great household; so that this man was the greatest of all the men of the east. substance: or, cattle household: or, husbandry men: Heb. sons
18_1_3.ESV: He possessed 7,000 sheep, 3,000 camels, 500 yoke of oxen, and 500 female donkeys, and very many servants, so that this man was the greatest of all the people of the east.
18_1_3.NASB: His possessions also were 7,000 sheep, 3,000 camels, 500 yoke of oxen, 500 female donkeys, and very many servants; and that man was the greatest of all the men of the east.
18_1_3.NLT: He owned 7,000 sheep, 3,000 camels, 500 teams of oxen, and 500 female donkeys. He also had many servants. He was, in fact, the richest person in that entire area.
18_1_3.WEB: His possessions also were seven thousand sheep, three thousand camels, five hundred yoke of oxen, five hundred female donkeys, and a very great household; so that this man was the greatest of all the children of the east.
18_1_3.CSB17: His estate included seven thousand sheep and goats, three thousand camels, five hundred yoke of oxen, five hundred female donkeys, and a very large number of servants. Job was the greatest man among all the people of the east.
18_1_3.AMP: He also possessed 7,000 sheep, 3,000 camels, 500 yoke (pairs) of oxen, 500 female donkeys, and a very great number of servants, so that this man was the greatest [and wealthiest and most respected] of all the men of the east (northern Arabia).
18_1_3.MSG: He was also very wealthy—seven thousand head of sheep, three thousand camels, five hundred teams of oxen, five hundred donkeys, and a huge staff of servants—the most influential man in all the East!
18_1_3.NIV: and he owned seven thousand sheep, three thousand camels, five hundred yoke of oxen and five hundred donkeys, and had a large number of servants. He was the greatest man among all the people of the East.
18_1_3.NRSVCE: He had seven thousand sheep, three thousand camels, five hundred yoke of oxen, five hundred donkeys, and very many servants; so that this man was the greatest of all the people of the east.

18_1_4.NKJV: And his sons would go and feast in their houses, each on his appointed day, and would send and invite their three sisters to eat and drink with them.
18_1_4.KJV: And his sons went and feasted in their houses, every one his day; and sent and called for their three sisters to eat and to drink with them.
18_1_4.ESV: His sons used to go and hold a feast in the house of each one on his day, and they would send and invite their three sisters to eat and drink with them.
18_1_4.NASB: His sons used to go and hold a feast in the house of each one on his day, and they would send and invite their three sisters to eat and drink with them.
18_1_4.NLT: Job’s sons would take turns preparing feasts in their homes, and they would also invite their three sisters to celebrate with them.
18_1_4.WEB: His sons went and held a feast in the house of each one on his birthday; and they sent and called for their three sisters to eat and to drink with them.
18_1_4.CSB17: His sons used to take turns having banquets at their homes. They would send an invitation to their three sisters to eat and drink with them.
18_1_4.AMP: His sons used to go [in turn] and feast in the house of each one on his day, and they would send word and invite their three sisters to eat and drink with them.
18_1_4.MSG: His sons used to take turns hosting parties in their homes, always inviting their three sisters to join them in their merrymaking.
18_1_4.NIV: His sons used to take turns holding feasts in their homes, and they would invite their three sisters to eat and drink with them.
18_1_4.NRSVCE: His sons used to go and hold feasts in one another’s houses in turn; and they would send and invite their three sisters to eat and drink with them.

18_1_5.NKJV: So it was, when the days of feasting had run their course, that Job would send and sanctify them, and he would rise early in the morning and offer burnt offerings according to the number of them all. For Job said, “It may be that my sons have sinned and cursed God in their hearts.” Thus Job did regularly.
18_1_5.KJV: And it was so, when the days of their feasting were gone about, that Job sent and sanctified them, and rose up early in the morning, and offered burnt offerings according to the number of them all: for Job said, It may be that my sons have sinned, and cursed God in their hearts. Thus did Job continually. continually: Heb. all the days
18_1_5.ESV: And when the days of the feast had run their course, Job would send and consecrate them, and he would rise early in the morning and offer burnt offerings according to the number of them all. For Job said, “It may be that my children have sinned, and cursed God in their hearts.” Thus Job did continually.
18_1_5.NASB: When the days of feasting had completed their cycle, Job would send and consecrate them, rising up early in the morning and offering burnt offerings [according to] the number of them all; for Job said, 'Perhaps my sons have sinned and cursed God in their hearts.' Thus Job did continually.
18_1_5.NLT: When these celebrations ended — sometimes after several days — Job would purify his children. He would get up early in the morning and offer a burnt offering for each of them. For Job said to himself, “Perhaps my children have sinned and have cursed God in their hearts.” This was Job’s regular practice.
18_1_5.WEB: It was so, when the days of their feasting had run their course, that Job sent and sanctified them, and rose up early in the morning, and offered burnt offerings according to the number of them all. For Job said, “It may be that my sons have sinned, and renounced God in their hearts.” Job did so continually.
18_1_5.CSB17: Whenever a round of banqueting was over, Job would send for his children and purify them, rising early in the morning to offer burnt offerings for all of them. For Job thought, “Perhaps my children have sinned, having cursed God in their hearts.” This was Job’s regular practice.
18_1_5.AMP: When the days of their feasting were over, Job would send [for them] and consecrate them, rising early in the morning and offering burnt offerings according to the number of them all; for Job said, "It may be that my sons have sinned and cursed God in their hearts." Job did this at all [such] times.
18_1_5.MSG: When the parties were over, Job would get up early in the morning and sacrifice a burnt offering for each of his children, thinking, "Maybe one of them sinned by defying God inwardly." Job made a habit of this sacrificial atonement, just in case they'd sinned.
18_1_5.NIV: When a period of feasting had run its course, Job would send and have them purified. Early in the morning he would sacrifice a burnt offering for each of them, thinking, “Perhaps my children have sinned and cursed God in their hearts.” This was Job’s regular custom.
18_1_5.NRSVCE: And when the feast days had run their course, Job would send and sanctify them, and he would rise early in the morning and offer burnt offerings according to the number of them all; for Job said, “It may be that my children have sinned, and cursed God in their hearts.” This is what Job always did.

18_1_6.NKJV: Now there was a day when the sons of God came to present themselves before the LORD, and Satan also came among them.

18_1_6.NASB: Now there was a day when the sons of God came to present themselves before the LORD, and Satan also came among them.

18_1_8.NKJV: Then the LORD said to Satan, “Have you considered My servant Job, that there is none like him on the earth, a blameless and upright man, one who fears God and shuns evil?”
18_1_8.KJV: And the LORD said unto Satan, Hast thou considered my servant Job, that there is none like him in the earth, a perfect and an upright man, one that feareth God, and escheweth evil? considered: Heb. set thy heart on

18_1_8.MSG: God said to Satan, "Have you noticed my friend Job? There's no one quite like him—honest and true to his word, totally devoted to God and hating evil."
18_1_8.NIV: Then the Lord said to Satan, “Have you considered my servant Job? There is no one on earth like him; he is blameless and upright, a man who fears God and shuns evil.”

For there is none like him on the earth, a blameless and upright man, one who fears God [with reverence] and abstains from and turns away from evil [because he honors God]. And still he maintains and holds tightly to his integrity, although you incited Me against him to destroy him without cause."

18_2_11.KJV: Now when Job's three friends heard of all this evil that was come upon him, they came every one from his own place; Eliphaz the Temanite, and Bildad the Shuhite, and Zophar the Naamathite: for they had made an appointment together to come to mourn with him and to comfort him.

18_2_11.NASB: Now when Job's three friends heard of all this adversity that had come upon him, they came each one from his own place, Eliphaz the Temanite, Bildad the Shuhite and Zophar the Naamathite; and they made an appointment together to come to sympathize with him and comfort him.
18_2_11.NLT: When three of Job’s friends heard of the tragedy he had suffered, they got together and traveled from their homes to comfort and console him. Their names were Eliphaz the Temanite, Bildad the Shuhite, and Zophar the Naamathite.
18_2_11.WEB: Now when Job’s three friends heard of all this evil that had come on him, they each came from his own place: Eliphaz the Temanite, Bildad the Shuhite, and Zophar the Naamathite; and they made an appointment together to come to sympathize with him and to comfort him.
18_2_11.CSB17: Now when Job’s three friends — Eliphaz the Temanite, Bildad the Shuhite, and Zophar the Naamathite — heard about all this adversity that had happened to him, each of them came from his home. They met together to go and sympathize with him and comfort him.
18_2_11.AMP: Now when Job's three friends heard of all this adversity that had come upon him, each one came from his own place, Eliphaz the Temanite, Bildad the Shuhite, and Zophar the Naamathite; for they had made an appointment together to come to sympathize with him and to comfort him.
18_2_11.MSG: Three of Job's friends heard of all the trouble that had fallen on him. Each traveled from his own country—Eliphaz from Teman, Bildad from Shuhah, Zophar from Naamath—and went together to Job to keep him company and comfort him.
18_2_11.NIV: Job’s Three FriendsWhen Job’s three friends, Eliphaz the Temanite, Bildad the Shuhite and Zophar the Naamathite, heard about all the troubles that had come upon him, they set out from their homes and met together by agreement to go and sympathize with him and comfort him.
18_2_11.NRSVCE: Now when Job’s three friends heard of all these troubles that had come upon him, each of them set out from his home — Eliphaz the Temanite, Bildad the Shuhite, and Zophar the Naamathite. They met together to go and console and comfort him.

18_2_12.NKJV: And when they raised their eyes from afar, and did not recognize him, they lifted their voices and wept; and each one tore his robe and sprinkled dust on his head toward heaven.
18_2_12.KJV: And when they lifted up their eyes afar off, and knew him not, they lifted up their voice, and wept; and they rent every one his mantle, and sprinkled dust upon their heads toward heaven.
18_2_12.ESV: And when they saw him from a distance, they did not recognize him. And they raised their voices and wept, and they tore their robes and sprinkled dust on their heads toward heaven.

18_2_12.NLT: When they saw Job from a distance, they scarcely recognized him. Wailing loudly, they tore their robes and threw dust into the air over their heads to show their grief.
18_2_12.WEB: When they lifted up their eyes from a distance, and didn’t recognize him, they raised their voices, and wept; and they each tore his robe, and sprinkled dust on their heads toward the sky.

18_2_12.NRSVCE: When they saw him from a distance, they did not recognize him, and they raised their voices and wept aloud; they tore their robes and threw dust in the air upon their heads.

18_3_23.NIV: Why is life given to a manwhose way is hidden,whom God has hedged in?

18_3_24.NKJV: For my sighing comes before I eat, And my groanings pour out like water.

18_3_24.CSB17: I sigh when food is put before me, and my groans pour out like water.

18_4_2.NASB: 'If one ventures a word with you, will you become impatient? But who can refrain from speaking?
18_4_2.NLT: “Will you be patient and let me say a word?For who could keep from speaking out?

18_4_17.WEB: ‘Shall mortal man be more just than God? Shall a man be more pure than his Maker?
18_4_17.CSB17: “Can a mortal be righteous before God? Can a man be more pure than his Maker?”
18_4_17.AMP: 'Can [mortal] man be just before God or be more righteous than He? Can a man be pure before his Maker or be more cleansed than He?
18_4_17.MSG: " 'How can mere mortals be more righteous than God?How can humans be purer than their Creator?
18_4_17.NIV: ‘Can a mortal be more righteous than God?Can a man be more pure than his Maker?
18_4_17.NRSVCE: ‘Can mortals be righteous before God? Can human beings be pure before their Maker?

18_4_18.NKJV: If He puts no trust in His servants, If He charges His angels with error,
18_4_18.KJV: Behold, he put no trust in his servants; and his angels he charged with folly: and his: or, nor in his angels, in whom he put light
18_4_18.ESV: Even in his servants he puts no trust, and his angels he charges with error;
18_4_18.NASB: 'He puts no trust even in His servants; And against His angels He charges error.
18_4_18.NLT: “If God does not trust his own angelsand has charged his messengers with foolishness,
18_4_18.WEB: Behold, he puts no trust in his servants. He charges his angels with error.
18_4_18.CSB17: If God puts no trust in his servants and he charges his angels with foolishness,
18_4_18.AMP: 'God puts no trust or confidence, even in His [heavenly] servants, And He charges His angels with error.
18_4_18.MSG: Why, God doesn't even trust his own servants,doesn't even cheer his angels,
18_4_18.NIV: If God places no trust in his servants,if he charges his angels with error,
18_4_18.NRSVCE: Even in his servants he puts no trust, and his angels he charges with error;

18_4_19.NKJV: How much more those who dwell in houses of clay, Whose foundation is in the dust, Who are crushed before a moth?
18_4_19.KJV: How much less in them that dwell in houses of clay, whose foundation is in the dust, which are crushed before the moth?
18_4_19.ESV: how much more those who dwell in houses of clay, whose foundation is in the dust, who are crushed like the moth.
18_4_19.NASB: 'How much more those who dwell in houses of clay, Whose foundation is in the dust, Who are crushed before the moth!
18_4_19.NLT: how much less will he trust people made of clay!They are made of dust, crushed as easily as a moth.
18_4_19.WEB: How much more, those who dwell in houses of clay, whose foundation is in the dust, who are crushed before the moth!
18_4_19.CSB17: how much more those who dwell in clay houses, whose foundation is in the dust, who are crushed like a moth!
18_4_19.AMP: 'How much more [will He blame and charge] those who dwell in houses (bodies) of clay, Whose foundations are in the dust, Who are crushed like a moth.
18_4_19.MSG: So how much less these bodies composed of mud,fragile as moths?
18_4_19.NIV: how much more those who live in houses of clay,whose foundations are in the dust,who are crushed more readily than a moth!
18_4_19.NRSVCE: how much more those who live in houses of clay, whose foundation is in the dust, who are crushed like a moth.

18_4_20.NKJV: They are broken in pieces from morning till evening; They perish forever, with no one regarding.
18_4_20.KJV: They are destroyed from morning to evening: they perish for ever without any regarding it. destroyed: Heb. beaten in pieces
18_4_20.ESV: Between morning and evening they are beaten to pieces; they perish forever without anyone regarding it.
18_4_20.NASB: 'Between morning and evening they are broken in pieces; Unobserved, they perish forever.
18_4_20.NLT: They are alive in the morning but dead by evening,gone forever without a trace.
18_4_20.WEB: Between morning and evening they are destroyed. They perish forever without any regarding it.
18_4_20.CSB17: They are smashed to pieces from dawn to dusk; they perish forever while no one notices.
18_4_20.AMP: 'Between morning and evening they are broken in pieces and destroyed; Unobserved and unnoticed, they perish forever.
18_4_20.MSG: These bodies of ours are here today and gone tomorrow,and no one even notices—gone without a trace.
18_4_20.NIV: Between dawn and dusk they are broken to pieces;unnoticed, they perish forever.
18_4_20.NRSVCE: Between morning and evening they are destroyed; they perish forever without any regarding it.

18_4_21.NKJV: Does not their own excellence go away? They die, even without wisdom.’
18_4_21.KJV: Doth not their excellency which is in them go away? they die, even without wisdom.
18_4_21.ESV: Is not their tent-cord plucked up within them, do they not die, and that without wisdom?’
18_4_21.NASB: 'Is not their tent-cord plucked up within them? They die, yet without wisdom.'
18_4_21.NLT: Their tent-cords are pulled and the tent collapses,and they die in ignorance.
18_4_21.WEB: Isn’t their tent cord plucked up within them? They die, and that without wisdom.’
18_4_21.CSB17: Are their tent cords not pulled up? They die without wisdom.
18_4_21.AMP: 'Is not their tent-cord drawn up within them [so that the tent collapses]? Do they not die, and yet without [acquiring] wisdom?'
18_4_21.MSG: When the tent stakes are ripped up, the tent collapses—we die and are never the wiser for having lived.'
18_4_21.NIV: Are not the cords of their tent pulled up,so that they die without wisdom?’
18_4_21.NRSVCE: Their tent-cord is plucked up within them, and they die devoid of wisdom.’

18_5_1.NKJV: “Call out now; Is there anyone who will answer you? And to which of the holy ones will you turn?
18_5_1.KJV: Call now, if there be any that will answer thee; and to which of the saints wilt thou turn? turn: or, look?
18_5_1.ESV: “Call now; is there anyone who will answer you? To which of the holy ones will you turn?
18_5_1.NASB: 'Call now, is there anyone who will answer you? And to which of the holy ones will you turn?
18_5_1.NLT: “Cry for help, but will anyone answer you?Which of the angels will help you?
18_5_1.WEB: “Call now; is there any who will answer you? To which of the holy ones will you turn?
18_5_1.CSB17: Call out! Will anyone answer you? Which of the holy ones will you turn to?
18_5_1.AMP: "Call now—is there anyone who will answer you? And to which of the holy ones (angels) will you turn?
18_5_1.MSG: "Call for help, Job, if you think anyone will answer!To which of the holy angels will you turn?
18_5_1.NIV: “Call if you will, but who will answer you?To which of the holy ones will you turn?
18_5_1.NRSVCE: “Call now; is there anyone who will answer you? To which of the holy ones will you turn?

18_5_2.NKJV: For wrath kills a foolish man, And envy slays a simple one.
18_5_2.KJV: For wrath killeth the foolish man, and envy slayeth the silly one. envy: or, indignation
18_5_2.ESV: Surely vexation kills the fool, and jealousy slays the simple.
18_5_2.NASB: 'For anger slays the foolish man, And jealousy kills the simple.
18_5_2.NLT: Surely resentment destroys the fool,and jealousy kills the simple.
18_5_2.WEB: For resentment kills the foolish man, and jealousy kills the simple.
18_5_2.CSB17: For anger kills a fool, and jealousy slays the gullible.
18_5_2.AMP: "For anger slays the foolish man, And jealousy kills the simple (naive).
18_5_2.MSG: The hot temper of a fool eventually kills him,the jealous anger of a simpleton does her in.
18_5_2.NIV: Resentment kills a fool,and envy slays the simple.
18_5_2.NRSVCE: Surely vexation kills the fool, and jealousy slays the simple.

18_5_3.NKJV: I have seen the foolish taking root, But suddenly I cursed his dwelling place.
18_5_3.KJV: I have seen the foolish taking root: but suddenly I cursed his habitation.
18_5_3.ESV: I have seen the fool taking root, but suddenly I cursed his dwelling.
18_5_3.NASB: 'I have seen the foolish taking root, And I cursed his abode immediately.
18_5_3.NLT: I have seen that fools may be successful for the moment,but then comes sudden disaster.
18_5_3.WEB: I have seen the foolish taking root, but suddenly I cursed his habitation.
18_5_3.CSB17: I have seen a fool taking root, but I immediately pronounced a curse on his home.
18_5_3.AMP: "I have seen the foolish taking root [and outwardly prospering], But I cursed his dwelling immediately [for his destruction was certain].
18_5_3.MSG: I've seen it myself—seen fools putting down roots,and then, suddenly, their houses are cursed.
18_5_3.NIV: I myself have seen a fool taking root,but suddenly his house was cursed.
18_5_3.NRSVCE: I have seen fools taking root, but suddenly I cursed their dwelling.

18_5_4.NKJV: His sons are far from safety, They are crushed in the gate, And there is no deliverer.
18_5_4.KJV: His children are far from safety, and they are crushed in the gate, neither is there any to deliver them.
18_5_4.ESV: His children are far from safety; they are crushed in the gate, and there is no one to deliver them.
18_5_4.NASB: 'His sons are far from safety, They are even oppressed in the gate, And there is no deliverer.
18_5_4.NLT: Their children are abandoned far from help;they are crushed in court with no one to defend them.
18_5_4.WEB: His children are far from safety. They are crushed in the gate. Neither is there any to deliver them,
18_5_4.CSB17: His children are far from safety. They are crushed at the city gate, with no one to rescue them.
18_5_4.AMP: "His children are far from safety [and included in their father's ruin], They are oppressed and crushed in the [court of justice in the city's] gate, And there is no one to rescue them.
18_5_4.MSG: Their children out in the cold, abused and exploited,with no one to stick up for them.
18_5_4.NIV: His children are far from safety,crushed in court without a defender.
18_5_4.NRSVCE: Their children are far from safety, they are crushed in the gate, and there is no one to deliver them.

18_5_5.NKJV: Because the hungry eat up his harvest, Taking it even from the thorns, And a snare snatches their substance.
18_5_5.KJV: Whose harvest the hungry eateth up, and taketh it even out of the thorns, and the robber swalloweth up their substance.
18_5_5.ESV: The hungry eat his harvest, and he takes it even out of thorns, and the thirsty pant after his wealth.
18_5_5.NASB: 'His harvest the hungry devour And take it to a [place of] thorns, And the schemer is eager for their wealth.
18_5_5.NLT: The hungry devour their harvest,even when it is guarded by brambles.The thirsty pant after their wealth.
18_5_5.WEB: whose harvest the hungry eats up, and take it even out of the thorns. The snare gapes for their substance.
18_5_5.CSB17: The hungry consume his harvest, even taking it out of the thorns. The thirsty pant for his children’s wealth.
18_5_5.AMP: "The hungry devour his harvest And take it even [when it grows] among the thorns; The trap opens for [his] wealth.
18_5_5.MSG: Hungry people off the street plunder their harvests,cleaning them out completely, taking thorns and all,insatiable for everything they have.
18_5_5.NIV: The hungry consume his harvest,taking it even from among thorns,and the thirsty pant after his wealth.
18_5_5.NRSVCE: The hungry eat their harvest, and they take it even out of the thorns; and the thirsty pant after their wealth.

18_5_6.NKJV: For affliction does not come from the dust, Nor does trouble spring from the ground;
18_5_6.KJV: Although affliction cometh not forth of the dust, neither doth trouble spring out of the ground; affliction: or, iniquity
18_5_6.ESV: For affliction does not come from the dust, nor does trouble sprout from the ground,
18_5_6.NASB: 'For affliction does not come from the dust, Nor does trouble sprout from the ground,
18_5_6.NLT: But evil does not spring from the soil,and trouble does not sprout from the earth.
18_5_6.WEB: For affliction doesn’t come out of the dust, neither does trouble spring out of the ground;
18_5_6.CSB17: For distress does not grow out of the soil, and trouble does not sprout from the ground.
18_5_6.AMP: "For affliction does not come forth from the dust, Nor does trouble spring forth from the ground.
18_5_6.MSG: Don't blame fate when things go wrong—trouble doesn't come from nowhere.
18_5_6.NIV: For hardship does not spring from the soil,nor does trouble sprout from the ground.
18_5_6.NRSVCE: For misery does not come from the earth, nor does trouble sprout from the ground;

18_5_7.NKJV: Yet man is born to trouble, As the sparks fly upward.
18_5_7.KJV: Yet man is born unto trouble, as the sparks fly upward. trouble: or, labour sparks: Heb. the sons of the burning coal lift up to fly
18_5_7.ESV: but man is born to trouble as the sparks fly upward.
18_5_7.NASB: For man is born for trouble, As sparks fly upward.
18_5_7.NLT: People are born for troubleas readily as sparks fly up from a fire.
18_5_7.WEB: but man is born to trouble, as the sparks fly upward.
18_5_7.CSB17: But humans are born for trouble as surely as sparks fly upward.
18_5_7.AMP: "For man is born for trouble, [As naturally] as sparks fly upward.
18_5_7.MSG: It's human! Mortals are born and bred for trouble,as certainly as sparks fly upward.
18_5_7.NIV: Yet man is born to troubleas surely as sparks fly upward.
18_5_7.NRSVCE: but human beings are born to trouble just as sparks fly upward.

18_5_8.NKJV: “But as for me, I would seek God, And to God I would commit my cause—
18_5_8.KJV: I would seek unto God, and unto God would I commit my cause:
18_5_8.ESV: “As for me, I would seek God, and to God would I commit my cause,
18_5_8.NASB: 'But as for me, I would seek God, And I would place my cause before God;
18_5_8.NLT: “If I were you, I would go to Godand present my case to him.
18_5_8.WEB: “But as for me, I would seek God. I would commit my cause to God,
18_5_8.CSB17: However, if I were you, I would appeal to God and would present my case to him.
18_5_8.AMP: "As for me, I would seek God and inquire of Him, And I would commit my cause to God;
18_5_8.MSG: "If I were in your shoes, I'd go straight to God,I'd throw myself on the mercy of God.
18_5_8.NIV: “But if it were I, I would appeal to God;I would lay my cause before him.
18_5_8.NRSVCE: “As for me, I would seek God, and to God I would commit my cause.

18_5_9.NKJV: Who does great things, and unsearchable, Marvelous things without number.
18_5_9.KJV: Which doeth great things and unsearchable ; marvellous things without number: unsearchable: Heb. there is no search without: Heb. till there be no number
18_5_9.ESV: who does great things and unsearchable, marvelous things without number:
18_5_9.NASB: Who does great and unsearchable things, Wonders without number.
18_5_9.NLT: He does great things too marvelous to understand.He performs countless miracles.
18_5_9.WEB: who does great things that can’t be fathomed, marvelous things without number;
18_5_9.CSB17: He does great and unsearchable things, wonders without number.
18_5_9.AMP: Who does great and unsearchable things, Marvelous things without number.
18_5_9.MSG: After all, he's famous for great and unexpected acts;there's no end to his surprises.
18_5_9.NIV: He performs wonders that cannot be fathomed,miracles that cannot be counted.
18_5_9.NRSVCE: He does great things and unsearchable, marvelous things without number.

18_5_10.NKJV: He gives rain on the earth, And sends waters on the fields.
18_5_10.KJV: Who giveth rain upon the earth, and sendeth waters upon the fields: fields: Heb. outplaces
18_5_10.ESV: he gives rain on the earth and sends waters on the fields;
18_5_10.NASB: 'He gives rain on the earth And sends water on the fields,
18_5_10.NLT: He gives rain for the earthand water for the fields.
18_5_10.WEB: who gives rain on the earth, and sends waters on the fields;
18_5_10.CSB17: He gives rain to the earth and sends water to the fields.
18_5_10.AMP: "He gives rain upon the earth And sends waters upon the fields,
18_5_10.MSG: He gives rain, for instance, across the wide earth,sends water to irrigate the fields.
18_5_10.NIV: He bestows rain on the earth;he sends water upon the countryside.
18_5_10.NRSVCE: He gives rain on the earth and sends waters on the fields;

18_5_11.NKJV: He sets on high those who are lowly, And those who mourn are lifted to safety.
18_5_11.KJV: To set up on high those that be low; that those which mourn may be exalted to safety.
18_5_11.ESV: he sets on high those who are lowly, and those who mourn are lifted to safety.
18_5_11.NASB: So that He sets on high those who are lowly, And those who mourn are lifted to safety.
18_5_11.NLT: He gives prosperity to the poorand protects those who suffer.
18_5_11.WEB: so that he sets up on high those who are low, those who mourn are exalted to safety.
18_5_11.CSB17: He sets the lowly on high, and mourners are lifted to safety.
18_5_11.AMP: So that He sets on high those who are lowly, And He lifts to safety those who mourn.
18_5_11.MSG: He raises up the down-and-out,gives firm footing to those sinking in grief.
18_5_11.NIV: The lowly he sets on high,and those who mourn are lifted to safety.
18_5_11.NRSVCE: he sets on high those who are lowly, and those who mourn are lifted to safety.

18_5_12.NKJV: He frustrates the devices of the crafty, So that their hands cannot carry out their plans.
18_5_12.KJV: He disappointeth the devices of the crafty, so that their hands cannot perform their enterprise. their enterprise: or, any thing
18_5_12.ESV: He frustrates the devices of the crafty, so that their hands achieve no success.
18_5_12.NASB: 'He frustrates the plotting of the shrewd, So that their hands cannot attain success.
18_5_12.NLT: He frustrates the plans of schemersso the work of their hands will not succeed.
18_5_12.WEB: He frustrates the devices of the crafty, So that their hands can’t perform their enterprise.
18_5_12.CSB17: He frustrates the schemes of the crafty so that they achieve no success.
18_5_12.AMP: "He frustrates the devices and schemes of the crafty, So that their hands cannot attain success or achieve anything of [lasting] worth.
18_5_12.MSG: He aborts the schemes of conniving crooks,so that none of their plots come to term.
18_5_12.NIV: He thwarts the plans of the crafty,so that their hands achieve no success.
18_5_12.NRSVCE: He frustrates the devices of the crafty, so that their hands achieve no success.

18_5_13.NKJV: He catches the wise in their own craftiness, And the counsel of the cunning comes quickly upon them.
18_5_13.KJV: He taketh the wise in their own craftiness: and the counsel of the froward is carried headlong.
18_5_13.ESV: He catches the wise in their own craftiness, and the schemes of the wily are brought to a quick end.
18_5_13.NASB: 'He captures the wise by their own shrewdness, And the advice of the cunning is quickly thwarted.
18_5_13.NLT: He traps the wise in their own clevernessso their cunning schemes are thwarted.
18_5_13.WEB: He takes the wise in their own craftiness; the counsel of the cunning is carried headlong.
18_5_13.CSB17: He traps the wise in their craftiness so that the plans of the deceptive are quickly brought to an end.
18_5_13.AMP: "He catches the [so-called] wise in their own shrewdness, And the advice of the devious is quickly thwarted.
18_5_13.MSG: He catches the know-it-alls in their conspiracies—all that intricate intrigue swept out with the trash!
18_5_13.NIV: He catches the wise in their craftiness,and the schemes of the wily are swept away.
18_5_13.NRSVCE: He takes the wise in their own craftiness; and the schemes of the wily are brought to a quick end.

18_5_14.NKJV: They meet with darkness in the daytime, And grope at noontime as in the night.
18_5_14.KJV: They meet with darkness in the daytime, and grope in the noonday as in the night. meet: or, run into
18_5_14.ESV: They meet with darkness in the daytime and grope at noonday as in the night.
18_5_14.NASB: 'By day they meet with darkness, And grope at noon as in the night.
18_5_14.NLT: They find it is dark in the daytime,and they grope at noon as if it were night.
18_5_14.WEB: They meet with darkness in the day time, and grope at noonday as in the night.
18_5_14.CSB17: They encounter darkness by day, and they grope at noon as if it were night.
18_5_14.AMP: "In the daytime they meet in darkness, And at noon they grope as in the night.
18_5_14.MSG: Suddenly they're disoriented, plunged into darkness;they can't see to put one foot in front of the other.
18_5_14.NIV: Darkness comes upon them in the daytime;at noon they grope as in the night.
18_5_14.NRSVCE: They meet with darkness in the daytime, and grope at noonday as in the night.

18_5_15.NKJV: But He saves the needy from the sword, From the mouth of the mighty, And from their hand.
18_5_15.KJV: But he saveth the poor from the sword, from their mouth, and from the hand of the mighty.
18_5_15.ESV: But he saves the needy from the sword of their mouth and from the hand of the mighty.
18_5_15.NASB: 'But He saves from the sword of their mouth, And the poor from the hand of the mighty.
18_5_15.NLT: He rescues the poor from the cutting words of the strong,and rescues them from the clutches of the powerful.
18_5_15.WEB: But he saves from the sword of their mouth, even the needy from the hand of the mighty.
18_5_15.CSB17: He saves the needy from their sharp words and from the clutches of the powerful.
18_5_15.AMP: "But God saves [the innocent] from the sword of the mouth of the devious, And the poor from the hand of the mighty.
18_5_15.MSG: But the downtrodden are saved by God,saved from the murderous plots, saved from the iron fist.
18_5_15.NIV: He saves the needy from the sword in their mouth;he saves them from the clutches of the powerful.
18_5_15.NRSVCE: But he saves the needy from the sword of their mouth, from the hand of the mighty.

18_5_16.NKJV: So the poor have hope, And injustice shuts her mouth.
18_5_16.KJV: So the poor hath hope, and iniquity stoppeth her mouth.
18_5_16.ESV: So the poor have hope, and injustice shuts her mouth.
18_5_16.NASB: 'So the helpless has hope, And unrighteousness must shut its mouth.
18_5_16.NLT: And so at last the poor have hope,and the snapping jaws of the wicked are shut.
18_5_16.WEB: So the poor has hope, and injustice shuts her mouth.
18_5_16.CSB17: So the poor have hope, and injustice shuts its mouth.
18_5_16.AMP: "So the helpless have hope, And injustice shuts its mouth.
18_5_16.MSG: And so the poor continue to hope,while injustice is bound and gagged.
18_5_16.NIV: So the poor have hope,and injustice shuts its mouth.
18_5_16.NRSVCE: So the poor have hope, and injustice shuts its mouth.

18_5_17.NKJV: “Behold, happy is the man whom God corrects; Therefore do not despise the chastening of the Almighty.
18_5_17.KJV: Behold, happy is the man whom God correcteth: therefore despise not thou the chastening of the Almighty:
18_5_17.ESV: “Behold, blessed is the one whom God reproves; therefore despise not the discipline of the Almighty.
18_5_17.NASB: 'Behold, how happy is the man whom God reproves, So do not despise the discipline of the Almighty.
18_5_17.NLT: “But consider the joy of those corrected by God!Do not despise the discipline of the Almighty when you sin.
18_5_17.WEB: “Behold, happy is the man whom God corrects. Therefore do not despise the chastening of the Almighty.
18_5_17.CSB17: See how happy is the person whom God corrects; so do not reject the discipline of the Almighty.
18_5_17.AMP: "Behold, how happy and fortunate is the man whom God reproves, So do not despise or reject the discipline of the Almighty [subjecting you to trial and suffering].
18_5_17.MSG: "So, what a blessing when God steps in and corrects you!Mind you, don't despise the discipline of Almighty God!
18_5_17.NIV: “Blessed is the man whom God corrects;so do not despise the discipline of the Almighty.
18_5_17.NRSVCE: “How happy is the one whom God reproves; therefore do not despise the discipline of the Almighty.

18_5_18.NKJV: For He bruises, but He binds up; He wounds, but His hands make whole.
18_5_18.KJV: For he maketh sore, and bindeth up: he woundeth, and his hands make whole.
18_5_18.ESV: For he wounds, but he binds up; he shatters, but his hands heal.
18_5_18.NASB: 'For He inflicts pain, and gives relief; He wounds, and His hands [also] heal.
18_5_18.NLT: For though he wounds, he also bandages.He strikes, but his hands also heal.
18_5_18.WEB: For he wounds, and binds up. He injures, and his hands make whole.
18_5_18.CSB17: For he wounds but he also bandages; he strikes, but his hands also heal.
18_5_18.AMP: "For He inflicts pain, but He binds up and gives relief; He wounds, but His hands also heal.
18_5_18.MSG: True, he wounds, but he also dresses the wound;the same hand that hurts you, heals you.
18_5_18.NIV: For he wounds, but he also binds up;he injures, but his hands also heal.
18_5_18.NRSVCE: For he wounds, but he binds up; he strikes, but his hands heal.

18_5_19.NKJV: He shall deliver you in six troubles, Yes, in seven no evil shall touch you.
18_5_19.KJV: He shall deliver thee in six troubles: yea, in seven there shall no evil touch thee.
18_5_19.ESV: He will deliver you from six troubles; in seven no evil shall touch you.
18_5_19.NASB: 'From six troubles He will deliver you, Even in seven evil will not touch you.
18_5_19.NLT: From six disasters he will rescue you;even in the seventh, he will keep you from evil.
18_5_19.WEB: He will deliver you in six troubles; yes, in seven no evil shall touch you.
18_5_19.CSB17: He will rescue you from six calamities; no harm will touch you in seven.
18_5_19.AMP: "He will rescue you from six troubles; Even in seven, evil will not touch you.
18_5_19.MSG: From one disaster after another he delivers you;no matter what the calamity, the evil can't touch you—
18_5_19.NIV: From six calamities he will rescue you;in seven no harm will befall you.

18_8_10.MSG: So why not let the ancients teach you, tell you what's what,instruct you in what they knew from experience?
18_8_10.NIV: Will they not instruct you and tell you?Will they not bring forth words from their understanding?
18_8_10.NRSVCE: Will they not teach you and tell you and utter words out of their understanding?

18_8_11.NKJV: “Can the papyrus grow up without a marsh? Can the reeds flourish without water?
18_8_11.KJV: Can the rush grow up without mire? can the flag grow without water?
18_8_11.ESV: “Can papyrus grow where there is no marsh? Can reeds flourish where there is no water?
18_8_11.NASB: 'Can the papyrus grow up without a marsh? Can the rushes grow without water?
18_8_11.NLT: “Can papyrus reeds grow tall without a marsh?Can marsh grass flourish without water?
18_8_11.WEB: “Can the papyrus grow up without mire? Can the rushes grow without water?
18_8_11.CSB17: Does papyrus grow where there is no marsh? Do reeds flourish without water?
18_8_11.AMP: "Can the papyrus grow up without a marsh? Can the rushes or reed grass grow without water?
18_8_11.MSG: Can mighty pine trees grow tall without soil?Can luscious tomatoes flourish without water?
18_8_11.NIV: Can papyrus grow tall where there is no marsh?Can reeds thrive without water?
18_8_11.NRSVCE: “Can papyrus grow where there is no marsh? Can reeds flourish where there is no water?

18_8_12.NKJV: While it is yet green and not cut down, It withers before any other plant.
18_8_12.KJV: Whilst it is yet in his greenness, and not cut down, it withereth before any other herb.
18_8_12.ESV: While yet in flower and not cut down, they wither before any other plant.
18_8_12.NASB: 'While it is still green [and] not cut down, Yet it withers before any [other] plant.
18_8_12.NLT: While they are still flowering, not ready to be cut,they begin to wither more quickly than grass.
18_8_12.WEB: While it is yet in its greenness, not cut down, it withers before any other reed.
18_8_12.CSB17: While still uncut shoots, they would dry up quicker than any other plant.
18_8_12.AMP: "While it is still green (in flower) and not cut down, Yet it withers before any other plant [when without water].
18_8_12.MSG: Blossoming flowers look great before they're cut or picked,but without soil or water they wither more quickly than grass.
18_8_12.NIV: While still growing and uncut,they wither more quickly than grass.
18_8_12.NRSVCE: While yet in flower and not cut down, they wither before any other plant.

18_8_13.NKJV: So are the paths of all who forget God; And the hope of the hypocrite shall perish,
18_8_13.KJV: So are the paths of all that forget God; and the hypocrite's hope shall perish:
18_8_13.ESV: Such are the paths of all who forget God; the hope of the godless shall perish.
18_8_13.NASB: 'So are the paths of all who forget God; And the hope of the godless will perish,
18_8_13.NLT: The same happens to all who forget God.The hopes of the godless evaporate.
18_8_13.WEB: So are the paths of all who forget God. The hope of the godless man shall perish,
18_8_13.CSB17: Such is the destiny of all who forget God; the hope of the godless will perish.
18_8_13.AMP: "So are the paths of all who forget God; And the hope of the godless will perish,
18_8_13.MSG: That's what happens to all who forget God—all their hopes come to nothing.
18_8_13.NIV: Such is the destiny of all who forget God;so perishes the hope of the godless.
18_8_13.NRSVCE: Such are the paths of all who forget God; the hope of the godless shall perish.

18_8_14.NKJV: Whose confidence shall be cut off, And whose trust is a spider’s web.
18_8_14.KJV: Whose hope shall be cut off, and whose trust shall be a spider's web. web: Heb. house
18_8_14.ESV: His confidence is severed, and his trust is a spider's web.
18_8_14.NASB: Whose confidence is fragile, And whose trust a spider's web.
18_8_14.NLT: Their confidence hangs by a thread.They are leaning on a spider’s web.
18_8_14.WEB: Whose confidence shall break apart, Whose trust is a spider’s web.
18_8_14.CSB17: His source of confidence is fragile; what he trusts in is a spider’s web.
18_8_14.AMP: For his confidence is fragile and breaks, And his trust is [like] a spider's web.
18_8_14.MSG: They hang their life from one thin thread,they hitch their fate to a spider web.
18_8_14.NIV: What he trusts in is fragile;what he relies on is a spider’s web.
18_8_14.NRSVCE: Their confidence is gossamer, a spider’s house their trust.

18_8_15.NKJV: He leans on his house, but it does not stand. He holds it fast, but it does not endure.
18_8_15.KJV: He shall lean upon his house, but it shall not stand: he shall hold it fast, but it shall not endure.
18_8_15.ESV: He leans against his house, but it does not stand; he lays hold of it, but it does not endure.
18_8_15.NASB: 'He trusts in his house, but it does not stand; He holds fast to it, but it does not endure.
18_8_15.NLT: They cling to their home for security, but it won’t last.They try to hold it tight, but it will not endure.
18_8_15.WEB: He shall lean on his house, but it shall not stand. He shall cling to it, but it shall not endure.
18_8_15.CSB17: He leans on his web, but it doesn’t stand firm. He grabs it, but it does not hold up.
18_8_15.AMP: "He trusts in his house, but it does not stand; He holds tightly to it, but it does not endure.
18_8_15.MSG: One jiggle and the thread breaks,one jab and the web collapses.
18_8_15.NIV: He leans on his web, but it gives way;he clings to it, but it does not hold.
18_8_15.NRSVCE: If one leans against its house, it will not stand; if one lays hold of it, it will not endure.

18_8_16.NKJV: He grows green in the sun, And his branches spread out in his garden.
18_8_16.KJV: He is green before the sun, and his branch shooteth forth in his garden.
18_8_16.ESV: He is a lush plant before the sun, and his shoots spread over his garden.
18_8_16.NASB: 'He thrives before the sun, And his shoots spread out over his garden.
18_8_16.NLT: The godless seem like a lush plant growing in the sunshine,its branches spreading across the garden.
18_8_16.WEB: He is green before the sun. His shoots go out along his garden.
18_8_16.CSB17: He is a well-watered plant in the sunshine; his shoots spread out over his garden.
18_8_16.AMP: "He thrives and prospers [like a green plant] before the sun, And his branches spread out over his garden.
18_8_16.MSG: Or they're like weeds springing up in the sunshine,invading the garden,
18_8_16.NIV: He is like a well-watered plant in the sunshine,spreading its shoots over the garden;
18_8_16.NRSVCE: The wicked thrive before the sun, and their shoots spread over the garden.

18_8_17.NKJV: His roots wrap around the rock heap, And look for a place in the stones.
18_8_17.KJV: His roots are wrapped about the heap, and seeth the place of stones.
18_8_17.ESV: His roots entwine the stone heap; he looks upon a house of stones.
18_8_17.NASB: 'His roots wrap around a rock pile, He grasps a house of stones.
18_8_17.NLT: Its roots grow down through a pile of stones;it takes hold on a bed of rocks.
18_8_17.WEB: His roots are wrapped around the rock pile. He sees the place of stones.
18_8_17.CSB17: His roots are intertwined around a pile of rocks. He looks for a home among the stones.
18_8_17.AMP: "His [godless] roots are wrapped around a pile of rocks, And he gazes at a house of stones.
18_8_17.MSG: Spreading everywhere, overtaking the flowers,getting a foothold even in the rocks.
18_8_17.NIV: it entwines its roots around a pile of rocksand looks for a place among the stones.
18_8_17.NRSVCE: Their roots twine around the stoneheap; they live among the rocks.

18_8_18.NKJV: If he is destroyed from his place, Then it will deny him, saying, ‘I have not seen you.’
18_8_18.KJV: If he destroy him from his place, then it shall deny him, saying, I have not seen thee.
18_8_18.ESV: If he is destroyed from his place, then it will deny him, saying, ‘I have never seen you.’
18_8_18.NASB: 'If he is removed from his place, Then it will deny him, [saying], 'I never saw you.'
18_8_18.NLT: But when it is uprooted,it’s as though it never existed!
18_8_18.WEB: If he is destroyed from his place, then it shall deny him, saying, ‘I have not seen you.’
18_8_18.CSB17: If he is uprooted from his place, it will deny knowing him, saying, “I never saw you.”
18_8_18.AMP: "If he is snatched from his place [in the garden], Then his place will forget him, saying, 'I have never seen you.'
18_8_18.MSG: But when the gardener rips them out by the roots,the garden doesn't miss them one bit.
18_8_18.NIV: But when it is torn from its spot,that place disowns it and says, ‘I never saw you.’
18_8_18.NRSVCE: If they are destroyed from their place, then it will deny them, saying, ‘I have never seen you.’

18_8_19.NKJV: “Behold, this is the joy of His way, And out of the earth others will grow.
18_8_19.KJV: Behold, this is the joy of his way, and out of the earth shall others grow.
18_8_19.ESV: Behold, this is the joy of his way, and out of the soil others will spring.
18_8_19.NASB: 'Behold, this is the joy of His way; And out of the dust others will spring.
18_8_19.NLT: That’s the end of its life,and others spring up from the earth to replace it.
18_8_19.WEB: Behold, this is the joy of his way: out of the earth, others shall spring.
18_8_19.CSB17: Surely this is the joy of his way of life; yet others will sprout from the dust.
18_8_19.AMP: "Behold, this is the joy of His way; And from out of the dust others will spring up and grow [to take his place].
18_8_19.MSG: The sooner the godless are gone, the better;then good plants can grow in their place.
18_8_19.NIV: Surely its life withers away,and from the soil other plants grow.
18_8_19.NRSVCE: See, these are their happy ways, and out of the earth still others will spring.

18_8_20.NKJV: Behold, God will not cast away the blameless, Nor will He uphold the evildoers.
18_8_20.KJV: Behold, God will not cast away a perfect man, neither will he help the evil doers: help: Heb. take the ungodly by the hand
18_8_20.ESV: “Behold, God will not reject a blameless man, nor take the hand of evildoers.
18_8_20.NASB: 'Lo, God will not reject [a man of] integrity, Nor will He support the evildoers.
18_8_20.NLT: “But look, God will not reject a person of integrity,nor will he lend a hand to the wicked.
18_8_20.WEB: “Behold, God will not cast away a blameless man, neither will he uphold the evildoers.
18_8_20.CSB17: Look, God does not reject a person of integrity, and he will not support evildoers.
18_8_20.AMP: "Behold, God will not reject a man of integrity, Nor will He strengthen or support evildoers.
18_8_20.MSG: "There's no way that God will reject a good person,and there is no way he'll help a bad one.
18_8_20.NIV: “Surely God does not reject a blameless manor strengthen the hands of evildoers.
18_8_20.NRSVCE: “See, God will not reject a blameless person, nor take the hand of evildoers.

18_8_21.NKJV: He will yet fill your mouth with laughing, And your lips with rejoicing.
18_8_21.KJV: Till he fill thy mouth with laughing, and thy lips with rejoicing. rejoicing: Heb. shouting for joy
18_8_21.ESV: He will yet fill your mouth with laughter, and your lips with shouting.
18_8_21.NASB: 'He will yet fill your mouth with laughter And your lips with shouting.
18_8_21.NLT: He will once again fill your mouth with laughterand your lips with shouts of joy.
18_8_21.WEB: He will still fill your mouth with laughter, your lips with shouting.
18_8_21.CSB17: He will yet fill your mouth with laughter and your lips with a shout of joy.
18_8_21.AMP: "He will yet fill your mouth with laughter And your lips with joyful shouting [if you are found blameless].
18_8_21.MSG: God will let you laugh again;you'll raise the roof with shouts of joy,
18_8_21.NIV: He will yet fill your mouth with laughterand your lips with shouts of joy.
18_8_21.NRSVCE: He will yet fill your mouth with laughter, and your lips with shouts of joy.

18_8_22.NKJV: Those who hate you will be clothed with shame, And the dwelling place of the wicked will come to nothing.”
18_8_22.KJV: They that hate thee shall be clothed with shame; and the dwelling place of the wicked shall come to nought. shall come: Heb. shall not be
18_8_22.ESV: Those who hate you will be clothed with shame, and the tent of the wicked will be no more.”
18_8_22.NASB: 'Those who hate you will be clothed with shame, And the tent of the wicked will be no longer.'
18_8_22.NLT: Those who hate you will be clothed with shame,and the home of the wicked will be destroyed.”
18_8_22.WEB: Those who hate you shall be clothed with shame. The tent of the wicked shall be no more.”
18_8_22.CSB17: Your enemies will be clothed with shame; the tent of the wicked will no longer exist.
18_8_22.AMP: "Those who hate you will be clothed with shame, And the tents of the wicked will be no longer."
18_8_22.MSG: With your enemies thoroughly discredited,their house of cards collapsed."
18_8_22.NIV: Your enemies will be clothed in shame,and the tents of the wicked will be no more.”
18_8_22.NRSVCE: Those who hate you will be clothed with shame, and the tent of the wicked will be no more.”

18_9_2.NKJV: “Truly I know it is so, But how can a man be righteous before God?
18_9_2.KJV: I know it is so of a truth: but how should man be just with God? with God: or, before God?
18_9_2.ESV: “Truly I know that it is so: But how can a man be in the right before God?
18_9_2.NASB: 'In truth I know that this is so; But how can a man be in the right before God?
18_9_2.NLT: “Yes, I know all this is true in principle.But how can a person be declared innocent in God’s sight?
18_9_2.WEB: “Truly I know that it is so, but how can man be just with God?
18_9_2.CSB17: Yes, I know what you’ve said is true, but how can a person be justified before God?
18_9_2.AMP: "Yes, I know it is true. But how can a mortal man be right before God?
18_9_2.MSG: "So what's new? I know all this.The question is, 'How can mere mortals get right with God?'
18_9_2.NIV: “Indeed, I know that this is true.But how can a mortal be righteous before God?
18_9_2.NRSVCE: “Indeed I know that this is so; but how can a mortal be just before God?

18_9_3.NKJV: If one wished to contend with Him, He could not answer Him one time out of a thousand.
18_9_3.KJV: If he will contend with him, he cannot answer him one of a thousand.
18_9_3.ESV: If one wished to contend with him, one could not answer him once in a thousand times.
18_9_3.NASB: 'If one wished to dispute with Him, He could not answer Him once in a thousand [times].
18_9_3.NLT: If someone wanted to take God to court,would it be possible to answer him even once in a thousand times?
18_9_3.WEB: If he is pleased to contend with him, he can’t answer him one time in a thousand.
18_9_3.CSB17: If one wanted to take him to court, he could not answer God once in a thousand times.
18_9_3.AMP: "If one should want to contend or dispute with Him, He could not answer Him once in a thousand times.
18_9_3.MSG: If we wanted to bring our case before him,what chance would we have? Not one in a thousand!
18_9_3.NIV: Though one wished to dispute with him,he could not answer him one time out of a thousand.
18_9_3.NRSVCE: If one wished to contend with him, one could not answer him once in a thousand.

18_9_4.NKJV: God is wise in heart and mighty in strength. Who has hardened himself against Him and prospered?
18_9_4.KJV: He is wise in heart, and mighty in strength: who hath hardened himself against him, and hath prospered?
18_9_4.ESV: He is wise in heart and mighty in strength — who has hardened himself against him, and succeeded? —
18_9_4.NASB: 'Wise in heart and mighty in strength, Who has defied Him without harm?
18_9_4.NLT: For God is so wise and so mighty.Who has ever challenged him successfully?
18_9_4.WEB: God who is wise in heart, and mighty in strength: who has hardened himself against him, and prospered?
18_9_4.CSB17: God is wise and all-powerful. Who has opposed him and come out unharmed?
18_9_4.AMP: "God is wise in heart and mighty in strength; Who has [ever] defied or challenged Him and remained unharmed?
18_9_4.MSG: God's wisdom is so deep, God's power so immense,who could take him on and come out in one piece?
18_9_4.NIV: His wisdom is profound, his power is vast.Who has resisted him and come out unscathed?
18_9_4.NRSVCE: He is wise in heart, and mighty in strength — who has resisted him, and succeeded? —

18_9_5.NKJV: He removes the mountains, and they do not know When He overturns them in His anger;
18_9_5.KJV: Which removeth the mountains, and they know not: which overturneth them in his anger.
18_9_5.ESV: he who removes mountains, and they know it not, when he overturns them in his anger,
18_9_5.NASB: '[It is God] who removes the mountains, they know not [how], When He overturns them in His anger;
18_9_5.NLT: “Without warning, he moves the mountains,overturning them in his anger.
18_9_5.WEB: He removes the mountains, and they don’t know it, when he overturns them in his anger.
18_9_5.CSB17: He removes mountains without their knowledge, overturning them in his anger.
18_9_5.AMP: "It is God who removes the mountains, and they do not know it, When He overturns them in His anger;
18_9_5.MSG: He moves mountains before they know what's happened,flips them on their heads on a whim.
18_9_5.NIV: He moves mountains without their knowing itand overturns them in his anger.
18_9_5.NRSVCE: he who removes mountains, and they do not know it, when he overturns them in his anger;

18_9_6.NKJV: He shakes the earth out of its place, And its pillars tremble;
18_9_6.KJV: Which shaketh the earth out of her place, and the pillars thereof tremble.
18_9_6.ESV: who shakes the earth out of its place, and its pillars tremble;
18_9_6.NASB: Who shakes the earth out of its place, And its pillars tremble;
18_9_6.NLT: He shakes the earth from its place,and its foundations tremble.
18_9_6.WEB: He shakes the earth out of its place. Its pillars tremble.
18_9_6.CSB17: He shakes the earth from its place so that its pillars tremble.
18_9_6.AMP: Who shakes the earth out of its place, And its pillars tremble;
18_9_6.MSG: He gives the earth a good shaking up,rocks it down to its very foundations.
18_9_6.NIV: He shakes the earth from its placeand makes its pillars tremble.
18_9_6.NRSVCE: who shakes the earth out of its place, and its pillars tremble;

18_9_7.NKJV: He commands the sun, and it does not rise; He seals off the stars;
18_9_7.KJV: Which commandeth the sun, and it riseth not; and sealeth up the stars.
18_9_7.ESV: who commands the sun, and it does not rise; who seals up the stars;
18_9_7.NASB: Who commands the sun not to shine, And sets a seal upon the stars;
18_9_7.NLT: If he commands it, the sun won’t riseand the stars won’t shine.
18_9_7.WEB: He commands the sun, and it doesn’t rise, and seals up the stars.
18_9_7.CSB17: He commands the sun not to shine and seals off the stars.
18_9_7.AMP: Who commands the sun, and it does not shine; Who seals up the stars [from view];
18_9_7.MSG: He tells the sun, 'Don't shine,' and it doesn't;he pulls the blinds on the stars.
18_9_7.NIV: He speaks to the sun and it does not shine;he seals off the light of the stars.
18_9_7.NRSVCE: who commands the sun, and it does not rise; who seals up the stars;

18_9_8.NKJV: He alone spreads out the heavens, And treads on the waves of the sea;
18_9_8.KJV: Which alone spreadeth out the heavens, and treadeth upon the waves of the sea. waves: Heb. heights
18_9_8.ESV: who alone stretched out the heavens and trampled the waves of the sea;
18_9_8.NASB: Who alone stretches out the heavens And tramples down the waves of the sea;
18_9_8.NLT: He alone has spread out the heavensand marches on the waves of the sea.
18_9_8.WEB: He alone stretches out the heavens, and treads on the waves of the sea.
18_9_8.CSB17: He alone stretches out the heavens and treads on the waves of the sea.
18_9_8.AMP: Who alone stretches out the heavens And tramples down the waves of the sea;
18_9_8.MSG: All by himself he stretches out the heavensand strides on the waves of the sea.
18_9_8.NIV: He alone stretches out the heavensand treads on the waves of the sea.
18_9_8.NRSVCE: who alone stretched out the heavens and trampled the waves of the Sea;

18_9_9.NKJV: He made the Bear, Orion, and the Pleiades, And the chambers of the south;
18_9_9.KJV: Which maketh Arcturus, Orion, and Pleiades, and the chambers of the south. Arcturus: Heb. Ash, Cesil, and Cimah
18_9_9.ESV: who made the Bear and Orion, the Pleiades and the chambers of the south;
18_9_9.NASB: Who makes the Bear, Orion and the Pleiades, And the chambers of the south;
18_9_9.NLT: He made all the stars — the Bear and Orion,the Pleiades and the constellations of the southern sky.
18_9_9.WEB: He makes the Bear, Orion, and the Pleiades, and the rooms of the south.
18_9_9.CSB17: He makes the stars: the Bear, Orion, the Pleiades, and the constellations of the southern sky.
18_9_9.AMP: Who made [the constellations] the Bear, Orion, and the Pleiades, And the [vast starry] spaces of the south;
18_9_9.MSG: He designed the Big Dipper and Orion,the Pleiades and Alpha Centauri.
18_9_9.NIV: He is the Maker of the Bear and Orion,the Pleiades and the constellations of the south.
18_9_9.NRSVCE: who made the Bear and Orion, the Pleiades and the chambers of the south;

18_9_10.NKJV: He does great things past finding out, Yes, wonders without number.
18_9_10.KJV: Which doeth great things past finding out; yea, and wonders without number.
18_9_10.ESV: who does great things beyond searching out, and marvelous things beyond number.
18_9_10.NASB: Who does great things, unfathomable, And wondrous works without number.
18_9_10.NLT: He does great things too marvelous to understand.He performs countless miracles.
18_9_10.WEB: He does great things past finding out; yes, marvelous things without number.
18_9_10.CSB17: He does great and unsearchable things, wonders without number.
18_9_10.AMP: Who does great things, [beyond understanding,] unfathomable, Yes, marvelous and wondrous things without number.
18_9_10.MSG: We'll never comprehend all the great things he does;his miracle-surprises can't be counted.
18_9_10.NIV: He performs wonders that cannot be fathomed,miracles that cannot be counted.
18_9_10.NRSVCE: who does great things beyond understanding, and marvelous things without number.

18_9_11.NKJV: If He goes by me, I do not see Him; If He moves past, I do not perceive Him;
18_9_11.KJV: Lo, he goeth by me, and I see him not: he passeth on also, but I perceive him not.
18_9_11.ESV: Behold, he passes by me, and I see him not; he moves on, but I do not perceive him.
18_9_11.NASB: 'Were He to pass by me, I would not see Him; Were He to move past [me], I would not perceive Him.
18_9_11.NLT: “Yet when he comes near, I cannot see him.When he moves by, I do not see him go.
18_9_11.WEB: Behold, he goes by me, and I don’t see him. He passes on also, but I don’t perceive him.
18_9_11.CSB17: If he passed by me, I wouldn’t see him; if he went by, I wouldn’t recognize him.
18_9_11.AMP: "Behold, He passes by me, and I do not see Him; He moves past me, but I do not perceive Him.
18_9_11.MSG: Somehow, though he moves right in front of me, I don't see him;quietly but surely he's active, and I miss it.
18_9_11.NIV: When he passes me, I cannot see him;when he goes by, I cannot perceive him.
18_9_11.NRSVCE: Look, he passes by me, and I do not see him; he moves on, but I do not perceive him.

18_9_12.NKJV: If He takes away, who can hinder Him? Who can say to Him, ‘What are You doing?’
18_9_12.KJV: Behold, he taketh away, who can hinder him? who will say unto him, What doest thou? hinder: Heb. turn him away?
18_9_12.ESV: Behold, he snatches away; who can turn him back? Who will say to him, ‘What are you doing?’
18_9_12.NASB: 'Were He to snatch away, who could restrain Him? Who could say to Him, 'What are You doing?'
18_9_12.NLT: If he snatches someone in death, who can stop him?Who dares to ask, ‘What are you doing?’
18_9_12.WEB: Behold, he snatches away. Who can hinder him? Who will ask him, ‘What are you doing?’
18_9_12.CSB17: If he snatches something, who can stop him? Who can ask him, “What are you doing?”
18_9_12.AMP: "Behold, He snatches away; who can restrain or turn Him back? Who will say to Him, 'What are You doing?'
18_9_12.MSG: If he steals you blind, who can stop him?Who's going to say, 'Hey, what are you doing?'
18_9_12.NIV: If he snatches away, who can stop him?Who can say to him, ‘What are you doing?’
18_9_12.NRSVCE: He snatches away; who can stop him? Who will say to him, ‘What are you doing?’

18_9_13.NKJV: God will not withdraw His anger, The allies of the proud lie prostrate beneath Him.

18_10_2.NKJV: I will say to God, ‘Do not condemn me; Show me why You contend with me.

18_10_2.MSG: Job prayed:"Here's what I want to say:Don't, God, bring in a verdict of guiltywithout letting me know the charges you're bringing.

18_10_3.NKJV: Does it seem good to You that You should oppress, That You should despise the work of Your hands, And smile on the counsel of the wicked?
