18_10_22.MSG: And banished for good to the land of the dead,blind in the final dark?"
18_10_22.NIV: to the land of deepest night,of deep shadow and disorder,where even the light is like darkness.”
18_10_22.NRSVCE: the land of gloom and chaos, where light is like darkness.”

18_11_1.NKJV: Then Zophar the Naamathite answered and said:
18_11_1.KJV: Then answered Zophar the Naamathite, and said,
18_11_1.ESV: Then Zophar the Naamathite answered and said:
18_11_1.NLT: Then Zophar the Naamathite replied to Job:
18_11_1.AMP: Then Zophar the Naamathite answered and said,
18_11_1.MSG: Now it was the turn of Zophar from Naamath:

18_11_2.NKJV: “Should not the multitude of words be answered? And should a man full of talk be vindicated?
18_11_2.KJV: Should not the multitude of words be answered? and should a man full of talk be justified? full: Heb. of lips
18_11_2.ESV: “Should a multitude of words go unanswered, and a man full of talk be judged right?
18_11_2.NASB: 'Shall a multitude of words go unanswered, And a talkative man be acquitted?
18_11_2.NLT: “Shouldn’t someone answer this torrent of words?Is a person proved innocent just by a lot of talking?
18_11_2.WEB: “Shouldn’t the multitude of words be answered? Should a man full of talk be justified?
18_11_2.CSB17: Should this abundance of words go unanswered and such a talker be acquitted?
18_11_2.AMP: "Shall a multitude of words not be answered? And should a talkative man [making such a long-winded defense] be acquitted?
18_11_2.MSG: "What a flood of words! Shouldn't we put a stop to it?Should this kind of loose talk be permitted?
18_11_2.NIV: “Are all these words to go unanswered?Is this talker to be vindicated?
18_11_2.NRSVCE: “Should a multitude of words go unanswered, and should one full of talk be vindicated?

18_11_3.NKJV: Should your empty talk make men hold their peace? And when you mock, should no one rebuke you?
18_11_3.KJV: Should thy lies make men hold their peace? and when thou mockest, shall no man make thee ashamed? lies: or, devices
18_11_3.ESV: Should your babble silence men, and when you mock, shall no one shame you?
18_11_3.NASB: 'Shall your boasts silence men? And shall you scoff and none rebuke?
18_11_3.NLT: Should I remain silent while you babble on?When you mock God, shouldn’t someone make you ashamed?
18_11_3.WEB: Should your boastings make men hold their peace? When you mock, shall no man make you ashamed?
18_11_3.CSB17: Should your babbling put others to silence, so that you can keep on ridiculing with no one to humiliate you?
18_11_3.AMP: "Should your boasts and babble silence men? And shall you scoff and no one put you to shame?
18_11_3.MSG: Job, do you think you can carry on like this and we'll say nothing?That we'll let you rail and mock and not step in?
18_11_3.NIV: Will your idle talk reduce men to silence?Will no one rebuke you when you mock?
18_11_3.NRSVCE: Should your babble put others to silence, and when you mock, shall no one shame you?

18_11_4.NKJV: For you have said, ‘My doctrine is pure, And I am clean in your eyes.’
18_11_4.KJV: For thou hast said, My doctrine is pure, and I am clean in thine eyes.
18_11_4.ESV: For you say, ‘My doctrine is pure, and I am clean in God's eyes.’
18_11_4.NASB: 'For you have said, 'My teaching is pure, And I am innocent in your eyes.'
18_11_4.NLT: You claim, ‘My beliefs are pure,’and ‘I am clean in the sight of God.’
18_11_4.WEB: For you say, ‘My doctrine is pure. I am clean in your eyes.’
18_11_4.CSB17: You have said, “My teaching is sound, and I am pure in your sight.”
18_11_4.AMP: "For you have said, 'My teaching (doctrine) [that God knowingly afflicts the righteous] is pure, And I am innocent in your eyes.'
18_11_4.MSG: You claim, 'My doctrine is soundand my conduct impeccable.'
18_11_4.NIV: You say to God, ‘My beliefs are flawlessand I am pure in your sight.’
18_11_4.NRSVCE: For you say, ‘My conduct is pure, and I am clean in God’s sight.’

18_11_5.NKJV: But oh, that God would speak, And open His lips against you,
18_11_5.KJV: But oh that God would speak, and open his lips against thee;
18_11_5.ESV: But oh, that God would speak and open his lips to you,
18_11_5.NASB: 'But would that God might speak, And open His lips against you,
18_11_5.NLT: If only God would speak;if only he would tell you what he thinks!
18_11_5.WEB: But oh that God would speak, and open his lips against you,
18_11_5.CSB17: But if only God would speak and open his lips against you!
18_11_5.AMP: "But oh, that God would speak, And open His lips [to speak] against you,
18_11_5.MSG: How I wish God would give you a piece of his mind,tell you what's what!
18_11_5.NIV: Oh, how I wish that God would speak,that he would open his lips against you
18_11_5.NRSVCE: But O that God would speak, and open his lips to you,

18_11_6.NKJV: That He would show you the secrets of wisdom! For they would double your prudence. Know therefore that God exacts from you Less than your iniquity deserves.
18_11_6.KJV: And that he would shew thee the secrets of wisdom, that they are double to that which is! Know therefore that God exacteth of thee less than thine iniquity deserveth.
18_11_6.ESV: and that he would tell you the secrets of wisdom! For he is manifold in understanding. Know then that God exacts of you less than your guilt deserves.
18_11_6.NASB: And show you the secrets of wisdom! For sound wisdom has two sides. Know then that God forgets a part of your iniquity.
18_11_6.NLT: If only he would tell you the secrets of wisdom,for true wisdom is not a simple matter.Listen! God is doubtless punishing youfar less than you deserve!
18_11_6.WEB: that he would show you the secrets of wisdom! For true wisdom has two sides. Know therefore that God exacts of you less than your iniquity deserves.
18_11_6.CSB17: He would show you the secrets of wisdom, for true wisdom has two sides. Know then that God has chosen to overlook some of your iniquity.
18_11_6.AMP: And [that He would] show you the secrets of wisdom! For sound wisdom has two sides. Know therefore that God forgets a part of your wickedness and guilt.
18_11_6.MSG: I wish he'd show you how wisdom looks from the inside,for true wisdom is mostly 'inside.'But you can be sure of this,you haven't gotten half of what you deserve.
18_11_6.NIV: and disclose to you the secrets of wisdom,for true wisdom has two sides.Know this: God has even forgotten some of your sin.
18_11_6.NRSVCE: and that he would tell you the secrets of wisdom! For wisdom is many-sided. Know then that God exacts of you less than your guilt deserves.

18_11_7.NKJV: “Can you search out the deep things of God? Can you find out the limits of the Almighty?
18_11_7.KJV: Canst thou by searching find out God? canst thou find out the Almighty unto perfection?
18_11_7.ESV: “Can you find out the deep things of God? Can you find out the limit of the Almighty?
18_11_7.NASB: 'Can you discover the depths of God? Can you discover the limits of the Almighty?
18_11_7.NLT: “Can you solve the mysteries of God?Can you discover everything about the Almighty?
18_11_7.WEB: “Can you fathom the mystery of God? Or can you probe the limits of the Almighty?
18_11_7.CSB17: Can you fathom the depths of God or discover the limits of the Almighty?
18_11_7.AMP: "Can you discover the depths of God? Can you [by searching] discover the limits of the Almighty [ascend to His heights, extend to His widths, and comprehend His infinite perfection]?
18_11_7.MSG: "Do you think you can explain the mystery of God?Do you think you can diagram God Almighty?
18_11_7.NIV: “Can you fathom the mysteries of God?Can you probe the limits of the Almighty?
18_11_7.NRSVCE: “Can you find out the deep things of God? Can you find out the limit of the Almighty?

18_11_8.NKJV: They are higher than heaven— what can you do? Deeper than Sheol— what can you know?
18_11_8.KJV: It is as high as heaven; what canst thou do? deeper than hell; what canst thou know? as high: Heb. the heights of heaven
18_11_8.ESV: It is higher than heaven — what can you do? Deeper than Sheol — what can you know?
18_11_8.NASB: '[They are] high as the heavens, what can you do? Deeper than Sheol, what can you know?
18_11_8.NLT: Such knowledge is higher than the heavens —and who are you?It is deeper than the underworld —what do you know?
18_11_8.WEB: They are high as heaven. What can you do? They are deeper than Sheol. What can you know?
18_11_8.CSB17: They are higher than the heavens — what can you do? They are deeper than Sheol — what can you know?
18_11_8.AMP: "His wisdom is as high as the heights of heaven. What can you do? It is deeper than Sheol (the nether world, the place of the dead). What can you know?
18_11_8.MSG: God is far higher than you can imagine,far deeper than you can comprehend,
18_11_8.NIV: They are higher than the heavens—what can you do?They are deeper than the depths of the grave—what can you know?
18_11_8.NRSVCE: It is higher than heaven — what can you do? Deeper than Sheol — what can you know?

18_11_9.NKJV: Their measure is longer than the earth And broader than the sea.
18_11_9.KJV: The measure thereof is longer than the earth, and broader than the sea.
18_11_9.ESV: Its measure is longer than the earth and broader than the sea.
18_11_9.NASB: 'Its measure is longer than the earth And broader than the sea.
18_11_9.NLT: It is broader than the earthand wider than the sea.
18_11_9.WEB: Its measure is longer than the earth, and broader than the sea.
18_11_9.CSB17: Their measure is longer than the earth and wider than the sea.
18_11_9.AMP: "It is longer in measure [and scope] than the earth, And broader than the sea.
18_11_9.MSG: Stretching farther than earth's horizons,far wider than the endless ocean.
18_11_9.NIV: Their measure is longer than the earthand wider than the sea.
18_11_9.NRSVCE: Its measure is longer than the earth, and broader than the sea.

18_11_10.NKJV: “If He passes by, imprisons, and gathers to judgment, Then who can hinder Him?
18_11_10.KJV: If he cut off, and shut up, or gather together, then who can hinder him? cut: or, make a change hinder: Heb. turn him away?
18_11_10.ESV: If he passes through and imprisons and summons the court, who can turn him back?
18_11_10.NASB: 'If He passes by or shuts up, Or calls an assembly, who can restrain Him?
18_11_10.NLT: If God comes and puts a person in prisonor calls the court to order, who can stop him?
18_11_10.WEB: If he passes by, or confines, or convenes a court, then who can oppose him?
18_11_10.CSB17: If he passes by and throws someone in prison or convenes a court, who can stop him?
18_11_10.AMP: "If God passes by or arrests, Or calls an assembly [of judgment], who can restrain Him? [If He is against a man, who can call Him to account for it?]
18_11_10.MSG: If he happens along, throws you in jailthen hauls you into court, can you do anything about it?
18_11_10.NIV: “If he comes along and confines you in prisonand convenes a court, who can oppose him?
18_11_10.NRSVCE: If he passes through, and imprisons, and assembles for judgment, who can hinder him?

18_11_11.NKJV: For He knows deceitful men; He sees wickedness also. Will He not then consider it?
18_11_11.KJV: For he knoweth vain men: he seeth wickedness also; will he not then consider it?
18_11_11.ESV: For he knows worthless men; when he sees iniquity, will he not consider it?
18_11_11.NASB: 'For He knows false men, And He sees iniquity without investigating.
18_11_11.NLT: For he knows those who are false,and he takes note of all their sins.
18_11_11.WEB: For he knows false men. He sees iniquity also, even though he doesn’t consider it.
18_11_11.CSB17: Surely he knows which people are worthless. If he sees iniquity, will he not take note of it?
18_11_11.AMP: "For He recognizes and knows false and worthless men, And He sees wickedness, will He not consider it?
18_11_11.MSG: He sees through vain pretensions,spots evil a long way off—no one pulls the wool over his eyes!
18_11_11.NIV: Surely he recognizes deceitful men;and when he sees evil, does he not take note?
18_11_11.NRSVCE: For he knows those who are worthless; when he sees iniquity, will he not consider it?

18_11_12.NKJV: For an empty-headed man will be wise, When a wild donkey’s colt is born a man.
18_11_12.KJV: For vain man would be wise, though man be born like a wild ass's colt. vain: Heb. empty
18_11_12.ESV: But a stupid man will get understanding when a wild donkey's colt is born a man!
18_11_12.NASB: 'An idiot will become intelligent When the foal of a wild donkey is born a man.
18_11_12.NLT: An empty-headed person won’t become wiseany more than a wild donkey can bear a human child.
18_11_12.WEB: An empty-headed man becomes wise when a man is born as a wild donkey’s colt.
18_11_12.CSB17: But a stupid person will gain understanding as soon as a wild donkey is born a human!
18_11_12.AMP: "But a hollow (empty-headed) man will become intelligent and wise [Only] when the colt of a wild donkey is born as a man.
18_11_12.MSG: Hollow men, hollow women, will wise upabout the same time mules learn to talk.
18_11_12.NIV: But a witless man can no more become wisethan a wild donkey’s colt can be born a man.
18_11_12.NRSVCE: But a stupid person will get understanding, when a wild ass is born human.

18_11_13.NKJV: “If you would prepare your heart, And stretch out your hands toward Him;
18_11_13.KJV: If thou prepare thine heart, and stretch out thine hands toward him;
18_11_13.ESV: “If you prepare your heart, you will stretch out your hands toward him.
18_11_13.NASB: 'If you would direct your heart right And spread out your hand to Him,
18_11_13.NLT: “If only you would prepare your heartand lift up your hands to him in prayer!
18_11_13.WEB: “If you set your heart aright, stretch out your hands toward him.
18_11_13.CSB17: As for you, if you redirect your heart and spread out your hands to him in prayer —
18_11_13.AMP: "If you direct your heart [on the right path] And stretch out your hands to Him,
18_11_13.MSG: "Still, if you set your heart on Godand reach out to him,
18_11_13.NIV: “Yet if you devote your heart to himand stretch out your hands to him,
18_11_13.NRSVCE: “If you direct your heart rightly, you will stretch out your hands toward him.

18_11_14.NKJV: If iniquity were in your hand, and you put it far away, And would not let wickedness dwell in your tents;
18_11_14.KJV: If iniquity be in thine hand, put it far away, and let not wickedness dwell in thy tabernacles.
18_11_14.ESV: If iniquity is in your hand, put it far away, and let not injustice dwell in your tents.
18_11_14.NASB: If iniquity is in your hand, put it far away, And do not let wickedness dwell in your tents;
18_11_14.NLT: Get rid of your sins,and leave all iniquity behind you.
18_11_14.WEB: If iniquity is in your hand, put it far away. Don’t let unrighteousness dwell in your tents.
18_11_14.CSB17: if there is iniquity in your hand, remove it, and don’t allow injustice to dwell in your tents —
18_11_14.AMP: If sin is in your hand, put it far away [from you], And do not let wrongdoing dwell in your tents;
18_11_14.MSG: If you scrub your hands of sinand refuse to entertain evil in your home,
18_11_14.NIV: if you put away the sin that is in your handand allow no evil to dwell in your tent,
18_11_14.NRSVCE: If iniquity is in your hand, put it far away, and do not let wickedness reside in your tents.

18_11_15.NKJV: Then surely you could lift up your face without spot; Yes, you could be steadfast, and not fear;
18_11_15.KJV: For then shalt thou lift up thy face without spot; yea, thou shalt be stedfast, and shalt not fear:
18_11_15.ESV: Surely then you will lift up your face without blemish; you will be secure and will not fear.
18_11_15.NASB: 'Then, indeed, you could lift up your face without [moral] defect, And you would be steadfast and not fear.
18_11_15.NLT: Then your face will brighten with innocence.You will be strong and free of fear.
18_11_15.WEB: Surely then you shall lift up your face without spot; Yes, you shall be steadfast, and shall not fear:
18_11_15.CSB17: then you will hold your head high, free from fault. You will be firmly established and unafraid.
18_11_15.AMP: Then, indeed, you could lift up your face [to Him] without moral defect, And you would be firmly established and secure and not fear.
18_11_15.MSG: You'll be able to face the world unashamedand keep a firm grip on life, guiltless and fearless.
18_11_15.NIV: then you will lift up your face without shame;you will stand firm and without fear.
18_11_15.NRSVCE: Surely then you will lift up your face without blemish; you will be secure, and will not fear.

18_11_16.NKJV: Because you would forget your misery, And remember it as waters that have passed away,
18_11_16.KJV: Because thou shalt forget thy misery, and remember it as waters that pass away:
18_11_16.ESV: You will forget your misery; you will remember it as waters that have passed away.
18_11_16.NASB: 'For you would forget [your] trouble, As waters that have passed by, you would remember [it].
18_11_16.NLT: You will forget your misery;it will be like water flowing away.
18_11_16.WEB: for you shall forget your misery. You shall remember it like waters that have passed away.
18_11_16.CSB17: For you will forget your suffering, recalling it only as water that has flowed by.
18_11_16.AMP: "For you would forget your trouble; You would remember it as waters that have passed by.
18_11_16.MSG: You'll forget your troubles;they'll be like old, faded photographs.
18_11_16.NIV: You will surely forget your trouble,recalling it only as waters gone by.
18_11_16.NRSVCE: You will forget your misery; you will remember it as waters that have passed away.

18_11_17.NKJV: And your life would be brighter than noonday. Though you were dark, you would be like the morning.
18_11_17.KJV: And thine age shall be clearer than the noonday; thou shalt shine forth, thou shalt be as the morning. be clearer: Heb. shall arise above the noon-day
18_11_17.ESV: And your life will be brighter than the noonday; its darkness will be like the morning.
18_11_17.NASB: 'Your life would be brighter than noonday; Darkness would be like the morning.
18_11_17.NLT: Your life will be brighter than the noonday.Even darkness will be as bright as morning.
18_11_17.WEB: Life shall be clearer than the noonday. Though there is darkness, it shall be as the morning.
18_11_17.CSB17: Your life will be brighter than noonday; its darkness will be like the morning.
18_11_17.AMP: "And your life would be brighter than the noonday; Darkness [then] would be like the morning.
18_11_17.MSG: Your world will be washed in sunshine,every shadow dispersed by dayspring.
18_11_17.NIV: Life will be brighter than noonday,and darkness will become like morning.
18_11_17.NRSVCE: And your life will be brighter than the noonday; its darkness will be like the morning.

18_11_18.NKJV: And you would be secure, because there is hope; Yes, you would dig around you, and take your rest in safety.
18_11_18.KJV: And thou shalt be secure, because there is hope; yea, thou shalt dig about thee, and thou shalt take thy rest in safety.
18_11_18.ESV: And you will feel secure, because there is hope; you will look around and take your rest in security.
18_11_18.NASB: 'Then you would trust, because there is hope; And you would look around and rest securely.
18_11_18.NLT: Having hope will give you courage.You will be protected and will rest in safety.
18_11_18.WEB: You shall be secure, because there is hope. Yes, you shall search, and shall take your rest in safety.
18_11_18.CSB17: You will be confident, because there is hope. You will look carefully about and lie down in safety.
18_11_18.AMP: "Then you would trust [with confidence], because there is hope; You would look around you and rest securely.
18_11_18.MSG: Full of hope, you'll relax, confident again;you'll look around, sit back, and take it easy.
18_11_18.NIV: You will be secure, because there is hope;you will look about you and take your rest in safety.
18_11_18.NRSVCE: And you will have confidence, because there is hope; you will be protected and take your rest in safety.

18_11_19.NKJV: You would also lie down, and no one would make you afraid; Yes, many would court your favor.
18_11_19.KJV: Also thou shalt lie down, and none shall make thee afraid; yea, many shall make suit unto thee. make suit: Heb. intreat thy face
18_11_19.ESV: You will lie down, and none will make you afraid; many will court your favor.
18_11_19.NASB: 'You would lie down and none would disturb [you], And many would entreat your favor.
18_11_19.NLT: You will lie down unafraid,and many will look to you for help.
18_11_19.WEB: Also you shall lie down, and no one shall make you afraid. Yes, many shall court your favor.
18_11_19.CSB17: You will lie down with no one to frighten you, and many will seek your favor.
18_11_19.AMP: "You would lie down with no one to frighten you, And many would entreat and seek your favor.
18_11_19.MSG: Expansive, without a care in the world,you'll be hunted out by many for your blessing.
18_11_19.NIV: You will lie down, with no one to make you afraid,and many will court your favor.
18_11_19.NRSVCE: You will lie down, and no one will make you afraid; many will entreat your favor.

18_11_20.NKJV: But the eyes of the wicked will fail, And they shall not escape, And their hope—loss of life!”
18_11_20.KJV: But the eyes of the wicked shall fail, and they shall not escape , and their hope shall be as the giving up of the ghost. they shall: Heb. flight shall perish from them the giving: or, a puff of breath
18_11_20.ESV: But the eyes of the wicked will fail; all way of escape will be lost to them, and their hope is to breathe their last.”
18_11_20.NASB: 'But the eyes of the wicked will fail, And there will be no escape for them; And their hope is to breathe their last.'
18_11_20.NLT: But the wicked will be blinded.They will have no escape.Their only hope is death.”
18_11_20.WEB: But the eyes of the wicked shall fail. They shall have no way to flee. Their hope shall be the giving up of the spirit.”
18_11_20.CSB17: But the sight of the wicked will fail. Their way of escape will be cut off, and their only hope is their last breath.
18_11_20.AMP: "But the eyes of the wicked will fail, And they will not escape [the justice of God]; And their hope is to breathe their last [and die]."
18_11_20.MSG: But the wicked will see none of this.They're headed down a dead-end roadwith nothing to look forward to—nothing."
18_11_20.NIV: But the eyes of the wicked will fail,and escape will elude them;their hope will become a dying gasp.”
18_11_20.NRSVCE: But the eyes of the wicked will fail; all way of escape will be lost to them, and their hope is to breathe their last.”

18_12_2.NKJV: “No doubt you are the people, And wisdom will die with you!
18_12_2.KJV: No doubt but ye are the people, and wisdom shall die with you.
18_12_2.ESV: “No doubt you are the people, and wisdom will die with you.
18_12_2.NASB: 'Truly then you are the people, And with you wisdom will die!
18_12_2.NLT: “You people really know everything, don’t you?And when you die, wisdom will die with you!
18_12_2.WEB: “No doubt, but you are the people, and wisdom shall die with you.
18_12_2.CSB17: No doubt you are the people, and wisdom will die with you!
18_12_2.AMP: "No doubt you are the [only wise] people [in the world], And wisdom will die with you!
18_12_2.MSG: "I'm sure you speak for all the experts,and when you die there'll be no one left to tell us how to live.
18_12_2.NIV: “Doubtless you are the people,and wisdom will die with you!
18_12_2.NRSVCE: “No doubt you are the people, and wisdom will die with you.

18_12_3.NKJV: But I have understanding as well as you; I am not inferior to you. Indeed, who does not know such things as these?
18_12_3.KJV: But I have understanding as well as you; I am not inferior to you: yea, who knoweth not such things as these? understanding: Heb. an heart I am: Heb. I fall not lower than you who: Heb. with whom are not such as these?
18_12_3.ESV: But I have understanding as well as you; I am not inferior to you. Who does not know such things as these?
18_12_3.NASB: 'But I have intelligence as well as you; I am not inferior to you. And who does not know such things as these?
18_12_3.NLT: Well, I know a few things myself —and you’re no better than I am.Who doesn’t know these things you’ve been saying?
18_12_3.WEB: But I have understanding as well as you; I am not inferior to you. Yes, who doesn’t know such things as these?
18_12_3.CSB17: But I also have a mind like you; I am not inferior to you. Who doesn’t know the things you are talking about?
18_12_3.AMP: "But I have intelligence and understanding as well as you; I am not inferior to you. Who does not know such things as these [of God's wisdom and might]?
18_12_3.MSG: But don't forget that I also have a brain—I don't intend to play second fiddle to you.It doesn't take an expert to know these things.
18_12_3.NIV: But I have a mind as well as you;I am not inferior to you.Who does not know all these things?
18_12_3.NRSVCE: But I have understanding as well as you; I am not inferior to you. Who does not know such things as these?

18_12_4.NKJV: “I am one mocked by his friends, Who called on God, and He answered him, The just and blameless who is ridiculed.
18_12_4.KJV: I am as one mocked of his neighbour, who calleth upon God, and he answereth him: the just upright man is laughed to scorn.
18_12_4.ESV: I am a laughingstock to my friends; I, who called to God and he answered me, a just and blameless man, am a laughingstock.
18_12_4.NASB: 'I am a joke to my friends, The one who called on God and He answered him; The just [and] blameless [man] is a joke.
18_12_4.NLT: Yet my friends laugh at me,for I call on God and expect an answer.I am a just and blameless man,yet they laugh at me.
18_12_4.WEB: I am like one who is a joke to his neighbor, I, who called on God, and he answered. The just, the blameless man is a joke.
18_12_4.CSB17: I am a laughingstock to my friends, by calling on God, who answers me. The righteous and upright man is a laughingstock.
18_12_4.AMP: "I am a joke to my friends; I, one whom God answered when he called upon Him— A just and blameless man is a joke [and laughed to scorn].
18_12_4.MSG: "I'm ridiculed by my friends:'So that's the man who had conversations with God!'Ridiculed without mercy:'Look at the man who never did wrong!'
18_12_4.NIV: “I have become a laughingstock to my friends,though I called upon God and he answered—a mere laughingstock, though righteous and blameless!
18_12_4.NRSVCE: I am a laughingstock to my friends; I, who called upon God and he answered me, a just and blameless man, I am a laughingstock.

18_12_5.NKJV: A lamp is despised in the thought of one who is at ease; It is made ready for those whose feet slip.
18_12_5.KJV: He that is ready to slip with his feet is as a lamp despised in the thought of him that is at ease.
18_12_5.ESV: In the thought of one who is at ease there is contempt for misfortune; it is ready for those whose feet slip.
18_12_5.NASB: 'He who is at ease holds calamity in contempt, As prepared for those whose feet slip.
18_12_5.NLT: People who are at ease mock those in trouble.They give a push to people who are stumbling.
18_12_5.WEB: In the thought of him who is at ease there is contempt for misfortune. It is ready for them whose foot slips.
18_12_5.CSB17: The one who is at ease holds calamity in contempt and thinks it is prepared for those whose feet are slipping.
18_12_5.AMP: "He who is at ease has contempt for misfortune, But misfortune is ready [and anxiously waiting] for those whose feet slip.
18_12_5.MSG: It's easy for the well-to-do to point their fingers in blame,for the well-fixed to pour scorn on the strugglers.
18_12_5.NIV: Men at ease have contempt for misfortuneas the fate of those whose feet are slipping.
18_12_5.NRSVCE: Those at ease have contempt for misfortune, but it is ready for those whose feet are unstable.

18_12_6.NKJV: The tents of robbers prosper, And those who provoke God are secure— In what God provides by His hand.
18_12_6.KJV: The tabernacles of robbers prosper, and they that provoke God are secure; into whose hand God bringeth abundantly.
18_12_6.ESV: The tents of robbers are at peace, and those who provoke God are secure, who bring their god in their hand.
18_12_6.NASB: 'The tents of the destroyers prosper, And those who provoke God are secure, Whom God brings into their power.
18_12_6.NLT: But robbers are left in peace,and those who provoke God live in safety —though God keeps them in his power.
18_12_6.WEB: The tents of robbers prosper. Those who provoke God are secure, who carry their God in their hands.
18_12_6.CSB17: The tents of robbers are safe, and those who trouble God are secure; God holds them in his hands.
18_12_6.AMP: "The tents of the destroyers prosper; And those who provoke God are [apparently] secure, Whom God brings into their power.
18_12_6.MSG: Crooks reside safely in high-security houses,insolent blasphemers live in luxury;they've bought and paid for a god who'll protect them.
18_12_6.NIV: The tents of marauders are undisturbed,and those who provoke God are secure—those who carry their god in their hands.
18_12_6.NRSVCE: The tents of robbers are at peace, and those who provoke God are secure, who bring their god in their hands.

18_12_7.NKJV: “But now ask the beasts, and they will teach you; And the birds of the air, and they will tell you;
18_12_7.KJV: But ask now the beasts, and they shall teach thee; and the fowls of the air, and they shall tell thee:
18_12_7.ESV: “But ask the beasts, and they will teach you; the birds of the heavens, and they will tell you;
18_12_7.NASB: 'But now ask the beasts, and let them teach you; And the birds of the heavens, and let them tell you.
18_12_7.NLT: “Just ask the animals, and they will teach you.Ask the birds of the sky, and they will tell you.
18_12_7.WEB: “But ask the animals, now, and they shall teach you; the birds of the sky, and they shall tell you.
18_12_7.CSB17: But ask the animals, and they will instruct you; ask the birds of the sky, and they will tell you.
18_12_7.AMP: "Now ask the animals, and let them teach you [that God does not deal with His creatures according to their character]; And ask the birds of the air, and let them tell you;
18_12_7.MSG: "But ask the animals what they think—let them teach you;let the birds tell you what's going on.
18_12_7.NIV: “But ask the animals, and they will teach you,or the birds of the air, and they will tell you;
18_12_7.NRSVCE: “But ask the animals, and they will teach you; the birds of the air, and they will tell you;

18_12_8.NKJV: Or speak to the earth, and it will teach you; And the fish of the sea will explain to you.
18_12_8.KJV: Or speak to the earth, and it shall teach thee: and the fishes of the sea shall declare unto thee.
18_12_8.ESV: or the bushes of the earth, and they will teach you; and the fish of the sea will declare to you.
18_12_8.NASB: 'Or speak to the earth, and let it teach you; And let the fish of the sea declare to you.
18_12_8.NLT: Speak to the earth, and it will instruct you.Let the fish in the sea speak to you.
18_12_8.WEB: Or speak to the earth, and it shall teach you. The fish of the sea shall declare to you.
18_12_8.CSB17: Or speak to the earth, and it will instruct you; let the fish of the sea inform you.
18_12_8.AMP: Or speak to the earth [with its many forms of life], and it will teach you; And let the fish of the sea declare [this truth] to you.
18_12_8.MSG: Put your ear to the earth—learn the basics.Listen—the fish in the ocean will tell you their stories.
18_12_8.NIV: or speak to the earth, and it will teach you,or let the fish of the sea inform you.
18_12_8.NRSVCE: ask the plants of the earth, and they will teach you; and the fish of the sea will declare to you.

18_12_9.NKJV: Who among all these does not know That the hand of the LORD has done this,
18_12_9.KJV: Who knoweth not in all these that the hand of the LORD hath wrought this?
18_12_9.ESV: Who among all these does not know that the hand of the LORD has done this?
18_12_9.NASB: 'Who among all these does not know That the hand of the LORD has done this,
18_12_9.NLT: For they all knowthat my disaster has come from the hand of the LORD.
18_12_9.WEB: Who doesn’t know that in all these, Yahweh’s hand has done this,
18_12_9.CSB17: Which of all these does not know that the hand of the LORD has done this?
18_12_9.AMP: "Who among all these does not recognize [in all these things that good and evil are randomly scattered throughout nature and human life] That the hand of the LORD has done this,
18_12_9.MSG: Isn't it clear that they all know and agreethat God is sovereign, that he holds all things in his hand—
18_12_9.NIV: Which of all these does not knowthat the hand of the Lord has done this?
18_12_9.NRSVCE: Who among all these does not know that the hand of the LORD has done this?

18_12_10.NKJV: In whose hand is the life of every living thing, And the breath of all mankind?
18_12_10.KJV: In whose hand is the soul of every living thing, and the breath of all mankind . soul: or, life all: Heb. all flesh of man
18_12_10.ESV: In his hand is the life of every living thing and the breath of all mankind.
18_12_10.NASB: In whose hand is the life of every living thing, And the breath of all mankind?
18_12_10.NLT: For the life of every living thing is in his hand,and the breath of every human being.
18_12_10.WEB: in whose hand is the life of every living thing, and the breath of all mankind?
18_12_10.CSB17: The life of every living thing is in his hand, as well as the breath of all mankind.
18_12_10.AMP: In whose hand is the life of every living thing, And the breath of all mankind?
18_12_10.MSG: Every living soul, yes,every breathing creature?
18_12_10.NIV: In his hand is the life of every creatureand the breath of all mankind.
18_12_10.NRSVCE: In his hand is the life of every living thing and the breath of every human being.

18_12_11.NKJV: Does not the ear test words And the mouth taste its food?
18_12_11.KJV: Doth not the ear try words? and the mouth taste his meat? mouth: Heb. palate
18_12_11.ESV: Does not the ear test words as the palate tastes food?
18_12_11.NASB: 'Does not the ear test words, As the palate tastes its food?
18_12_11.NLT: The ear tests the words it hearsjust as the mouth distinguishes between foods.
18_12_11.WEB: Doesn’t the ear try words, even as the palate tastes its food?
18_12_11.CSB17: Doesn’t the ear test words as the palate tastes food?
18_12_11.AMP: "Does the ear not put words to the test, Just as the palate tastes its food [distinguishing between the desirable and the undesirable]?
18_12_11.MSG: Isn't this all just common sense,as common as the sense of taste?
18_12_11.NIV: Does not the ear test wordsas the tongue tastes food?
18_12_11.NRSVCE: Does not the ear test words as the palate tastes food?

18_12_12.NKJV: Wisdom is with aged men, And with length of days, understanding.
18_12_12.KJV: With the ancient is wisdom; and in length of days understanding.
18_12_12.ESV: Wisdom is with the aged, and understanding in length of days.
18_12_12.NASB: 'Wisdom is with aged men, [With] long life is understanding.
18_12_12.NLT: Wisdom belongs to the aged,and understanding to the old.
18_12_12.WEB: With aged men is wisdom, in length of days understanding.
18_12_12.CSB17: Wisdom is found with the elderly, and understanding comes with long life.
18_12_12.AMP: "With the aged [you say] is wisdom, And with long life is understanding.
18_12_12.MSG: Do you think the elderly have a corner on wisdom,that you have to grow old before you understand life?
18_12_12.NIV: Is not wisdom found among the aged?Does not long life bring understanding?
18_12_12.NRSVCE: Is wisdom with the aged, and understanding in length of days?

18_12_13.NKJV: “With Him are wisdom and strength, He has counsel and understanding.
18_12_13.KJV: With him is wisdom and strength, he hath counsel and understanding. With: that is, With God
18_12_13.ESV: “With God are wisdom and might; he has counsel and understanding.
18_12_13.NASB: 'With Him are wisdom and might; To Him belong counsel and understanding.
18_12_13.NLT: “But true wisdom and power are found in God;counsel and understanding are his.
18_12_13.WEB: “With God is wisdom and might. He has counsel and understanding.
18_12_13.CSB17: Wisdom and strength belong to God; counsel and understanding are his.
18_12_13.AMP: "But [only] with Him are [perfect] wisdom and might; He [alone] has [true] counsel and understanding.
18_12_13.MSG: "True wisdom and real power belong to God;from him we learn how to live,and also what to live for.
18_12_13.NIV: “To God belong wisdom and power;counsel and understanding are his.
18_12_13.NRSVCE: “With God are wisdom and strength; he has counsel and understanding.

18_12_14.NKJV: If He breaks a thing down, it cannot be rebuilt; If He imprisons a man, there can be no release.
18_12_14.KJV: Behold, he breaketh down, and it cannot be built again: he shutteth up a man, and there can be no opening. up: Heb. upon
18_12_14.ESV: If he tears down, none can rebuild; if he shuts a man in, none can open.
18_12_14.NASB: 'Behold, He tears down, and it cannot be rebuilt; He imprisons a man, and there can be no release.
18_12_14.NLT: What he destroys cannot be rebuilt.When he puts someone in prison, there is no escape.
18_12_14.WEB: Behold, he breaks down, and it can’t be built again. He imprisons a man, and there can be no release.
18_12_14.CSB17: Whatever he tears down cannot be rebuilt; whoever he imprisons cannot be released.
18_12_14.AMP: "Behold, He tears down, and it cannot be rebuilt; He imprisons a man, and there can be no release.
18_12_14.MSG: If he tears something down, it's down for good;if he locks people up, they're locked up for good.
18_12_14.NIV: What he tears down cannot be rebuilt;the man he imprisons cannot be released.
18_12_14.NRSVCE: If he tears down, no one can rebuild; if he shuts someone in, no one can open up.

18_12_15.NKJV: If He withholds the waters, they dry up; If He sends them out, they overwhelm the earth.
18_12_15.KJV: Behold, he withholdeth the waters, and they dry up: also he sendeth them out, and they overturn the earth.
18_12_15.ESV: If he withholds the waters, they dry up; if he sends them out, they overwhelm the land.
18_12_15.NASB: 'Behold, He restrains the waters, and they dry up; And He sends them out, and they inundate the earth.
18_12_15.NLT: If he holds back the rain, the earth becomes a desert.If he releases the waters, they flood the earth.
18_12_15.WEB: Behold, he withholds the waters, and they dry up. Again, he sends them out, and they overturn the earth.
18_12_15.CSB17: When he withholds water, everything dries up, and when he releases it, it destroys the land.
18_12_15.AMP: "Behold, He restrains the waters, and they dry up; Again, He sends the waters out, and they overwhelm and devastate the earth.
18_12_15.MSG: If he holds back the rain, there's a drought;if he lets it loose, there's a flood.
18_12_15.NIV: If he holds back the waters, there is drought;if he lets them loose, they devastate the land.
18_12_15.NRSVCE: If he withholds the waters, they dry up; if he sends them out, they overwhelm the land.

18_12_16.NKJV: With Him are strength and prudence. The deceived and the deceiver are His.
18_12_16.KJV: With him is strength and wisdom: the deceived and the deceiver are his.
18_12_16.ESV: With him are strength and sound wisdom; the deceived and the deceiver are his.
18_12_16.NASB: 'With Him are strength and sound wisdom, The misled and the misleader belong to Him.
18_12_16.NLT: Yes, strength and wisdom are his;deceivers and deceived are both in his power.
18_12_16.WEB: With him is strength and wisdom. The deceived and the deceiver are his.
18_12_16.CSB17: True wisdom and power belong to him. The deceived and the deceiver are his.
18_12_16.AMP: "With Him are might and sound wisdom, The misled and the misleader are His [and in His power].
18_12_16.MSG: Strength and success belong to God;both deceived and deceiver must answer to him.
18_12_16.NIV: To him belong strength and victory;both deceived and deceiver are his.
18_12_16.NRSVCE: With him are strength and wisdom; the deceived and the deceiver are his.

18_12_17.NKJV: He leads counselors away plundered, And makes fools of the judges.
18_12_17.KJV: He leadeth counsellors away spoiled, and maketh the judges fools.
18_12_17.ESV: He leads counselors away stripped, and judges he makes fools.
18_12_17.NASB: 'He makes counselors walk barefoot And makes fools of judges.
18_12_17.NLT: He leads counselors away, stripped of good judgment;wise judges become fools.
18_12_17.WEB: He leads counselors away stripped. He makes judges fools.
18_12_17.CSB17: He leads counselors away barefoot and makes judges go mad.
18_12_17.AMP: "He makes [great and scheming] counselors walk barefoot And makes fools of judges.
18_12_17.MSG: He strips experts of their vaunted credentials,exposes judges as witless fools.
18_12_17.NIV: He leads counselors away strippedand makes fools of judges.
18_12_17.NRSVCE: He leads counselors away stripped, and makes fools of judges.

18_12_18.NKJV: He loosens the bonds of kings, And binds their waist with a belt.
18_12_18.KJV: He looseth the bond of kings, and girdeth their loins with a girdle.
18_12_18.ESV: He looses the bonds of kings and binds a waistcloth on their hips.
18_12_18.NASB: 'He loosens the bond of kings And binds their loins with a girdle.
18_12_18.NLT: He removes the royal robe of kings.They are led away with ropes around their waist.
18_12_18.WEB: He loosens the bond of kings. He binds their waist with a belt.
18_12_18.CSB17: He releases the bonds put on by kings and fastens a belt around their waists.
18_12_18.AMP: "He loosens the bond of kings And binds their loins with a loincloth.
18_12_18.MSG: He divests kings of their royal garments,then ties a rag around their waists.
18_12_18.NIV: He takes off the shackles put on by kingsand ties a loincloth around their waist.
18_12_18.NRSVCE: He looses the sash of kings, and binds a waistcloth on their loins.

18_12_19.NKJV: He leads princes away plundered, And overthrows the mighty.
18_12_19.KJV: He leadeth princes away spoiled, and overthroweth the mighty.
18_12_19.ESV: He leads priests away stripped and overthrows the mighty.
18_12_19.NASB: 'He makes priests walk barefoot And overthrows the secure ones.
18_12_19.NLT: He leads priests away, stripped of status;he overthrows those with long years in power.
18_12_19.WEB: He leads priests away stripped, and overthrows the mighty.
18_12_19.CSB17: He leads priests away barefoot and overthrows established leaders.
18_12_19.AMP: "He makes priests walk barefoot, And He overturns men firmly seated and secure.
18_12_19.MSG: He strips priests of their robes,and fires high officials from their jobs.
18_12_19.NIV: He leads priests away strippedand overthrows men long established.
18_12_19.NRSVCE: He leads priests away stripped, and overthrows the mighty.

18_12_20.NKJV: He deprives the trusted ones of speech, And takes away the discernment of the elders.
18_12_20.KJV: He removeth away the speech of the trusty, and taketh away the understanding of the aged. speech: Heb. lip of the faithful
18_12_20.ESV: He deprives of speech those who are trusted and takes away the discernment of the elders.
18_12_20.NASB: 'He deprives the trusted ones of speech And takes away the discernment of the elders.
18_12_20.NLT: He silences the trusted adviserand removes the insight of the elders.
18_12_20.WEB: He removes the speech of those who are trusted, and takes away the understanding of the elders.
18_12_20.CSB17: He deprives trusted advisers of speech and takes away the elders’ good judgment.
18_12_20.AMP: "He deprives the trusted ones of speech And takes away the discernment and discretion of the aged.
18_12_20.MSG: He forces trusted sages to keep silence,deprives elders of their good sense and wisdom.
18_12_20.NIV: He silences the lips of trusted advisersand takes away the discernment of elders.
18_12_20.NRSVCE: He deprives of speech those who are trusted, and takes away the discernment of the elders.

18_12_21.NKJV: He pours contempt on princes, And disarms the mighty.
18_12_21.KJV: He poureth contempt upon princes, and weakeneth the strength of the mighty. weakeneth: or, looseth the girdle of the strong
18_12_21.ESV: He pours contempt on princes and loosens the belt of the strong.
18_12_21.NASB: 'He pours contempt on nobles And loosens the belt of the strong.
18_12_21.NLT: He pours disgrace upon princesand disarms the strong.
18_12_21.WEB: He pours contempt on princes, and loosens the belt of the strong.
18_12_21.CSB17: He pours out contempt on nobles and disarms the strong.
18_12_21.AMP: "He pours contempt on princes and nobles And loosens the belt of the strong [disabling them].
18_12_21.MSG: He dumps contempt on famous people,disarms the strong and mighty.
18_12_21.NIV: He pours contempt on noblesand disarms the mighty.
18_12_21.NRSVCE: He pours contempt on princes, and looses the belt of the strong.

18_12_22.NKJV: He uncovers deep things out of darkness, And brings the shadow of death to light.
18_12_22.KJV: He discovereth deep things out of darkness, and bringeth out to light the shadow of death.
18_12_22.ESV: He uncovers the deeps out of darkness and brings deep darkness to light.
18_12_22.NASB: 'He reveals mysteries from the darkness And brings the deep darkness into light.
18_12_22.NLT: “He uncovers mysteries hidden in darkness;he brings light to the deepest gloom.
18_12_22.WEB: He uncovers deep things out of darkness, and brings out to light the shadow of death.
18_12_22.CSB17: He reveals mysteries from the darkness and brings the deepest darkness into the light.
18_12_22.AMP: "He uncovers mysteries [that are difficult to grasp and understand] out of the darkness And brings black gloom and the shadow of death into light.
18_12_22.MSG: He shines a spotlight into caves of darkness,hauls deepest darkness into the noonday sun.
18_12_22.NIV: He reveals the deep things of darknessand brings deep shadows into the light.
18_12_22.NRSVCE: He uncovers the deeps out of darkness, and brings deep darkness to light.

18_12_23.NKJV: He makes nations great, and destroys them; He enlarges nations, and guides them.
18_12_23.KJV: He increaseth the nations, and destroyeth them: he enlargeth the nations, and straiteneth them again. straiteneth: Heb. leadeth in
18_12_23.ESV: He makes nations great, and he destroys them; he enlarges nations, and leads them away.
18_12_23.NASB: 'He makes the nations great, then destroys them; He enlarges the nations, then leads them away.
18_12_23.NLT: He builds up nations, and he destroys them.He expands nations, and he abandons them.
18_12_23.WEB: He increases the nations, and he destroys them. He enlarges the nations, and he leads them captive.
18_12_23.CSB17: He makes nations great, then destroys them; he enlarges nations, then leads them away.
18_12_23.AMP: "He makes nations great, and He destroys them; He enlarges nations, and leads them away [captive].
18_12_23.MSG: He makes nations rise and then fall,builds up some and abandons others.
18_12_23.NIV: He makes nations great, and destroys them;he enlarges nations, and disperses them.
18_12_23.NRSVCE: He makes nations great, then destroys them; he enlarges nations, then leads them away.

18_12_24.NKJV: He takes away the understanding of the chiefs of the people of the earth, And makes them wander in a pathless wilderness.
18_12_24.KJV: He taketh away the heart of the chief of the people of the earth, and causeth them to wander in a wilderness where there is no way.
18_12_24.ESV: He takes away understanding from the chiefs of the people of the earth and makes them wander in a trackless waste.
18_12_24.NASB: 'He deprives of intelligence the chiefs of the earth's people And makes them wander in a pathless waste.
18_12_24.NLT: He strips kings of understandingand leaves them wandering in a pathless wasteland.
18_12_24.WEB: He takes away understanding from the chiefs of the people of the earth, and causes them to wander in a wilderness where there is no way.
18_12_24.CSB17: He deprives the world’s leaders of reason, and makes them wander in a trackless wasteland.
18_12_24.AMP: "He removes intelligence and understanding from the leaders of the people of the earth And makes them wander and move blindly in a pathless waste.
18_12_24.MSG: He robs world leaders of their reason,and sends them off into no man's land.
18_12_24.NIV: He deprives the leaders of the earth of their reason;he sends them wandering through a trackless waste.
18_12_24.NRSVCE: He strips understanding from the leaders of the earth, and makes them wander in a pathless waste.

18_12_25.NKJV: They grope in the dark without light, And He makes them stagger like a drunken man.
18_12_25.KJV: They grope in the dark without light, and he maketh them to stagger like a drunken man. stagger: Heb. wander
18_12_25.ESV: They grope in the dark without light, and he makes them stagger like a drunken man.
18_12_25.NASB: 'They grope in darkness with no light, And He makes them stagger like a drunken man.
18_12_25.NLT: They grope in the darkness without a light.He makes them stagger like drunkards.
18_12_25.WEB: They grope in the dark without light. He makes them stagger like a drunken man.
18_12_25.CSB17: They grope around in darkness without light; he makes them stagger like a drunkard.
18_12_25.AMP: "They grope in darkness without light, And He makes them stagger like a drunken man."
18_12_25.MSG: They grope in the dark without a clue,lurching and staggering like drunks.
18_12_25.NIV: They grope in darkness with no light;he makes them stagger like drunkards.
18_12_25.NRSVCE: They grope in the dark without light; he makes them stagger like a drunkard.

18_13_1.NKJV: “Behold, my eye has seen all this, My ear has heard and understood it.
18_13_1.KJV: Lo, mine eye hath seen all this, mine ear hath heard and understood it.
18_13_1.ESV: “Behold, my eye has seen all this, my ear has heard and understood it.
18_13_1.NASB: 'Behold, my eye has seen all [this], My ear has heard and understood it.
18_13_1.NLT: “Look, I have seen all this with my own eyesand heard it with my own ears, and now I understand.
18_13_1.WEB: “Behold, my eye has seen all this. My ear has heard and understood it.
18_13_1.CSB17: Look, my eyes have seen all this; my ears have heard and understood it.
18_13_1.AMP: [Job continued:] "Behold, my eye has seen all this, My ear has heard and understood it.
18_13_1.MSG: "Yes, I've seen all this with my own eyes,heard and understood it with my very own ears.
18_13_1.NIV: “My eyes have seen all this,my ears have heard and understood it.
18_13_1.NRSVCE: “Look, my eye has seen all this, my ear has heard and understood it.

18_13_2.NKJV: What you know, I also know; I am not inferior to you.
18_13_2.KJV: What ye know, the same do I know also: I am not inferior unto you.
18_13_2.ESV: What you know, I also know; I am not inferior to you.
18_13_2.NASB: 'What you know I also know; I am not inferior to you.
18_13_2.NLT: I know as much as you do.You are no better than I am.
18_13_2.WEB: What you know, I know also. I am not inferior to you.
18_13_2.CSB17: Everything you know, I also know; I am not inferior to you.
18_13_2.AMP: "What you know I also know; I am not inferior to you.
18_13_2.MSG: Everything you know, I know,so I'm not taking a back seat to any of you.
18_13_2.NIV: What you know, I also know;I am not inferior to you.
18_13_2.NRSVCE: What you know, I also know; I am not inferior to you.

18_13_3.NKJV: But I would speak to the Almighty, And I desire to reason with God.
18_13_3.KJV: Surely I would speak to the Almighty, and I desire to reason with God.
18_13_3.ESV: But I would speak to the Almighty, and I desire to argue my case with God.
18_13_3.NASB: 'But I would speak to the Almighty, And I desire to argue with God.
18_13_3.NLT: As for me, I would speak directly to the Almighty.I want to argue my case with God himself.
18_13_3.WEB: “Surely I would speak to the Almighty. I desire to reason with God.
18_13_3.CSB17: Yet I prefer to speak to the Almighty and argue my case before God.
18_13_3.AMP: "But I wish to speak to the Almighty, And I desire to argue with God.
18_13_3.MSG: I'm taking my case straight to God Almighty;I've had it with you—I'm going directly to God.
18_13_3.NIV: But I desire to speak to the Almightyand to argue my case with God.
18_13_3.NRSVCE: But I would speak to the Almighty, and I desire to argue my case with God.

18_13_4.NKJV: But you forgers of lies, You are all worthless physicians.
18_13_4.KJV: But ye are forgers of lies, ye are all physicians of no value.
18_13_4.ESV: As for you, you whitewash with lies; worthless physicians are you all.
18_13_4.NASB: 'But you smear with lies; You are all worthless physicians.
18_13_4.NLT: As for you, you smear me with lies.As physicians, you are worthless quacks.
18_13_4.WEB: But you are forgers of lies. You are all physicians of no value.
18_13_4.CSB17: You use lies like plaster; you are all worthless healers.
18_13_4.AMP: "But you smear me with lies [you defame my character most untruthfully]; You are all worthless physicians and have no remedy to offer.
18_13_4.MSG: You graffiti my life with lies.You're a bunch of pompous quacks!
18_13_4.NIV: You, however, smear me with lies;you are worthless physicians, all of you!
18_13_4.NRSVCE: As for you, you whitewash with lies; all of you are worthless physicians.

18_13_5.NKJV: Oh, that you would be silent, And it would be your wisdom!
18_13_5.KJV: O that ye would altogether hold your peace! and it should be your wisdom.
18_13_5.ESV: Oh that you would keep silent, and it would be your wisdom!
18_13_5.NASB: 'O that you would be completely silent, And that it would become your wisdom!
18_13_5.NLT: If only you could be silent!That’s the wisest thing you could do.
18_13_5.WEB: Oh that you would be completely silent! Then you would be wise.
18_13_5.CSB17: If only you would shut up and let that be your wisdom!
18_13_5.AMP: "Oh, that you would be completely silent, And that silence would be your wisdom!
18_13_5.MSG: I wish you'd shut your mouths—silence is your only claim to wisdom.
18_13_5.NIV: If only you would be altogether silent!For you, that would be wisdom.
18_13_5.NRSVCE: If you would only keep silent, that would be your wisdom!

18_13_6.NKJV: Now hear my reasoning, And heed the pleadings of my lips.
18_13_6.KJV: Hear now my reasoning, and hearken to the pleadings of my lips.
18_13_6.ESV: Hear now my argument and listen to the pleadings of my lips.
18_13_6.NASB: 'Please hear my argument And listen to the contentions of my lips.
18_13_6.NLT: Listen to my charge;pay attention to my arguments.
18_13_6.WEB: Hear now my reasoning. Listen to the pleadings of my lips.
18_13_6.CSB17: Hear now my argument, and listen to my defense.
18_13_6.AMP: "Please hear my argument And listen to the pleadings of my lips.
18_13_6.MSG: "Listen now while I make my case,consider my side of things for a change.
18_13_6.NIV: Hear now my argument;listen to the plea of my lips.
18_13_6.NRSVCE: Hear now my reasoning, and listen to the pleadings of my lips.

18_13_7.NKJV: Will you speak wickedly for God, And talk deceitfully for Him?
18_13_7.KJV: Will ye speak wickedly for God? and talk deceitfully for him?
18_13_7.ESV: Will you speak falsely for God and speak deceitfully for him?
18_13_7.NASB: 'Will you speak what is unjust for God, And speak what is deceitful for Him?
18_13_7.NLT: “Are you defending God with lies?Do you make your dishonest arguments for his sake?
18_13_7.WEB: Will you speak unrighteously for God, and talk deceitfully for him?
18_13_7.CSB17: Would you testify unjustly on God’s behalf or speak deceitfully for him?
18_13_7.AMP: "Will you speak what is unjust for God, And speak what is deceitful for Him?
18_13_7.MSG: Or are you going to keep on lying 'to do God a service'?to make up stories 'to get him off the hook'?
18_13_7.NIV: Will you speak wickedly on God’s behalf?Will you speak deceitfully for him?
18_13_7.NRSVCE: Will you speak falsely for God, and speak deceitfully for him?

18_13_8.NKJV: Will you show partiality for Him? Will you contend for God?
18_13_8.KJV: Will ye accept his person? will ye contend for God?
18_13_8.ESV: Will you show partiality toward him? Will you plead the case for God?
18_13_8.NASB: 'Will you show partiality for Him? Will you contend for God?
18_13_8.NLT: Will you slant your testimony in his favor?Will you argue God’s case for him?
18_13_8.WEB: Will you show partiality to him? Will you contend for God?
18_13_8.CSB17: Would you show partiality to him or argue the case in his defense?
18_13_8.AMP: "Will you show partiality for Him [and be unjust to me so that you may gain favor with Him]? Will you contend and plead for God?
18_13_8.MSG: Why do you always take his side?Do you think he needs a lawyer to defend himself?
18_13_8.NIV: Will you show him partiality?Will you argue the case for God?
18_13_8.NRSVCE: Will you show partiality toward him, will you plead the case for God?

18_13_9.NKJV: Will it be well when He searches you out? Or can you mock Him as one mocks a man?
18_13_9.KJV: Is it good that he should search you out? or as one man mocketh another, do ye so mock him?
18_13_9.ESV: Will it be well with you when he searches you out? Or can you deceive him, as one deceives a man?
18_13_9.NASB: 'Will it be well when He examines you? Or will you deceive Him as one deceives a man?
18_13_9.NLT: What will happen when he finds out what you are doing?Can you fool him as easily as you fool people?
18_13_9.WEB: Is it good that he should search you out? Or as one deceives a man, will you deceive him?
18_13_9.CSB17: Would it go well if he examined you? Could you deceive him as you would deceive a man?
18_13_9.AMP: "Will it be well for you when He investigates you [and your tactics against me]? Or will you deceive Him as one deceives a man?
18_13_9.MSG: How would you fare if you were in the dock?Your lies might convince a jury—but would theyconvince God?
18_13_9.NIV: Would it turn out well if he examined you?Could you deceive him as you might deceive men?
18_13_9.NRSVCE: Will it be well with you when he searches you out? Or can you deceive him, as one person deceives another?

18_13_10.NKJV: He will surely rebuke you If you secretly show partiality.
18_13_10.KJV: He will surely reprove you, if ye do secretly accept persons.
18_13_10.ESV: He will surely rebuke you if in secret you show partiality.
18_13_10.NASB: 'He will surely reprove you If you secretly show partiality.
18_13_10.NLT: No, you will be in trouble with himif you secretly slant your testimony in his favor.
18_13_10.WEB: He will surely reprove you, if you secretly show partiality.
18_13_10.CSB17: Surely he would rebuke you if you secretly showed partiality.
18_13_10.AMP: "He will surely reprimand you If you secretly show partiality.
18_13_10.MSG: He'd reprimand you on the spotif he detected a bias in your witness.
18_13_10.NIV: He would surely rebuke youif you secretly showed partiality.
18_13_10.NRSVCE: He will surely rebuke you if in secret you show partiality.

18_13_11.NKJV: Will not His excellence make you afraid, And the dread of Him fall upon you?
18_13_11.KJV: Shall not his excellency make you afraid? and his dread fall upon you?
18_13_11.ESV: Will not his majesty terrify you, and the dread of him fall upon you?
18_13_11.NASB: 'Will not His majesty terrify you, And the dread of Him fall on you?
18_13_11.NLT: Doesn’t his majesty terrify you?Doesn’t your fear of him overwhelm you?
18_13_11.WEB: Shall not his majesty make you afraid, And his dread fall on you?
18_13_11.CSB17: Would God’s majesty not terrify you? Would his dread not fall on you?
18_13_11.AMP: "Will not His majesty terrify you, And will not the dread of Him fall upon you?
18_13_11.MSG: Doesn't his splendor put you in awe?Aren't you afraid to speak cheap lies before him?
18_13_11.NIV: Would not his splendor terrify you?Would not the dread of him fall on you?
18_13_11.NRSVCE: Will not his majesty terrify you, and the dread of him fall upon you?

18_13_12.NKJV: Your platitudes are proverbs of ashes, Your defenses are defenses of clay.
18_13_12.KJV: Your remembrances are like unto ashes, your bodies to bodies of clay.
18_13_12.ESV: Your maxims are proverbs of ashes; your defenses are defenses of clay.
18_13_12.NASB: 'Your memorable sayings are proverbs of ashes, Your defenses are defenses of clay.
18_13_12.NLT: Your platitudes are as valuable as ashes.Your defense is as fragile as a clay pot.
18_13_12.WEB: Your memorable sayings are proverbs of ashes, Your defenses are defenses of clay.
18_13_12.CSB17: Your memorable sayings are proverbs of ash; your defenses are made of clay.
18_13_12.AMP: "Your memorable sayings are [worthless, merely] proverbs of ashes; Your defenses are defenses of [crumbling] clay.
18_13_12.MSG: Your wise sayings are knickknack wisdom,good for nothing but gathering dust.
18_13_12.NIV: Your maxims are proverbs of ashes;your defenses are defenses of clay.
18_13_12.NRSVCE: Your maxims are proverbs of ashes, your defenses are defenses of clay.

18_13_13.NKJV: “Hold your peace with me, and let me speak, Then let come on me what may!
18_13_13.KJV: Hold your peace, let me alone, that I may speak, and let come on me what will. Hold: Heb. Be silent from me
18_13_13.ESV: “Let me have silence, and I will speak, and let come on me what may.
18_13_13.NASB: 'Be silent before me so that I may speak; Then let come on me what may.
18_13_13.NLT: “Be silent now and leave me alone.Let me speak, and I will face the consequences.
18_13_13.WEB: “Be silent, leave me alone, that I may speak. Let come on me what will.
18_13_13.CSB17: Be quiet, and I will speak. Let whatever comes happen to me.
18_13_13.AMP: "Be silent before me so that I may speak; And let happen to me what may.
18_13_13.MSG: "So hold your tongue while I have my say,then I'll take whatever I have coming to me.
18_13_13.NIV: “Keep silent and let me speak;then let come to me what may.
18_13_13.NRSVCE: “Let me have silence, and I will speak, and let come on me what may.

18_13_14.NKJV: Why do I take my flesh in my teeth, And put my life in my hands?
18_13_14.KJV: Wherefore do I take my flesh in my teeth, and put my life in mine hand?
18_13_14.ESV: Why should I take my flesh in my teeth and put my life in my hand?
18_13_14.NASB: 'Why should I take my flesh in my teeth And put my life in my hands?
18_13_14.NLT: Why should I put myself in mortal dangerand take my life in my own hands?
18_13_14.WEB: Why should I take my flesh in my teeth, and put my life in my hand?
18_13_14.CSB17: I will put myself at risk and take my life in my own hands.
18_13_14.AMP: "Why should I take my flesh in my teeth And put my life in my hands [incurring the wrath of God]?
18_13_14.MSG: Why do I go out on a limb like thisand take my life in my hands?
18_13_14.NIV: Why do I put myself in jeopardyand take my life in my hands?
18_13_14.NRSVCE: I will take my flesh in my teeth, and put my life in my hand.

18_13_15.NKJV: Though He slay me, yet will I trust Him. Even so, I will defend my own ways before Him.
18_13_15.KJV: Though he slay me, yet will I trust in him: but I will maintain mine own ways before him. maintain: Heb. prove, or, argue
18_13_15.ESV: Though he slay me, I will hope in him; yet I will argue my ways to his face.
18_13_15.NASB: 'Though He slay me, I will hope in Him. Nevertheless I will argue my ways before Him.
18_13_15.NLT: God might kill me, but I have no other hope.I am going to argue my case with him.
18_13_15.WEB: Behold, he will kill me. I have no hope. Nevertheless, I will maintain my ways before him.
18_13_15.CSB17: Even if he kills me, I will hope in him. I will still defend my ways before him.
18_13_15.AMP: "Even though He kills me; I will hope in Him. Nevertheless, I will argue my ways to His face.
18_13_15.MSG: Because even if he killed me, I'd keep on hoping.I'd defend my innocence to the very end.
18_13_15.NIV: Though he slay me, yet will I hope in him;I will surely defend my ways to his face.
18_13_15.NRSVCE: See, he will kill me; I have no hope; but I will defend my ways to his face.

18_13_16.NKJV: He also shall be my salvation, For a hypocrite could not come before Him.
18_13_16.KJV: He also shall be my salvation: for an hypocrite shall not come before him.
18_13_16.ESV: This will be my salvation, that the godless shall not come before him.
18_13_16.NASB: 'This also will be my salvation, For a godless man may not come before His presence.
18_13_16.NLT: But this is what will save me — I am not godless.If I were, I could not stand before him.
18_13_16.WEB: This also shall be my salvation, that a godless man shall not come before him.
18_13_16.CSB17: Yes, this will result in my deliverance, for no godless person can appear before him.
18_13_16.AMP: "This also will be my salvation, For a godless man may not come before Him.
18_13_16.MSG: Just wait, this is going to work out for the best—my salvation!If I were guilt-stricken do you think I'd be doing this—laying myself on the line before God?
18_13_16.NIV: Indeed, this will turn out for my deliverance,for no godless man would dare come before him!
18_13_16.NRSVCE: This will be my salvation, that the godless shall not come before him.

18_13_17.NKJV: Listen carefully to my speech, And to my declaration with your ears.
18_13_17.KJV: Hear diligently my speech, and my declaration with your ears.
18_13_17.ESV: Keep listening to my words, and let my declaration be in your ears.
18_13_17.NASB: 'Listen carefully to my speech, And let my declaration [fill] your ears.
18_13_17.NLT: “Listen closely to what I am about to say.Hear me out.
18_13_17.WEB: Hear diligently my speech. Let my declaration be in your ears.
18_13_17.CSB17: Pay close attention to my words; let my declaration ring in your ears.
18_13_17.AMP: "Listen diligently to my speech, And let my declaration fill your ears.
18_13_17.MSG: You'd better pay attention to what I'm telling you,listen carefully with both ears.
18_13_17.NIV: Listen carefully to my words;let your ears take in what I say.
18_13_17.NRSVCE: Listen carefully to my words, and let my declaration be in your ears.

18_13_18.NKJV: See now, I have prepared my case, I know that I shall be vindicated.
18_13_18.KJV: Behold now, I have ordered my cause; I know that I shall be justified.
18_13_18.ESV: Behold, I have prepared my case; I know that I shall be in the right.
18_13_18.NASB: 'Behold now, I have prepared my case; I know that I will be vindicated.
18_13_18.NLT: I have prepared my case;I will be proved innocent.
18_13_18.WEB: See now, I have set my cause in order. I know that I am righteous.
18_13_18.CSB17: Now then, I have prepared my case; I know that I am right.
18_13_18.AMP: "Behold now, I have prepared my case; I know that I will be vindicated.
18_13_18.MSG: Now that I've laid out my defense,I'm sure that I'll be acquitted.
18_13_18.NIV: Now that I have prepared my case,I know I will be vindicated.
18_13_18.NRSVCE: I have indeed prepared my case; I know that I shall be vindicated.

18_13_19.NKJV: Who is he who will contend with me? If now I hold my tongue, I perish.
18_13_19.KJV: Who is he that will plead with me? for now, if I hold my tongue, I shall give up the ghost.
18_13_19.ESV: Who is there who will contend with me? For then I would be silent and die.
18_13_19.NASB: 'Who will contend with me? For then I would be silent and die.
18_13_19.NLT: Who can argue with me over this?And if you prove me wrong, I will remain silent and die.
18_13_19.WEB: Who is he who will contend with me? For then would I hold my peace and give up the spirit.
18_13_19.CSB17: Can anyone indict me? If so, I will be silent and die.
18_13_19.AMP: "Who will argue and contend with me? For then I would be silent and die.
18_13_19.MSG: Can anyone prove charges against me?I've said my piece. I rest my case.
18_13_19.NIV: Can anyone bring charges against me?If so, I will be silent and die.
18_13_19.NRSVCE: Who is there that will contend with me? For then I would be silent and die.

18_13_20.NKJV: “Only two things do not do to me, Then I will not hide myself from You:
18_13_20.KJV: Only do not two things unto me: then will I not hide myself from thee.
18_13_20.ESV: Only grant me two things, then I will not hide myself from your face:
18_13_20.NASB: 'Only two things do not do to me, Then I will not hide from Your face:
18_13_20.NLT: “O God, grant me these two things,and then I will be able to face you.
18_13_20.WEB: “Only don’t do two things to me; then I will not hide myself from your face:
18_13_20.CSB17: Only grant these two things to me, God, so that I will not have to hide from your presence:
18_13_20.AMP: "Only [O Lord,] do not do two things to me, And then I will not hide myself from Your face:
18_13_20.MSG: "Please, God, I have two requests;grant them so I'll know I count with you:
18_13_20.NIV: “Only grant me these two things, O God,and then I will not hide from you:
18_13_20.NRSVCE: Only grant two things to me, then I will not hide myself from your face:

18_13_21.NKJV: Withdraw Your hand far from me, And let not the dread of You make me afraid.
18_13_21.KJV: Withdraw thine hand far from me: and let not thy dread make me afraid.
18_13_21.ESV: withdraw your hand far from me, and let not dread of you terrify me.
18_13_21.NASB: Remove Your hand from me, And let not the dread of You terrify me.
18_13_21.NLT: Remove your heavy hand from me,and don’t terrify me with your awesome presence.
18_13_21.WEB: withdraw your hand far from me; and don’t let your terror make me afraid.
18_13_21.CSB17: remove your hand from me, and do not let your terror frighten me.
18_13_21.AMP: Withdraw Your hand from me and remove this bodily suffering, And let not the dread of You terrify me.
18_13_21.MSG: First, lay off the afflictions;the terror is too much for me.
18_13_21.NIV: Withdraw your hand far from me,and stop frightening me with your terrors.
18_13_21.NRSVCE: withdraw your hand far from me, and do not let dread of you terrify me.

18_13_22.NKJV: Then call, and I will answer; Or let me speak, then You respond to me.
18_13_22.KJV: Then call thou, and I will answer: or let me speak, and answer thou me.
18_13_22.ESV: Then call, and I will answer; or let me speak, and you reply to me.
18_13_22.NASB: 'Then call, and I will answer; Or let me speak, then reply to me.
18_13_22.NLT: Now summon me, and I will answer!Or let me speak to you, and you reply.
18_13_22.WEB: Then call, and I will answer; or let me speak, and you answer me.
18_13_22.CSB17: Then call, and I will answer, or I will speak, and you can respond to me.
18_13_22.AMP: "Then [Lord,] call, and I will answer; Or let me speak, and then reply to me.
18_13_22.MSG: Second, address me directly so I can answer you,or let me speak and then you answer me.
18_13_22.NIV: Then summon me and I will answer,or let me speak, and you reply.
18_13_22.NRSVCE: Then call, and I will answer; or let me speak, and you reply to me.

18_13_23.NKJV: How many are my iniquities and sins? Make me know my transgression and my sin.
18_13_23.KJV: How many are mine iniquities and sins? make me to know my transgression and my sin.
18_13_23.ESV: How many are my iniquities and my sins? Make me know my transgression and my sin.
18_13_23.NASB: 'How many are my iniquities and sins? Make known to me my rebellion and my sin.
18_13_23.NLT: Tell me, what have I done wrong?Show me my rebellion and my sin.
18_13_23.WEB: How many are my iniquities and sins? Make me know my disobedience and my sin.
18_13_23.CSB17: How many iniquities and sins have I committed? Reveal to me my transgression and sin.
18_13_23.AMP: "How many are my iniquities and sins [that so much sorrow should come to me]? Make me recognize and understand my transgression and my sin.
18_13_23.MSG: How many sins have been charged against me?Show me the list—how bad is it?
18_13_23.NIV: How many wrongs and sins have I committed?Show me my offense and my sin.
18_13_23.NRSVCE: How many are my iniquities and my sins? Make me know my transgression and my sin.

18_13_24.NKJV: Why do You hide Your face, And regard me as Your enemy?
18_13_24.KJV: Wherefore hidest thou thy face, and holdest me for thine enemy?
18_13_24.ESV: Why do you hide your face and count me as your enemy?
18_13_24.NASB: 'Why do You hide Your face And consider me Your enemy?
18_13_24.NLT: Why do you turn away from me?Why do you treat me as your enemy?
18_13_24.WEB: Why hide you your face, and hold me for your enemy?
18_13_24.CSB17: Why do you hide your face and consider me your enemy?
18_13_24.AMP: "Why do You hide Your face [as if offended] And consider me Your enemy?
18_13_24.MSG: Why do you stay hidden and silent?Why treat me like I'm your enemy?
18_13_24.NIV: Why do you hide your faceand consider me your enemy?
18_13_24.NRSVCE: Why do you hide your face, and count me as your enemy?

18_13_25.NKJV: Will You frighten a leaf driven to and fro? And will You pursue dry stubble?
18_13_25.KJV: Wilt thou break a leaf driven to and fro? and wilt thou pursue the dry stubble?
18_13_25.ESV: Will you frighten a driven leaf and pursue dry chaff?
18_13_25.NASB: 'Will You cause a driven leaf to tremble? Or will You pursue the dry chaff?
18_13_25.NLT: Would you terrify a leaf blown by the wind?Would you chase dry straw?
18_13_25.WEB: Will you harass a driven leaf? Will you pursue the dry stubble?
18_13_25.CSB17: Will you frighten a wind-driven leaf? Will you chase after dry straw?
18_13_25.AMP: "Will You cause a windblown leaf to tremble? Will You pursue the chaff of the dry stubble?
18_13_25.MSG: Why kick me around like an old tin can?Why beat a dead horse?
18_13_25.NIV: Will you torment a windblown leaf?Will you chase after dry chaff?
18_13_25.NRSVCE: Will you frighten a windblown leaf and pursue dry chaff?

18_13_26.NKJV: For You write bitter things against me, And make me inherit the iniquities of my youth.
18_13_26.KJV: For thou writest bitter things against me, and makest me to possess the iniquities of my youth.
18_13_26.ESV: For you write bitter things against me and make me inherit the iniquities of my youth.
18_13_26.NASB: 'For You write bitter things against me And make me to inherit the iniquities of my youth.
18_13_26.NLT: “You write bitter accusations against meand bring up all the sins of my youth.
18_13_26.WEB: For you write bitter things against me, and make me inherit the iniquities of my youth:
18_13_26.CSB17: For you record bitter accusations against me and make me inherit the iniquities of my youth.
18_13_26.AMP: "For You write bitter things against me [in Your indictment] And make me inherit and suffer for the iniquities of my youth.
18_13_26.MSG: You compile a long list of mean things about me,even hold me accountable for the sins of my youth.
18_13_26.NIV: For you write down bitter things against meand make me inherit the sins of my youth.
18_13_26.NRSVCE: For you write bitter things against me, and make me reap the iniquities of my youth.

18_13_27.NKJV: You put my feet in the stocks, And watch closely all my paths. You set a limit for the soles of my feet.
18_13_27.KJV: Thou puttest my feet also in the stocks, and lookest narrowly unto all my paths; thou settest a print upon the heels of my feet. lookest: Heb. observest heels: Heb. roots
18_13_27.ESV: You put my feet in the stocks and watch all my paths; you set a limit for the soles of my feet.
18_13_27.NASB: 'You put my feet in the stocks And watch all my paths; You set a limit for the soles of my feet,
18_13_27.NLT: You put my feet in stocks.You examine all my paths.You trace all my footprints.
18_13_27.WEB: You also put my feet in the stocks, and mark all my paths. You set a bound to the soles of my feet,
18_13_27.CSB17: You put my feet in the stocks and stand watch over all my paths, setting a limit for the soles of my feet.
18_13_27.AMP: "You also put my feet in the stocks [as punishment] And [critically] observe all my paths; You set a circle and limit around the soles of my feet [which I must not overstep],
18_13_27.MSG: You hobble me so I can't move about.You watch every move I make,and brand me as a dangerous character.
18_13_27.NIV: You fasten my feet in shackles;you keep close watch on all my pathsby putting marks on the soles of my feet.
18_13_27.NRSVCE: You put my feet in the stocks, and watch all my paths; you set a bound to the soles of my feet.

18_13_28.NKJV: “Man decays like a rotten thing, Like a garment that is moth-eaten.

18_13_28.NLT: I waste away like rotting wood,like a moth-eaten coat.
18_13_28.WEB: though I am decaying like a rotten thing, like a garment that is moth-eaten.
18_13_28.CSB17: A person wears out like something rotten, like a moth-eaten garment.
18_13_28.AMP: While I waste away like a rotten thing, Like a garment that is moth-eaten.
18_13_28.MSG: "Like something rotten, human life fast decomposes,like a moth-eaten shirt or a mildewed blouse.
18_13_28.NIV: “So man wastes away like something rotten,like a garment eaten by moths.
18_13_28.NRSVCE: One wastes away like a rotten thing, like a garment that is moth-eaten.

18_14_1.NKJV: “Man who is born of woman Is of few days and full of trouble.
18_14_1.KJV: Man that is born of a woman is of few days, and full of trouble. few: Heb. short of days
18_14_1.ESV: “Man who is born of a woman is few of days and full of trouble.
18_14_1.NASB: 'Man, who is born of woman, Is short-lived and full of turmoil.
18_14_1.NLT: “How frail is humanity!How short is life, how full of trouble!
