18_14_1.NRSVCE: “A mortal, born of woman, few of days and full of trouble,

18_14_2.NKJV: He comes forth like a flower and fades away; He flees like a shadow and does not continue.
18_14_2.KJV: He cometh forth like a flower, and is cut down: he fleeth also as a shadow, and continueth not.
18_14_2.ESV: He comes out like a flower and withers; he flees like a shadow and continues not.
18_14_2.NASB: 'Like a flower he comes forth and withers. He also flees like a shadow and does not remain.
18_14_2.NLT: We blossom like a flower and then wither.Like a passing shadow, we quickly disappear.
18_14_2.WEB: He grows up like a flower, and is cut down. He also flees like a shadow, and doesn’t continue.
18_14_2.CSB17: He blossoms like a flower, then withers; he flees like a shadow and does not last.
18_14_2.AMP: "Like a flower he comes forth and withers; He also flees like a shadow and does not remain.
18_14_2.MSG: We spring up like wildflowers in the desert and then wilt,transient as the shadow of a cloud.
18_14_2.NIV: He springs up like a flower and withers away;like a fleeting shadow, he does not endure.
18_14_2.NRSVCE: comes up like a flower and withers, flees like a shadow and does not last.

18_14_3.NKJV: And do You open Your eyes on such a one, And bring me to judgment with Yourself?
18_14_3.KJV: And dost thou open thine eyes upon such an one, and bringest me into judgment with thee?
18_14_3.ESV: And do you open your eyes on such a one and bring me into judgment with you?
18_14_3.NASB: 'You also open Your eyes on him And bring him into judgment with Yourself.
18_14_3.NLT: Must you keep an eye on such a frail creatureand demand an accounting from me?
18_14_3.WEB: Do you open your eyes on such a one, and bring me into judgment with you?
18_14_3.CSB17: Do you really take notice of one like this? Will you bring me into judgment against you?
18_14_3.AMP: "You also open Your eyes upon him And bring him into judgment with Yourself.
18_14_3.MSG: Do you occupy your time with such fragile wisps?Why even bother hauling me into court?
18_14_3.NIV: Do you fix your eye on such a one?Will you bring him before you for judgment?
18_14_3.NRSVCE: Do you fix your eyes on such a one? Do you bring me into judgment with you?

18_14_4.NKJV: Who can bring a clean thing out of an unclean? No one!
18_14_4.KJV: Who can bring a clean thing out of an unclean? not one. can: Heb. will give
18_14_4.ESV: Who can bring a clean thing out of an unclean? There is not one.
18_14_4.NASB: 'Who can make the clean out of the unclean? No one!
18_14_4.NLT: Who can bring purity out of an impure person?No one!
18_14_4.WEB: Who can bring a clean thing out of an unclean? Not one.
18_14_4.CSB17: Who can produce something pure from what is impure? No one!
18_14_4.AMP: "Who can make a clean thing out of the unclean? No one!
18_14_4.MSG: There's nothing much to us to start with;how do you expect us to amount to anything?
18_14_4.NIV: Who can bring what is pure from the impure?No one!
18_14_4.NRSVCE: Who can bring a clean thing out of an unclean? No one can.

18_14_5.NKJV: Since his days are determined, The number of his months is with You; You have appointed his limits, so that he cannot pass.
18_14_5.KJV: Seeing his days are determined, the number of his months are with thee, thou hast appointed his bounds that he cannot pass;
18_14_5.ESV: Since his days are determined, and the number of his months is with you, and you have appointed his limits that he cannot pass,
18_14_5.NASB: 'Since his days are determined, The number of his months is with You; And his limits You have set so that he cannot pass.
18_14_5.NLT: You have decided the length of our lives.You know how many months we will live,and we are not given a minute longer.
18_14_5.WEB: Seeing his days are determined, the number of his months is with you, and you have appointed his bounds that he can’t pass;
18_14_5.CSB17: Since a person’s days are determined and the number of his months depends on you, and since you have set limits he cannot pass,
18_14_5.AMP: "Since his days are determined, The number of his months is with You [in Your control], And You have made his limits so he cannot pass [his allotted time].
18_14_5.MSG: Mortals have a limited life span.You've already decided how long we'll live—you set the boundary and no one can cross it.
18_14_5.NIV: Man’s days are determined;you have decreed the number of his monthsand have set limits he cannot exceed.
18_14_5.NRSVCE: Since their days are determined, and the number of their months is known to you, and you have appointed the bounds that they cannot pass,

18_14_6.NKJV: Look away from him that he may rest, Till like a hired man he finishes his day.
18_14_6.KJV: Turn from him, that he may rest, till he shall accomplish, as an hireling, his day. rest: Heb. cease
18_14_6.ESV: look away from him and leave him alone, that he may enjoy, like a hired hand, his day.
18_14_6.NASB: 'Turn Your gaze from him that he may rest, Until he fulfills his day like a hired man.
18_14_6.NLT: So leave us alone and let us rest!We are like hired hands, so let us finish our work in peace.
18_14_6.WEB: Look away from him, that he may rest, until he shall accomplish, as a hireling, his day.
18_14_6.CSB17: look away from him and let him rest so that he can enjoy his day like a hired worker.
18_14_6.AMP: "[O God] turn your gaze from him so that he may rest, Until he fulfills his day [on earth] like a hired man.
18_14_6.MSG: So why not give us a break? Ease up!Even ditchdiggers get occasional days off.
18_14_6.NIV: So look away from him and let him alone,till he has put in his time like a hired man.
18_14_6.NRSVCE: look away from them, and desist, that they may enjoy, like laborers, their days.

18_14_7.NKJV: “For there is hope for a tree, If it is cut down, that it will sprout again, And that its tender shoots will not cease.
18_14_7.KJV: For there is hope of a tree, if it be cut down, that it will sprout again, and that the tender branch thereof will not cease.
18_14_7.ESV: “For there is hope for a tree, if it be cut down, that it will sprout again, and that its shoots will not cease.
18_14_7.NASB: 'For there is hope for a tree, When it is cut down, that it will sprout again, And its shoots will not fail.
18_14_7.NLT: “Even a tree has more hope!If it is cut down, it will sprout againand grow new branches.

18_14_7.CSB17: There is hope for a tree: If it is cut down, it will sprout again, and its shoots will not die.

18_14_7.MSG: For a tree there is always hope.Chop it down and it still has a chance—its roots can put out fresh sprouts.

18_14_7.NRSVCE: “For there is hope for a tree, if it is cut down, that it will sprout again, and that its shoots will not cease.

18_14_10.NKJV: But man dies and is laid away; Indeed he breathes his last And where is he?

18_14_12.NRSVCE: so mortals lie down and do not rise again; until the heavens are no more, they will not awake or be roused out of their sleep.

18_14_13.NKJV: “Oh, that You would hide me in the grave, That You would conceal me until Your wrath is past, That You would appoint me a set time, and remember me!
18_14_13.KJV: O that thou wouldest hide me in the grave, that thou wouldest keep me secret, until thy wrath be past, that thou wouldest appoint me a set time, and remember me!
18_14_13.ESV: Oh that you would hide me in Sheol, that you would conceal me until your wrath be past, that you would appoint me a set time, and remember me!
18_14_13.NASB: 'Oh that You would hide me in Sheol, That You would conceal me until Your wrath returns [to You], That You would set a limit for me and remember me!
18_14_13.NLT: “I wish you would hide me in the graveand forget me there until your anger has passed.But mark your calendar to think of me again!
18_14_13.WEB: “Oh that you would hide me in Sheol, that you would keep me secret, until your wrath is past, that you would appoint me a set time, and remember me!
18_14_13.CSB17: If only you would hide me in Sheol and conceal me until your anger passes. If only you would appoint a time for me and then remember me.
18_14_13.AMP: "Oh, that You would hide me in Sheol (the nether world, the place of the dead), That You would conceal me until Your wrath is past, That You would set a definite time and then remember me [and in Your lovingkindness imprint me on your heart]!
18_14_13.MSG: Why don't you just bury me alive,get me out of the way until your anger cools?But don't leave me there!Set a date when you'll see me again.
18_14_13.NIV: “If only you would hide me in the graveand conceal me till your anger has passed!If only you would set me a timeand then remember me!
18_14_13.NRSVCE: O that you would hide me in Sheol, that you would conceal me until your wrath is past, that you would appoint me a set time, and remember me!

18_14_14.NKJV: If a man dies, shall he live again? All the days of my hard service I will wait, Till my change comes.
18_14_14.KJV: If a man die, shall he live again? all the days of my appointed time will I wait, till my change come.
18_14_14.ESV: If a man dies, shall he live again? All the days of my service I would wait, till my renewal should come.
18_14_14.NASB: 'If a man dies, will he live [again]? All the days of my struggle I will wait Until my change comes.
18_14_14.NLT: Can the dead live again?If so, this would give me hope through all my years of struggle,and I would eagerly await the release of death.
18_14_14.WEB: If a man dies, shall he live again? All the days of my warfare would I wait, until my release should come.
18_14_14.CSB17: When a person dies, will he come back to life? If so, I would wait all the days of my struggle until my relief comes.
18_14_14.AMP: "If a man dies, will he live again? I will wait all the days of my struggle Until my change and release will come.
18_14_14.MSG: If we humans die, will we live again? That's my question.All through these difficult days I keep hoping,waiting for the final change—for resurrection!
18_14_14.NIV: If a man dies, will he live again?All the days of my hard serviceI will wait for my renewal to come.
18_14_14.NRSVCE: If mortals die, will they live again? All the days of my service I would wait until my release should come.

18_14_15.NKJV: You shall call, and I will answer You; You shall desire the work of Your hands.

18_14_15.ESV: You would call, and I would answer you; you would long for the work of your hands.
18_14_15.NASB: 'You will call, and I will answer You; You will long for the work of Your hands.
18_14_15.NLT: You would call and I would answer,and you would yearn for me, your handiwork.

18_14_15.NIV: You will call and I will answer you;you will long for the creature your hands have made.
18_14_15.NRSVCE: You would call, and I would answer you; you would long for the work of your hands.

18_14_16.ESV: For then you would number my steps; you would not keep watch over my sin;
18_14_16.NASB: 'For now You number my steps, You do not observe my sin.
18_14_16.NLT: For then you would guard my steps,instead of watching for my sins.

18_14_20.CSB17: You completely overpower him, and he passes on; you change his appearance and send him away.

18_14_20.MSG: You're too much for us.As always, you get the last word.We don't like it and our faces show it,but you send us off anyway.

18_14_21.KJV: His sons come to honour, and he knoweth it not; and they are brought low, but he perceiveth it not of them.

18_14_21.NLT: They never know if their children grow up in honoror sink to insignificance.
18_14_21.WEB: His sons come to honor, and he doesn’t know it. They are brought low, but he doesn’t perceive it of them.
18_14_21.CSB17: If his sons receive honor, he does not know it; if they become insignificant, he is unaware of it.
18_14_21.AMP: "His sons achieve honor, and he does not know it; They become insignificant, and he is not aware of it.
18_14_21.MSG: If our children do well for themselves, we never know it;if they do badly, we're spared the hurt.
18_14_21.NIV: If his sons are honored, he does not know it;if they are brought low, he does not see it.
18_14_21.NRSVCE: Their children come to honor, and they do not know it; they are brought low, and it goes unnoticed.

18_14_22.NKJV: But his flesh will be in pain over it, And his soul will mourn over it.”

18_14_22.NASB: 'But his body pains him, And he mourns only for himself.'
18_14_22.NLT: They suffer painfully;their life is full of trouble.”
18_14_22.WEB: But his flesh on him has pain, and his soul within him mourns.”
18_14_22.CSB17: He feels only the pain of his own body and mourns only for himself.

18_14_22.MSG: Body and soul, that's it for us—a lifetime of pain, a lifetime of sorrow."

18_15_1.NKJV: Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered and said:
18_15_1.KJV: Then answered Eliphaz the Temanite, and said,

18_15_1.AMP: Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered [Job] and said,
18_15_1.MSG: Eliphaz of Teman spoke a second time:

18_15_2.NKJV: “Should a wise man answer with empty knowledge, And fill himself with the east wind?
18_15_2.KJV: Should a wise man utter vain knowledge, and fill his belly with the east wind? vain: Heb. knowledge of wind
18_15_2.ESV: “Should a wise man answer with windy knowledge, and fill his belly with the east wind?
18_15_2.NASB: 'Should a wise man answer with windy knowledge And fill himself with the east wind?
18_15_2.NLT: “A wise man wouldn’t answer with such empty talk!You are nothing but a windbag.
18_15_2.WEB: “Should a wise man answer with vain knowledge, and fill himself with the east wind?
18_15_2.CSB17: Does a wise man answer with empty counsel or fill himself with the hot east wind?
18_15_2.AMP: "Should a wise man [such as you] utter such windy and vain knowledge [as we have just heard] And fill himself with the east wind [of withering, parching, and violent accusations]?
18_15_2.MSG: "If you were truly wise, would you sound so much like awindbag, belching hot air?
18_15_2.NIV: “Would a wise man answer with empty notionsor fill his belly with the hot east wind?
18_15_2.NRSVCE: “Should the wise answer with windy knowledge, and fill themselves with the east wind?

18_15_3.NKJV: Should he reason with unprofitable talk, Or by speeches with which he can do no good?
18_15_3.KJV: Should he reason with unprofitable talk? or with speeches wherewith he can do no good?
18_15_3.ESV: Should he argue in unprofitable talk, or in words with which he can do no good?
18_15_3.NASB: 'Should he argue with useless talk, Or with words which are not profitable?
18_15_3.NLT: The wise don’t engage in empty chatter.What good are such words?
18_15_3.WEB: Should he reason with unprofitable talk, or with speeches with which he can do no good?
18_15_3.CSB17: Should he argue with useless talk or with words that serve no good purpose?
18_15_3.AMP: "Should he rebuke and argue with useless talk? Or with words in which there is no benefit?
18_15_3.MSG: Would you talk nonsense in the middle of a serious argument,babbling baloney?
18_15_3.NIV: Would he argue with useless words,with speeches that have no value?
18_15_3.NRSVCE: Should they argue in unprofitable talk, or in words with which they can do no good?

18_15_4.NKJV: Yes, you cast off fear, And restrain prayer before God.
18_15_4.KJV: Yea, thou castest off fear, and restrainest prayer before God. castest: Heb. makest void prayer: or, speech
18_15_4.ESV: But you are doing away with the fear of God and hindering meditation before God.
18_15_4.NASB: 'Indeed, you do away with reverence And hinder meditation before God.
18_15_4.NLT: Have you no fear of God,no reverence for him?
18_15_4.WEB: Yes, you do away with fear, and hinder devotion before God.
18_15_4.CSB17: But you even undermine the fear of God and hinder meditation before him.
18_15_4.AMP: "Indeed, you are doing away with fear, And you are diminishing meditation before God.
18_15_4.MSG: Look at you! You trivialize religion,turn spiritual conversation into empty gossip.
18_15_4.NIV: But you even undermine pietyand hinder devotion to God.
18_15_4.NRSVCE: But you are doing away with the fear of God, and hindering meditation before God.

18_15_5.NKJV: For your iniquity teaches your mouth, And you choose the tongue of the crafty.
18_15_5.KJV: For thy mouth uttereth thine iniquity, and thou choosest the tongue of the crafty. uttereth: Heb. teacheth
18_15_5.ESV: For your iniquity teaches your mouth, and you choose the tongue of the crafty.
18_15_5.NASB: 'For your guilt teaches your mouth, And you choose the language of the crafty.
18_15_5.NLT: Your sins are telling your mouth what to say.Your words are based on clever deception.
18_15_5.WEB: For your iniquity teaches your mouth, and you choose the language of the crafty.
18_15_5.CSB17: Your iniquity teaches you what to say, and you choose the language of the crafty.
18_15_5.AMP: "For your guilt teaches your mouth, And you choose [to speak] the language of the crafty and cunning.
18_15_5.MSG: It's your sin that taught you to talk this way.You chose an education in fraud.
18_15_5.NIV: Your sin prompts your mouth;you adopt the tongue of the crafty.
18_15_5.NRSVCE: For your iniquity teaches your mouth, and you choose the tongue of the crafty.

18_15_6.NKJV: Your own mouth condemns you, and not I; Yes, your own lips testify against you.
18_15_6.KJV: Thine own mouth condemneth thee, and not I: yea, thine own lips testify against thee.
18_15_6.ESV: Your own mouth condemns you, and not I; your own lips testify against you.
18_15_6.NASB: 'Your own mouth condemns you, and not I; And your own lips testify against you.
18_15_6.NLT: Your own mouth condemns you, not I.Your own lips testify against you.

18_15_6.AMP: "Your own mouth condemns you, and not I; Yes, your own lips testify against you.
18_15_6.MSG: Your own words have exposed your guilt.It's nothing I've said—you've incriminated yourself!
18_15_6.NIV: Your own mouth condemns you, not mine;your own lips testify against you.
18_15_6.NRSVCE: Your own mouth condemns you, and not I; your own lips testify against you.

18_15_7.NKJV: “Are you the first man who was born? Or were you made before the hills?

18_15_7.ESV: “Are you the first man who was born? Or were you brought forth before the hills?
18_15_7.NASB: 'Were you the first man to be born, Or were you brought forth before the hills?

18_15_7.NIV: “Are you the first man ever born?Were you brought forth before the hills?
18_15_7.NRSVCE: “Are you the firstborn of the human race? Were you brought forth before the hills?

18_15_8.NKJV: Have you heard the counsel of God? Do you limit wisdom to yourself?

18_15_11.NIV: Are God’s consolations not enough for you,words spoken gently to you?

18_15_16.NRSVCE: how much less one who is abominable and corrupt, one who drinks iniquity like water!

18_15_23.NIV: He wanders about—food for vultures;he knows the day of darkness is at hand.

18_15_24.NRSVCE: distress and anguish terrify them; they prevail against them, like a king prepared for battle.

18_15_27.NKJV: “Though he has covered his face with his fatness, And made his waist heavy with fat,

18_15_27.NASB: 'For he has covered his face with his fat And made his thighs heavy with flesh.

18_15_28.NKJV: He dwells in desolate cities, In houses which no one inhabits, Which are destined to become ruins.

18_15_28.NASB: 'He has lived in desolate cities, In houses no one would inhabit, Which are destined to become ruins.
18_15_28.NLT: But their cities will be ruined.They will live in abandoned housesthat are ready to tumble down.

18_15_28.CSB17: he will dwell in ruined cities, in abandoned houses destined to become piles of rubble.

18_15_29.NKJV: He will not be rich, Nor will his wealth continue, Nor will his possessions overspread the earth.

18_15_29.NASB: 'He will not become rich, nor will his wealth endure; And his grain will not bend down to the ground.
18_15_29.NLT: Their riches will not last,and their wealth will not endure.Their possessions will no longer spread across the horizon.
18_15_29.WEB: He shall not be rich, neither shall his substance continue, neither shall their possessions be extended on the earth.
18_15_29.CSB17: He will no longer be rich; his wealth will not endure. His possessions will not increase in the land.

18_15_29.NIV: He will no longer be rich and his wealth will not endure,nor will his possessions spread over the land.
18_15_29.NRSVCE: they will not be rich, and their wealth will not endure, nor will they strike root in the earth;

18_15_30.NKJV: He will not depart from darkness; The flame will dry out his branches, And by the breath of His mouth he will go away.
18_15_30.KJV: He shall not depart out of darkness; the flame shall dry up his branches, and by the breath of his mouth shall he go away.
18_15_30.ESV: he will not depart from darkness; the flame will dry up his shoots, and by the breath of his mouth he will depart.
18_15_30.NASB: 'He will not escape from darkness; The flame will wither his shoots, And by the breath of His mouth he will go away.
18_15_30.NLT: “They will not escape the darkness.The burning sun will wither their shoots,and the breath of God will destroy them.
18_15_30.WEB: He shall not depart out of darkness. The flame shall dry up his branches. By the breath of God’s mouth shall he go away.
18_15_30.CSB17: He will not escape from the darkness; flames will wither his shoots, and by the breath of God’s mouth, he will depart.
18_15_30.AMP: "He will not escape from darkness [fleeing disaster]; The flame [of God's wrath] will wither his branch, And by the blast of His mouth he will go away.
18_15_30.MSG: And then death—don't think they'll escape that!They'll end up shriveled weeds,brought down by a puff of God's breath.
18_15_30.NIV: He will not escape the darkness;a flame will wither his shoots,and the breath of God’s mouth will carry him away.
18_15_30.NRSVCE: they will not escape from darkness; the flame will dry up their shoots, and their blossom will be swept away by the wind.

18_15_31.NKJV: Let him not trust in futile things, deceiving himself, For futility will be his reward.
18_15_31.KJV: Let not him that is deceived trust in vanity: for vanity shall be his recompence.
18_15_31.ESV: Let him not trust in emptiness, deceiving himself, for emptiness will be his payment.
18_15_31.NASB: 'Let him not trust in emptiness, deceiving himself; For emptiness will be his reward.
18_15_31.NLT: Let them no longer fool themselves by trusting in empty riches,for emptiness will be their only reward.
18_15_31.WEB: Let him not trust in emptiness, deceiving himself; for emptiness shall be his reward.
18_15_31.CSB17: Let him not put trust in worthless things, being led astray, for what he gets in exchange will prove worthless.
18_15_31.AMP: "Let him not trust in vanity (emptiness, futility) and be led astray; For emptiness will be his reward [for such living].
18_15_31.MSG: There's a lesson here: Whoever invests in lies,gets lies for interest,
18_15_31.NIV: Let him not deceive himself by trusting what is worthless,for he will get nothing in return.
18_15_31.NRSVCE: Let them not trust in emptiness, deceiving themselves; for emptiness will be their recompense.

18_15_32.NKJV: It will be accomplished before his time, And his branch will not be green.
18_15_32.KJV: It shall be accomplished before his time, and his branch shall not be green. accomplished: or, cut off

18_15_32.NASB: 'It will be accomplished before his time, And his palm branch will not be green.
18_15_32.NLT: They will be cut down in the prime of life;their branches will never again be green.
18_15_32.WEB: It shall be accomplished before his time. His branch shall not be green.
18_15_32.CSB17: It will be accomplished before his time, and his branch will not flourish.

18_15_32.NIV: Before his time he will be paid in full,and his branches will not flourish.
18_15_32.NRSVCE: It will be paid in full before their time, and their branch will not be green.

18_15_33.NKJV: He will shake off his unripe grape like a vine, And cast off his blossom like an olive tree.
18_15_33.KJV: He shall shake off his unripe grape as the vine, and shall cast off his flower as the olive.
18_15_33.ESV: He will shake off his unripe grape like the vine, and cast off his blossom like the olive tree.
18_15_33.NASB: 'He will drop off his unripe grape like the vine, And will cast off his flower like the olive tree.
18_15_33.NLT: They will be like a vine whose grapes are harvested too early,like an olive tree that loses its blossoms before the fruit can form.
18_15_33.WEB: He shall shake off his unripe grape as the vine, and shall cast off his flower as the olive tree.
18_15_33.CSB17: He will be like a vine that drops its unripe grapes and like an olive tree that sheds its blossoms.
18_15_33.AMP: "He will fail to bring his grapes to maturity [leaving them to wither unnourished] on the vine, And will cast off blossoms [and fail to bring forth fruit] like the olive tree.
18_15_33.MSG: They'll be like fruit frost-killed before it ripens,like buds sheared off before they bloom.
18_15_33.NIV: He will be like a vine stripped of its unripe grapes,like an olive tree shedding its blossoms.
18_15_33.NRSVCE: They will shake off their unripe grape, like the vine, and cast off their blossoms, like the olive tree.

18_15_34.NKJV: For the company of hypocrites will be barren, And fire will consume the tents of bribery.

18_15_34.CSB17: For the company of the godless will have no children, and fire will consume the tents of those who offer bribes.
18_15_34.AMP: "For the company of the godless is barren, And fire consumes the tents of bribery (wrong and injustice).

18_15_34.NIV: For the company of the godless will be barren,and fire will consume the tents of those who love bribes.
18_15_34.NRSVCE: For the company of the godless is barren, and fire consumes the tents of bribery.

18_15_35.NKJV: They conceive trouble and bring forth futility; Their womb prepares deceit.”

18_16_2.NLT: “I have heard all this before.What miserable comforters you are!

18_16_2.CSB17: I have heard many things like these. You are all miserable comforters.

18_16_3.NKJV: Shall words of wind have an end? Or what provokes you that you answer?

18_16_3.ESV: Shall windy words have an end? Or what provokes you that you answer?
18_16_3.NASB: 'Is there [no] limit to windy words? Or what plagues you that you answer?
18_16_3.NLT: Won’t you ever stop blowing hot air?What makes you keep on talking?
18_16_3.WEB: Shall vain words have an end? Or what provokes you that you answer?
18_16_3.CSB17: Is there no end to your empty words? What provokes you that you continue testifying?
18_16_3.AMP: "Is there no end to [your futile] words of wind? Or what plagues you [so much] that you [so boldly] answer [me like this]?
18_16_3.MSG: Is there no end to your windbag speeches?What's your problem that you go on and on like this?
18_16_3.NIV: Will your long-winded speeches never end?What ails you that you keep on arguing?
18_16_3.NRSVCE: Have windy words no limit? Or what provokes you that you keep on talking?

18_16_4.NKJV: I also could speak as you do, If your soul were in my soul’s place. I could heap up words against you, And shake my head at you;
18_16_4.KJV: I also could speak as ye do: if your soul were in my soul's stead, I could heap up words against you, and shake mine head at you.
18_16_4.ESV: I also could speak as you do, if you were in my place; I could join words together against you and shake my head at you.
18_16_4.NASB: 'I too could speak like you, If I were in your place. I could compose words against you And shake my head at you.
18_16_4.NLT: I could say the same things if you were in my place.I could spout off criticism and shake my head at you.
18_16_4.WEB: I also could speak as you do. If your soul were in my soul’s place, I could join words together against you, and shake my head at you,
18_16_4.CSB17: If you were in my place I could also talk like you. I could string words together against you and shake my head at you.
18_16_4.AMP: "I also could speak like you, If you were in my place; I could compose and join words together against you And shake my head at you.
18_16_4.MSG: If you were in my shoes,I could talk just like you.I could put together a terrific harangueand really let you have it.
18_16_4.NIV: I also could speak like you,if you were in my place;I could make fine speeches against youand shake my head at you.
18_16_4.NRSVCE: I also could talk as you do, if you were in my place; I could join words together against you, and shake my head at you.

18_16_5.NKJV: But I would strengthen you with my mouth, And the comfort of my lips would relieve your grief.
18_16_5.KJV: But I would strengthen you with my mouth, and the moving of my lips should asswage your grief.
18_16_5.ESV: I could strengthen you with my mouth, and the solace of my lips would assuage your pain.
18_16_5.NASB: 'I could strengthen you with my mouth, And the solace of my lips could lessen [your pain].
18_16_5.NLT: But if it were me, I would encourage you.I would try to take away your grief.
18_16_5.WEB: but I would strengthen you with my mouth. The solace of my lips would relieve you.
18_16_5.CSB17: Instead, I would encourage you with my mouth, and the consolation from my lips would bring relief.
18_16_5.AMP: "[But instead] I could strengthen and encourage you with [the words of] my mouth, And the consolation and solace of my lips would soothe your suffering and lessen your anguish.
18_16_5.MSG: But I'd never do that. I'd console and comfort,make things better, not worse!
18_16_5.NIV: But my mouth would encourage you;comfort from my lips would bring you relief.
18_16_5.NRSVCE: I could encourage you with my mouth, and the solace of my lips would assuage your pain.

18_16_6.NKJV: “Though I speak, my grief is not relieved; And if I remain silent, how am I eased?
18_16_6.KJV: Though I speak, my grief is not asswaged: and though I forbear, what am I eased? what: Heb. what goeth from me?
18_16_6.ESV: “If I speak, my pain is not assuaged, and if I forbear, how much of it leaves me?
18_16_6.NASB: 'If I speak, my pain is not lessened, And if I hold back, what has left me?
18_16_6.NLT: Instead, I suffer if I defend myself,and I suffer no less if I refuse to speak.
18_16_6.WEB: “Though I speak, my grief is not subsided. Though I forbear, what am I eased?
18_16_6.CSB17: If I speak, my suffering is not relieved, and if I hold back, does any of it leave me?
18_16_6.AMP: "If I speak [to you miserable comforters], my pain is not relieved; And if I refrain [from speaking], what [pain or anguish] leaves me?
18_16_6.MSG: "When I speak up, I feel no better;if I say nothing, that doesn't help either.
18_16_6.NIV: “Yet if I speak, my pain is not relieved;and if I refrain, it does not go away.
18_16_6.NRSVCE: “If I speak, my pain is not assuaged, and if I forbear, how much of it leaves me?

18_16_7.NKJV: But now He has worn me out; You have made desolate all my company.
18_16_7.KJV: But now he hath made me weary: thou hast made desolate all my company.
18_16_7.ESV: Surely now God has worn me out; he has made desolate all my company.
18_16_7.NASB: 'But now He has exhausted me; You have laid waste all my company.
18_16_7.NLT: “O God, you have ground me downand devastated my family.
18_16_7.WEB: But now, God, you have surely worn me out. You have made desolate all my company.
18_16_7.CSB17: Surely he has now exhausted me. You have devastated my entire family.
18_16_7.AMP: "But now God has exhausted me. You [O Lord] have destroyed all my family and my household.
18_16_7.MSG: I feel worn down.God, you have wasted me totally—me and my family!
18_16_7.NIV: Surely, O God, you have worn me out;you have devastated my entire household.
18_16_7.NRSVCE: Surely now God has worn me out; he has made desolate all my company.

18_16_8.NKJV: You have shriveled me up, And it is a witness against me; My leanness rises up against me And bears witness to my face.
18_16_8.KJV: And thou hast filled me with wrinkles, which is a witness against me: and my leanness rising up in me beareth witness to my face.
18_16_8.ESV: And he has shriveled me up, which is a witness against me, and my leanness has risen up against me; it testifies to my face.
18_16_8.NASB: 'You have shriveled me up, It has become a witness; And my leanness rises up against me, It testifies to my face.
18_16_8.NLT: As if to prove I have sinned, you’ve reduced me to skin and bones.My gaunt flesh testifies against me.
18_16_8.WEB: You have shriveled me up. This is a witness against me. My leanness rises up against me. It testifies to my face.
18_16_8.CSB17: You have shriveled me up — it has become a witness; my frailty rises up against me and testifies to my face.
18_16_8.AMP: "You have taken a firm hold on me and have shriveled me up, It has become a witness [against me]; And my leanness [and infirmity] rises up [as evidence] against me, It testifies to my face [about my guilt].
18_16_8.MSG: You've shriveled me like a dried prune,showing the world that you're against me.My gaunt face stares back at me from the mirror,a mute witness to your treatment of me.
18_16_8.NIV: You have bound me—and it has become a witness;my gauntness rises up and testifies against me.
18_16_8.NRSVCE: And he has shriveled me up, which is a witness against me; my leanness has risen up against me, and it testifies to my face.

18_16_9.NKJV: He tears me in His wrath, and hates me; He gnashes at me with His teeth; My adversary sharpens His gaze on me.
18_16_9.KJV: He teareth me in his wrath, who hateth me: he gnasheth upon me with his teeth; mine enemy sharpeneth his eyes upon me.
18_16_9.ESV: He has torn me in his wrath and hated me; he has gnashed his teeth at me; my adversary sharpens his eyes against me.
18_16_9.NASB: 'His anger has torn me and hunted me down, He has gnashed at me with His teeth; My adversary glares at me.
18_16_9.NLT: God hates me and angrily tears me apart.He snaps his teeth at meand pierces me with his eyes.
18_16_9.WEB: He has torn me in his wrath, and persecuted me. He has gnashed on me with his teeth. My adversary sharpens his eyes on me.
18_16_9.CSB17: His anger tears at me, and he harasses me. He gnashes his teeth at me. My enemy pierces me with his eyes.
18_16_9.AMP: "His wrath has torn me and hunted me down, He has gnashed at me with his teeth; My adversary sharpens His gaze and glares [with piercing eyes] at me.
18_16_9.MSG: Your anger tears at me,your teeth rip me to shreds,your eyes burn holes in me—God, my enemy!
18_16_9.NIV: God assails me and tears me in his angerand gnashes his teeth at me;my opponent fastens on me his piercing eyes.
18_16_9.NRSVCE: He has torn me in his wrath, and hated me; he has gnashed his teeth at me; my adversary sharpens his eyes against me.

18_16_10.NKJV: They gape at me with their mouth, They strike me reproachfully on the cheek, They gather together against me.
18_16_10.KJV: They have gaped upon me with their mouth; they have smitten me upon the cheek reproachfully; they have gathered themselves together against me.
18_16_10.ESV: Men have gaped at me with their mouth; they have struck me insolently on the cheek; they mass themselves together against me.
