18_16_10.NLT: People jeer and laugh at me.They slap my cheek in contempt.A mob gathers against me.
18_16_10.WEB: They have gaped on me with their mouth. They have struck me on the cheek reproachfully. They gather themselves together against me.
18_16_10.CSB17: They open their mouths against me and strike my cheeks with contempt; they join themselves together against me.
18_16_10.AMP: "They have gaped at me with their mouths, With contempt they have struck me on the cheek; They massed themselves together [and conspired] against me.
18_16_10.MSG: People take one look at me and gasp.Contemptuous, they slap me aroundand gang up against me.
18_16_10.NIV: Men open their mouths to jeer at me;they strike my cheek in scornand unite together against me.
18_16_10.NRSVCE: They have gaped at me with their mouths; they have struck me insolently on the cheek; they mass themselves together against me.

18_16_11.NKJV: God has delivered me to the ungodly, And turned me over to the hands of the wicked.
18_16_11.KJV: God hath delivered me to the ungodly, and turned me over into the hands of the wicked. hath: Heb. hath shut me up
18_16_11.ESV: God gives me up to the ungodly and casts me into the hands of the wicked.
18_16_11.NASB: 'God hands me over to ruffians And tosses me into the hands of the wicked.
18_16_11.NLT: God has handed me over to sinners.He has tossed me into the hands of the wicked.
18_16_11.WEB: God delivers me to the ungodly, and casts me into the hands of the wicked.
18_16_11.CSB17: God hands me over to the unjust; he throws me to the wicked.
18_16_11.AMP: "God hands me over to criminals And tosses me [headlong] into the hands of the wicked.
18_16_11.MSG: And God just stands there and lets them do it,lets wicked people do what they want with me.
18_16_11.NIV: God has turned me over to evil menand thrown me into the clutches of the wicked.
18_16_11.NRSVCE: God gives me up to the ungodly, and casts me into the hands of the wicked.

18_16_12.NKJV: I was at ease, but He has shattered me; He also has taken me by my neck, and shaken me to pieces; He has set me up for His target,
18_16_12.KJV: I was at ease, but he hath broken me asunder: he hath also taken me by my neck, and shaken me to pieces, and set me up for his mark.
18_16_12.ESV: I was at ease, and he broke me apart; he seized me by the neck and dashed me to pieces; he set me up as his target;
18_16_12.NASB: 'I was at ease, but He shattered me, And He has grasped me by the neck and shaken me to pieces; He has also set me up as His target.
18_16_12.NLT: “I was living quietly until he shattered me.He took me by the neck and broke me in pieces.Then he set me up as his target,
18_16_12.WEB: I was at ease, and he broke me apart. Yes, he has taken me by the neck, and dashed me to pieces. He has also set me up for his target.
18_16_12.CSB17: I was at ease, but he shattered me; he seized me by the scruff of the neck and smashed me to pieces. He set me up as his target;
18_16_12.AMP: "I was [living] at ease, but He crushed me and broke me apart, And He has seized me by the neck and has shaken me to pieces; He has also set me up as His target.
18_16_12.MSG: I was contentedly minding my business when God beat me up.He grabbed me by the neck and threw me around.He set me up as his target,
18_16_12.NIV: All was well with me, but he shattered me;he seized me by the neck and crushed me.He has made me his target;
18_16_12.NRSVCE: I was at ease, and he broke me in two; he seized me by the neck and dashed me to pieces; he set me up as his target;

18_16_13.NKJV: His archers surround me. He pierces my heart and does not pity; He pours out my gall on the ground.
18_16_13.KJV: His archers compass me round about, he cleaveth my reins asunder, and doth not spare; he poureth out my gall upon the ground.
18_16_13.ESV: his archers surround me. He slashes open my kidneys and does not spare; he pours out my gall on the ground.
18_16_13.NASB: 'His arrows surround me. Without mercy He splits my kidneys open; He pours out my gall on the ground.
18_16_13.NLT: and now his archers surround me.His arrows pierce me without mercy.The ground is wet with my blood.
18_16_13.WEB: His archers surround me. He splits my kidneys apart, and does not spare. He pours out my gall on the ground.
18_16_13.CSB17: his archers surround me. He pierces my kidneys without mercy and pours my bile on the ground.
18_16_13.AMP: "His arrows surround me. He pierces my kidneys (vital organs) without mercy; He pours out my gall on the ground.
18_16_13.MSG: then rounded up archers to shoot at me.Merciless, they shot me full of arrows;bitter bile poured from my gut to the ground.
18_16_13.NIV: his archers surround me.Without pity, he pierces my kidneysand spills my gall on the ground.
18_16_13.NRSVCE: his archers surround me. He slashes open my kidneys, and shows no mercy; he pours out my gall on the ground.

18_16_14.NKJV: He breaks me with wound upon wound; He runs at me like a warrior.
18_16_14.KJV: He breaketh me with breach upon breach, he runneth upon me like a giant.
18_16_14.ESV: He breaks me with breach upon breach; he runs upon me like a warrior.
18_16_14.NASB: 'He breaks through me with breach after breach; He runs at me like a warrior.
18_16_14.NLT: Again and again he smashes against me,charging at me like a warrior.
18_16_14.WEB: He breaks me with breach on breach. He runs on me like a giant.
18_16_14.CSB17: He breaks through my defenses again and again; he charges at me like a warrior.
18_16_14.AMP: "He attacks me, making wound after wound; He runs at me like a warrior.
18_16_14.MSG: He burst in on me, onslaught after onslaught,charging me like a mad bull.
18_16_14.NIV: Again and again he bursts upon me;he rushes at me like a warrior.
18_16_14.NRSVCE: He bursts upon me again and again; he rushes at me like a warrior.

18_16_15.NKJV: “I have sewn sackcloth over my skin, And laid my head in the dust.
18_16_15.KJV: I have sewed sackcloth upon my skin, and defiled my horn in the dust.
18_16_15.ESV: I have sewed sackcloth upon my skin and have laid my strength in the dust.
18_16_15.NASB: 'I have sewed sackcloth over my skin And thrust my horn in the dust.
18_16_15.NLT: I wear burlap to show my grief.My pride lies in the dust.
18_16_15.WEB: I have sewed sackcloth on my skin, and have thrust my horn in the dust.
18_16_15.CSB17: I have sewn sackcloth over my skin; I have buried my strength in the dust.
18_16_15.AMP: "I have sewed sackcloth over my skin [as a sign of mourning] And have defiled my horn (symbol of strength) in the dust.
18_16_15.MSG: "I sewed myself a shroud and wore it like a shirt;I lay face down in the dirt.
18_16_15.NIV: “I have sewed sackcloth over my skinand buried my brow in the dust.
18_16_15.NRSVCE: I have sewed sackcloth upon my skin, and have laid my strength in the dust.

18_16_16.NKJV: My face is flushed from weeping, And on my eyelids is the shadow of death;
18_16_16.KJV: My face is foul with weeping, and on my eyelids is the shadow of death;
18_16_16.ESV: My face is red with weeping, and on my eyelids is deep darkness,
18_16_16.NASB: 'My face is flushed from weeping, And deep darkness is on my eyelids,
18_16_16.NLT: My eyes are red with weeping;dark shadows circle my eyes.
18_16_16.WEB: My face is red with weeping. Deep darkness is on my eyelids.
18_16_16.CSB17: My face has grown red with weeping, and darkness covers my eyes,
18_16_16.AMP: "My face is red and swollen with weeping, And on my eyelids is the shadow of death [my eyes are dimmed],
18_16_16.MSG: Now my face is blotched red from weeping;look at the dark shadows under my eyes,
18_16_16.NIV: My face is red with weeping,deep shadows ring my eyes;
18_16_16.NRSVCE: My face is red with weeping, and deep darkness is on my eyelids,

18_16_17.NKJV: Although no violence is in my hands, And my prayer is pure.
18_16_17.KJV: Not for any injustice in mine hands: also my prayer is pure.
18_16_17.ESV: although there is no violence in my hands, and my prayer is pure.
18_16_17.NASB: Although there is no violence in my hands, And my prayer is pure.
18_16_17.NLT: Yet I have done no wrong,and my prayer is pure.
18_16_17.WEB: Although there is no violence in my hands, and my prayer is pure.
18_16_17.CSB17: although my hands are free from violence and my prayer is pure.
18_16_17.AMP: Although there is no violence or wrongdoing in my hands, And my prayer is pure.
18_16_17.MSG: Even though I've never hurt a souland my prayers are sincere!
18_16_17.NIV: yet my hands have been free of violenceand my prayer is pure.
18_16_17.NRSVCE: though there is no violence in my hands, and my prayer is pure.

18_16_18.NKJV: “O earth, do not cover my blood, And let my cry have no resting place!
18_16_18.KJV: O earth, cover not thou my blood, and let my cry have no place.
18_16_18.ESV: “O earth, cover not my blood, and let my cry find no resting place.
18_16_18.NASB: 'O earth, do not cover my blood, And let there be no [resting] place for my cry.
18_16_18.NLT: “O earth, do not conceal my blood.Let it cry out on my behalf.
18_16_18.WEB: “Earth, don’t cover my blood. Let my cry have no place to rest.
18_16_18.CSB17: Earth, do not cover my blood; may my cry for help find no resting place.
18_16_18.AMP: "O earth, do not cover my blood, And let there be no [resting] place for my cry [where it will cease being heard].
18_16_18.MSG: "O Earth, don't cover up the wrong done to me!Don't muffle my cry!
18_16_18.NIV: “O earth, do not cover my blood;may my cry never be laid to rest!
18_16_18.NRSVCE: “O earth, do not cover my blood; let my outcry find no resting place.

18_16_19.NKJV: Surely even now my witness is in heaven, And my evidence is on high.
18_16_19.KJV: Also now, behold, my witness is in heaven, and my record is on high. on high: Heb. in the high places
18_16_19.ESV: Even now, behold, my witness is in heaven, and he who testifies for me is on high.
18_16_19.NASB: 'Even now, behold, my witness is in heaven, And my advocate is on high.
18_16_19.NLT: Even now my witness is in heaven.My advocate is there on high.
18_16_19.WEB: Even now, behold, my witness is in heaven. He who vouches for me is on high.
18_16_19.CSB17: Even now my witness is in heaven, and my advocate is in the heights!
18_16_19.AMP: "Even now, behold, my Witness is in heaven, And my Advocate [who vouches and testifies for me] is on high.
18_16_19.MSG: There must be Someone in heaven who knows the truth about me,in highest heaven, some Attorney who can clear my name—
18_16_19.NIV: Even now my witness is in heaven;my advocate is on high.
18_16_19.NRSVCE: Even now, in fact, my witness is in heaven, and he that vouches for me is on high.

18_16_20.NKJV: My friends scorn me; My eyes pour out tears to God.
18_16_20.KJV: My friends scorn me: but mine eye poureth out tears unto God. scorn me: Heb. are my scorners
18_16_20.ESV: My friends scorn me; my eye pours out tears to God,
18_16_20.NASB: 'My friends are my scoffers; My eye weeps to God.
18_16_20.NLT: My friends scorn me,but I pour out my tears to God.
18_16_20.WEB: My friends scoff at me. My eyes pour out tears to God,
18_16_20.CSB17: My friends scoff at me as I weep before God.
18_16_20.AMP: "My friends are scoffers [who ridicule]; My eye pours out tears to God.
18_16_20.MSG: My Champion, my Friend,while I'm weeping my eyes out before God.
18_16_20.NIV: My intercessor is my friendas my eyes pour out tears to God;
18_16_20.NRSVCE: My friends scorn me; my eye pours out tears to God,

18_16_21.NKJV: Oh, that one might plead for a man with God, As a man pleads for his neighbor!
18_16_21.KJV: O that one might plead for a man with God, as a man pleadeth for his neighbour ! neighbour: or, friend
18_16_21.ESV: that he would argue the case of a man with God, as a son of man does with his neighbor.
18_16_21.NASB: 'O that a man might plead with God As a man with his neighbor!
18_16_21.NLT: I need someone to mediate between God and me,as a person mediates between friends.
18_16_21.WEB: that he would maintain the right of a man with God, of a son of man with his neighbor!
18_16_21.CSB17: I wish that someone might argue for a man with God just as anyone would for a friend.
18_16_21.AMP: "Oh, that a man would mediate and plead with God [for me] Just as a man [mediates and pleads] with his neighbor and friend.
18_16_21.MSG: I appeal to the One who represents mortals before Godas a neighbor stands up for a neighbor.
18_16_21.NIV: on behalf of a man he pleads with Godas a man pleads for his friend.
18_16_21.NRSVCE: that he would maintain the right of a mortal with God, as one does for a neighbor.

18_16_22.NKJV: For when a few years are finished, I shall go the way of no return.
18_16_22.KJV: When a few years are come, then I shall go the way whence I shall not return. a few: Heb. years of number
18_16_22.ESV: For when a few years have come I shall go the way from which I shall not return.
18_16_22.NASB: 'For when a few years are past, I shall go the way of no return.
18_16_22.NLT: For soon I must go down that roadfrom which I will never return.
18_16_22.WEB: For when a few years have come, I shall go the way of no return.
18_16_22.CSB17: For only a few years will pass before I go the way of no return.
18_16_22.AMP: "For when a few years are past, I shall go the way of no return.
18_16_22.MSG: "Only a few years are leftbefore I set out on the road of no return.
18_16_22.NIV: “Only a few years will passbefore I go on the journey of no return.
18_16_22.NRSVCE: For when a few years have come, I shall go the way from which I shall not return.

18_17_1.NKJV: “My spirit is broken, My days are extinguished, The grave is ready for me.
18_17_1.KJV: My breath is corrupt, my days are extinct, the graves are ready for me. breath: or, spirit is spent
18_17_1.ESV: “My spirit is broken; my days are extinct; the graveyard is ready for me.
18_17_1.NASB: 'My spirit is broken, my days are extinguished, The grave is [ready] for me.
18_17_1.NLT: “My spirit is crushed,and my life is nearly snuffed out.The grave is ready to receive me.
18_17_1.WEB: “My spirit is consumed. My days are extinct, And the grave is ready for me.
18_17_1.CSB17: My spirit is broken. My days are extinguished. A graveyard awaits me.
18_17_1.AMP: "My spirit is broken, my days are extinguished, The grave is ready for me.
18_17_1.MSG: "My spirit is broken,my days used up,my grave dug and waiting.
18_17_1.NIV: My spirit is broken,my days are cut short,the grave awaits me.
18_17_1.NRSVCE: My spirit is broken, my days are extinct, the grave is ready for me.

18_17_2.NKJV: Are not mockers with me? And does not my eye dwell on their provocation?
18_17_2.KJV: Are there not mockers with me? and doth not mine eye continue in their provocation? continue: Heb. lodge
18_17_2.ESV: Surely there are mockers about me, and my eye dwells on their provocation.
18_17_2.NASB: 'Surely mockers are with me, And my eye gazes on their provocation.
18_17_2.NLT: I am surrounded by mockers.I watch how bitterly they taunt me.
18_17_2.WEB: Surely there are mockers with me. My eye dwells on their provocation.
18_17_2.CSB17: Surely mockers surround me, and my eyes must gaze at their rebellion.
18_17_2.AMP: "Surely there are mockers and mockery with me, And my eye gazes on their obstinacy and provocation.
18_17_2.MSG: See how these mockers close in on me?How long do I have to put up with their insolence?
18_17_2.NIV: Surely mockers surround me;my eyes must dwell on their hostility.
18_17_2.NRSVCE: Surely there are mockers around me, and my eye dwells on their provocation.

18_17_3.NKJV: “Now put down a pledge for me with Yourself. Who is he who will shake hands with me?
18_17_3.KJV: Lay down now, put me in a surety with thee; who is he that will strike hands with me?
18_17_3.ESV: “Lay down a pledge for me with you; who is there who will put up security for me?
18_17_3.NASB: 'Lay down, now, a pledge for me with Yourself; Who is there that will be my guarantor?
18_17_3.NLT: “You must defend my innocence, O God,since no one else will stand up for me.
18_17_3.WEB: “Now give a pledge, be collateral for me with yourself. Who is there who will strike hands with me?
18_17_3.CSB17: Accept my pledge! Put up security for me. Who else will be my sponsor?
18_17_3.AMP: "Give me a pledge (guarantee, promise) with Yourself [acknowledge my innocence before my death]; Who is there that will be my guarantor and give security for me?
18_17_3.MSG: "O God, pledge your support for me.Give it to me in writing, with your signature.You're the only one who can do it!
18_17_3.NIV: “Give me, O God, the pledge you demand.Who else will put up security for me?
18_17_3.NRSVCE: “Lay down a pledge for me with yourself; who is there that will give surety for me?

18_17_4.NKJV: For You have hidden their heart from understanding; Therefore You will not exalt them.
18_17_4.KJV: For thou hast hid their heart from understanding: therefore shalt thou not exalt them.
18_17_4.ESV: Since you have closed their hearts to understanding, therefore you will not let them triumph.
18_17_4.NASB: 'For You have kept their heart from understanding, Therefore You will not exalt [them].
18_17_4.NLT: You have closed their minds to understanding,but do not let them triumph.
18_17_4.WEB: For you have hidden their heart from understanding, Therefore you shall not exalt them.
18_17_4.CSB17: You have closed their minds to understanding, therefore you will not honor them.
18_17_4.AMP: "But You [Lord] have closed their hearts to understanding, Therefore You will not exalt them [by giving a verdict against me].
18_17_4.MSG: These people are so useless!You know firsthand how stupid they can be.You wouldn't let them have the last word, would you?
18_17_4.NIV: You have closed their minds to understanding;therefore you will not let them triumph.
18_17_4.NRSVCE: Since you have closed their minds to understanding, therefore you will not let them triumph.

18_17_5.NKJV: He who speaks flattery to his friends, Even the eyes of his children will fail.
18_17_5.KJV: He that speaketh flattery to his friends, even the eyes of his children shall fail.
18_17_5.ESV: He who informs against his friends to get a share of their property — the eyes of his children will fail.
18_17_5.NASB: 'He who informs against friends for a share [of the spoil], The eyes of his children also will languish.
18_17_5.NLT: They betray their friends for their own advantage,so let their children faint with hunger.
18_17_5.WEB: He who denounces his friends for plunder, Even the eyes of his children shall fail.
18_17_5.CSB17: If a man denounces his friends for a price, the eyes of his children will fail.
18_17_5.AMP: "He who denounces and informs against his friends for a share of the spoil, The eyes of his children will also languish and fail.
18_17_5.MSG: Those who betray their own friendsleave a legacy of abuse to their children.
18_17_5.NIV: If a man denounces his friends for reward,the eyes of his children will fail.
18_17_5.NRSVCE: Those who denounce friends for reward — the eyes of their children will fail.

18_17_6.NKJV: “But He has made me a byword of the people, And I have become one in whose face men spit.
18_17_6.KJV: He hath made me also a byword of the people; and aforetime I was as a tabret. aforetime: or, before them
18_17_6.ESV: “He has made me a byword of the peoples, and I am one before whom men spit.
18_17_6.NASB: 'But He has made me a byword of the people, And I am one at whom men spit.
18_17_6.NLT: “God has made a mockery of me among the people;they spit in my face.
18_17_6.WEB: “But he has made me a byword of the people. They spit in my face.
18_17_6.CSB17: He has made me an object of scorn to the people; I have become a man people spit at.
18_17_6.AMP: "But He has made me a byword and mockery among the people, And I have become one in whose face people spit.
18_17_6.MSG: "God, you've made me the talk of the town—people spit in my face;
18_17_6.NIV: “God has made me a byword to everyone,a man in whose face people spit.
18_17_6.NRSVCE: “He has made me a byword of the peoples, and I am one before whom people spit.

18_17_7.NKJV: My eye has also grown dim because of sorrow, And all my members are like shadows.
18_17_7.KJV: Mine eye also is dim by reason of sorrow, and all my members are as a shadow. my members: or, my thoughts
18_17_7.ESV: My eye has grown dim from vexation, and all my members are like a shadow.
18_17_7.NASB: 'My eye has also grown dim because of grief, And all my members are as a shadow.
18_17_7.NLT: My eyes are swollen with weeping,and I am but a shadow of my former self.
18_17_7.WEB: My eye also is dim by reason of sorrow. All my members are as a shadow.
18_17_7.CSB17: My eyes have grown dim from grief, and my whole body has become but a shadow.
18_17_7.AMP: "My eye has grown dim (unexpressive) because of grief, And all my [body's] members are [wasted away] like a shadow.
18_17_7.MSG: I can hardly see from crying so much;I'm nothing but skin and bones.
18_17_7.NIV: My eyes have grown dim with grief;my whole frame is but a shadow.
18_17_7.NRSVCE: My eye has grown dim from grief, and all my members are like a shadow.

18_17_8.NKJV: Upright men are astonished at this, And the innocent stirs himself up against the hypocrite.
18_17_8.KJV: Upright men shall be astonied at this, and the innocent shall stir up himself against the hypocrite.
18_17_8.ESV: The upright are appalled at this, and the innocent stirs himself up against the godless.
18_17_8.NASB: 'The upright will be appalled at this, And the innocent will stir up himself against the godless.
18_17_8.NLT: The virtuous are horrified when they see me.The innocent rise up against the ungodly.
18_17_8.WEB: Upright men shall be astonished at this. The innocent shall stir up himself against the godless.
18_17_8.CSB17: The upright are appalled at this, and the innocent are roused against the godless.
18_17_8.AMP: "The upright will be [astonished and] appalled at this, And the innocent will stir himself up against the godless and polluted.
18_17_8.MSG: Decent people can't believe what they're seeing;the good-hearted wake up and insist I've given up on God.
18_17_8.NIV: Upright men are appalled at this;the innocent are aroused against the ungodly.
18_17_8.NRSVCE: The upright are appalled at this, and the innocent stir themselves up against the godless.

18_17_9.NKJV: Yet the righteous will hold to his way, And he who has clean hands will be stronger and stronger.
18_17_9.KJV: The righteous also shall hold on his way, and he that hath clean hands shall be stronger and stronger. be: Heb. add strength
18_17_9.ESV: Yet the righteous holds to his way, and he who has clean hands grows stronger and stronger.
18_17_9.NASB: 'Nevertheless the righteous will hold to his way, And he who has clean hands will grow stronger and stronger.
18_17_9.NLT: The righteous keep moving forward,and those with clean hands become stronger and stronger.
18_17_9.WEB: Yet shall the righteous hold on his way. He who has clean hands shall grow stronger and stronger.
18_17_9.CSB17: Yet the righteous person will hold to his way, and the one whose hands are clean will grow stronger.
18_17_9.AMP: "Nevertheless the righteous will hold to his ways, And he who has clean hands will grow stronger and stronger.
18_17_9.MSG: "But principled people hold tight, keep a firm grip on life,sure that their clean, pure hands will get stronger and stronger!
18_17_9.NIV: Nevertheless, the righteous will hold to their ways,and those with clean hands will grow stronger.
18_17_9.NRSVCE: Yet the righteous hold to their way, and they that have clean hands grow stronger and stronger.

18_17_10.NKJV: “But please, come back again, all of you, For I shall not find one wise man among you.
18_17_10.KJV: But as for you all, do ye return, and come now: for I cannot find one wise man among you.
18_17_10.ESV: But you, come on again, all of you, and I shall not find a wise man among you.
18_17_10.NASB: 'But come again all of you now, For I do not find a wise man among you.
18_17_10.NLT: “As for all of you, come back with a better argument,though I still won’t find a wise man among you.
18_17_10.WEB: But as for you all, come on now again; I shall not find a wise man among you.
18_17_10.CSB17: But come back and try again, all of you. I will not find a wise man among you.
18_17_10.AMP: "But as for all of you, come back again, Even though I do not find a wise man among you.
18_17_10.MSG: "Maybe you'd all like to start over,to try it again, the bunch of you.So far I haven't come across one scrapof wisdom in anything you've said.
18_17_10.NIV: “But come on, all of you, try again!I will not find a wise man among you.
18_17_10.NRSVCE: But you, come back now, all of you, and I shall not find a sensible person among you.

18_17_11.NKJV: My days are past, My purposes are broken off, Even the thoughts of my heart.
18_17_11.KJV: My days are past, my purposes are broken off, even the thoughts of my heart. the thoughts: Heb. the possessions
18_17_11.ESV: My days are past; my plans are broken off, the desires of my heart.
18_17_11.NASB: 'My days are past, my plans are torn apart, [Even] the wishes of my heart.
18_17_11.NLT: My days are over.My hopes have disappeared.My heart’s desires are broken.
18_17_11.WEB: My days are past, my plans are broken off, as are the thoughts of my heart.
18_17_11.CSB17: My days have slipped by; my plans have been ruined, even the things dear to my heart.
18_17_11.AMP: "My days are past, my purposes and plans are frustrated and torn apart; The wishes of my heart [are broken].
18_17_11.MSG: My life's about over. All my plans are smashed,all my hopes are snuffed out—
18_17_11.NIV: My days have passed, my plans are shattered,and so are the desires of my heart.
18_17_11.NRSVCE: My days are past, my plans are broken off, the desires of my heart.

18_17_12.NKJV: They change the night into day; ‘The light is near,’ they say, in the face of darkness.
18_17_12.KJV: They change the night into day: the light is short because of darkness. short: Heb. near
18_17_12.ESV: They make night into day: ‘The light,’ they say, ‘is near to the darkness.’
18_17_12.NASB: 'They make night into day, [saying], 'The light is near,' in the presence of darkness.
18_17_12.NLT: These men say that night is day;they claim that the darkness is light.
18_17_12.WEB: They change the night into day, saying ‘The light is near’ in the presence of darkness.
18_17_12.CSB17: They turned night into day and made light seem near in the face of darkness.
18_17_12.AMP: "These [thoughts try to] make the night into the day; 'The light is near,' they say in the presence of darkness [but they pervert the truth].
18_17_12.MSG: My hope that night would turn into day,my hope that dawn was about to break.
18_17_12.NIV: These men turn night into day;in the face of darkness they say, ‘Light is near.’
18_17_12.NRSVCE: They make night into day; ‘The light,’ they say, ‘is near to the darkness.’

18_17_13.NKJV: If I wait for the grave as my house, If I make my bed in the darkness,
18_17_13.KJV: If I wait, the grave is mine house: I have made my bed in the darkness.
18_17_13.ESV: If I hope for Sheol as my house, if I make my bed in darkness,
18_17_13.NASB: 'If I look for Sheol as my home, I make my bed in the darkness;
18_17_13.NLT: What if I go to the graveand make my bed in darkness?
18_17_13.WEB: If I look for Sheol as my house, if I have spread my couch in the darkness,
18_17_13.CSB17: If I await Sheol as my home, spread out my bed in darkness,
18_17_13.AMP: "But if I look to Sheol (the nether world, the place of the dead) as my home, If I make my bed in the darkness,
18_17_13.MSG: If all I have to look forward to is a home in the graveyard,if my only hope for comfort is a well-built coffin,
18_17_13.NIV: If the only home I hope for is the grave,if I spread out my bed in darkness,
18_17_13.NRSVCE: If I look for Sheol as my house, if I spread my couch in darkness,

18_17_14.NKJV: If I say to corruption, ‘You are my father,’ And to the worm, ‘You are my mother and my sister,’
18_17_14.KJV: I have said to corruption, Thou art my father: to the worm, Thou art my mother, and my sister. said: Heb. cried, or, called
18_17_14.ESV: if I say to the pit, ‘You are my father,’ and to the worm, ‘My mother,’ or ‘My sister,’
18_17_14.NASB: If I call to the pit, 'You are my father'; To the worm, 'my mother and my sister';
18_17_14.NLT: What if I call the grave my father,and the maggot my mother or my sister?
18_17_14.WEB: If I have said to corruption, ‘You are my father;’ to the worm, ‘My mother,’ and ‘my sister;’
18_17_14.CSB17: and say to corruption, “You are my father,” and to the maggot, “My mother” or “My sister,”
18_17_14.AMP: If I call out to the pit (grave), 'You are my father'; And to the worm [that feeds on decay], 'You are my mother and my sister [because I will soon be closest to you],'
18_17_14.MSG: If a family reunion means going six feet under,and the only family that shows up is worms,
18_17_14.NIV: if I say to corruption, ‘You are my father,’and to the worm, ‘My mother’ or ‘My sister,’
18_17_14.NRSVCE: if I say to the Pit, ‘You are my father,’ and to the worm, ‘My mother,’ or ‘My sister,’

18_17_15.NKJV: Where then is my hope? As for my hope, who can see it?
18_17_15.KJV: And where is now my hope? as for my hope, who shall see it?
18_17_15.ESV: where then is my hope? Who will see my hope?
18_17_15.NASB: Where now is my hope? And who regards my hope?
18_17_15.NLT: Where then is my hope?Can anyone find it?
18_17_15.WEB: where then is my hope? as for my hope, who shall see it?
18_17_15.CSB17: where then is my hope? Who can see any hope for me?
18_17_15.AMP: Where now is my hope? And who regards or considers or is even concerned about my hope?
18_17_15.MSG: Do you call that hope?Who on earth could find any hope in that?
18_17_15.NIV: where then is my hope?Who can see any hope for me?
18_17_15.NRSVCE: where then is my hope? Who will see my hope?

18_17_16.NKJV: Will they go down to the gates of Sheol? Shall we have rest together in the dust?”
18_17_16.KJV: They shall go down to the bars of the pit, when our rest together is in the dust.
18_17_16.ESV: Will it go down to the bars of Sheol? Shall we descend together into the dust?”
18_17_16.NASB: 'Will it go down with me to Sheol? Shall we together go down into the dust?'
18_17_16.NLT: No, my hope will go down with me to the grave.We will rest together in the dust!”
18_17_16.WEB: Shall it go down with me to the gates of Sheol, or descend together into the dust?”
18_17_16.CSB17: Will it go down to the gates of Sheol, or will we descend together to the dust?
18_17_16.AMP: "Will my hope go down with me to Sheol (the nether world, the place of the dead)? Shall we go down together in the dust?"
18_17_16.MSG: No. If hope and I are to be buried together,I suppose you'll all come to the double funeral!"
18_17_16.NIV: Will it go down to the gates of death?Will we descend together into the dust?”
18_17_16.NRSVCE: Will it go down to the bars of Sheol? Shall we descend together into the dust?”

18_18_1.NKJV: Then Bildad the Shuhite answered and said:
18_18_1.KJV: Then answered Bildad the Shuhite, and said,
18_18_1.ESV: Then Bildad the Shuhite answered and said:
18_18_1.AMP: Then Bildad the Shuhite answered and said,

18_18_2.NKJV: “How long till you put an end to words? Gain understanding, and afterward we will speak.
18_18_2.KJV: How long will it be ere ye make an end of words? mark, and afterwards we will speak.
18_18_2.ESV: “How long will you hunt for words? Consider, and then we will speak.
18_18_2.NASB: 'How long will you hunt for words? Show understanding and then we can talk.
18_18_2.NLT: “How long before you stop talking?Speak sense if you want us to answer!
18_18_2.WEB: “How long will you hunt for words? Consider, and afterwards we will speak.
18_18_2.CSB17: How long until you stop talking? Show some sense, and then we can talk.
18_18_2.AMP: "How long will you hunt for words and continue these speeches? Do some clear thinking and show understanding and then we can talk.
18_18_2.MSG: "How monotonous these word games are getting!Get serious! We need to get down to business.
18_18_2.NIV: “When will you end these speeches?Be sensible, and then we can talk.
18_18_2.NRSVCE: “How long will you hunt for words? Consider, and then we shall speak.

18_18_3.NKJV: Why are we counted as beasts, And regarded as stupid in your sight?
18_18_3.KJV: Wherefore are we counted as beasts, and reputed vile in your sight?
18_18_3.ESV: Why are we counted as cattle? Why are we stupid in your sight?
18_18_3.NASB: 'Why are we regarded as beasts, As stupid in your eyes?
18_18_3.NLT: Do you think we are mere animals?Do you think we are stupid?
18_18_3.WEB: Why are we counted as animals, which have become unclean in your sight?
18_18_3.CSB17: Why are we regarded as cattle, as stupid in your sight?
18_18_3.AMP: "Why are we regarded as beasts, As if [we are] stupid (senseless) in your eyes?
18_18_3.MSG: Why do you treat your friends like slow-witted animals?You look down on us as if we don't know anything.
18_18_3.NIV: Why are we regarded as cattleand considered stupid in your sight?
18_18_3.NRSVCE: Why are we counted as cattle? Why are we stupid in your sight?

18_18_4.NKJV: You who tear yourself in anger, Shall the earth be forsaken for you? Or shall the rock be removed from its place?
18_18_4.KJV: He teareth himself in his anger: shall the earth be forsaken for thee? and shall the rock be removed out of his place? himself: Heb. his soul
18_18_4.ESV: You who tear yourself in your anger, shall the earth be forsaken for you, or the rock be removed out of its place?
18_18_4.NASB: 'O you who tear yourself in your anger-- For your sake is the earth to be abandoned, Or the rock to be moved from its place?
18_18_4.NLT: You may tear out your hair in anger,but will that destroy the earth?Will it make the rocks tremble?
18_18_4.WEB: You who tear yourself in your anger, shall the earth be forsaken for you? Or shall the rock be removed out of its place?
18_18_4.CSB17: You who tear yourself in anger — should the earth be abandoned on your account, or a rock be removed from its place?
18_18_4.AMP: "You who tear yourself apart in anger, Is the earth to be abandoned for your sake, Or the rock to be moved out of its place?
18_18_4.MSG: Why are you working yourself up like this?Do you want the world redesigned to suit you?Should reality be suspended to accommodate you?
18_18_4.NIV: You who tear yourself to pieces in your anger,is the earth to be abandoned for your sake?Or must the rocks be moved from their place?
18_18_4.NRSVCE: You who tear yourself in your anger — shall the earth be forsaken because of you, or the rock be removed out of its place?

18_18_5.NKJV: “The light of the wicked indeed goes out, And the flame of his fire does not shine.
18_18_5.KJV: Yea, the light of the wicked shall be put out, and the spark of his fire shall not shine.
18_18_5.ESV: “Indeed, the light of the wicked is put out, and the flame of his fire does not shine.
18_18_5.NASB: 'Indeed, the light of the wicked goes out, And the flame of his fire gives no light.
18_18_5.NLT: “Surely the light of the wicked will be snuffed out.The sparks of their fire will not glow.
18_18_5.WEB: “Yes, the light of the wicked shall be put out, The spark of his fire shall not shine.
18_18_5.CSB17: Yes, the light of the wicked is extinguished; the flame of his fire does not glow.
18_18_5.AMP: "Indeed, the light of the wicked will be put out, And the flame of his fire will not shine.
18_18_5.MSG: "Here's the rule: The light of the wicked is put out.Their flame dies down and is extinguished.
18_18_5.NIV: “The lamp of the wicked is snuffed out;the flame of his fire stops burning.
18_18_5.NRSVCE: “Surely the light of the wicked is put out, and the flame of their fire does not shine.

18_18_6.NKJV: The light is dark in his tent, And his lamp beside him is put out.
18_18_6.KJV: The light shall be dark in his tabernacle, and his candle shall be put out with him. candle: or, lamp
18_18_6.ESV: The light is dark in his tent, and his lamp above him is put out.
18_18_6.NASB: 'The light in his tent is darkened, And his lamp goes out above him.
18_18_6.NLT: The light in their tent will grow dark.The lamp hanging above them will be quenched.
18_18_6.WEB: The light shall be dark in his tent. His lamp above him shall be put out.
18_18_6.CSB17: The light in his tent grows dark, and the lamp beside him is put out.
18_18_6.AMP: "The light will be dark in his tent, And his lamp beside him will be put out.
18_18_6.MSG: Their house goes dark—every lamp in the place goes out.
18_18_6.NIV: The light in his tent becomes dark;the lamp beside him goes out.
18_18_6.NRSVCE: The light is dark in their tent, and the lamp above them is put out.

18_18_7.NKJV: The steps of his strength are shortened, And his own counsel casts him down.
18_18_7.KJV: The steps of his strength shall be straitened, and his own counsel shall cast him down.
18_18_7.ESV: His strong steps are shortened, and his own schemes throw him down.
18_18_7.NASB: 'His vigorous stride is shortened, And his own scheme brings him down.
18_18_7.NLT: The confident stride of the wicked will be shortened.Their own schemes will be their downfall.
18_18_7.WEB: The steps of his strength shall be shortened. His own counsel shall cast him down.
18_18_7.CSB17: His powerful stride is shortened, and his own schemes trip him up.
18_18_7.AMP: "The vigorous stride will be shortened, And his own counsel and the scheme [in which he trusted] will bring his downfall.
18_18_7.MSG: Their strong strides weaken, falter;they stumble into their own traps.
18_18_7.NIV: The vigor of his step is weakened;his own schemes throw him down.
18_18_7.NRSVCE: Their strong steps are shortened, and their own schemes throw them down.

18_18_8.NKJV: For he is cast into a net by his own feet, And he walks into a snare.
18_18_8.KJV: For he is cast into a net by his own feet, and he walketh upon a snare.
18_18_8.ESV: For he is cast into a net by his own feet, and he walks on its mesh.
18_18_8.NASB: 'For he is thrown into the net by his own feet, And he steps on the webbing.
18_18_8.NLT: The wicked walk into a net.They fall into a pit.
18_18_8.WEB: For he is cast into a net by his own feet, and he wanders into its mesh.
18_18_8.CSB17: For his own feet lead him into a net, and he strays into its mesh.
18_18_8.AMP: "For the wicked is thrown into a net by his own feet (wickedness), And he steps on the webbing [of the lattice-covered pit].
18_18_8.MSG: They get all tangled upin their own red tape,
18_18_8.NIV: His feet thrust him into a netand he wanders into its mesh.
18_18_8.NRSVCE: For they are thrust into a net by their own feet, and they walk into a pitfall.

18_18_9.NKJV: The net takes him by the heel, And a snare lays hold of him.
18_18_9.KJV: The gin shall take him by the heel, and the robber shall prevail against him.
18_18_9.ESV: A trap seizes him by the heel; a snare lays hold of him.
18_18_9.NASB: 'A snare seizes [him] by the heel, [And] a trap snaps shut on him.
18_18_9.NLT: A trap grabs them by the heel.A snare holds them tight.
18_18_9.WEB: A snare will take him by the heel. A trap will catch him.
18_18_9.CSB17: A trap catches him by the heel; a noose seizes him.
18_18_9.AMP: "A snare catches him by the heel, And a trap snaps shut on him.
18_18_9.MSG: Their feet are grabbed and caught,their necks in a noose.
18_18_9.NIV: A trap seizes him by the heel;a snare holds him fast.
18_18_9.NRSVCE: A trap seizes them by the heel; a snare lays hold of them.

18_18_10.NKJV: A noose is hidden for him on the ground, And a trap for him in the road.
18_18_10.KJV: The snare is laid for him in the ground, and a trap for him in the way. laid: Heb. hidden
18_18_10.ESV: A rope is hidden for him in the ground, a trap for him in the path.
18_18_10.NASB: 'A noose for him is hidden in the ground, And a trap for him on the path.
18_18_10.NLT: A noose lies hidden on the ground.A rope is stretched across their path.
18_18_10.WEB: A noose is hidden for him in the ground, a trap for him on the path.
18_18_10.CSB17: A rope lies hidden for him on the ground, and a snare waits for him along the path.
18_18_10.AMP: "A noose is hidden for him on the ground, And a trap for him on the path.
18_18_10.MSG: They trip on ropes they've hidden,and fall into pits they've dug themselves.
18_18_10.NIV: A noose is hidden for him on the ground;a trap lies in his path.
18_18_10.NRSVCE: A rope is hid for them in the ground, a trap for them in the path.

18_18_11.NKJV: Terrors frighten him on every side, And drive him to his feet.
18_18_11.KJV: Terrors shall make him afraid on every side, and shall drive him to his feet. drive: Heb. scatter
18_18_11.ESV: Terrors frighten him on every side, and chase him at his heels.
18_18_11.NASB: 'All around terrors frighten him, And harry him at every step.
18_18_11.NLT: “Terrors surround the wickedand trouble them at every step.
18_18_11.WEB: Terrors shall make him afraid on every side, and shall chase him at his heels.
18_18_11.CSB17: Terrors frighten him on every side and harass him at every step.
18_18_11.AMP: "Terrors frighten him on every side And chase at his heels.
18_18_11.MSG: Terrors come at them from all sides.They run helter-skelter.
18_18_11.NIV: Terrors startle him on every sideand dog his every step.
18_18_11.NRSVCE: Terrors frighten them on every side, and chase them at their heels.

18_18_12.NKJV: His strength is starved, And destruction is ready at his side.
18_18_12.KJV: His strength shall be hungerbitten, and destruction shall be ready at his side.
18_18_12.ESV: His strength is famished, and calamity is ready for his stumbling.
18_18_12.NASB: 'His strength is famished, And calamity is ready at his side.
18_18_12.NLT: Hunger depletes their strength,and calamity waits for them to stumble.
18_18_12.WEB: His strength shall be famished. Calamity shall be ready at his side.
18_18_12.CSB17: His strength is depleted; disaster lies ready for him to stumble.
18_18_12.AMP: "The strength [of the wicked] is famished and weakened, And disaster is ready at his side [if he stops].
18_18_12.MSG: The hungry grave is readyto gobble them up for supper,
18_18_12.NIV: Calamity is hungry for him;disaster is ready for him when he falls.
18_18_12.NRSVCE: Their strength is consumed by hunger, and calamity is ready for their stumbling.

18_18_13.NKJV: It devours patches of his skin; The firstborn of death devours his limbs.
18_18_13.KJV: It shall devour the strength of his skin: even the firstborn of death shall devour his strength. strength: Heb. bars
18_18_13.ESV: It consumes the parts of his skin; the firstborn of death consumes his limbs.
18_18_13.NASB: 'His skin is devoured by disease, The firstborn of death devours his limbs.
18_18_13.NLT: Disease eats their skin;death devours their limbs.
18_18_13.WEB: The members of his body shall be devoured. The firstborn of death shall devour his members.
18_18_13.CSB17: Parts of his skin are eaten away; death’s firstborn consumes his limbs.
18_18_13.AMP: "His skin is devoured by disease; The firstborn of death [the worst of diseases] consumes his limbs.
18_18_13.MSG: To lay them out for a gourmet meal,a treat for ravenous Death.
18_18_13.NIV: It eats away parts of his skin;death’s firstborn devours his limbs.
18_18_13.NRSVCE: By disease their skin is consumed, the firstborn of Death consumes their limbs.

18_18_14.NKJV: He is uprooted from the shelter of his tent, And they parade him before the king of terrors.
18_18_14.KJV: His confidence shall be rooted out of his tabernacle, and it shall bring him to the king of terrors.
18_18_14.ESV: He is torn from the tent in which he trusted and is brought to the king of terrors.
18_18_14.NASB: 'He is torn from the security of his tent, And they march him before the king of terrors.
18_18_14.NLT: They are torn from the security of their homesand are brought down to the king of terrors.
18_18_14.WEB: He shall be rooted out of the security of his tent. He shall be brought to the king of terrors.
18_18_14.CSB17: He is ripped from the security of his tent and marched away to the king of terrors.
18_18_14.AMP: "He is torn from his tent which he trusted [for safety], And he is marched and brought to the king of terrors (death).
18_18_14.MSG: They are snatched from their home sweet homeand marched straight to the death house.
18_18_14.NIV: He is torn from the security of his tentand marched off to the king of terrors.
18_18_14.NRSVCE: They are torn from the tent in which they trusted, and are brought to the king of terrors.

18_18_15.NKJV: They dwell in his tent who are none of his; Brimstone is scattered on his dwelling.
18_18_15.KJV: It shall dwell in his tabernacle, because it is none of his: brimstone shall be scattered upon his habitation.
18_18_15.ESV: In his tent dwells that which is none of his; sulfur is scattered over his habitation.
18_18_15.NASB: 'There dwells in his tent nothing of his; Brimstone is scattered on his habitation.
18_18_15.NLT: The homes of the wicked will burn down;burning sulfur rains on their houses.
18_18_15.WEB: There shall dwell in his tent that which is none of his. Sulfur shall be scattered on his habitation.
18_18_15.CSB17: Nothing he owned remains in his tent. Burning sulfur is scattered over his home.
18_18_15.AMP: "Nothing of his dwells in his tent; Brimstone (burning sulfur) is scattered over his dwelling [to purify it].
18_18_15.MSG: Their lives go up in smoke;acid rain soaks their ruins.
18_18_15.NIV: Fire resides in his tent;burning sulfur is scattered over his dwelling.
18_18_15.NRSVCE: In their tents nothing remains; sulfur is scattered upon their habitations.

18_18_16.NKJV: His roots are dried out below, And his branch withers above.
18_18_16.KJV: His roots shall be dried up beneath, and above shall his branch be cut off.
18_18_16.ESV: His roots dry up beneath, and his branches wither above.
18_18_16.NASB: 'His roots are dried below, And his branch is cut off above.
18_18_16.NLT: Their roots will dry up,and their branches will wither.
18_18_16.WEB: His roots shall be dried up beneath. Above shall his branch be cut off.
18_18_16.CSB17: His roots below dry up, and his branches above wither away.
18_18_16.AMP: "The roots [of the wicked] are dried up below, And above, his branch is cut off and withers.
18_18_16.MSG: Their roots rotand their branches wither.
18_18_16.NIV: His roots dry up belowand his branches wither above.
18_18_16.NRSVCE: Their roots dry up beneath, and their branches wither above.

18_18_17.NKJV: The memory of him perishes from the earth, And he has no name among the renowned.
18_18_17.KJV: His remembrance shall perish from the earth, and he shall have no name in the street .
18_18_17.ESV: His memory perishes from the earth, and he has no name in the street.
18_18_17.NASB: 'Memory of him perishes from the earth, And he has no name abroad.
18_18_17.NLT: All memory of their existence will fade from the earth;no one will remember their names.
18_18_17.WEB: His memory shall perish from the earth. He shall have no name in the street.
18_18_17.CSB17: All memory of him perishes from the earth; he has no name anywhere.
18_18_17.AMP: "Memory of him perishes from the earth, And he has no name on the street.
18_18_17.MSG: They'll never again be remembered—nameless in unmarked graves.
18_18_17.NIV: The memory of him perishes from the earth;he has no name in the land.
18_18_17.NRSVCE: Their memory perishes from the earth, and they have no name in the street.

18_18_18.NKJV: He is driven from light into darkness, And chased out of the world.
18_18_18.KJV: He shall be driven from light into darkness, and chased out of the world. He: Heb. They shall drive him
18_18_18.ESV: He is thrust from light into darkness, and driven out of the world.
18_18_18.NASB: 'He is driven from light into darkness, And chased from the inhabited world.
18_18_18.NLT: They will be thrust from light into darkness,driven from the world.
18_18_18.WEB: He shall be driven from light into darkness, and chased out of the world.
18_18_18.CSB17: He is driven from light to darkness and chased from the inhabited world.
18_18_18.AMP: "He is driven and propelled from light into darkness, And chased from the inhabited world.
18_18_18.MSG: They are plunged from light into darkness,banished from the world.
18_18_18.NIV: He is driven from light into darknessand is banished from the world.
18_18_18.NRSVCE: They are thrust from light into darkness, and driven out of the world.

18_18_19.NKJV: He has neither son nor posterity among his people, Nor any remaining in his dwellings.
18_18_19.KJV: He shall neither have son nor nephew among his people, nor any remaining in his dwellings.
18_18_19.ESV: He has no posterity or progeny among his people, and no survivor where he used to live.
18_18_19.NASB: 'He has no offspring or posterity among his people, Nor any survivor where he sojourned.
18_18_19.NLT: They will have neither children nor grandchildren,nor any survivor in the place where they lived.
18_18_19.WEB: He shall have neither son nor grandson among his people, nor any remaining where he lived.
18_18_19.CSB17: He has no children or descendants among his people, no survivor where he used to live.
18_18_19.AMP: "He has no offspring or prosperity among his people, Nor any survivor where he sojourned.
18_18_19.MSG: And they leave empty-handed—not one single child—nothing to show for their life on this earth.
18_18_19.NIV: He has no offspring or descendants among his people,no survivor where once he lived.
18_18_19.NRSVCE: They have no offspring or descendant among their people, and no survivor where they used to live.

18_18_20.NKJV: Those in the west are astonished at his day, As those in the east are frightened.
18_18_20.KJV: They that come after him shall be astonied at his day, as they that went before were affrighted . went: or, lived with him were: Heb. laid hold on horror
18_18_20.ESV: They of the west are appalled at his day, and horror seizes them of the east.
18_18_20.NASB: 'Those in the west are appalled at his fate, And those in the east are seized with horror.
18_18_20.NLT: People in the west are appalled at their fate;people in the east are horrified.
18_18_20.WEB: Those who come after shall be astonished at his day, as those who went before were frightened.
18_18_20.CSB17: Those in the west are appalled at his fate, while those in the east tremble in horror.
18_18_20.AMP: "Those in the west are astonished and appalled at his fate, And those in the east are seized with horror.
18_18_20.MSG: Westerners are aghast at their fate,easterners are horrified:
18_18_20.NIV: Men of the west are appalled at his fate;men of the east are seized with horror.
18_18_20.NRSVCE: They of the west are appalled at their fate, and horror seizes those of the east.

18_18_21.NKJV: Surely such are the dwellings of the wicked, And this is the place of him who does not know God.”
