18_20_18.NRSVCE: They will give back the fruit of their toil, and will not swallow it down; from the profit of their trading they will get no enjoyment.

18_20_19.NKJV: For he has oppressed and forsaken the poor, He has violently seized a house which he did not build.
18_20_19.KJV: Because he hath oppressed and hath forsaken the poor; because he hath violently taken away an house which he builded not; oppressed: Heb. crushed
18_20_19.ESV: For he has crushed and abandoned the poor; he has seized a house that he did not build.
18_20_19.NASB: 'For he has oppressed [and] forsaken the poor; He has seized a house which he has not built.
18_20_19.NLT: For they oppressed the poor and left them destitute.They foreclosed on their homes.
18_20_19.WEB: For he has oppressed and forsaken the poor. He has violently taken away a house, and he shall not build it up.
18_20_19.CSB17: For he oppressed and abandoned the poor; he seized a house he did not build.
18_20_19.AMP: "For he has oppressed and neglected the poor; He has violently taken away houses which he did not build.
18_20_19.MSG: And why? Because they exploited the poor,took what never belonged to them.
18_20_19.NIV: For he has oppressed the poor and left them destitute;he has seized houses he did not build.
18_20_19.NRSVCE: For they have crushed and abandoned the poor, they have seized a house that they did not build.

18_20_20.NKJV: “Because he knows no quietness in his heart, He will not save anything he desires.
18_20_20.KJV: Surely he shall not feel quietness in his belly, he shall not save of that which he desired. feel: Heb. know
18_20_20.ESV: “Because he knew no contentment in his belly, he will not let anything in which he delights escape him.
18_20_20.NASB: 'Because he knew no quiet within him, He does not retain anything he desires.
18_20_20.NLT: They were always greedy and never satisfied.Nothing remains of all the things they dreamed about.
18_20_20.WEB: “Because he knew no quietness within him, he shall not save anything of that in which he delights.
18_20_20.CSB17: Because his appetite is never satisfied, he does not let anything he desires escape.
18_20_20.AMP: "Because he knew no quietness or calm within him [because of his greed], He does not retain anything he desires.
18_20_20.MSG: "Such God-denying people are never content with what they haveor who they are;their greed drives them relentlessly.
18_20_20.NIV: “Surely he will have no respite from his craving;he cannot save himself by his treasure.
18_20_20.NRSVCE: “They knew no quiet in their bellies; in their greed they let nothing escape.

18_20_21.NKJV: Nothing is left for him to eat; Therefore his well-being will not last.
18_20_21.KJV: There shall none of his meat be left; therefore shall no man look for his goods. none: or, be none left for his meat
18_20_21.ESV: There was nothing left after he had eaten; therefore his prosperity will not endure.
18_20_21.NASB: 'Nothing remains for him to devour, Therefore his prosperity does not endure.
18_20_21.NLT: Nothing is left after they finish gorging themselves.Therefore, their prosperity will not endure.
18_20_21.WEB: There was nothing left that he didn’t devour, therefore his prosperity shall not endure.
18_20_21.CSB17: Nothing is left for him to consume; therefore, his prosperity will not last.
18_20_21.AMP: "There is nothing left of what he devoured; Therefore his prosperity does not endure.
18_20_21.MSG: They plunder everythingbut they can't hold on to any of it.
18_20_21.NIV: Nothing is left for him to devour;his prosperity will not endure.
18_20_21.NRSVCE: There was nothing left after they had eaten; therefore their prosperity will not endure.

18_20_22.NKJV: In his self-sufficiency he will be in distress; Every hand of misery will come against him.
18_20_22.KJV: In the fulness of his sufficiency he shall be in straits: every hand of the wicked shall come upon him. wicked: or, troublesome
18_20_22.ESV: In the fullness of his sufficiency he will be in distress; the hand of everyone in misery will come against him.
18_20_22.NASB: 'In the fullness of his plenty he will be cramped; The hand of everyone who suffers will come [against] him.
18_20_22.NLT: “In the midst of plenty, they will run into troubleand be overcome by misery.
18_20_22.WEB: In the fullness of his sufficiency, distress shall overtake him. The hand of everyone who is in misery shall come on him.
18_20_22.CSB17: At the height of his success distress will come to him; the full weight of misery will crush him.
18_20_22.AMP: "In the fullness of his excess (great abundance) he will be in trouble; The hand of everyone who suffers will come against him [he is miserable on every side].
18_20_22.MSG: Just when they think they have it all, disaster strikes;they're served up a plate full of misery.
18_20_22.NIV: In the midst of his plenty, distress will overtake him;the full force of misery will come upon him.
18_20_22.NRSVCE: In full sufficiency they will be in distress; all the force of misery will come upon them.

18_20_23.NKJV: When he is about to fill his stomach, God will cast on him the fury of His wrath, And will rain it on him while he is eating.
18_20_23.KJV: When he is about to fill his belly, God shall cast the fury of his wrath upon him, and shall rain it upon him while he is eating.
18_20_23.ESV: To fill his belly to the full, God will send his burning anger against him and rain it upon him into his body.
18_20_23.NASB: 'When he fills his belly, [God] will send His fierce anger on him And will rain [it] on him while he is eating.
18_20_23.NLT: May God give them a bellyful of trouble.May God rain down his anger upon them.
18_20_23.WEB: When he is about to fill his belly, God will cast the fierceness of his wrath on him. It will rain on him while he is eating.
18_20_23.CSB17: When he fills his stomach, God will send his burning anger against him, raining it down on him while he is eating.
18_20_23.AMP: "When he fills his belly, God will send His fierce anger on him And will rain it upon him while he is eating.
18_20_23.MSG: When they've filled their bellies with that,God gives them a taste of his anger,and they get to chew on that for a while.
18_20_23.NIV: When he has filled his belly,God will vent his burning anger against himand rain down his blows upon him.
18_20_23.NRSVCE: To fill their belly to the full God will send his fierce anger into them, and rain it upon them as their food.

18_20_24.NKJV: He will flee from the iron weapon; A bronze bow will pierce him through.
18_20_24.KJV: He shall flee from the iron weapon, and the bow of steel shall strike him through.
18_20_24.ESV: He will flee from an iron weapon; a bronze arrow will strike him through.
18_20_24.NASB: 'He may flee from the iron weapon, [But] the bronze bow will pierce him.
18_20_24.NLT: When they try to escape an iron weapon,a bronze-tipped arrow will pierce them.
18_20_24.WEB: He shall flee from the iron weapon. The bronze arrow shall strike him through.
18_20_24.CSB17: If he flees from an iron weapon, an arrow from a bronze bow will pierce him.
18_20_24.AMP: "He may flee from the iron weapon, But the bow of bronze will pierce him through.
18_20_24.MSG: As they run for their lives from one disaster,they run smack into another.
18_20_24.NIV: Though he flees from an iron weapon,a bronze-tipped arrow pierces him.
18_20_24.NRSVCE: They will flee from an iron weapon; a bronze arrow will strike them through.

18_20_25.NKJV: It is drawn, and comes out of the body; Yes, the glittering point comes out of his gall. Terrors come upon him;
18_20_25.KJV: It is drawn, and cometh out of the body; yea, the glittering sword cometh out of his gall: terrors are upon him.
18_20_25.ESV: It is drawn forth and comes out of his body; the glittering point comes out of his gallbladder; terrors come upon him.
18_20_25.NASB: 'It is drawn forth and comes out of his back, Even the glittering point from his gall. Terrors come upon him,
18_20_25.NLT: The arrow is pulled from their back,and the arrowhead glistens with blood.The terrors of death are upon them.
18_20_25.WEB: He draws it out, and it comes out of his body. Yes, the glittering point comes out of his liver. Terrors are on him.
18_20_25.CSB17: He pulls it out of his back, the flashing tip out of his liver. Terrors come over him.
18_20_25.AMP: "The arrow is drawn forth and it comes out of his back [after passing through his body]; Yes, the glittering point comes out of his gall. Terrors march in upon him;
18_20_25.MSG: They're knocked around from pillar to post,beaten to within an inch of their lives.They're trapped in a house of horrors,
18_20_25.NIV: He pulls it out of his back,the gleaming point out of his liver.Terrors will come over him;
18_20_25.NRSVCE: It is drawn forth and comes out of their body, and the glittering point comes out of their gall; terrors come upon them.

18_20_26.NKJV: Total darkness is reserved for his treasures. An unfanned fire will consume him; It shall go ill with him who is left in his tent.
18_20_26.KJV: All darkness shall be hid in his secret places: a fire not blown shall consume him; it shall go ill with him that is left in his tabernacle.
18_20_26.ESV: Utter darkness is laid up for his treasures; a fire not fanned will devour him; what is left in his tent will be consumed.
18_20_26.NASB: Complete darkness is held in reserve for his treasures, And unfanned fire will devour him; It will consume the survivor in his tent.
18_20_26.NLT: Their treasures will be thrown into deepest darkness.A wildfire will devour their goods,consuming all they have left.
18_20_26.WEB: All darkness is laid up for his treasures. An unfanned fire shall devour him. It shall consume that which is left in his tent.
18_20_26.CSB17: Total darkness is reserved for his treasures. A fire unfanned by human hands will consume him; it will feed on what is left in his tent.
18_20_26.AMP: Complete darkness (misfortune) is held in reserve for his treasures. An unfanned fire will devour him; It will consume the survivor in his tent.
18_20_26.MSG: and see their loot disappear down a black hole.Their lives are a total loss—not a penny to their name, not so much as a bean.
18_20_26.NIV: total darkness lies in wait for his treasures.A fire unfanned will consume himand devour what is left in his tent.
18_20_26.NRSVCE: Utter darkness is laid up for their treasures; a fire fanned by no one will devour them; what is left in their tent will be consumed.

18_20_27.NKJV: The heavens will reveal his iniquity, And the earth will rise up against him.
18_20_27.KJV: The heaven shall reveal his iniquity; and the earth shall rise up against him.
18_20_27.ESV: The heavens will reveal his iniquity, and the earth will rise up against him.
18_20_27.NASB: 'The heavens will reveal his iniquity, And the earth will rise up against him.
18_20_27.NLT: The heavens will reveal their guilt,and the earth will testify against them.
18_20_27.WEB: The heavens shall reveal his iniquity. The earth shall rise up against him.
18_20_27.CSB17: The heavens will expose his iniquity, and the earth will rise up against him.
18_20_27.AMP: "The heavens will reveal his wickedness and guilt, And the earth will rise up against him.
18_20_27.MSG: God will strip them of their sin-soaked clothesand hang their dirty laundry out for all to see.
18_20_27.NIV: The heavens will expose his guilt;the earth will rise up against him.
18_20_27.NRSVCE: The heavens will reveal their iniquity, and the earth will rise up against them.

18_20_28.NKJV: The increase of his house will depart, And his goods will flow away in the day of His wrath.
18_20_28.KJV: The increase of his house shall depart, and his goods shall flow away in the day of his wrath.
18_20_28.ESV: The possessions of his house will be carried away, dragged off in the day of God's wrath.
18_20_28.NASB: 'The increase of his house will depart; [His possessions] will flow away in the day of His anger.
18_20_28.NLT: A flood will sweep away their house.God’s anger will descend on them in torrents.
18_20_28.WEB: The increase of his house shall depart. They shall rush away in the day of his wrath.
18_20_28.CSB17: The possessions in his house will be removed, flowing away on the day of God’s anger.
18_20_28.AMP: "The produce and increase of his house will depart [with the victors]; His possessions will be dragged away in the day of God's wrath.
18_20_28.MSG: Life is a complete wipeout for them,nothing surviving God's wrath.
18_20_28.NIV: A flood will carry off his house,rushing waters on the day of God’s wrath.
18_20_28.NRSVCE: The possessions of their house will be carried away, dragged off in the day of God’s wrath.

18_20_29.NKJV: This is the portion from God for a wicked man, The heritage appointed to him by God.”
18_20_29.KJV: This is the portion of a wicked man from God, and the heritage appointed unto him by God. appointed: Heb. of his decree from God
18_20_29.ESV: This is the wicked man's portion from God, the heritage decreed for him by God.”
18_20_29.NASB: 'This is the wicked man's portion from God, Even the heritage decreed to him by God.'
18_20_29.NLT: This is the reward that God gives the wicked.It is the inheritance decreed by God.”
18_20_29.WEB: This is the portion of a wicked man from God, the heritage appointed to him by God.”
18_20_29.CSB17: This is the wicked person’s lot from God, the inheritance God ordained for him.
18_20_29.AMP: "This is the wicked man's portion from God, And the heritage decreed and appointed to him by God."
18_20_29.MSG: There! That's God's blueprint for the wicked—what they have to look forward to."
18_20_29.NIV: Such is the fate God allots the wicked,the heritage appointed for them by God.”
18_20_29.NRSVCE: This is the portion of the wicked from God, the heritage decreed for them by God.”

18_21_2.NKJV: “Listen carefully to my speech, And let this be your consolation.
18_21_2.KJV: Hear diligently my speech, and let this be your consolations.
18_21_2.ESV: “Keep listening to my words, and let this be your comfort.
18_21_2.NASB: 'Listen carefully to my speech, And let this be your [way of] consolation.
18_21_2.NLT: “Listen closely to what I am saying.That’s one consolation you can give me.
18_21_2.WEB: “Listen diligently to my speech. Let this be your consolation.
18_21_2.CSB17: Pay close attention to my words; let this be the consolation you offer.
18_21_2.AMP: "Listen carefully to my speech, And let this be the consolation.
18_21_2.MSG: "Now listen to me carefully, please listen,at least do me the favor of listening.
18_21_2.NIV: “Listen carefully to my words;let this be the consolation you give me.
18_21_2.NRSVCE: “Listen carefully to my words, and let this be your consolation.

18_21_3.NKJV: Bear with me that I may speak, And after I have spoken, keep mocking.
18_21_3.KJV: Suffer me that I may speak; and after that I have spoken, mock on.
18_21_3.ESV: Bear with me, and I will speak, and after I have spoken, mock on.
18_21_3.NASB: 'Bear with me that I may speak; Then after I have spoken, you may mock.
18_21_3.NLT: Bear with me, and let me speak.After I have spoken, you may resume mocking me.
18_21_3.WEB: Allow me, and I also will speak; After I have spoken, mock on.
18_21_3.CSB17: Bear with me while I speak; then after I have spoken, you may continue mocking.
18_21_3.AMP: "Bear with me, and I also will speak; And after I have spoken, you may [continue to] mock [me].
18_21_3.MSG: Put up with me while I have my say—then you can mock me later to your heart's content.
18_21_3.NIV: Bear with me while I speak,and after I have spoken, mock on.
18_21_3.NRSVCE: Bear with me, and I will speak; then after I have spoken, mock on.

18_21_4.NKJV: “As for me, is my complaint against man? And if it were, why should I not be impatient?
18_21_4.KJV: As for me, is my complaint to man? and if it were so, why should not my spirit be troubled? troubled: Heb. shortened?
18_21_4.ESV: As for me, is my complaint against man? Why should I not be impatient?
18_21_4.NASB: 'As for me, is my complaint to man? And why should I not be impatient?
18_21_4.NLT: “My complaint is with God, not with people.I have good reason to be so impatient.
18_21_4.WEB: As for me, is my complaint to man? Why shouldn’t I be impatient?
18_21_4.CSB17: As for me, is my complaint against a human being? Then why shouldn’t I be impatient?
18_21_4.AMP: "As for me, is my complaint to man or about him? And why should I not be impatient and my spirit troubled?
18_21_4.MSG: "It's not you I'm complaining to—it's God.Is it any wonder I'm getting fed up with his silence?
18_21_4.NIV: “Is my complaint directed to man?Why should I not be impatient?
18_21_4.NRSVCE: As for me, is my complaint addressed to mortals? Why should I not be impatient?

18_21_5.NKJV: Look at me and be astonished; Put your hand over your mouth.
18_21_5.KJV: Mark me, and be astonished, and lay your hand upon your mouth. Mark: Heb. Look unto me
18_21_5.ESV: Look at me and be appalled, and lay your hand over your mouth.
18_21_5.NASB: 'Look at me, and be astonished, And put [your] hand over [your] mouth.
18_21_5.NLT: Look at me and be stunned.Put your hand over your mouth in shock.
18_21_5.WEB: Look at me, and be astonished. Lay your hand on your mouth.
18_21_5.CSB17: Look at me and shudder; put your hand over your mouth.
18_21_5.AMP: "Look at me and be astonished and appalled; And put your hand over your mouth.
18_21_5.MSG: Take a good look at me. Aren't you appalled by what's happened?No! Don't say anything. I can do without your comments.
18_21_5.NIV: Look at me and be astonished;clap your hand over your mouth.
18_21_5.NRSVCE: Look at me, and be appalled, and lay your hand upon your mouth.

18_21_6.NKJV: Even when I remember I am terrified, And trembling takes hold of my flesh.
18_21_6.KJV: Even when I remember I am afraid, and trembling taketh hold on my flesh.
18_21_6.ESV: When I remember, I am dismayed, and shuddering seizes my flesh.
18_21_6.NASB: 'Even when I remember, I am disturbed, And horror takes hold of my flesh.
18_21_6.NLT: When I think about what I am saying, I shudder.My body trembles.
18_21_6.WEB: When I remember, I am troubled. Horror takes hold of my flesh.
18_21_6.CSB17: When I think about it, I am terrified and my body trembles in horror.
18_21_6.AMP: "Even when I remember, I am troubled and afraid; Horror and trembling take hold of my flesh.
18_21_6.MSG: When I look back, I go into shock,my body is racked with spasms.
18_21_6.NIV: When I think about this, I am terrified;trembling seizes my body.
18_21_6.NRSVCE: When I think of it I am dismayed, and shuddering seizes my flesh.

18_21_7.NKJV: Why do the wicked live and become old, Yes, become mighty in power?
18_21_7.KJV: Wherefore do the wicked live, become old, yea, are mighty in power?
18_21_7.ESV: Why do the wicked live, reach old age, and grow mighty in power?
18_21_7.NASB: 'Why do the wicked [still] live, Continue on, also become very powerful?
18_21_7.NLT: “Why do the wicked prosper,growing old and powerful?
18_21_7.WEB: “Why do the wicked live, become old, yes, and grow mighty in power?
18_21_7.CSB17: Why do the wicked continue to live, growing old and becoming powerful?
18_21_7.AMP: "Why do the wicked still live, Become old, and become mighty in power?
18_21_7.MSG: Why do the wicked have it so good,live to a ripe old age and get rich?
18_21_7.NIV: Why do the wicked live on,growing old and increasing in power?
18_21_7.NRSVCE: Why do the wicked live on, reach old age, and grow mighty in power?

18_21_8.NKJV: Their descendants are established with them in their sight, And their offspring before their eyes.
18_21_8.KJV: Their seed is established in their sight with them, and their offspring before their eyes.
18_21_8.ESV: Their offspring are established in their presence, and their descendants before their eyes.
18_21_8.NASB: 'Their descendants are established with them in their sight, And their offspring before their eyes,
18_21_8.NLT: They live to see their children grow up and settle down,and they enjoy their grandchildren.
18_21_8.WEB: Their child is established with them in their sight, their offspring before their eyes.
18_21_8.CSB17: Their children are established while they are still alive, and their descendants, before their eyes.
18_21_8.AMP: "Their children and descendants are established with them in their sight, And their offspring before their eyes.
18_21_8.MSG: They get to see their children succeed,get to watch and enjoy their grandchildren.
18_21_8.NIV: They see their children established around them,their offspring before their eyes.
18_21_8.NRSVCE: Their children are established in their presence, and their offspring before their eyes.

18_21_9.NKJV: Their houses are safe from fear, Neither is the rod of God upon them.
18_21_9.KJV: Their houses are safe from fear, neither is the rod of God upon them. safe: Heb. peace from
18_21_9.ESV: Their houses are safe from fear, and no rod of God is upon them.
18_21_9.NASB: Their houses are safe from fear, And the rod of God is not on them.
18_21_9.NLT: Their homes are safe from every fear,and God does not punish them.
18_21_9.WEB: Their houses are safe from fear, neither is the rod of God upon them.
18_21_9.CSB17: Their homes are secure and free of fear; no rod from God strikes them.
18_21_9.AMP: "Their houses are safe from fear; And the rod of God is not on them.
18_21_9.MSG: Their homes are peaceful and free from fear;they never experience God's disciplining rod.
18_21_9.NIV: Their homes are safe and free from fear;the rod of God is not upon them.
18_21_9.NRSVCE: Their houses are safe from fear, and no rod of God is upon them.

18_21_10.NKJV: Their bull breeds without failure; Their cow calves without miscarriage.
18_21_10.KJV: Their bull gendereth, and faileth not; their cow calveth, and casteth not her calf.
18_21_10.ESV: Their bull breeds without fail; their cow calves and does not miscarry.
18_21_10.NASB: 'His ox mates without fail; His cow calves and does not abort.
18_21_10.NLT: Their bulls never fail to breed.Their cows bear calves and never miscarry.
18_21_10.WEB: Their bulls breed without fail. Their cows calve, and don’t miscarry.
18_21_10.CSB17: Their bulls breed without fail; their cows calve and do not miscarry.
18_21_10.AMP: "His bull breeds and does not fail; His cow calves and does not miscarry.
18_21_10.MSG: Their bulls breed with great vigorand their cows calve without fail.
18_21_10.NIV: Their bulls never fail to breed;their cows calve and do not miscarry.
18_21_10.NRSVCE: Their bull breeds without fail; their cow calves and never miscarries.

18_21_11.NKJV: They send forth their little ones like a flock, And their children dance.
18_21_11.KJV: They send forth their little ones like a flock, and their children dance.
18_21_11.ESV: They send out their little boys like a flock, and their children dance.
18_21_11.NASB: 'They send forth their little ones like the flock, And their children skip about.
18_21_11.NLT: They let their children frisk about like lambs.Their little ones skip and dance.
18_21_11.WEB: They send out their little ones like a flock. Their children dance.
18_21_11.CSB17: They let their little ones run around like lambs; their children skip about,
18_21_11.AMP: "They send forth their little ones like a flock, And their children skip about.
18_21_11.MSG: They send their children out to playand watch them frolic like spring lambs.
18_21_11.NIV: They send forth their children as a flock;their little ones dance about.
18_21_11.NRSVCE: They send out their little ones like a flock, and their children dance around.

18_21_12.NKJV: They sing to the tambourine and harp, And rejoice to the sound of the flute.
18_21_12.KJV: They take the timbrel and harp, and rejoice at the sound of the organ.
18_21_12.ESV: They sing to the tambourine and the lyre and rejoice to the sound of the pipe.
18_21_12.NASB: 'They sing to the timbrel and harp And rejoice at the sound of the flute.
18_21_12.NLT: They sing with tambourine and harp.They celebrate to the sound of the flute.
18_21_12.WEB: They sing to the tambourine and harp, and rejoice at the sound of the pipe.
18_21_12.CSB17: singing to the tambourine and lyre and rejoicing at the sound of the flute.
18_21_12.AMP: "They lift up their voices and sing to the tambourine and the lyre And rejoice to the sound of the flute.
18_21_12.MSG: They make music with fiddles and flutes,have good times singing and dancing.
18_21_12.NIV: They sing to the music of tambourine and harp;they make merry to the sound of the flute.
18_21_12.NRSVCE: They sing to the tambourine and the lyre, and rejoice to the sound of the pipe.

18_21_13.NKJV: They spend their days in wealth, And in a moment go down to the grave.
18_21_13.KJV: They spend their days in wealth, and in a moment go down to the grave. in wealth: or, in mirth
18_21_13.ESV: They spend their days in prosperity, and in peace they go down to Sheol.
18_21_13.NASB: 'They spend their days in prosperity, And suddenly they go down to Sheol.
18_21_13.NLT: They spend their days in prosperity,then go down to the grave in peace.
18_21_13.WEB: They spend their days in prosperity. In an instant they go down to Sheol.
18_21_13.CSB17: They spend their days in prosperity and go down to Sheol in peace.
18_21_13.AMP: "They fully enjoy their days in prosperity, And so go down to Sheol (the nether world, the place of the dead) in a [peaceful] moment.
18_21_13.MSG: They have a long life on easy street,and die painlessly in their sleep.
18_21_13.NIV: They spend their years in prosperityand go down to the grave in peace.
18_21_13.NRSVCE: They spend their days in prosperity, and in peace they go down to Sheol.

18_21_14.NKJV: Yet they say to God, ‘Depart from us, For we do not desire the knowledge of Your ways.
18_21_14.KJV: Therefore they say unto God, Depart from us; for we desire not the knowledge of thy ways.
18_21_14.ESV: They say to God, ‘Depart from us! We do not desire the knowledge of your ways.
18_21_14.NASB: 'They say to God, 'Depart from us! We do not even desire the knowledge of Your ways.
18_21_14.NLT: And yet they say to God, ‘Go away.We want no part of you and your ways.
18_21_14.WEB: They tell God, ‘Depart from us, for we don’t want to know about your ways.
18_21_14.CSB17: Yet they say to God, “Leave us alone! We don’t want to know your ways.
18_21_14.AMP: "Yet they say to God, 'Depart from us, For we do not desire the knowledge of Your ways.
18_21_14.MSG: They say to God, 'Get lost!We've no interest in you or your ways.
18_21_14.NIV: Yet they say to God, ‘Leave us alone!We have no desire to know your ways.
18_21_14.NRSVCE: They say to God, ‘Leave us alone! We do not desire to know your ways.

18_21_15.NKJV: Who is the Almighty, that we should serve Him? And what profit do we have if we pray to Him?’
18_21_15.KJV: What is the Almighty, that we should serve him? and what profit should we have, if we pray unto him?
18_21_15.ESV: What is the Almighty, that we should serve him? And what profit do we get if we pray to him?’
18_21_15.NASB: 'Who is the Almighty, that we should serve Him, And what would we gain if we entreat Him?'
18_21_15.NLT: Who is the Almighty, and why should we obey him?What good will it do us to pray?’
18_21_15.WEB: What is the Almighty, that we should serve him? What profit should we have, if we pray to him?’
18_21_15.CSB17: Who is the Almighty, that we should serve him, and what will we gain by pleading with him?”
18_21_15.AMP: 'Who [and what] is the Almighty, that we should serve Him? And what would we gain if we plead with Him?'
18_21_15.MSG: Why should we have dealings with God Almighty?What's there in it for us?'
18_21_15.NIV: Who is the Almighty, that we should serve him?What would we gain by praying to him?’
18_21_15.NRSVCE: What is the Almighty, that we should serve him? And what profit do we get if we pray to him?’

18_21_16.NKJV: Indeed their prosperity is not in their hand; The counsel of the wicked is far from me.
18_21_16.KJV: Lo, their good is not in their hand: the counsel of the wicked is far from me.
18_21_16.ESV: Behold, is not their prosperity in their hand? The counsel of the wicked is far from me.
18_21_16.NASB: 'Behold, their prosperity is not in their hand; The counsel of the wicked is far from me.
18_21_16.NLT: (They think their prosperity is of their own doing,but I will have nothing to do with that kind of thinking.)
18_21_16.WEB: Behold, their prosperity is not in their hand. The counsel of the wicked is far from me.
18_21_16.CSB17: But their prosperity is not of their own doing. The counsel of the wicked is far from me!
18_21_16.AMP: "But notice, the prosperity of the wicked is not in their hand (in their power); The counsel of the wicked [and the mystery of God's dealings with the ungodly] is far from my comprehension.
18_21_16.MSG: But they're wrong, dead wrong—they're not gods.It's beyond me how they can carry on like this!
18_21_16.NIV: But their prosperity is not in their own hands,so I stand aloof from the counsel of the wicked.
18_21_16.NRSVCE: Is not their prosperity indeed their own achievement? The plans of the wicked are repugnant to me.

18_21_17.NKJV: “How often is the lamp of the wicked put out? How often does their destruction come upon them, The sorrows God distributes in His anger?
18_21_17.KJV: How oft is the candle of the wicked put out! and how oft cometh their destruction upon them! God distributeth sorrows in his anger. candle: or, lamp
18_21_17.ESV: “How often is it that the lamp of the wicked is put out? That their calamity comes upon them? That God distributes pains in his anger?
18_21_17.NASB: 'How often is the lamp of the wicked put out, Or does their calamity fall on them? Does God apportion destruction in His anger?
18_21_17.NLT: “Yet the light of the wicked never seems to be extinguished.Do they ever have trouble?Does God distribute sorrows to them in anger?
18_21_17.WEB: “How often is it that the lamp of the wicked is put out, that their calamity comes on them, that God distributes sorrows in his anger?
18_21_17.CSB17: How often is the lamp of the wicked put out? Does disaster come on them? Does he apportion destruction in his anger?
18_21_17.AMP: "How often [then] is it that the lamp of the wicked is put out, And that their disaster falls on them? Does God distribute pain and destruction and sorrow [to them] in His anger?
18_21_17.MSG: "Still, how often does it happen that the wicked fail,or disaster strikes,or they get their just deserts?
18_21_17.NIV: “Yet how often is the lamp of the wicked snuffed out?How often does calamity come upon them,the fate God allots in his anger?
18_21_17.NRSVCE: “How often is the lamp of the wicked put out? How often does calamity come upon them? How often does God distribute pains in his anger?

18_21_18.NKJV: They are like straw before the wind, And like chaff that a storm carries away.
18_21_18.KJV: They are as stubble before the wind, and as chaff that the storm carrieth away. carrieth: Heb. stealeth
18_21_18.ESV: That they are like straw before the wind, and like chaff that the storm carries away?
18_21_18.NASB: 'Are they as straw before the wind, And like chaff which the storm carries away?
18_21_18.NLT: Are they driven before the wind like straw?Are they carried away by the storm like chaff?Not at all!
18_21_18.WEB: How often is it that they are as stubble before the wind, as chaff that the storm carries away?
18_21_18.CSB17: Are they like straw before the wind, like chaff a storm sweeps away?
18_21_18.AMP: "Are they like straw before the wind, And like chaff that the storm steals and carries away?
18_21_18.MSG: How often are they blown away by bad luck?Not very often.
18_21_18.NIV: How often are they like straw before the wind,like chaff swept away by a gale?
18_21_18.NRSVCE: How often are they like straw before the wind, and like chaff that the storm carries away?

18_21_19.NKJV: They say, ‘God lays up one’s iniquity for his children’; Let Him recompense him, that he may know it.
18_21_19.KJV: God layeth up his iniquity for his children: he rewardeth him, and he shall know it. his iniquity: that is, the punishment of his iniquity
18_21_19.ESV: You say, ‘God stores up their iniquity for their children.’ Let him pay it out to them, that they may know it.
18_21_19.NASB: '[You say], 'God stores away a man's iniquity for his sons.' Let God repay him so that he may know [it].
18_21_19.NLT: “‘Well,’ you say, ‘at least God will punish their children!’But I say he should punish the ones who sin,so that they understand his judgment.
18_21_19.WEB: You say, ‘God lays up his iniquity for his children.’ Let him recompense it to himself, that he may know it.
18_21_19.CSB17: God reserves a person’s punishment for his children. Let God repay the person himself, so that he may know it.
18_21_19.AMP: "You say, 'God stores away [the punishment of] man's wickedness for his children.' Let God repay him so that he may know and experience it.
18_21_19.MSG: You might say, 'God is saving up the punishment for their children.'I say, 'Give it to them right now so they'll know whatthey've done!'
18_21_19.NIV: It is said, ‘God stores up a man’s punishment for his sons.’Let him repay the man himself, so that he will know it!
18_21_19.NRSVCE: You say, ‘God stores up their iniquity for their children.’ Let it be paid back to them, so that they may know it.

18_21_20.NKJV: Let his eyes see his destruction, And let him drink of the wrath of the Almighty.
18_21_20.KJV: His eyes shall see his destruction, and he shall drink of the wrath of the Almighty.
18_21_20.ESV: Let their own eyes see their destruction, and let them drink of the wrath of the Almighty.
18_21_20.NASB: 'Let his own eyes see his decay, And let him drink of the wrath of the Almighty.
18_21_20.NLT: Let them see their destruction with their own eyes.Let them drink deeply of the anger of the Almighty.
18_21_20.WEB: Let his own eyes see his destruction. Let him drink of the wrath of the Almighty.
18_21_20.CSB17: Let his own eyes see his demise; let him drink from the Almighty’s wrath!
18_21_20.AMP: "Let his own eyes see his destruction, And let him drink of the wrath of the Almighty.
18_21_20.MSG: They deserve to experience the effects of their evil,feel the full force of God's wrath firsthand.
18_21_20.NIV: Let his own eyes see his destruction;let him drink of the wrath of the Almighty.
18_21_20.NRSVCE: Let their own eyes see their destruction, and let them drink of the wrath of the Almighty.

18_21_21.NKJV: For what does he care about his household after him, When the number of his months is cut in half?
18_21_21.KJV: For what pleasure hath he in his house after him, when the number of his months is cut off in the midst?
18_21_21.ESV: For what do they care for their houses after them, when the number of their months is cut off?
18_21_21.NASB: 'For what does he care for his household after him, When the number of his months is cut off?
18_21_21.NLT: For they will not care what happens to their familyafter they are dead.
18_21_21.WEB: For what does he care for his house after him, when the number of his months is cut off?
18_21_21.CSB17: For what does he care about his family once he is dead, when the number of his months has run out?
18_21_21.AMP: "For what pleasure does he have in his house and family after he is dead, When the number of his months [of life] is cut off?
18_21_21.MSG: What do they care what happens to their familiesafter they're safely tucked away in the grave?
18_21_21.NIV: For what does he care about the family he leaves behindwhen his allotted months come to an end?
18_21_21.NRSVCE: For what do they care for their household after them, when the number of their months is cut off?

18_21_22.NKJV: “Can anyone teach God knowledge, Since He judges those on high?
18_21_22.KJV: Shall any teach God knowledge? seeing he judgeth those that are high.
18_21_22.ESV: Will any teach God knowledge, seeing that he judges those who are on high?
18_21_22.NASB: 'Can anyone teach God knowledge, In that He judges those on high?
18_21_22.NLT: “But who can teach a lesson to God,since he judges even the most powerful?
18_21_22.WEB: “Shall any teach God knowledge, since he judges those who are high?
18_21_22.CSB17: Can anyone teach God knowledge, since he judges the exalted ones?
18_21_22.AMP: "Can anyone teach God knowledge, Seeing that He judges those on high?
18_21_22.MSG: "But who are we to tell God how to run his affairs?He's dealing with matters that are way over our heads.
18_21_22.NIV: “Can anyone teach knowledge to God,since he judges even the highest?
18_21_22.NRSVCE: Will any teach God knowledge, seeing that he judges those that are on high?

18_21_23.NKJV: One dies in his full strength, Being wholly at ease and secure;
18_21_23.KJV: One dieth in his full strength, being wholly at ease and quiet. his: Heb. his very, or, the strength of, his perfection
18_21_23.ESV: One dies in his full vigor, being wholly at ease and secure,
18_21_23.NASB: 'One dies in his full strength, Being wholly at ease and satisfied;
18_21_23.NLT: One person dies in prosperity,completely comfortable and secure,
18_21_23.WEB: One dies in his full strength, being wholly at ease and quiet.
18_21_23.CSB17: One person dies in excellent health, completely secure and at ease.
18_21_23.AMP: "One dies in his full strength, Being wholly at ease and quiet and satisfied;
18_21_23.MSG: Some people die in the prime of life,with everything going for them—
18_21_23.NIV: One man dies in full vigor,completely secure and at ease,
18_21_23.NRSVCE: One dies in full prosperity, being wholly at ease and secure,

18_21_24.NKJV: His pails are full of milk, And the marrow of his bones is moist.
18_21_24.KJV: His breasts are full of milk, and his bones are moistened with marrow. breasts: or, milk pails
18_21_24.ESV: his pails full of milk and the marrow of his bones moist.
18_21_24.NASB: His sides are filled out with fat, And the marrow of his bones is moist,
18_21_24.NLT: the picture of good health,vigorous and fit.
18_21_24.WEB: His pails are full of milk. The marrow of his bones is moistened.
18_21_24.CSB17: His body is well fed, and his bones are full of marrow.
18_21_24.AMP: His pails are full of milk [his sides are filled out with fat], And the marrow of his bones is moist,
18_21_24.NIV: his body well nourished,his bones rich with marrow.
18_21_24.NRSVCE: his loins full of milk and the marrow of his bones moist.

18_21_25.NKJV: Another man dies in the bitterness of his soul, Never having eaten with pleasure.
18_21_25.KJV: And another dieth in the bitterness of his soul, and never eateth with pleasure.
18_21_25.ESV: Another dies in bitterness of soul, never having tasted of prosperity.
18_21_25.NASB: While another dies with a bitter soul, Never even tasting [anything] good.
18_21_25.NLT: Another person dies in bitter poverty,never having tasted the good life.
18_21_25.WEB: Another dies in bitterness of soul, and never tastes of good.
18_21_25.CSB17: Yet another person dies with a bitter soul, having never tasted prosperity.
18_21_25.AMP: Whereas another dies with a bitter soul, Never even tasting pleasure or good fortune.
18_21_25.MSG: Others die bitter and bereft,never getting a taste of happiness.
18_21_25.NIV: Another man dies in bitterness of soul,never having enjoyed anything good.
18_21_25.NRSVCE: Another dies in bitterness of soul, never having tasted of good.

18_21_26.NKJV: They lie down alike in the dust, And worms cover them.
18_21_26.KJV: They shall lie down alike in the dust, and the worms shall cover them.
18_21_26.ESV: They lie down alike in the dust, and the worms cover them.
18_21_26.NASB: 'Together they lie down in the dust, And worms cover them.
18_21_26.NLT: But both are buried in the same dust,both eaten by the same maggots.
18_21_26.WEB: They lie down alike in the dust. The worm covers them.
18_21_26.CSB17: But they both lie in the dust, and worms cover them.
18_21_26.AMP: "Together they lie down in the dust, And the worms cover them.
18_21_26.MSG: They're laid out side by side in the cemetery,where the worms can't tell one from the other.
18_21_26.NIV: Side by side they lie in the dust,and worms cover them both.
18_21_26.NRSVCE: They lie down alike in the dust, and the worms cover them.

18_21_27.NKJV: “Look, I know your thoughts, And the schemes with which you would wrong me.
18_21_27.KJV: Behold, I know your thoughts, and the devices which ye wrongfully imagine against me.
18_21_27.ESV: “Behold, I know your thoughts and your schemes to wrong me.
18_21_27.NASB: 'Behold, I know your thoughts, And the plans by which you would wrong me.
18_21_27.NLT: “Look, I know what you’re thinking.I know the schemes you plot against me.
18_21_27.WEB: “Behold, I know your thoughts, the devices with which you would wrong me.
18_21_27.CSB17: I know your thoughts very well, the schemes by which you would wrong me.
18_21_27.AMP: "Behold, I know your thoughts, And the plots by which you would wrong me.
18_21_27.MSG: "I'm not deceived. I know what you're up to,the plans you're cooking up to bring me down.
18_21_27.NIV: “I know full well what you are thinking,the schemes by which you would wrong me.
18_21_27.NRSVCE: “Oh, I know your thoughts, and your schemes to wrong me.

18_21_28.NKJV: For you say, ‘Where is the house of the prince? And where is the tent, The dwelling place of the wicked?’
18_21_28.KJV: For ye say, Where is the house of the prince? and where are the dwelling places of the wicked? the dwelling: Heb. the tent of the tabernacles
18_21_28.ESV: For you say, ‘Where is the house of the prince? Where is the tent in which the wicked lived?’
18_21_28.NASB: 'For you say, 'Where is the house of the nobleman, And where is the tent, the dwelling places of the wicked?'
18_21_28.NLT: You will tell me of rich and wicked peoplewhose houses have vanished because of their sins.
18_21_28.WEB: For you say, ‘Where is the house of the prince? Where is the tent in which the wicked lived?’
18_21_28.CSB17: For you say, “Where now is the nobleman’s house?” and “Where are the tents the wicked lived in?”
18_21_28.AMP: "For you say, 'Where is the house of the noble man? And where is the tent, the dwelling place of the wicked?'
18_21_28.MSG: Naively you claim that the castles of tyrants fall to pieces,that the achievements of the wicked collapse.
18_21_28.NIV: You say, ‘Where now is the great man’s house,the tents where wicked men lived?’
18_21_28.NRSVCE: For you say, ‘Where is the house of the prince? Where is the tent in which the wicked lived?’

18_21_29.NKJV: Have you not asked those who travel the road? And do you not know their signs?
18_21_29.KJV: Have ye not asked them that go by the way? and do ye not know their tokens,
18_21_29.ESV: Have you not asked those who travel the roads, and do you not accept their testimony
18_21_29.NASB: 'Have you not asked wayfaring men, And do you not recognize their witness?
18_21_29.NLT: But ask those who have been around,and they will tell you the truth.
18_21_29.WEB: Haven’t you asked wayfaring men? Don’t you know their evidences,
18_21_29.CSB17: Have you never consulted those who travel the roads? Don’t you accept their reports?
18_21_29.AMP: "Have you not asked those who travel this way, And do you not recognize their witness?
18_21_29.MSG: Have you ever asked world travelers how they see it?Have you not listened to their stories
18_21_29.NIV: Have you never questioned those who travel?Have you paid no regard to their accounts—
18_21_29.NRSVCE: Have you not asked those who travel the roads, and do you not accept their testimony,

18_21_30.NKJV: For the wicked are reserved for the day of doom; They shall be brought out on the day of wrath.
18_21_30.KJV: That the wicked is reserved to the day of destruction? they shall be brought forth to the day of wrath. wrath: Heb. wraths
18_21_30.ESV: that the evil man is spared in the day of calamity, that he is rescued in the day of wrath?
18_21_30.NASB: 'For the wicked is reserved for the day of calamity; They will be led forth at the day of fury.
18_21_30.NLT: Evil people are spared in times of calamityand are allowed to escape disaster.
18_21_30.WEB: that the evil man is reserved to the day of calamity, That they are led out to the day of wrath?
18_21_30.CSB17: Indeed, the evil person is spared from the day of disaster, rescued from the day of wrath.
18_21_30.AMP: "That evil men are [now] reserved for the day of disaster and destruction, They will be led away on the day of [God's] wrath?
18_21_30.MSG: Of evil men and women who got off scot-free,who never had to pay for their wickedness?
18_21_30.NIV: that the evil man is spared from the day of calamity,that he is delivered from the day of wrath?
18_21_30.NRSVCE: that the wicked are spared in the day of calamity, and are rescued in the day of wrath?

18_21_31.NKJV: Who condemns his way to his face? And who repays him for what he has done?
18_21_31.KJV: Who shall declare his way to his face? and who shall repay him what he hath done?
18_21_31.ESV: Who declares his way to his face, and who repays him for what he has done?
18_21_31.NASB: 'Who will confront him with his actions, And who will repay him for what he has done?
18_21_31.NLT: No one criticizes them openlyor pays them back for what they have done.
18_21_31.WEB: Who shall declare his way to his face? Who shall repay him what he has done?
18_21_31.CSB17: Who would denounce his behavior to his face? Who would repay him for what he has done?
18_21_31.AMP: "But who will confront him with his actions and rebuke him face to face, And who will repay him for what he has done?
18_21_31.MSG: Did anyone ever confront them with their crimes?Did they ever have to face the music?
18_21_31.NIV: Who denounces his conduct to his face?Who repays him for what he has done?
18_21_31.NRSVCE: Who declares their way to their face, and who repays them for what they have done?

18_21_32.NKJV: Yet he shall be brought to the grave, And a vigil kept over the tomb.
18_21_32.KJV: Yet shall he be brought to the grave, and shall remain in the tomb. grave: Heb. graves remain: Heb. watch in the heap
18_21_32.ESV: When he is carried to the grave, watch is kept over his tomb.
18_21_32.NASB: 'While he is carried to the grave, [Men] will keep watch over [his] tomb.
18_21_32.NLT: When they are carried to the grave,an honor guard keeps watch at their tomb.
18_21_32.WEB: Yet he will be borne to the grave. Men shall keep watch over the tomb.
18_21_32.CSB17: He is carried to the grave, and someone keeps watch over his tomb.
18_21_32.AMP: "When he is carried to his grave, A guard will keep watch over his tomb.
18_21_32.MSG: Not likely—they're given fancy funeralswith all the trimmings,
18_21_32.NIV: He is carried to the grave,and watch is kept over his tomb.
18_21_32.NRSVCE: When they are carried to the grave, a watch is kept over their tomb.

18_21_33.NKJV: The clods of the valley shall be sweet to him; Everyone shall follow him, As countless have gone before him.
18_21_33.KJV: The clods of the valley shall be sweet unto him, and every man shall draw after him, as there are innumerable before him.
18_21_33.ESV: The clods of the valley are sweet to him; all mankind follows after him, and those who go before him are innumerable.
18_21_33.NASB: 'The clods of the valley will gently cover him; Moreover, all men will follow after him, While countless ones [go] before him.
18_21_33.NLT: A great funeral procession goes to the cemetery.Many pay their respects as the body is laid to rest,and the earth gives sweet repose.
18_21_33.WEB: The clods of the valley shall be sweet to him. All men shall draw after him, as there were innumerable before him.
18_21_33.CSB17: The dirt on his grave is sweet to him. Everyone follows behind him, and those who go before him are without number.
18_21_33.AMP: "The [dirt] clods of the valley are sweet to him [and gently cover him], Moreover, all men will follow after him [to a grave], While countless ones go before him.
18_21_33.MSG: Gently lowered into expensive graves,with everyone telling lies about how wonderful they were.
18_21_33.NIV: The soil in the valley is sweet to him;all men follow after him,and a countless throng goes before him.
18_21_33.NRSVCE: The clods of the valley are sweet to them; everyone will follow after, and those who went before are innumerable.

18_21_34.NKJV: How then can you comfort me with empty words, Since falsehood remains in your answers?”
18_21_34.KJV: How then comfort ye me in vain, seeing in your answers there remaineth falsehood? falsehood: Heb. transgression?
18_21_34.ESV: How then will you comfort me with empty nothings? There is nothing left of your answers but falsehood.”
18_21_34.NASB: 'How then will you vainly comfort me, For your answers remain [full of] falsehood?'
18_21_34.NLT: “How can your empty clichés comfort me?All your explanations are lies!”
18_21_34.WEB: So how can you comfort me with nonsense, because in your answers there remains only falsehood?”
18_21_34.CSB17: So how can you offer me such futile comfort? Your answers are deceptive.
18_21_34.AMP: "How then can you vainly comfort me with empty words, Since your answers remain untrue?"
18_21_34.MSG: "So how do you expect me to get any comfort from your nonsense?Your so-called comfort is a tissue of lies."
18_21_34.NIV: “So how can you console me with your nonsense?Nothing is left of your answers but falsehood!”
18_21_34.NRSVCE: How then will you comfort me with empty nothings? There is nothing left of your answers but falsehood.”

18_22_1.NKJV: Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered and said:
18_22_1.KJV: Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered and said,
18_22_1.ESV: Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered and said:
18_22_1.AMP: Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered and said,
18_22_1.MSG: Once again Eliphaz the Temanite took up his theme:

18_22_2.NKJV: “Can a man be profitable to God, Though he who is wise may be profitable to himself?
18_22_2.KJV: Can a man be profitable unto God, as he that is wise may be profitable unto himself? as he: or, if he may be profitable, doth his good success depend thereon?
18_22_2.ESV: “Can a man be profitable to God? Surely he who is wise is profitable to himself.
18_22_2.NASB: 'Can a vigorous man be of use to God, Or a wise man be useful to himself?
18_22_2.NLT: “Can a person do anything to help God?Can even a wise person be helpful to him?
18_22_2.WEB: “Can a man be profitable to God? Surely he who is wise is profitable to himself.
18_22_2.CSB17: Can a man be of any use to God? Can even a wise man be of use to him?
18_22_2.AMP: "Can a vigorous man be of use to God, Or a wise man be useful to himself?
18_22_2.MSG: "Are any of us strong enough to give God a hand,or smart enough to give him advice?
18_22_2.NIV: “Can a man be of benefit to God?Can even a wise man benefit him?
18_22_2.NRSVCE: “Can a mortal be of use to God? Can even the wisest be of service to him?

18_22_3.NKJV: Is it any pleasure to the Almighty that you are righteous? Or is it gain to Him that you make your ways blameless?
18_22_3.KJV: Is it any pleasure to the Almighty, that thou art righteous? or is it gain to him, that thou makest thy ways perfect?
18_22_3.ESV: Is it any pleasure to the Almighty if you are in the right, or is it gain to him if you make your ways blameless?
18_22_3.NASB: 'Is there any pleasure to the Almighty if you are righteous, Or profit if you make your ways perfect?
18_22_3.NLT: Is it any advantage to the Almighty if you are righteous?Would it be any gain to him if you were perfect?
18_22_3.WEB: Is it any pleasure to the Almighty, that you are righteous? Or does it benefit him, that you make your ways perfect?
18_22_3.CSB17: Does it delight the Almighty if you are righteous? Does he profit if you perfect your behavior?
18_22_3.AMP: "Is it any pleasure or joy to the Almighty that you are righteous? Or is it of benefit to Him that you make your ways perfect?
18_22_3.MSG: So what if you were righteous—would God Almighty even notice?Even if you gave a perfect performance, do you thinkhe'd applaud?

18_22_27.ESV: You will make your prayer to him, and he will hear you, and you will pay your vows.

18_22_27.NLT: You will pray to him, and he will hear you,and you will fulfill your vows to him.
18_22_27.WEB: You shall make your prayer to him, and he will hear you. You shall pay your vows.
18_22_27.CSB17: You will pray to him, and he will hear you, and you will fulfill your vows.
18_22_27.AMP: "You will pray to Him, and He will hear you, And you will pay your vows.
18_22_27.MSG: You'll pray to him and he'll listen;he'll help you do what you've promised.
18_22_27.NIV: You will pray to him, and he will hear you,and you will fulfill your vows.
18_22_27.NRSVCE: You will pray to him, and he will hear you, and you will pay your vows.

18_22_28.NKJV: You will also declare a thing, And it will be established for you; So light will shine on your ways.
18_22_28.KJV: Thou shalt also decree a thing, and it shall be established unto thee: and the light shall shine upon thy ways.
18_22_28.ESV: You will decide on a matter, and it will be established for you, and light will shine on your ways.
18_22_28.NASB: 'You will also decree a thing, and it will be established for you; And light will shine on your ways.
18_22_28.NLT: You will succeed in whatever you choose to do,and light will shine on the road ahead of you.
18_22_28.WEB: You shall also decree a thing, and it shall be established to you. Light shall shine on your ways.
18_22_28.CSB17: When you make a decision, it will be carried out, and light will shine on your ways.
18_22_28.AMP: "You will also decide and decree a thing, and it will be established for you; And the light [of God's favor] will shine upon your ways.
18_22_28.MSG: You'll decide what you want and it will happen;your life will be bathed in light.
18_22_28.NIV: What you decide on will be done,and light will shine on your ways.
18_22_28.NRSVCE: You will decide on a matter, and it will be established for you, and light will shine on your ways.

18_22_29.NKJV: When they cast you down, and you say, ‘Exaltation will come!’ Then He will save the humble person.
18_22_29.KJV: When men are cast down, then thou shalt say, There is lifting up; and he shall save the humble person. the humble: Heb. him that hath low eyes
18_22_29.ESV: For when they are humbled you say, ‘It is because of pride’; but he saves the lowly.
18_22_29.NASB: 'When you are cast down, you will speak with confidence, And the humble person He will save.
18_22_29.NLT: If people are in trouble and you say, ‘Help them,’God will save them.
18_22_29.WEB: When they cast down, you shall say, ‘be lifted up.’ He will save the humble person.
18_22_29.CSB17: When others are humiliated and you say, “Lift them up,” God will save the humble.
18_22_29.AMP: "When you are cast down and humbled, you will speak with confidence, And the humble person He will lift up and save.
18_22_29.MSG: To those who feel low you'll say, 'Chin up! Be brave!'and God will save them.
18_22_29.NIV: When men are brought low and you say, ‘Lift them up!’then he will save the downcast.
18_22_29.NRSVCE: When others are humiliated, you say it is pride; for he saves the humble.

18_22_30.NKJV: He will even deliver one who is not innocent; Yes, he will be delivered by the purity of your hands.”
18_22_30.KJV: He shall deliver the island of the innocent: and it is delivered by the pureness of thine hands. He shall: or, The innocent shall deliver the island
18_22_30.ESV: He delivers even the one who is not innocent, who will be delivered through the cleanness of your hands.”
18_22_30.NASB: 'He will deliver one who is not innocent, And he will be delivered through the cleanness of your hands.'
18_22_30.NLT: Even sinners will be rescued;they will be rescued because your hands are pure.”
18_22_30.WEB: He will even deliver him who is not innocent. Yes, he shall be delivered through the cleanness of your hands.”
18_22_30.CSB17: He will even rescue the guilty one, who will be rescued by the purity of your hands.
18_22_30.AMP: "He will even rescue the one [for whom you intercede] who is not innocent; And he will be rescued through the cleanness of your hands."
18_22_30.MSG: Yes, even the guilty will escape,escape through God's grace in your life."
18_22_30.NIV: He will deliver even one who is not innocent,who will be delivered through the cleanness of your hands.”
18_22_30.NRSVCE: He will deliver even those who are guilty; they will escape because of the cleanness of your hands.”

18_23_2.NKJV: “Even today my complaint is bitter; My hand is listless because of my groaning.
18_23_2.KJV: Even to day is my complaint bitter: my stroke is heavier than my groaning. stroke: Heb. hand
18_23_2.ESV: “Today also my complaint is bitter; my hand is heavy on account of my groaning.
18_23_2.NASB: 'Even today my complaint is rebellion; His hand is heavy despite my groaning.
18_23_2.NLT: “My complaint today is still a bitter one,and I try hard not to groan aloud.
18_23_2.WEB: “Even today my complaint is rebellious. His hand is heavy in spite of my groaning.
18_23_2.CSB17: Today also my complaint is bitter. His hand is heavy despite my groaning.
18_23_2.AMP: "Even today my complaint is contentious; His hand is heavy despite my groaning.
18_23_2.MSG: "I'm not letting up—I'm standing my ground.My complaint is legitimate.God has no right to treat me like this—it isn't fair!
18_23_2.NIV: “Even today my complaint is bitter;his hand is heavy in spite of my groaning.
18_23_2.NRSVCE: “Today also my complaint is bitter; his hand is heavy despite my groaning.

18_23_3.NKJV: Oh, that I knew where I might find Him, That I might come to His seat!
18_23_3.KJV: Oh that I knew where I might find him! that I might come even to his seat!
18_23_3.ESV: Oh, that I knew where I might find him, that I might come even to his seat!
18_23_3.NASB: 'Oh that I knew where I might find Him, That I might come to His seat!
18_23_3.NLT: If only I knew where to find God,I would go to his court.
18_23_3.WEB: Oh that I knew where I might find him! That I might come even to his seat!
18_23_3.CSB17: If only I knew how to find him, so that I could go to his throne.
18_23_3.AMP: "Oh, that I knew where I might find Him, That I might [even] come to His seat!
18_23_3.MSG: If I knew where on earth to find him,I'd go straight to him.
18_23_3.NIV: If only I knew where to find him;if only I could go to his dwelling!
18_23_3.NRSVCE: Oh, that I knew where I might find him, that I might come even to his dwelling!

18_23_4.NKJV: I would present my case before Him, And fill my mouth with arguments.
18_23_4.KJV: I would order my cause before him, and fill my mouth with arguments.
18_23_4.ESV: I would lay my case before him and fill my mouth with arguments.
18_23_4.NASB: 'I would present [my] case before Him And fill my mouth with arguments.
18_23_4.NLT: I would lay out my caseand present my arguments.
18_23_4.WEB: I would set my cause in order before him, and fill my mouth with arguments.
18_23_4.CSB17: I would plead my case before him and fill my mouth with arguments.
18_23_4.AMP: "I would present my cause before Him And fill my mouth with arguments.
18_23_4.MSG: I'd lay my case before him face-to-face,give him all my arguments firsthand.
18_23_4.NIV: I would state my case before himand fill my mouth with arguments.
18_23_4.NRSVCE: I would lay my case before him, and fill my mouth with arguments.

18_23_5.NKJV: I would know the words which He would answer me, And understand what He would say to me.
18_23_5.KJV: I would know the words which he would answer me, and understand what he would say unto me.
18_23_5.ESV: I would know what he would answer me and understand what he would say to me.
18_23_5.NASB: 'I would learn the words [which] He would answer, And perceive what He would say to me.
18_23_5.NLT: Then I would listen to his replyand understand what he says to me.
18_23_5.WEB: I would know the words which he would answer me, and understand what he would tell me.
18_23_5.CSB17: I would learn how he would answer me; and understand what he would say to me.
18_23_5.AMP: "I would learn the words which He would answer, And understand what He would say to me.
18_23_5.MSG: I'd find out exactly what he's thinking,discover what's going on in his head.
18_23_5.NIV: I would find out what he would answer me,and consider what he would say.
18_23_5.NRSVCE: I would learn what he would answer me, and understand what he would say to me.

18_23_6.NKJV: Would He contend with me in His great power? No! But He would take note of me.
18_23_6.KJV: Will he plead against me with his great power? No; but he would put strength in me.
18_23_6.ESV: Would he contend with me in the greatness of his power? No; he would pay attention to me.
18_23_6.NASB: 'Would He contend with me by the greatness of [His] power? No, surely He would pay attention to me.
18_23_6.NLT: Would he use his great power to argue with me?No, he would give me a fair hearing.
18_23_6.WEB: Would he contend with me in the greatness of his power? No, but he would listen to me.
18_23_6.CSB17: Would he prosecute me forcefully? No, he would certainly pay attention to me.
18_23_6.AMP: "Would He contend against me with His great power? No, surely He would give attention to me.
18_23_6.MSG: Do you think he'd dismiss me or bully me?No, he'd take me seriously.
18_23_6.NIV: Would he oppose me with great power?No, he would not press charges against me.
18_23_6.NRSVCE: Would he contend with me in the greatness of his power? No; but he would give heed to me.

18_23_7.NKJV: There the upright could reason with Him, And I would be delivered forever from my Judge.
18_23_7.KJV: There the righteous might dispute with him; so should I be delivered for ever from my judge.
18_23_7.ESV: There an upright man could argue with him, and I would be acquitted forever by my judge.
18_23_7.NASB: 'There the upright would reason with Him; And I would be delivered forever from my Judge.
18_23_7.NLT: Honest people can reason with him,so I would be forever acquitted by my judge.
18_23_7.WEB: There the upright might reason with him, so I should be delivered forever from my judge.
18_23_7.CSB17: Then an upright man could reason with him, and I would escape from my Judge forever.
18_23_7.AMP: "There the righteous and upright could reason with Him; So I would be acquitted forever by my Judge.
18_23_7.MSG: He'd see a straight-living man standing before him;my Judge would acquit me for good of all charges.
18_23_7.NIV: There an upright man could present his case before him,and I would be delivered forever from my judge.
18_23_7.NRSVCE: There an upright person could reason with him, and I should be acquitted forever by my judge.

18_23_8.NKJV: “Look, I go forward, but He is not there, And backward, but I cannot perceive Him;
18_23_8.KJV: Behold, I go forward, but he is not there; and backward, but I cannot perceive him:
18_23_8.ESV: “Behold, I go forward, but he is not there, and backward, but I do not perceive him;
18_23_8.NASB: 'Behold, I go forward but He is not [there], And backward, but I cannot perceive Him;
18_23_8.NLT: I go east, but he is not there.I go west, but I cannot find him.
18_23_8.WEB: “If I go east, he is not there; if west, I can’t find him;
18_23_8.CSB17: If I go east, he is not there, and if I go west, I cannot perceive him.
18_23_8.AMP: "Behold, I go forward (to the east), but He is not there; I go backward (to the west), but I cannot perceive Him;
18_23_8.MSG: "I travel East looking for him—I find no one;then West, but not a trace;
18_23_8.NIV: “But if I go to the east, he is not there;if I go to the west, I do not find him.
18_23_8.NRSVCE: “If I go forward, he is not there; or backward, I cannot perceive him;

18_23_9.NKJV: When He works on the left hand, I cannot behold Him; When He turns to the right hand, I cannot see Him.
18_23_9.KJV: On the left hand, where he doth work, but I cannot behold him: he hideth himself on the right hand, that I cannot see him:
18_23_9.ESV: on the left hand when he is working, I do not behold him; he turns to the right hand, but I do not see him.
18_23_9.NASB: When He acts on the left, I cannot behold [Him]; He turns on the right, I cannot see Him.
18_23_9.NLT: I do not see him in the north, for he is hidden.I look to the south, but he is concealed.
18_23_9.WEB: He works to the north, but I can’t see him. He turns south, but I can’t catch a glimpse of him.
18_23_9.CSB17: When he is at work to the north, I cannot see him; when he turns south, I cannot find him.
18_23_9.AMP: To the left (north) He turns, but I cannot behold Him; He turns to the right hand (south), but I cannot see Him.
18_23_9.MSG: I go North, but he's hidden his tracks;then South, but not even a glimpse.
18_23_9.NIV: When he is at work in the north, I do not see him;when he turns to the south, I catch no glimpse of him.
18_23_9.NRSVCE: on the left he hides, and I cannot behold him; I turn to the right, but I cannot see him.

18_23_10.NKJV: But He knows the way that I take; When He has tested me, I shall come forth as gold.
18_23_10.KJV: But he knoweth the way that I take: when he hath tried me, I shall come forth as gold. that: Heb. that is with me
18_23_10.ESV: But he knows the way that I take; when he has tried me, I shall come out as gold.
18_23_10.NASB: 'But He knows the way I take; [When] He has tried me, I shall come forth as gold.
18_23_10.NLT: “But he knows where I am going.And when he tests me, I will come out as pure as gold.
18_23_10.WEB: But he knows the way that I take. When he has tried me, I shall come out like gold.
18_23_10.CSB17: Yet he knows the way I have taken; when he has tested me, I will emerge as pure gold.
18_23_10.AMP: "But He knows the way that I take [and He pays attention to it]. When He has tried me, I will come forth as [refined] gold [pure and luminous].
18_23_10.MSG: "But he knows where I am and what I've done.He can cross-examine me all he wants, and I'll pass the testwith honors.
18_23_10.NIV: But he knows the way that I take;when he has tested me, I will come forth as gold.
18_23_10.NRSVCE: But he knows the way that I take; when he has tested me, I shall come out like gold.

18_23_11.NKJV: My foot has held fast to His steps; I have kept His way and not turned aside.
18_23_11.KJV: My foot hath held his steps, his way have I kept, and not declined.
18_23_11.ESV: My foot has held fast to his steps; I have kept his way and have not turned aside.
18_23_11.NASB: 'My foot has held fast to His path; I have kept His way and not turned aside.
18_23_11.NLT: For I have stayed on God’s paths;I have followed his ways and not turned aside.
18_23_11.WEB: My foot has held fast to his steps. I have kept his way, and not turned aside.
18_23_11.CSB17: My feet have followed in his tracks; I have kept to his way and not turned aside.
18_23_11.AMP: "My feet have carefully followed His steps; I have kept His ways and not turned aside.
18_23_11.MSG: I've followed him closely, my feet in his footprints,not once swerving from his way.
18_23_11.NIV: My feet have closely followed his steps;I have kept to his way without turning aside.
18_23_11.NRSVCE: My foot has held fast to his steps; I have kept his way and have not turned aside.

18_23_12.NKJV: I have not departed from the commandment of His lips; I have treasured the words of His mouth More than my necessary food.
18_23_12.KJV: Neither have I gone back from the commandment of his lips; I have esteemed the words of his mouth more than my necessary food. esteemed: Heb. hid, or, laid up my: or, my appointed portion
18_23_12.ESV: I have not departed from the commandment of his lips; I have treasured the words of his mouth more than my portion of food.
18_23_12.NASB: 'I have not departed from the command of His lips; I have treasured the words of His mouth more than my necessary food.
18_23_12.NLT: I have not departed from his commands,but have treasured his words more than daily food.
18_23_12.WEB: I haven’t gone back from the commandment of his lips. I have treasured up the words of his mouth more than my necessary food.
18_23_12.CSB17: I have not departed from the commands from his lips; I have treasured the words from his mouth more than my daily food.
18_23_12.AMP: "I have not departed from the commandment of His lips; I have kept the words of His mouth more than my necessary food.
18_23_12.MSG: I've obeyed every word he's spoken,and not just obeyed his advice—I've treasured it.
18_23_12.NIV: I have not departed from the commands of his lips;I have treasured the words of his mouth more than my daily bread.
18_23_12.NRSVCE: I have not departed from the commandment of his lips; I have treasured in my bosom the words of his mouth.

18_23_13.NKJV: “But He is unique, and who can make Him change? And whatever His soul desires, that He does.
18_23_13.KJV: But he is in one mind, and who can turn him? and what his soul desireth, even that he doeth.
18_23_13.ESV: But he is unchangeable, and who can turn him back? What he desires, that he does.
18_23_13.NASB: 'But He is unique and who can turn Him? And [what] His soul desires, that He does.
18_23_13.NLT: But once he has made his decision, who can change his mind?Whatever he wants to do, he does.
18_23_13.WEB: But he stands alone, and who can oppose him? What his soul desires, even that he does.
18_23_13.CSB17: But he is unchangeable; who can oppose him? He does what he desires.
18_23_13.AMP: "But He is unique and unchangeable, and who can turn Him? And what His soul desires, that He does.
18_23_13.MSG: "But he is singular and sovereign. Who can argue with him?He does what he wants, when he wants to.
18_23_13.NIV: “But he stands alone, and who can oppose him?He does whatever he pleases.
18_23_13.NRSVCE: But he stands alone and who can dissuade him? What he desires, that he does.

18_23_14.NKJV: For He performs what is appointed for me, And many such things are with Him.
18_23_14.KJV: For he performeth the thing that is appointed for me: and many such things are with him.
18_23_14.ESV: For he will complete what he appoints for me, and many such things are in his mind.
18_23_14.NASB: 'For He performs what is appointed for me, And many such [decrees] are with Him.
18_23_14.NLT: So he will do to me whatever he has planned.He controls my destiny.
18_23_14.WEB: For he performs that which is appointed for me. Many such things are with him.
18_23_14.CSB17: He will certainly accomplish what he has decreed for me, and he has many more things like these in mind.
18_23_14.AMP: "For He performs what is planned (appointed) for me, And He is mindful of many such things.
18_23_14.MSG: He'll complete in detail what he's decided about me,and whatever else he determines to do.
18_23_14.NIV: He carries out his decree against me,and many such plans he still has in store.
18_23_14.NRSVCE: For he will complete what he appoints for me; and many such things are in his mind.

18_23_15.NKJV: Therefore I am terrified at His presence; When I consider this, I am afraid of Him.
18_23_15.KJV: Therefore am I troubled at his presence: when I consider, I am afraid of him.
18_23_15.ESV: Therefore I am terrified at his presence; when I consider, I am in dread of him.
18_23_15.NASB: 'Therefore, I would be dismayed at His presence; [When] I consider, I am terrified of Him.
18_23_15.NLT: No wonder I am so terrified in his presence.When I think of it, terror grips me.
18_23_15.WEB: Therefore I am terrified at his presence. When I consider, I am afraid of him.
18_23_15.CSB17: Therefore I am terrified in his presence; when I consider this, I am afraid of him.
18_23_15.AMP: "Therefore I would be terrified at His presence; When I consider [all of this], I tremble in dread of Him.
18_23_15.MSG: Is it any wonder that I dread meeting him?Whenever I think about it, I get scared all over again.
18_23_15.NIV: That is why I am terrified before him;when I think of all this, I fear him.
18_23_15.NRSVCE: Therefore I am terrified at his presence; when I consider, I am in dread of him.

18_23_16.NKJV: For God made my heart weak, And the Almighty terrifies me;
18_23_16.KJV: For God maketh my heart soft, and the Almighty troubleth me:
18_23_16.ESV: God has made my heart faint; the Almighty has terrified me;
18_23_16.NASB: '[It is] God [who] has made my heart faint, And the Almighty [who] has dismayed me,
18_23_16.NLT: God has made me sick at heart;the Almighty has terrified me.
18_23_16.WEB: For God has made my heart faint. The Almighty has terrified me.
18_23_16.CSB17: God has made my heart faint; the Almighty has terrified me.
18_23_16.AMP: "For God has made my heart faint, And the Almighty has terrified me,
18_23_16.MSG: God makes my heart sink!God Almighty gives me the shudders!
18_23_16.NIV: God has made my heart faint;the Almighty has terrified me.
18_23_16.NRSVCE: God has made my heart faint; the Almighty has terrified me;

18_23_17.NKJV: Because I was not cut off from the presence of darkness, And He did not hide deep darkness from my face.
18_23_17.KJV: Because I was not cut off before the darkness, neither hath he covered the darkness from my face.
18_23_17.ESV: yet I am not silenced because of the darkness, nor because thick darkness covers my face.
18_23_17.NASB: But I am not silenced by the darkness, Nor deep gloom [which] covers me.
18_23_17.NLT: Darkness is all around me;thick, impenetrable darkness is everywhere.
18_23_17.WEB: Because I was not cut off before the darkness, neither did he cover the thick darkness from my face.
18_23_17.CSB17: Yet I am not destroyed by the darkness, by the thick darkness that covers my face.
18_23_17.AMP: But I am not silenced by the darkness [of these woes that fell on me], Nor by the thick darkness which covers my face.
18_23_17.MSG: I'm completely in the dark,I can't see my hand in front of my face.
18_23_17.NIV: Yet I am not silenced by the darkness,by the thick darkness that covers my face.
18_23_17.NRSVCE: If only I could vanish in darkness, and thick darkness would cover my face!

18_24_1.NKJV: “Since times are not hidden from the Almighty, Why do those who know Him see not His days?
18_24_1.KJV: Why, seeing times are not hidden from the Almighty, do they that know him not see his days?
18_24_1.ESV: “Why are not times of judgment kept by the Almighty, and why do those who know him never see his days?
18_24_1.NASB: 'Why are times not stored up by the Almighty, And why do those who know Him not see His days?
18_24_1.NLT: “Why doesn’t the Almighty bring the wicked to judgment?Why must the godly wait for him in vain?
18_24_1.WEB: “Why aren’t times laid up by the Almighty? Why don’t those who know him see his days?
18_24_1.CSB17: Why does the Almighty not reserve times for judgment? Why do those who know him never see his days?
18_24_1.AMP: "Why does the Almighty not set seasons for judgment? Why do those who know Him not see His days [for punishment of the wicked]?
18_24_1.MSG: "But if Judgment Day isn't hidden from the Almighty,why are we kept in the dark?
18_24_1.NIV: “Why does the Almighty not set times for judgment?Why must those who know him look in vain for such days?
18_24_1.NRSVCE: “Why are times not kept by the Almighty, and why do those who know him never see his days?

18_24_2.NKJV: “Some remove landmarks; They seize flocks violently and feed on them;
18_24_2.KJV: Some remove the landmarks; they violently take away flocks, and feed thereof. feed: or, feed them
18_24_2.ESV: Some move landmarks; they seize flocks and pasture them.
18_24_2.NASB: 'Some remove the landmarks; They seize and devour flocks.
18_24_2.NLT: Evil people steal land by moving the boundary markers.They steal livestock and put them in their own pastures.
18_24_2.WEB: There are people who remove the landmarks. They violently take away flocks, and feed them.
18_24_2.CSB17: The wicked displace boundary markers. They steal a flock and provide pasture for it.
18_24_2.AMP: "Some remove the landmarks; They [violently] seize and pasture flocks [appropriating land and flocks openly].
18_24_2.MSG: There are people out there getting by with murder—stealing and lying and cheating.
18_24_2.NIV: Men move boundary stones;they pasture flocks they have stolen.
18_24_2.NRSVCE: The wicked remove landmarks; they seize flocks and pasture them.

18_24_3.NKJV: They drive away the donkey of the fatherless; They take the widow’s ox as a pledge.
18_24_3.KJV: They drive away the ass of the fatherless, they take the widow's ox for a pledge.
18_24_3.ESV: They drive away the donkey of the fatherless; they take the widow's ox for a pledge.
18_24_3.NASB: 'They drive away the donkeys of the orphans; They take the widow's ox for a pledge.
18_24_3.NLT: They take the orphan’s donkeyand demand the widow’s ox as security for a loan.
18_24_3.WEB: They drive away the donkey of the fatherless, and they take the widow’s ox for a pledge.
18_24_3.CSB17: They drive away the donkeys owned by the fatherless and take the widow’s ox as collateral.
18_24_3.AMP: "They drive away the donkeys of the orphans; They take the widow's ox for a pledge.
18_24_3.MSG: They rip off the poorand exploit the unfortunate,
18_24_3.NIV: They drive away the orphan’s donkeyand take the widow’s ox in pledge.
18_24_3.NRSVCE: They drive away the donkey of the orphan; they take the widow’s ox for a pledge.

18_24_4.NKJV: They push the needy off the road; All the poor of the land are forced to hide.
18_24_4.KJV: They turn the needy out of the way: the poor of the earth hide themselves together.
18_24_4.ESV: They thrust the poor off the road; the poor of the earth all hide themselves.
18_24_4.NASB: 'They push the needy aside from the road; The poor of the land are made to hide themselves altogether.
18_24_4.NLT: The poor are pushed off the path;the needy must hide together for safety.
18_24_4.WEB: They turn the needy out of the way. The poor of the earth all hide themselves.
18_24_4.CSB17: They push the needy off the road; the poor of the land are forced into hiding.
18_24_4.AMP: "They crowd the needy off the road; The poor of the land all hide themselves.
18_24_4.MSG: Push the helpless into the ditch,bully the weak so that they fear for their lives.
18_24_4.NIV: They thrust the needy from the pathand force all the poor of the land into hiding.
18_24_4.NRSVCE: They thrust the needy off the road; the poor of the earth all hide themselves.

18_24_5.NKJV: Indeed, like wild donkeys in the desert, They go out to their work, searching for food. The wilderness yields food for them and for their children.
18_24_5.KJV: Behold, as wild asses in the desert, go they forth to their work; rising betimes for a prey: the wilderness yieldeth food for them and for their children.
18_24_5.ESV: Behold, like wild donkeys in the desert the poor go out to their toil, seeking game; the wasteland yields food for their children.
18_24_5.NASB: 'Behold, as wild donkeys in the wilderness They go forth seeking food in their activity, As bread for [their] children in the desert.
18_24_5.NLT: Like wild donkeys in the wilderness,the poor must spend all their time looking for food,searching even in the desert for food for their children.
18_24_5.WEB: Behold, as wild donkeys in the desert, they go out to their work, seeking diligently for food. The wilderness yields them bread for their children.
18_24_5.CSB17: Like wild donkeys in the wilderness, the poor go out to their task of foraging for food; the desert provides nourishment for their children.
18_24_5.AMP: "Behold, as wild donkeys in the desert, The poor go to their work, diligently seeking food; As bread for their children in the desert.
18_24_5.MSG: The poor, like stray dogs and cats,scavenge for food in back alleys.
18_24_5.NIV: Like wild donkeys in the desert,the poor go about their labor of foraging food;the wasteland provides food for their children.
18_24_5.NRSVCE: Like wild asses in the desert they go out to their toil, scavenging in the wasteland food for their young.
