18_24_6.NKJV: They gather their fodder in the field And glean in the vineyard of the wicked.
18_24_6.KJV: They reap every one his corn in the field: and they gather the vintage of the wicked. corn: Heb. mingled corn, or, dredge they gather: Heb. the wicked gather the vintage
18_24_6.ESV: They gather their fodder in the field, and they glean the vineyard of the wicked man.
18_24_6.NASB: 'They harvest their fodder in the field And glean the vineyard of the wicked.
18_24_6.NLT: They harvest a field they do not own,and they glean in the vineyards of the wicked.
18_24_6.WEB: They cut their food in the field. They glean the vineyard of the wicked.
18_24_6.CSB17: They gather their fodder in the field and glean the vineyards of the wicked.
18_24_6.AMP: "They harvest their fodder in a field [that is not their own], And glean the vineyard of the wicked.
18_24_6.MSG: They sort through the garbage of the rich,eke out survival on handouts.
18_24_6.NIV: They gather fodder in the fieldsand glean in the vineyards of the wicked.
18_24_6.NRSVCE: They reap in a field not their own and they glean in the vineyard of the wicked.

18_24_7.NKJV: They spend the night naked, without clothing, And have no covering in the cold.
18_24_7.KJV: They cause the naked to lodge without clothing, that they have no covering in the cold.
18_24_7.ESV: They lie all night naked, without clothing, and have no covering in the cold.
18_24_7.NASB: 'They spend the night naked, without clothing, And have no covering against the cold.
18_24_7.NLT: All night they lie naked in the cold,without clothing or covering.
18_24_7.WEB: They lie all night naked without clothing, and have no covering in the cold.
18_24_7.CSB17: Without clothing, they spend the night naked, having no covering against the cold.
18_24_7.AMP: "They spend the night naked, without clothing, And have no covering against the cold.
18_24_7.MSG: Homeless, they shiver through cold nights on the street;they've no place to lay their heads.
18_24_7.NIV: Lacking clothes, they spend the night naked;they have nothing to cover themselves in the cold.
18_24_7.NRSVCE: They lie all night naked, without clothing, and have no covering in the cold.

18_24_8.NKJV: They are wet with the showers of the mountains, And huddle around the rock for want of shelter.
18_24_8.KJV: They are wet with the showers of the mountains, and embrace the rock for want of a shelter.
18_24_8.ESV: They are wet with the rain of the mountains and cling to the rock for lack of shelter.
18_24_8.NASB: 'They are wet with the mountain rains And hug the rock for want of a shelter.
18_24_8.NLT: They are soaked by mountain showers,and they huddle against the rocks for want of a home.
18_24_8.WEB: They are wet with the showers of the mountains, and embrace the rock for lack of a shelter.
18_24_8.CSB17: Drenched by mountain rains, they huddle against the rocks, shelterless.
18_24_8.AMP: "They are wet from the rain of the mountains And cling to the rock for lack of shelter.
18_24_8.MSG: Exposed to the weather, wet and frozen,they huddle in makeshift shelters.
18_24_8.NIV: They are drenched by mountain rainsand hug the rocks for lack of shelter.
18_24_8.NRSVCE: They are wet with the rain of the mountains, and cling to the rock for want of shelter.

18_24_9.NKJV: “Some snatch the fatherless from the breast, And take a pledge from the poor.
18_24_9.KJV: They pluck the fatherless from the breast, and take a pledge of the poor.
18_24_9.ESV: (There are those who snatch the fatherless child from the breast, and they take a pledge against the poor.)
18_24_9.NASB: 'Others snatch the orphan from the breast, And against the poor they take a pledge.
18_24_9.NLT: “The wicked snatch a widow’s child from her breast,taking the baby as security for a loan.
18_24_9.WEB: There are those who pluck the fatherless from the breast, and take a pledge of the poor,
18_24_9.CSB17: The fatherless infant is snatched from the breast; the nursing child of the poor is seized as collateral.
18_24_9.AMP: "Others snatch the fatherless [infants] from the breast [to sell or make them slaves], And against the poor they take a pledge [of clothing].
18_24_9.MSG: Nursing mothers have their babies snatched from them;the infants of the poor are kidnapped and sold.
18_24_9.NIV: The fatherless child is snatched from the breast;the infant of the poor is seized for a debt.
18_24_9.NRSVCE: “There are those who snatch the orphan child from the breast, and take as a pledge the infant of the poor.

18_24_10.NKJV: They cause the poor to go naked, without clothing; And they take away the sheaves from the hungry.
18_24_10.KJV: They cause him to go naked without clothing, and they take away the sheaf from the hungry;
18_24_10.ESV: They go about naked, without clothing; hungry, they carry the sheaves;
18_24_10.NASB: 'They cause [the poor] to go about naked without clothing, And they take away the sheaves from the hungry.
18_24_10.NLT: The poor must go about naked, without any clothing.They harvest food for others while they themselves are starving.
18_24_10.WEB: So that they go around naked without clothing. Being hungry, they carry the sheaves.
18_24_10.CSB17: Without clothing, they wander about naked. They carry sheaves but go hungry.
18_24_10.AMP: "They cause the poor to go about naked without clothing, And they take away the sheaves [of grain] from the hungry.
18_24_10.MSG: They go about patched and threadbare;even the hard workers go hungry.
18_24_10.NIV: Lacking clothes, they go about naked;they carry the sheaves, but still go hungry.
18_24_10.NRSVCE: They go about naked, without clothing; though hungry, they carry the sheaves;

18_24_11.NKJV: They press out oil within their walls, And tread winepresses, yet suffer thirst.
18_24_11.KJV: Which make oil within their walls, and tread their winepresses, and suffer thirst.
18_24_11.ESV: among the olive rows of the wicked they make oil; they tread the winepresses, but suffer thirst.
18_24_11.NASB: 'Within the walls they produce oil; They tread wine presses but thirst.
18_24_11.NLT: They press out olive oil without being allowed to taste it,and they tread in the winepress as they suffer from thirst.
18_24_11.WEB: They make oil within the walls of these men. They tread wine presses, and suffer thirst.
18_24_11.CSB17: They crush olives in their presses; they tread the winepresses, but go thirsty.
18_24_11.AMP: "Within the walls [of the wicked] the poor make [olive] oil; They tread [the grapes in] the wine presses, but thirst.
18_24_11.MSG: No matter how back-breaking their labor,they can never make ends meet.
18_24_11.NIV: They crush olives among the terraces;they tread the winepresses, yet suffer thirst.
18_24_11.NRSVCE: between their terraces they press out oil; they tread the wine presses, but suffer thirst.

18_24_12.NKJV: The dying groan in the city, And the souls of the wounded cry out; Yet God does not charge them with wrong.
18_24_12.KJV: Men groan from out of the city, and the soul of the wounded crieth out: yet God layeth not folly to them.
18_24_12.ESV: From out of the city the dying groan, and the soul of the wounded cries for help; yet God charges no one with wrong.
18_24_12.NASB: 'From the city men groan, And the souls of the wounded cry out; Yet God does not pay attention to folly.
18_24_12.NLT: The groans of the dying rise from the city,and the wounded cry for help,yet God ignores their moaning.
18_24_12.WEB: From out of the populous city, men groan. The soul of the wounded cries out, yet God doesn’t regard the folly.
18_24_12.CSB17: From the city, men groan; the mortally wounded cry for help, yet God pays no attention to this crime.
18_24_12.AMP: "From the [populous and crowded] city men groan, And the souls of the wounded cry out for help; Yet God [seemingly] does not pay attention to the wrong [done to them].
18_24_12.MSG: People are dying right and left, groaning in torment.The wretched cry out for helpand God does nothing, acts like nothing's wrong!
18_24_12.NIV: The groans of the dying rise from the city,and the souls of the wounded cry out for help.But God charges no one with wrongdoing.
18_24_12.NRSVCE: From the city the dying groan, and the throat of the wounded cries for help; yet God pays no attention to their prayer.

18_24_13.NKJV: “There are those who rebel against the light; They do not know its ways Nor abide in its paths.
18_24_13.KJV: They are of those that rebel against the light; they know not the ways thereof, nor abide in the paths thereof.
18_24_13.ESV: “There are those who rebel against the light, who are not acquainted with its ways, and do not stay in its paths.
18_24_13.NASB: 'Others have been with those who rebel against the light; They do not want to know its ways Nor abide in its paths.
18_24_13.NLT: “Wicked people rebel against the light.They refuse to acknowledge its waysor stay in its paths.
18_24_13.WEB: “These are of those who rebel against the light. They don’t know its ways, nor stay in its paths.
18_24_13.CSB17: The wicked are those who rebel against the light. They do not recognize its ways or stay on its paths.
18_24_13.AMP: "Others have been with those who rebel against the light; They do not want to know its ways Nor stay in its paths.
18_24_13.MSG: "Then there are those who avoid light at all costs,who scorn the light-filled path.
18_24_13.NIV: “There are those who rebel against the light,who do not know its waysor stay in its paths.
18_24_13.NRSVCE: “There are those who rebel against the light, who are not acquainted with its ways, and do not stay in its paths.

18_24_14.NKJV: The murderer rises with the light; He kills the poor and needy; And in the night he is like a thief.
18_24_14.KJV: The murderer rising with the light killeth the poor and needy, and in the night is as a thief.
18_24_14.ESV: The murderer rises before it is light, that he may kill the poor and needy, and in the night he is like a thief.
18_24_14.NASB: 'The murderer arises at dawn; He kills the poor and the needy, And at night he is as a thief.
18_24_14.NLT: The murderer rises in the early dawnto kill the poor and needy;at night he is a thief.
18_24_14.WEB: The murderer rises with the light. He kills the poor and needy. In the night he is like a thief.
18_24_14.CSB17: The murderer rises at dawn to kill the poor and needy, and by night he becomes a thief.
18_24_14.AMP: "The murderer rises at dawn; He kills the poor and the needy, And at night he becomes a thief.
18_24_14.MSG: When the sun goes down, the murderer gets up—kills the poor and robs the defenseless.
18_24_14.NIV: When daylight is gone, the murderer rises upand kills the poor and needy;in the night he steals forth like a thief.
18_24_14.NRSVCE: The murderer rises at dusk to kill the poor and needy, and in the night is like a thief.

18_24_15.NKJV: The eye of the adulterer waits for the twilight, Saying, ‘No eye will see me’; And he disguises his face.
18_24_15.KJV: The eye also of the adulterer waiteth for the twilight, saying, No eye shall see me: and disguiseth his face. disguiseth: Heb. setteth his face in secret
18_24_15.ESV: The eye of the adulterer also waits for the twilight, saying, ‘No eye will see me’; and he veils his face.
18_24_15.NASB: 'The eye of the adulterer waits for the twilight, Saying, 'No eye will see me.' And he disguises his face.
18_24_15.NLT: The adulterer waits for the twilight,saying, ‘No one will see me then.’He hides his face so no one will know him.
18_24_15.WEB: The eye also of the adulterer waits for the twilight, saying, ‘No eye shall see me.’ He disguises his face.
18_24_15.CSB17: The adulterer’s eye watches for twilight, thinking, “No eye will see me,” and he covers his face.
18_24_15.AMP: "The eye of the adulterer waits for the twilight, Saying, 'No eye will see me,' And he covers his face.
18_24_15.MSG: Sexual predators can't wait for nightfall,thinking, 'No one can see us now.'
18_24_15.NIV: The eye of the adulterer watches for dusk;he thinks, ‘No eye will see me,’and he keeps his face concealed.
18_24_15.NRSVCE: The eye of the adulterer also waits for the twilight, saying, ‘No eye will see me’; and he disguises his face.

18_24_16.NKJV: In the dark they break into houses Which they marked for themselves in the daytime; They do not know the light.
18_24_16.KJV: In the dark they dig through houses, which they had marked for themselves in the daytime: they know not the light.
18_24_16.ESV: In the dark they dig through houses; by day they shut themselves up; they do not know the light.
18_24_16.NASB: 'In the dark they dig into houses, They shut themselves up by day; They do not know the light.
18_24_16.NLT: Thieves break into houses at nightand sleep in the daytime.They are not acquainted with the light.
18_24_16.WEB: In the dark they dig through houses. They shut themselves up in the daytime. They don’t know the light.
18_24_16.CSB17: In the dark they break into houses; by day they lock themselves in, never experiencing the light.
18_24_16.AMP: "In the dark they dig into [the penetrable walls of] houses; They shut themselves up by day; They do not know the light [of day].
18_24_16.MSG: Burglars do their work at night,but keep well out of sight through the day.They want nothing to do with light.
18_24_16.NIV: In the dark, men break into houses,but by day they shut themselves in;they want nothing to do with the light.
18_24_16.NRSVCE: In the dark they dig through houses; by day they shut themselves up; they do not know the light.

18_24_17.NKJV: For the morning is the same to them as the shadow of death; If someone recognizes them, They are in the terrors of the shadow of death.
18_24_17.KJV: For the morning is to them even as the shadow of death: if one know them, they are in the terrors of the shadow of death.
18_24_17.ESV: For deep darkness is morning to all of them; for they are friends with the terrors of deep darkness.
18_24_17.NASB: 'For the morning is the same to him as thick darkness, For he is familiar with the terrors of thick darkness.
18_24_17.NLT: The black night is their morning.They ally themselves with the terrors of the darkness.
18_24_17.WEB: For the morning is to all of them like thick darkness, for they know the terrors of the thick darkness.
18_24_17.CSB17: For the morning is like darkness to them. Surely they are familiar with the terrors of darkness!
18_24_17.AMP: "For the morning is the same to him as the thick darkness [of midnight]; For he is familiar with the terrors of thick darkness.
18_24_17.MSG: Deep darkness is morning for that bunch;they make the terrors of darkness their companions in crime.
18_24_17.NIV: For all of them, deep darkness is their morning;they make friends with the terrors of darkness.
18_24_17.NRSVCE: For deep darkness is morning to all of them; for they are friends with the terrors of deep darkness.

18_24_18.NKJV: “They should be swift on the face of the waters, Their portion should be cursed in the earth, So that no one would turn into the way of their vineyards.
18_24_18.KJV: He is swift as the waters ; their portion is cursed in the earth: he beholdeth not the way of the vineyards.
18_24_18.ESV: “You say, ‘Swift are they on the face of the waters; their portion is cursed in the land; no treader turns toward their vineyards.
18_24_18.NASB: 'They are insignificant on the surface of the water; Their portion is cursed on the earth. They do not turn toward the vineyards.
18_24_18.NLT: “But they disappear like foam down a river.Everything they own is cursed,and they are afraid to enter their own vineyards.
18_24_18.WEB: “They are foam on the surface of the waters. Their portion is cursed in the earth. They don’t turn into the way of the vineyards.
18_24_18.CSB17: They float on the surface of the water. Their section of the land is cursed, so that they never go to their vineyards.
18_24_18.AMP: "They are insignificant on the surface of the water; Their portion is cursed on the earth; They do not turn toward the vineyards.
18_24_18.MSG: "They are scraps of wood floating on the water—useless, cursed junk, good for nothing.
18_24_18.NIV: “Yet they are foam on the surface of the water;their portion of the land is cursed,so that no one goes to the vineyards.
18_24_18.NRSVCE: “Swift are they on the face of the waters; their portion in the land is cursed; no treader turns toward their vineyards.

18_24_19.NKJV: As drought and heat consume the snow waters, So the grave consumes those who have sinned.
18_24_19.KJV: Drought and heat consume the snow waters: so doth the grave those which have sinned. consume: Heb. violently take
18_24_19.ESV: Drought and heat snatch away the snow waters; so does Sheol those who have sinned.
18_24_19.NASB: 'Drought and heat consume the snow waters, [So does] Sheol [those who] have sinned.
18_24_19.NLT: The grave consumes sinnersjust as drought and heat consume snow.
18_24_19.WEB: Drought and heat consume the snow waters, so does Sheol those who have sinned.
18_24_19.CSB17: As dry ground and heat snatch away the melted snow, so Sheol steals those who have sinned.
18_24_19.AMP: "Drought and heat consume the snow waters; So does Sheol (the nether world, the place of the dead) [consume] those who have sinned.
18_24_19.MSG: As surely as snow melts under the hot, summer sun,sinners disappear in the grave.
18_24_19.NIV: As heat and drought snatch away the melted snow,so the grave snatches away those who have sinned.
18_24_19.NRSVCE: Drought and heat snatch away the snow waters; so does Sheol those who have sinned.

18_24_20.NKJV: The womb should forget him, The worm should feed sweetly on him; He should be remembered no more, And wickedness should be broken like a tree.
18_24_20.KJV: The womb shall forget him; the worm shall feed sweetly on him; he shall be no more remembered; and wickedness shall be broken as a tree.
18_24_20.ESV: The womb forgets them; the worm finds them sweet; they are no longer remembered, so wickedness is broken like a tree.’
18_24_20.NASB: 'A mother will forget him; The worm feeds sweetly till he is no longer remembered. And wickedness will be broken like a tree.
18_24_20.NLT: Their own mothers will forget them.Maggots will find them sweet to eat.No one will remember them.Wicked people are broken like a tree in the storm.
18_24_20.WEB: The womb shall forget him. The worm shall feed sweetly on him. He shall be no more remembered. Unrighteousness shall be broken as a tree.
18_24_20.CSB17: The womb forgets them; worms feed on them; they are remembered no more. So injustice is broken like a tree.
18_24_20.AMP: "A mother will forget him; The worm feeds on him until he is no longer remembered. And wickedness will be broken like a tree [which cannot be restored].
18_24_20.MSG: The womb has forgotten them, worms have relished them—nothing that is evil lasts.
18_24_20.NIV: The womb forgets them,the worm feasts on them;evil men are no longer rememberedbut are broken like a tree.
18_24_20.NRSVCE: The womb forgets them; the worm finds them sweet; they are no longer remembered; so wickedness is broken like a tree.

18_24_21.NKJV: For he preys on the barren who do not bear, And does no good for the widow.
18_24_21.KJV: He evil entreateth the barren that beareth not: and doeth not good to the widow.
18_24_21.ESV: “They wrong the barren, childless woman, and do no good to the widow.
18_24_21.NASB: 'He wrongs the barren woman And does no good for the widow.
18_24_21.NLT: They cheat the woman who has no son to help her.They refuse to help the needy widow.
18_24_21.WEB: He devours the barren who don’t bear. He shows no kindness to the widow.
18_24_21.CSB17: They prey on the childless woman who is unable to conceive, and do not deal kindly with the widow.
18_24_21.AMP: "He preys on the barren (childless) woman And does no good for the widow.
18_24_21.MSG: Unscrupulous,they prey on those less fortunate.
18_24_21.NIV: They prey on the barren and childless woman,and to the widow show no kindness.
18_24_21.NRSVCE: “They harm the childless woman, and do no good to the widow.

18_24_22.NKJV: “But God draws the mighty away with His power; He rises up, but no man is sure of life.
18_24_22.KJV: He draweth also the mighty with his power: he riseth up, and no man is sure of life. no: or, he trusteth not his own life
18_24_22.ESV: Yet God prolongs the life of the mighty by his power; they rise up when they despair of life.
18_24_22.NASB: 'But He drags off the valiant by His power; He rises, but no one has assurance of life.
18_24_22.NLT: “God, in his power, drags away the rich.They may rise high, but they have no assurance of life.
18_24_22.WEB: Yet God preserves the mighty by his power. He rises up who has no assurance of life.
18_24_22.CSB17: Yet God drags away the mighty by his power; when he rises up, they have no assurance of life.
18_24_22.AMP: "Yet God draws away the mighty by His power; He rises, but no one has assurance of life.
18_24_22.MSG: However much they strut and flex their muscles,there's nothing to them. They're hollow.
18_24_22.NIV: But God drags away the mighty by his power;though they become established, they have no assurance of life.
18_24_22.NRSVCE: Yet God prolongs the life of the mighty by his power; they rise up when they despair of life.

18_24_23.NKJV: He gives them security, and they rely on it; Yet His eyes are on their ways.
18_24_23.KJV: Though it be given him to be in safety, whereon he resteth; yet his eyes are upon their ways.
18_24_23.ESV: He gives them security, and they are supported, and his eyes are upon their ways.
18_24_23.NASB: 'He provides them with security, and they are supported; And His eyes are on their ways.
18_24_23.NLT: They may be allowed to live in security,but God is always watching them.
18_24_23.WEB: God gives them security, and they rest in it. His eyes are on their ways.
18_24_23.CSB17: He gives them a sense of security, so they can rely on it, but his eyes watch over their ways.
18_24_23.AMP: "God gives them security, and they are supported; And His eyes are on their ways.
18_24_23.MSG: They may have an illusion of security,but God has his eye on them.
18_24_23.NIV: He may let them rest in a feeling of security,but his eyes are on their ways.
18_24_23.NRSVCE: He gives them security, and they are supported; his eyes are upon their ways.

18_24_24.NKJV: They are exalted for a little while, Then they are gone. They are brought low; They are taken out of the way like all others; They dry out like the heads of grain.
18_24_24.KJV: They are exalted for a little while, but are gone and brought low; they are taken out of the way as all other, and cut off as the tops of the ears of corn. are gone: Heb. are not taken: Heb. closed up
18_24_24.ESV: They are exalted a little while, and then are gone; they are brought low and gathered up like all others; they are cut off like the heads of grain.
18_24_24.NASB: 'They are exalted a little while, then they are gone; Moreover, they are brought low and like everything gathered up; Even like the heads of grain they are cut off.
18_24_24.NLT: And though they are great now,in a moment they will be gone like all others,cut off like heads of grain.
18_24_24.WEB: They are exalted; yet a little while, and they are gone. Yes, they are brought low, they are taken out of the way as all others, and are cut off as the tops of the ears of grain.
18_24_24.CSB17: They are exalted for a moment, then gone; they are brought low and shrivel up like everything else. They wither like heads of grain.
18_24_24.AMP: "They are exalted for a little while, and then they are gone; Moreover, they are brought low and like everything [they are] gathered up and taken out of the way; Even like the heads of grain they are cut off.
18_24_24.MSG: They may get their brief successes,but then it's over, nothing to show for it.Like yesterday's newspaper,they're used to wrap up the garbage.
18_24_24.NIV: For a little while they are exalted, and then they are gone;they are brought low and gathered up like all others;they are cut off like heads of grain.
18_24_24.NRSVCE: They are exalted a little while, and then are gone; they wither and fade like the mallow; they are cut off like the heads of grain.

18_24_25.NKJV: “Now if it is not so, who will prove me a liar, And make my speech worth nothing?”
18_24_25.KJV: And if it be not so now, who will make me a liar, and make my speech nothing worth?
18_24_25.ESV: If it is not so, who will prove me a liar and show that there is nothing in what I say?”
18_24_25.NASB: 'Now if it is not so, who can prove me a liar, And make my speech worthless?'
18_24_25.NLT: Can anyone claim otherwise?Who can prove me wrong?”
18_24_25.WEB: If it isn’t so now, who will prove me a liar, and make my speech worth nothing?”
18_24_25.CSB17: If this is not true, then who can prove me a liar and show that my speech is worthless?
18_24_25.AMP: "And if this is not so, who can prove me a liar And make my speech worthless?"
18_24_25.MSG: You're free to try to prove me a liar,but you won't be able to do it."
18_24_25.NIV: “If this is not so, who can prove me falseand reduce my words to nothing?”
18_24_25.NRSVCE: If it is not so, who will prove me a liar, and show that there is nothing in what I say?”

18_25_1.NKJV: Then Bildad the Shuhite answered and said:
18_25_1.KJV: Then answered Bildad the Shuhite, and said,
18_25_1.ESV: Then Bildad the Shuhite answered and said:
18_25_1.AMP: Then Bildad the Shuhite answered and said,
18_25_1.MSG: Bildad the Shuhite again attacked Job:

18_25_2.NKJV: “Dominion and fear belong to Him; He makes peace in His high places.
18_25_2.KJV: Dominion and fear are with him, he maketh peace in his high places.
18_25_2.ESV: “Dominion and fear are with God; he makes peace in his high heaven.
18_25_2.NASB: 'Dominion and awe belong to Him Who establishes peace in His heights.
18_25_2.NLT: “God is powerful and dreadful.He enforces peace in the heavens.
18_25_2.WEB: “Dominion and fear are with him. He makes peace in his high places.
18_25_2.CSB17: Dominion and dread belong to him, the one who establishes harmony in his heights.
18_25_2.AMP: "Dominion and awe belong to God; He establishes peace and order in His high places.
18_25_2.MSG: "God is sovereign, God is fearsome—everything in the cosmos fits and works in his plan.
18_25_2.NIV: “Dominion and awe belong to God;he establishes order in the heights of heaven.
18_25_2.NRSVCE: “Dominion and fear are with God; he makes peace in his high heaven.

18_25_3.NKJV: Is there any number to His armies? Upon whom does His light not rise?
18_25_3.KJV: Is there any number of his armies? and upon whom doth not his light arise?
18_25_3.ESV: Is there any number to his armies? Upon whom does his light not arise?
18_25_3.NASB: 'Is there any number to His troops? And upon whom does His light not rise?
18_25_3.NLT: Who is able to count his heavenly army?Doesn’t his light shine on all the earth?
18_25_3.WEB: Can his armies be counted? On whom does his light not arise?
18_25_3.CSB17: Can his troops be numbered? Does his light not shine on everyone?
18_25_3.AMP: "Is there any number to His [vast celestial] armies? And upon whom does His light not rise?
18_25_3.MSG: Can anyone count his angel armies?Is there any place where his light doesn't shine?
18_25_3.NIV: Can his forces be numbered?Upon whom does his light not rise?
18_25_3.NRSVCE: Is there any number to his armies? Upon whom does his light not arise?

18_25_4.NKJV: How then can man be righteous before God? Or how can he be pure who is born of a woman?
18_25_4.KJV: How then can man be justified with God? or how can he be clean that is born of a woman?
18_25_4.ESV: How then can man be in the right before God? How can he who is born of woman be pure?
18_25_4.NASB: 'How then can a man be just with God? Or how can he be clean who is born of woman?
18_25_4.NLT: How can a mortal be innocent before God?Can anyone born of a woman be pure?
18_25_4.WEB: How then can man be just with God? Or how can he who is born of a woman be clean?
18_25_4.CSB17: How can a human be justified before God? How can one born of woman be pure?
18_25_4.AMP: "How then can man be justified and righteous with God? Or how can he who is born of a woman be pure and clean?
18_25_4.MSG: How can a mere mortal presume to stand up to God?How can an ordinary person pretend to be guiltless?
18_25_4.NIV: How then can a man be righteous before God?How can one born of woman be pure?
18_25_4.NRSVCE: How then can a mortal be righteous before God? How can one born of woman be pure?

18_25_5.NKJV: If even the moon does not shine, And the stars are not pure in His sight,
18_25_5.KJV: Behold even to the moon, and it shineth not; yea, the stars are not pure in his sight.
18_25_5.ESV: Behold, even the moon is not bright, and the stars are not pure in his eyes;
18_25_5.NASB: 'If even the moon has no brightness And the stars are not pure in His sight,
18_25_5.NLT: God is more glorious than the moon;he shines brighter than the stars.
18_25_5.WEB: Behold, even the moon has no brightness, and the stars are not pure in his sight;
18_25_5.CSB17: If even the moon does not shine and the stars are not pure in his sight,
18_25_5.AMP: "Behold, even the moon has no brightness [compared to God's majesty and glory] And the stars are not pure in His sight,
18_25_5.MSG: Why, even the moon has its flaws,even the stars aren't perfect in God's eyes,
18_25_5.NIV: If even the moon is not brightand the stars are not pure in his eyes,
18_25_5.NRSVCE: If even the moon is not bright and the stars are not pure in his sight,

18_25_6.NKJV: How much less man, who is a maggot, And a son of man, who is a worm?”
18_25_6.KJV: How much less man, that is a worm? and the son of man, which is a worm?
18_25_6.ESV: how much less man, who is a maggot, and the son of man, who is a worm!”
18_25_6.NASB: How much less man, [that] maggot, And the son of man, [that] worm!'
18_25_6.NLT: In comparison, people are maggots;we mortals are mere worms.”
18_25_6.WEB: How much less man, who is a worm, the son of man, who is a worm!”
18_25_6.CSB17: how much less a human, who is a maggot, a son of man, who is a worm!
18_25_6.AMP: How much less man, that maggot, And the son of man, that worm!"
18_25_6.MSG: So how much less, plain men and women—slugs and maggots by comparison!"
18_25_6.NIV: how much less man, who is but a maggot—a son of man, who is only a worm!”
18_25_6.NRSVCE: how much less a mortal, who is a maggot, and a human being, who is a worm!”

18_26_2.NKJV: “How have you helped him who is without power? How have you saved the arm that has no strength?
18_26_2.KJV: How hast thou helped him that is without power? how savest thou the arm that hath no strength?
18_26_2.ESV: “How you have helped him who has no power! How you have saved the arm that has no strength!
18_26_2.NASB: 'What a help you are to the weak! How you have saved the arm without strength!
18_26_2.NLT: “How you have helped the powerless!How you have saved the weak!
18_26_2.WEB: “How have you helped him who is without power! How have you saved the arm that has no strength!
18_26_2.CSB17: How you have helped the powerless and delivered the arm that is weak!
18_26_2.AMP: "What a help you are to the weak (powerless)! How you have saved the arm that is without strength!
18_26_2.MSG: "Well, you've certainly been a great help to a helpless man!You came to the rescue just in the nick of time!
18_26_2.NIV: “How you have helped the powerless!How you have saved the arm that is feeble!
18_26_2.NRSVCE: “How you have helped one who has no power! How you have assisted the arm that has no strength!

18_26_3.NKJV: How have you counseled one who has no wisdom? And how have you declared sound advice to many?
18_26_3.KJV: How hast thou counselled him that hath no wisdom? and how hast thou plentifully declared the thing as it is?
18_26_3.ESV: How you have counseled him who has no wisdom, and plentifully declared sound knowledge!
18_26_3.NASB: 'What counsel you have given to [one] without wisdom! What helpful insight you have abundantly provided!
18_26_3.NLT: How you have enlightened my stupidity!What wise advice you have offered!
18_26_3.WEB: How have you counseled him who has no wisdom, and plentifully declared sound knowledge!
18_26_3.CSB17: How you have counseled the unwise and abundantly provided insight!
18_26_3.AMP: "How you have counseled the one who has no wisdom! And how abundantly you have provided sound wisdom and helpful insight!
18_26_3.MSG: What wonderful advice you've given to a mixed-up man!What amazing insights you've provided!
18_26_3.NIV: What advice you have offered to one without wisdom!And what great insight you have displayed!
18_26_3.NRSVCE: How you have counseled one who has no wisdom, and given much good advice!

18_26_4.NKJV: To whom have you uttered words? And whose spirit came from you?
18_26_4.KJV: To whom hast thou uttered words? and whose spirit came from thee?
18_26_4.ESV: With whose help have you uttered words, and whose breath has come out from you?
18_26_4.NASB: 'To whom have you uttered words? And whose spirit was expressed through you?
18_26_4.NLT: Where have you gotten all these wise sayings?Whose spirit speaks through you?
18_26_4.WEB: To whom have you uttered words? Whose spirit came out of you?
18_26_4.CSB17: With whom did you speak these words? Whose breath came out of your mouth?
18_26_4.AMP: "To whom have you uttered [these] words? And whose spirit [inspired what] came forth from you?
18_26_4.MSG: Where in the world did you learn all this?How did you become so inspired?
18_26_4.NIV: Who has helped you utter these words?And whose spirit spoke from your mouth?
18_26_4.NRSVCE: With whose help have you uttered words, and whose spirit has come forth from you?

18_26_5.NKJV: “The dead tremble, Those under the waters and those inhabiting them.
18_26_5.KJV: Dead things are formed from under the waters, and the inhabitants thereof. and the: or, with the inhabitants
18_26_5.ESV: The dead tremble under the waters and their inhabitants.
18_26_5.NASB: 'The departed spirits tremble Under the waters and their inhabitants.
18_26_5.NLT: “The dead tremble —those who live beneath the waters.
18_26_5.WEB: “The departed spirits tremble, those beneath the waters and all that live in them.
18_26_5.CSB17: The departed spirits tremble beneath the waters and all that inhabit them.
18_26_5.AMP: "The spirits of the dead tremble Underneath the waters and their inhabitants.
18_26_5.MSG: "All the buried dead are in torment,and all who've been drowned in the deep, deep sea.
18_26_5.NIV: “The dead are in deep anguish,those beneath the waters and all that live in them.
18_26_5.NRSVCE: The shades below tremble, the waters and their inhabitants.

18_26_6.NKJV: Sheol is naked before Him, And Destruction has no covering.
18_26_6.KJV: Hell is naked before him, and destruction hath no covering.
18_26_6.ESV: Sheol is naked before God, and Abaddon has no covering.
18_26_6.NASB: 'Naked is Sheol before Him, And Abaddon has no covering.
18_26_6.NLT: The underworld is naked in God’s presence.The place of destruction is uncovered.
18_26_6.WEB: Sheol is naked before God, and Abaddon has no covering.
18_26_6.CSB17: Sheol is naked before God, and Abaddon has no covering.
18_26_6.AMP: "Sheol (the nether world, the place of the dead) is naked before God, And Abaddon (the place of destruction) has no covering [from His eyes].
18_26_6.MSG: Hell is ripped open before God,graveyards dug up and exposed.
18_26_6.NIV: Death is naked before God;Destruction lies uncovered.
18_26_6.NRSVCE: Sheol is naked before God, and Abaddon has no covering.

18_26_7.NKJV: He stretches out the north over empty space; He hangs the earth on nothing.
18_26_7.KJV: He stretcheth out the north over the empty place, and hangeth the earth upon nothing.
18_26_7.ESV: He stretches out the north over the void and hangs the earth on nothing.
18_26_7.NASB: 'He stretches out the north over empty space And hangs the earth on nothing.
18_26_7.NLT: God stretches the northern sky over empty spaceand hangs the earth on nothing.
18_26_7.WEB: He stretches out the north over empty space, and hangs the earth on nothing.
18_26_7.CSB17: He stretches the northern skies over empty space; he hangs the earth on nothing.
18_26_7.AMP: "It is He who spreads out the north over emptiness And hangs the earth on nothing.
18_26_7.MSG: He spreads the skies over unformed space,hangs the earth out in empty space.
18_26_7.NIV: He spreads out the northern skies over empty space;he suspends the earth over nothing.
18_26_7.NRSVCE: He stretches out Zaphon over the void, and hangs the earth upon nothing.

18_26_8.NKJV: He binds up the water in His thick clouds, Yet the clouds are not broken under it.
18_26_8.KJV: He bindeth up the waters in his thick clouds; and the cloud is not rent under them.
18_26_8.ESV: He binds up the waters in his thick clouds, and the cloud is not split open under them.
18_26_8.NASB: 'He wraps up the waters in His clouds, And the cloud does not burst under them.
18_26_8.NLT: He wraps the rain in his thick clouds,and the clouds don’t burst with the weight.
18_26_8.WEB: He binds up the waters in his thick clouds, and the cloud is not burst under them.
18_26_8.CSB17: He wraps up the water in his clouds, yet the clouds do not burst beneath its weight.
18_26_8.AMP: "He wraps the waters in His clouds [which otherwise would spill on earth all at once], And the cloud does not burst under them.
18_26_8.MSG: He pours water into cumulus cloud-bagsand the bags don't burst.
18_26_8.NIV: He wraps up the waters in his clouds,yet the clouds do not burst under their weight.
18_26_8.NRSVCE: He binds up the waters in his thick clouds, and the cloud is not torn open by them.

18_26_9.NKJV: He covers the face of His throne, And spreads His cloud over it.
18_26_9.KJV: He holdeth back the face of his throne, and spreadeth his cloud upon it.
18_26_9.ESV: He covers the face of the full moon and spreads over it his cloud.
18_26_9.NASB: 'He obscures the face of the full moon And spreads His cloud over it.
18_26_9.NLT: He covers the face of the moon,shrouding it with his clouds.
18_26_9.WEB: He encloses the face of his throne, and spreads his cloud on it.
18_26_9.CSB17: He obscures the view of his throne, spreading his cloud over it.
18_26_9.AMP: "He covers the face of the full moon And spreads His cloud over it.
18_26_9.MSG: He makes the moon wax and wane,putting it through its phases.
18_26_9.NIV: He covers the face of the full moon,spreading his clouds over it.
18_26_9.NRSVCE: He covers the face of the full moon, and spreads over it his cloud.

18_26_10.NKJV: He drew a circular horizon on the face of the waters, At the boundary of light and darkness.
18_26_10.KJV: He hath compassed the waters with bounds, until the day and night come to an end. until: Heb. until the end of light with darkness
18_26_10.ESV: He has inscribed a circle on the face of the waters at the boundary between light and darkness.
18_26_10.NASB: 'He has inscribed a circle on the surface of the waters At the boundary of light and darkness.
18_26_10.NLT: He created the horizon when he separated the waters;he set the boundary between day and night.
18_26_10.WEB: He has described a boundary on the surface of the waters, and to the confines of light and darkness.
18_26_10.CSB17: He laid out the horizon on the surface of the waters at the boundary between light and darkness.
18_26_10.AMP: "He has inscribed a circular limit (the horizon) on the face of the waters At the boundary between light and darkness.
18_26_10.MSG: He draws the horizon out over the ocean,sets a boundary between light and darkness.
18_26_10.NIV: He marks out the horizon on the face of the watersfor a boundary between light and darkness.
18_26_10.NRSVCE: He has described a circle on the face of the waters, at the boundary between light and darkness.

18_26_11.NKJV: The pillars of heaven tremble, And are astonished at His rebuke.
18_26_11.KJV: The pillars of heaven tremble and are astonished at his reproof.
18_26_11.ESV: The pillars of heaven tremble and are astounded at his rebuke.
18_26_11.NASB: 'The pillars of heaven tremble And are amazed at His rebuke.
18_26_11.NLT: The foundations of heaven tremble;they shudder at his rebuke.
18_26_11.WEB: The pillars of heaven tremble and are astonished at his rebuke.
18_26_11.CSB17: The pillars that hold up the sky tremble, astounded at his rebuke.
18_26_11.AMP: "The pillars of the heavens tremble And are terrified at His rebuke.
18_26_11.MSG: Thunder crashes and rumbles in the skies.Listen! It's God raising his voice!
18_26_11.NIV: The pillars of the heavens quake,aghast at his rebuke.
18_26_11.NRSVCE: The pillars of heaven tremble, and are astounded at his rebuke.

18_26_12.NKJV: He stirs up the sea with His power, And by His understanding He breaks up the storm.
18_26_12.KJV: He divideth the sea with his power, and by his understanding he smiteth through the proud. the proud: Heb. pride
18_26_12.ESV: By his power he stilled the sea; by his understanding he shattered Rahab.
18_26_12.NASB: 'He quieted the sea with His power, And by His understanding He shattered Rahab.
18_26_12.NLT: By his power the sea grew calm.By his skill he crushed the great sea monster.
18_26_12.WEB: He stirs up the sea with his power, and by his understanding he strikes through Rahab.
18_26_12.CSB17: By his power he stirred the sea, and by his understanding he crushed Rahab.
18_26_12.AMP: "He stirred up the sea by His power, And by His understanding He smashed [proud] Rahab.
18_26_12.MSG: By his power he stills sea storms,by his wisdom he tames sea monsters.
18_26_12.NIV: By his power he churned up the sea;by his wisdom he cut Rahab to pieces.
18_26_12.NRSVCE: By his power he stilled the Sea; by his understanding he struck down Rahab.

18_26_13.NKJV: By His Spirit He adorned the heavens; His hand pierced the fleeing serpent.
18_26_13.KJV: By his spirit he hath garnished the heavens; his hand hath formed the crooked serpent.
18_26_13.ESV: By his wind the heavens were made fair; his hand pierced the fleeing serpent.
18_26_13.NASB: 'By His breath the heavens are cleared; His hand has pierced the fleeing serpent.
18_26_13.NLT: His Spirit made the heavens beautiful,and his power pierced the gliding serpent.
18_26_13.WEB: By his Spirit the heavens are garnished. His hand has pierced the swift serpent.
18_26_13.CSB17: By his breath the heavens gained their beauty; his hand pierced the fleeing serpent.
18_26_13.AMP: "By His breath the heavens are cleared; His hand has pierced the [swiftly] fleeing serpent.
18_26_13.MSG: With one breath he clears the sky,with one finger he crushes the sea serpent.
18_26_13.NIV: By his breath the skies became fair;his hand pierced the gliding serpent.
18_26_13.NRSVCE: By his wind the heavens were made fair; his hand pierced the fleeing serpent.

18_26_14.NKJV: Indeed these are the mere edges of His ways, And how small a whisper we hear of Him! But the thunder of His power who can understand?”
18_26_14.KJV: Lo, these are parts of his ways: but how little a portion is heard of him? but the thunder of his power who can understand?
18_26_14.ESV: Behold, these are but the outskirts of his ways, and how small a whisper do we hear of him! But the thunder of his power who can understand?”
18_26_14.NASB: 'Behold, these are the fringes of His ways; And how faint a word we hear of Him! But His mighty thunder, who can understand?'
18_26_14.NLT: These are just the beginning of all that he does,merely a whisper of his power.Who, then, can comprehend the thunder of his power?”
18_26_14.WEB: Behold, these are but the outskirts of his ways. How small a whisper do we hear of him! But the thunder of his power who can understand?”
18_26_14.CSB17: These are but the fringes of his ways; how faint is the word we hear of him! Who can understand his mighty thunder?
18_26_14.AMP: "Yet these are just the fringes of His ways [mere samples of His power], The faintest whisper of His voice! Who can contemplate the thunder of His [full] mighty power?"
18_26_14.MSG: And this is only the beginning,a mere whisper of his rule.Whatever would we do if he really raised his voice!"
18_26_14.NIV: And these are but the outer fringe of his works;how faint the whisper we hear of him!Who then can understand the thunder of his power?”
18_26_14.NRSVCE: These are indeed but the outskirts of his ways; and how small a whisper do we hear of him! But the thunder of his power who can understand?”

18_27_1.NKJV: Moreover Job continued his discourse, and said:
18_27_1.KJV: Moreover Job continued his parable, and said, continued: Heb. added to take up
18_27_1.ESV: And Job again took up his discourse, and said:
18_27_1.NASB: Then Job continued his discourse and said,
18_27_1.WEB: Job again took up his parable, and said,
18_27_1.AMP: Job continued his discourse and said,
18_27_1.MSG: Having waited for Zophar, Job now resumed his defense:
18_27_1.NRSVCE: Job again took up his discourse and said:

18_27_2.NKJV: “As God lives, who has taken away my justice, And the Almighty, who has made my soul bitter,
18_27_2.KJV: As God liveth, who hath taken away my judgment; and the Almighty, who hath vexed my soul; vexed: Heb. made my soul bitter
18_27_2.ESV: “As God lives, who has taken away my right, and the Almighty, who has made my soul bitter,
18_27_2.NASB: 'As God lives, who has taken away my right, And the Almighty, who has embittered my soul,
18_27_2.NLT: “I vow by the living God, who has taken away my rights,by the Almighty who has embittered my soul —
18_27_2.WEB: “As God lives, who has taken away my right, the Almighty, who has made my soul bitter
18_27_2.CSB17: As God lives, who has deprived me of justice, and the Almighty who has made me bitter,
18_27_2.AMP: "As God lives, who has taken away my right and denied me justice, And the Almighty, who has caused bitterness and grief for my soul,
18_27_2.MSG: "God-Alive! He's denied me justice!God Almighty! He's ruined my life!
18_27_2.NIV: “As surely as God lives, who has denied me justice,the Almighty, who has made me taste bitterness of soul,
18_27_2.NRSVCE: “As God lives, who has taken away my right, and the Almighty, who has made my soul bitter,

18_27_3.NKJV: As long as my breath is in me, And the breath of God in my nostrils,
18_27_3.KJV: All the while my breath is in me, and the spirit of God is in my nostrils; the spirit: that is, the breath which God gave him
18_27_3.ESV: as long as my breath is in me, and the spirit of God is in my nostrils,
18_27_3.NASB: For as long as life is in me, And the breath of God is in my nostrils,
18_27_3.NLT: As long as I live,while I have breath from God,
18_27_3.WEB: (for the length of my life is still in me, and the spirit of God is in my nostrils);
18_27_3.CSB17: as long as my breath is still in me and the breath from God remains in my nostrils,
18_27_3.AMP: As long as my life is within me, And the breath of God is [still] in my nostrils,
18_27_3.MSG: But for as long as I draw breath,and for as long as God breathes life into me,
18_27_3.NIV: as long as I have life within me,the breath of God in my nostrils,
18_27_3.NRSVCE: as long as my breath is in me and the spirit of God is in my nostrils,

18_27_4.NKJV: My lips will not speak wickedness, Nor my tongue utter deceit.
18_27_4.KJV: My lips shall not speak wickedness, nor my tongue utter deceit.
18_27_4.ESV: my lips will not speak falsehood, and my tongue will not utter deceit.
18_27_4.NASB: My lips certainly will not speak unjustly, Nor will my tongue mutter deceit.
18_27_4.NLT: my lips will speak no evil,and my tongue will speak no lies.
18_27_4.WEB: surely my lips shall not speak unrighteousness, neither shall my tongue utter deceit.
18_27_4.CSB17: my lips will not speak unjustly, and my tongue will not utter deceit.
18_27_4.AMP: My lips will not speak unjustly, Nor will my tongue utter deceit.
18_27_4.MSG: I refuse to say one word that isn't true.I refuse to confess to any charge that's false.
18_27_4.NIV: my lips will not speak wickedness,and my tongue will utter no deceit.
18_27_4.NRSVCE: my lips will not speak falsehood, and my tongue will not utter deceit.

18_27_5.NKJV: Far be it from me That I should say you are right; Till I die I will not put away my integrity from me.
18_27_5.KJV: God forbid that I should justify you: till I die I will not remove mine integrity from me.
18_27_5.ESV: Far be it from me to say that you are right; till I die I will not put away my integrity from me.
18_27_5.NASB: 'Far be it from me that I should declare you right; Till I die I will not put away my integrity from me.
18_27_5.NLT: I will never concede that you are right;I will defend my integrity until I die.
18_27_5.WEB: Far be it from me that I should justify you. Until I die I will not put away my integrity from me.
18_27_5.CSB17: I will never affirm that you are right. I will maintain my integrity until I die.
18_27_5.AMP: "Far be it from me that I should admit you are right [in your accusations against me]; Until I die, I will not remove my integrity from me.
18_27_5.MSG: There is no way I'll ever agree to your accusations.I'll not deny my integrity even if it costs me my life.
18_27_5.NIV: I will never admit you are in the right;till I die, I will not deny my integrity.
18_27_5.NRSVCE: Far be it from me to say that you are right; until I die I will not put away my integrity from me.

18_27_6.NKJV: My righteousness I hold fast, and will not let it go; My heart shall not reproach me as long as I live.
18_27_6.KJV: My righteousness I hold fast, and will not let it go: my heart shall not reproach me so long as I live. so long: Heb. from my days
18_27_6.ESV: I hold fast my righteousness and will not let it go; my heart does not reproach me for any of my days.
18_27_6.NASB: 'I hold fast my righteousness and will not let it go. My heart does not reproach any of my days.
18_27_6.NLT: I will maintain my innocence without wavering.My conscience is clear for as long as I live.
18_27_6.WEB: I hold fast to my righteousness, and will not let it go. My heart shall not reproach me so long as I live.
18_27_6.CSB17: I will cling to my righteousness and never let it go. My conscience will not accuse me as long as I live!
18_27_6.AMP: "I hold fast my uprightness and my right standing with God and I will not let them go; My heart does not reproach me for any of my days.
18_27_6.MSG: I'm holding fast to my integrity and not loosening my grip—and, believe me, I'll never regret it.
18_27_6.NIV: I will maintain my righteousness and never let go of it;my conscience will not reproach me as long as I live.
18_27_6.NRSVCE: I hold fast my righteousness, and will not let it go; my heart does not reproach me for any of my days.

18_27_7.NKJV: “May my enemy be like the wicked, And he who rises up against me like the unrighteous.
18_27_7.KJV: Let mine enemy be as the wicked, and he that riseth up against me as the unrighteous.
18_27_7.ESV: “Let my enemy be as the wicked, and let him who rises up against me be as the unrighteous.
18_27_7.NASB: 'May my enemy be as the wicked And my opponent as the unjust.
18_27_7.NLT: “May my enemy be punished like the wicked,my adversary like those who do evil.
18_27_7.WEB: “Let my enemy be as the wicked. Let him who rises up against me be as the unrighteous.
18_27_7.CSB17: May my enemy be like the wicked and my opponent like the unjust.
18_27_7.AMP: "May my enemy be as the wicked, And he who rises up against me be as the unrighteous (unjust).
18_27_7.MSG: "Let my enemy be exposed as wicked!Let my adversary be proven guilty!
18_27_7.NIV: “May my enemies be like the wicked,my adversaries like the unjust!
18_27_7.NRSVCE: “May my enemy be like the wicked, and may my opponent be like the unrighteous.

18_27_8.NKJV: For what is the hope of the hypocrite, Though he may gain much, If God takes away his life?
18_27_8.KJV: For what is the hope of the hypocrite, though he hath gained, when God taketh away his soul?
18_27_8.ESV: For what is the hope of the godless when God cuts him off, when God takes away his life?
18_27_8.NASB: 'For what is the hope of the godless when he is cut off, When God requires his life?
18_27_8.NLT: For what hope do the godless have when God cuts them offand takes away their life?
18_27_8.WEB: For what is the hope of the godless, when he is cut off, when God takes away his life?
18_27_8.CSB17: For what hope does the godless person have when he is cut off, when God takes away his life?
18_27_8.AMP: "For what is the hope of the godless, even though he has gained [in this world], When God takes his life?
18_27_8.MSG: What hope do people without God have when life is cut short?when God puts an end to life?
18_27_8.NIV: For what hope has the godless when he is cut off,when God takes away his life?
18_27_8.NRSVCE: For what is the hope of the godless when God cuts them off, when God takes away their lives?

18_27_9.NKJV: Will God hear his cry When trouble comes upon him?
18_27_9.KJV: Will God hear his cry when trouble cometh upon him?
18_27_9.ESV: Will God hear his cry when distress comes upon him?
18_27_9.NASB: 'Will God hear his cry When distress comes upon him?
18_27_9.NLT: Will God listen to their crywhen trouble comes upon them?
18_27_9.WEB: Will God hear his cry when trouble comes on him?
18_27_9.CSB17: Will God hear his cry when distress comes on him?
18_27_9.AMP: "Will God hear his cry When trouble and distress come upon him?
18_27_9.MSG: Do you think God will listen to their cry for helpwhen disaster hits?
18_27_9.NIV: Does God listen to his crywhen distress comes upon him?
18_27_9.NRSVCE: Will God hear their cry when trouble comes upon them?

18_27_10.NKJV: Will he delight himself in the Almighty? Will he always call on God?
18_27_10.KJV: Will he delight himself in the Almighty? will he always call upon God?
18_27_10.ESV: Will he take delight in the Almighty? Will he call upon God at all times?
18_27_10.NASB: 'Will he take delight in the Almighty? Will he call on God at all times?
18_27_10.NLT: Can they take delight in the Almighty?Can they call to God at any time?
18_27_10.WEB: Will he delight himself in the Almighty, and call on God at all times?
18_27_10.CSB17: Will he delight in the Almighty? Will he call on God at all times?
18_27_10.AMP: "Will he take delight in the Almighty? Will he call on God at all times?
18_27_10.MSG: What interest have they ever shown in the Almighty?Have they ever been known to pray before?
18_27_10.NIV: Will he find delight in the Almighty?Will he call upon God at all times?
18_27_10.NRSVCE: Will they take delight in the Almighty? Will they call upon God at all times?

18_27_11.NKJV: “I will teach you about the hand of God; What is with the Almighty I will not conceal.
18_27_11.KJV: I will teach you by the hand of God: that which is with the Almighty will I not conceal. by: or, being in the hand, etc
18_27_11.ESV: I will teach you concerning the hand of God; what is with the Almighty I will not conceal.
18_27_11.NASB: 'I will instruct you in the power of God; What is with the Almighty I will not conceal.
18_27_11.NLT: I will teach you about God’s power.I will not conceal anything concerning the Almighty.
18_27_11.WEB: I will teach you about the hand of God. That which is with the Almighty will I not conceal.
18_27_11.CSB17: I will teach you about God’s power. I will not conceal what the Almighty has planned.
18_27_11.AMP: "I will teach you regarding the hand (power) of God; I will not conceal what is with the Almighty [God's actual treatment of the wicked].
18_27_11.MSG: "I've given you a clear account of God in action,suppressed nothing regarding God Almighty.
18_27_11.NIV: “I will teach you about the power of God;the ways of the Almighty I will not conceal.
18_27_11.NRSVCE: I will teach you concerning the hand of God; that which is with the Almighty I will not conceal.

18_27_12.NKJV: Surely all of you have seen it; Why then do you behave with complete nonsense?
18_27_12.KJV: Behold, all ye yourselves have seen it; why then are ye thus altogether vain?
18_27_12.ESV: Behold, all of you have seen it yourselves; why then have you become altogether vain?
18_27_12.NASB: 'Behold, all of you have seen [it]; Why then do you act foolishly?
18_27_12.NLT: But you have seen all this,yet you say all these useless things to me.
18_27_12.WEB: Behold, all of you have seen it yourselves; why then have you become altogether vain?
18_27_12.CSB17: All of you have seen this for yourselves, why do you keep up this empty talk?
18_27_12.AMP: "Behold, all of you have seen it; Why then do you act vainly and foolishly [cherishing worthless concepts]?
18_27_12.MSG: The evidence is right before you. You can all see it for yourselves,so why do you keep talking nonsense?
18_27_12.NIV: You have all seen this yourselves.Why then this meaningless talk?
18_27_12.NRSVCE: All of you have seen it yourselves; why then have you become altogether vain?

18_27_13.NKJV: “This is the portion of a wicked man with God, And the heritage of oppressors, received from the Almighty:
18_27_13.KJV: This is the portion of a wicked man with God, and the heritage of oppressors, which they shall receive of the Almighty.
18_27_13.ESV: “This is the portion of a wicked man with God, and the heritage that oppressors receive from the Almighty:
18_27_13.NASB: 'This is the portion of a wicked man from God, And the inheritance [which] tyrants receive from the Almighty.
18_27_13.NLT: “This is what the wicked will receive from God;this is their inheritance from the Almighty.
18_27_13.WEB: “This is the portion of a wicked man with God, the heritage of oppressors, which they receive from the Almighty.
18_27_13.CSB17: This is a wicked man’s lot from God, the inheritance the ruthless receive from the Almighty.
18_27_13.AMP: "This [which I am about to explain] is the portion of a wicked man from God, And the inheritance which tyrants and oppressors receive from the Almighty:
18_27_13.MSG: "I'll quote your own words back to you:" 'This is how God treats the wicked,this is what evil people can expect from God Almighty:
18_27_13.NIV: “Here is the fate God allots to the wicked,the heritage a ruthless man receives from the Almighty:
18_27_13.NRSVCE: “This is the portion of the wicked with God, and the heritage that oppressors receive from the Almighty:

18_27_14.NKJV: If his children are multiplied, it is for the sword; And his offspring shall not be satisfied with bread.
18_27_14.KJV: If his children be multiplied, it is for the sword: and his offspring shall not be satisfied with bread.
18_27_14.ESV: If his children are multiplied, it is for the sword, and his descendants have not enough bread.
18_27_14.NASB: 'Though his sons are many, they are destined for the sword; And his descendants will not be satisfied with bread.
18_27_14.NLT: They may have many children,but the children will die in war or starve to death.
18_27_14.WEB: If his children are multiplied, it is for the sword. His offspring shall not be satisfied with bread.
18_27_14.CSB17: Even if his children increase, they are destined for the sword; his descendants will never have enough food.
18_27_14.AMP: "Though his children are many, they are destined for the sword; And his descendants will not have sufficient bread.
18_27_14.MSG: Their children—all of them—will die violent deaths;they'll never have enough bread to put on the table.
18_27_14.NIV: However many his children, their fate is the sword;his offspring will never have enough to eat.
18_27_14.NRSVCE: If their children are multiplied, it is for the sword; and their offspring have not enough to eat.

18_27_15.NKJV: Those who survive him shall be buried in death, And their widows shall not weep,
18_27_15.KJV: Those that remain of him shall be buried in death: and his widows shall not weep.
18_27_15.ESV: Those who survive him the pestilence buries, and his widows do not weep.
18_27_15.NASB: 'His survivors will be buried because of the plague, And their widows will not be able to weep.
18_27_15.NLT: Those who survive will die of a plague,and not even their widows will mourn them.
18_27_15.WEB: Those who remain of him shall be buried in death. His widows shall make no lamentation.
18_27_15.CSB17: Those who survive him will be buried by the plague, yet their widows will not weep for them.
18_27_15.AMP: "Those who survive him will be buried because of the plague, And [their] widows will not be able to weep.
18_27_15.MSG: They'll be wiped out by the plague,and none of the widows will shed a tear when they're gone.
18_27_15.NIV: The plague will bury those who survive him,and their widows will not weep for them.
18_27_15.NRSVCE: Those who survive them the pestilence buries, and their widows make no lamentation.

18_27_16.NKJV: Though he heaps up silver like dust, And piles up clothing like clay—
18_27_16.KJV: Though he heap up silver as the dust, and prepare raiment as the clay;
18_27_16.ESV: Though he heap up silver like dust, and pile up clothing like clay,
18_27_16.NASB: 'Though he piles up silver like dust And prepares garments as [plentiful as] the clay,
18_27_16.NLT: “Evil people may have piles of moneyand may store away mounds of clothing.
18_27_16.WEB: Though he heap up silver as the dust, and prepare clothing as the clay;
18_27_16.CSB17: Though he piles up silver like dust and heaps up fine clothing like clay —
18_27_16.AMP: "Though he heaps up silver like dust And piles up clothing like clay,
18_27_16.MSG: Even if they make a lot of moneyand are resplendent in the latest fashions,
18_27_16.NIV: Though he heaps up silver like dustand clothes like piles of clay,
18_27_16.NRSVCE: Though they heap up silver like dust, and pile up clothing like clay —

18_27_17.NKJV: He may pile it up, but the just will wear it, And the innocent will divide the silver.
18_27_17.KJV: He may prepare it, but the just shall put it on, and the innocent shall divide the silver.
18_27_17.ESV: he may pile it up, but the righteous will wear it, and the innocent will divide the silver.
18_27_17.NASB: He may prepare [it], but the just will wear [it] And the innocent will divide the silver.
18_27_17.NLT: But the righteous will wear that clothing,and the innocent will divide that money.
18_27_17.WEB: he may prepare it, but the just shall put it on, and the innocent shall divide the silver.
18_27_17.CSB17: he may heap it up, but the righteous will wear it, and the innocent will divide up his silver.
18_27_17.AMP: He may prepare it, but the just will wear it And the innocent will divide the silver.
18_27_17.MSG: It's the good who will end up wearing the clothesand the decent who will divide up the money.
18_27_17.NIV: what he lays up the righteous will wear,and the innocent will divide his silver.
18_27_17.NRSVCE: they may pile it up, but the just will wear it, and the innocent will divide the silver.

18_27_18.NKJV: He builds his house like a moth, Like a booth which a watchman makes.
18_27_18.KJV: He buildeth his house as a moth, and as a booth that the keeper maketh.
18_27_18.ESV: He builds his house like a moth's, like a booth that a watchman makes.
18_27_18.NASB: 'He has built his house like the spider's web, Or as a hut [which] the watchman has made.
18_27_18.NLT: The wicked build houses as fragile as a spider’s web,as flimsy as a shelter made of branches.
18_27_18.WEB: He builds his house as the moth, as a booth which the watchman makes.
18_27_18.CSB17: The house he built is like a moth’s cocoon or a shelter set up by a watchman.
18_27_18.AMP: "He builds his house like a spider's web, Like a (temporary) hut which a watchman makes.
18_27_18.MSG: They build elaborate housesthat won't survive a single winter.
18_27_18.NIV: The house he builds is like a moth’s cocoon,like a hut made by a watchman.
18_27_18.NRSVCE: They build their houses like nests, like booths made by sentinels of the vineyard.

18_27_19.NKJV: The rich man will lie down, But not be gathered up; He opens his eyes, And he is no more.
18_27_19.KJV: The rich man shall lie down, but he shall not be gathered: he openeth his eyes, and he is not.
18_27_19.ESV: He goes to bed rich, but will do so no more; he opens his eyes, and his wealth is gone.
18_27_19.NASB: 'He lies down rich, but never again; He opens his eyes, and it is no longer.
18_27_19.NLT: The wicked go to bed richbut wake to find that all their wealth is gone.
18_27_19.WEB: He lies down rich, but he shall not do so again. He opens his eyes, and he is not.
18_27_19.CSB17: He lies down wealthy, but will do so no more; when he opens his eyes, it is gone.
18_27_19.AMP: "He lies down rich, but never will again; He opens his eyes, and it is gone.
18_27_19.MSG: They go to bed wealthyand wake up poor.
18_27_19.NIV: He lies down wealthy, but will do so no more;when he opens his eyes, all is gone.
18_27_19.NRSVCE: They go to bed with wealth, but will do so no more; they open their eyes, and it is gone.

18_27_20.NKJV: Terrors overtake him like a flood; A tempest steals him away in the night.
18_27_20.KJV: Terrors take hold on him as waters, a tempest stealeth him away in the night.
18_27_20.ESV: Terrors overtake him like a flood; in the night a whirlwind carries him off.
18_27_20.NASB: 'Terrors overtake him like a flood; A tempest steals him away in the night.
18_27_20.NLT: Terror overwhelms them like a flood,and they are blown away in the storms of the night.
18_27_20.WEB: Terrors overtake him like waters. A storm steals him away in the night.
18_27_20.CSB17: Terrors overtake him like a flood; a storm wind sweeps him away at night.
18_27_20.AMP: "Terrors overtake him like a [suddenly rising] flood; A windstorm steals him away in the night.
18_27_20.MSG: Terrors pour in on them like flash floods—a tornado snatches them away in the middle of the night,
18_27_20.NIV: Terrors overtake him like a flood;a tempest snatches him away in the night.
18_27_20.NRSVCE: Terrors overtake them like a flood; in the night a whirlwind carries them off.

18_27_21.NKJV: The east wind carries him away, and he is gone; It sweeps him out of his place.
18_27_21.KJV: The east wind carrieth him away, and he departeth: and as a storm hurleth him out of his place.
18_27_21.ESV: The east wind lifts him up and he is gone; it sweeps him out of his place.
18_27_21.NASB: 'The east wind carries him away, and he is gone, For it whirls him away from his place.
18_27_21.NLT: The east wind carries them away, and they are gone.It sweeps them away.
18_27_21.WEB: The east wind carries him away, and he departs. It sweeps him out of his place.
18_27_21.CSB17: An east wind picks him up, and he is gone; it carries him away from his place.
18_27_21.AMP: "The east wind lifts him up, and he is gone; It sweeps him out of his place.
18_27_21.MSG: A cyclone sweeps them up—gone!Not a trace of them left, not even a footprint.
18_27_21.NIV: The east wind carries him off, and he is gone;it sweeps him out of his place.
18_27_21.NRSVCE: The east wind lifts them up and they are gone; it sweeps them out of their place.

18_27_22.NKJV: It hurls against him and does not spare; He flees desperately from its power.
18_27_22.KJV: For God shall cast upon him, and not spare: he would fain flee out of his hand. he: Heb. in fleeing he would flee
18_27_22.ESV: It hurls at him without pity; he flees from its power in headlong flight.
18_27_22.NASB: 'For it will hurl at him without sparing; He will surely try to flee from its power.
18_27_22.NLT: It whirls down on them without mercy.They struggle to flee from its power.
18_27_22.WEB: For it hurls at him, and does not spare, as he flees away from his hand.
18_27_22.CSB17: It blasts at him without mercy, while he flees desperately from its force.
18_27_22.AMP: "For it will hurl [thunderbolts of God's wrath] at him unsparingly and without compassion; He flees in haste from its power.
18_27_22.MSG: Catastrophes relentlessly pursue them;they run this way and that, but there's no place to hide—
18_27_22.NIV: It hurls itself against him without mercyas he flees headlong from its power.
18_27_22.NRSVCE: It hurls at them without pity; they flee from its power in headlong flight.

18_27_23.NKJV: Men shall clap their hands at him, And shall hiss him out of his place.
18_27_23.KJV: Men shall clap their hands at him, and shall hiss him out of his place.
18_27_23.ESV: It claps its hands at him and hisses at him from its place.
18_27_23.NASB: '[Men] will clap their hands at him And will hiss him from his place.
18_27_23.NLT: But everyone jeers at themand mocks them.
18_27_23.WEB: Men shall clap their hands at him, and shall hiss him out of his place.
18_27_23.CSB17: It claps its hands at him and scoffs at him from its place.
18_27_23.AMP: "People will clap their hands at him [to mock and ridicule him] And hiss him out of his place.
18_27_23.MSG: Pummeled by the weather,blown to kingdom come by the storm.'
18_27_23.NIV: It claps its hands in derisionand hisses him out of his place.
18_27_23.NRSVCE: It claps its hands at them, and hisses at them from its place.

18_28_1.NKJV: “Surely there is a mine for silver, And a place where gold is refined.
18_28_1.KJV: Surely there is a vein for the silver, and a place for gold where they fine it. vein: or, mine
18_28_1.ESV: “Surely there is a mine for silver, and a place for gold that they refine.
18_28_1.NASB: 'Surely there is a mine for silver And a place where they refine gold.
18_28_1.NLT: “People know where to mine silverand how to refine gold.
18_28_1.WEB: “Surely there is a mine for silver, and a place for gold which they refine.
18_28_1.CSB17: Surely there is a mine for silver and a place where gold is refined.
18_28_1.AMP: "Surely there is a mine for silver, And a place where they refine gold.
18_28_1.MSG: "We all know how silver seams the rocks,we've seen the stuff from which gold is refined,
18_28_1.NIV: “There is a mine for silverand a place where gold is refined.
18_28_1.NRSVCE: “Surely there is a mine for silver, and a place for gold to be refined.

18_28_2.NKJV: Iron is taken from the earth, And copper is smelted from ore.
18_28_2.KJV: Iron is taken out of the earth, and brass is molten out of the stone. earth: or, dust
18_28_2.ESV: Iron is taken out of the earth, and copper is smelted from the ore.
18_28_2.NASB: 'Iron is taken from the dust, And copper is smelted from rock.
18_28_2.NLT: They know where to dig iron from the earthand how to smelt copper from rock.
18_28_2.WEB: Iron is taken out of the earth, and copper is smelted out of the ore.
18_28_2.CSB17: Iron is taken from the ground, and copper is smelted from ore.
18_28_2.AMP: "Iron is taken out of the earth, And copper is smelted from the stone ore.
18_28_2.MSG: We're aware of how iron is dug out of the groundand copper is smelted from rock.
18_28_2.NIV: Iron is taken from the earth,and copper is smelted from ore.
18_28_2.NRSVCE: Iron is taken out of the earth, and copper is smelted from ore.

18_28_3.NKJV: Man puts an end to darkness, And searches every recess For ore in the darkness and the shadow of death.
18_28_3.KJV: He setteth an end to darkness, and searcheth out all perfection: the stones of darkness, and the shadow of death.
18_28_3.ESV: Man puts an end to darkness and searches out to the farthest limit the ore in gloom and deep darkness.
18_28_3.NASB: '[Man] puts an end to darkness, And to the farthest limit he searches out The rock in gloom and deep shadow.
18_28_3.NLT: They know how to shine light in the darknessand explore the farthest regions of the earthas they search in the dark for ore.
18_28_3.WEB: Man sets an end to darkness, and searches out, to the furthest bound, the stones of obscurity and of thick darkness.
18_28_3.CSB17: A miner puts an end to the darkness; he probes the deepest recesses for ore in the gloomy darkness.
18_28_3.AMP: "Man puts an end to darkness [by bringing in a light], And to the farthest bounds he searches out The rock buried in gloom and deep shadow.
18_28_3.MSG: Miners penetrate the earth's darkness,searching the roots of the mountains for ore,digging away in the suffocating darkness.
18_28_3.NIV: Man puts an end to the darkness;he searches the farthest recessesfor ore in the blackest darkness.
18_28_3.NRSVCE: Miners put an end to darkness, and search out to the farthest bound the ore in gloom and deep darkness.

18_28_4.NKJV: He breaks open a shaft away from people; In places forgotten by feet They hang far away from men; They swing to and fro.
18_28_4.KJV: The flood breaketh out from the inhabitant; even the waters forgotten of the foot: they are dried up, they are gone away from men.
18_28_4.ESV: He opens shafts in a valley away from where anyone lives; they are forgotten by travelers; they hang in the air, far away from mankind; they swing to and fro.
18_28_4.NASB: 'He sinks a shaft far from habitation, Forgotten by the foot; They hang and swing to and fro far from men.
18_28_4.NLT: They sink a mine shaft into the earthfar from where anyone lives.They descend on ropes, swinging back and forth.
18_28_4.WEB: He breaks open a shaft away from where people live. They are forgotten by the foot. They hang far from men, they swing back and forth.
18_28_4.CSB17: He cuts a shaft far from human habitation, in places unknown to those who walk above ground. Suspended far away from people, the miners swing back and forth.
18_28_4.AMP: "He breaks open (mine) shafts far away from where people live, [In places] forgotten by the [human] foot; They dangle [in the mines] and hang away from men.
18_28_4.MSG: Far from civilization, far from the traffic,they cut a shaft,and are lowered into it by ropes.
18_28_4.NIV: Far from where people dwell he cuts a shaft,in places forgotten by the foot of man;far from men he dangles and sways.
18_28_4.NRSVCE: They open shafts in a valley away from human habitation; they are forgotten by travelers, they sway suspended, remote from people.

18_28_5.NKJV: As for the earth, from it comes bread, But underneath it is turned up as by fire;
18_28_5.KJV: As for the earth, out of it cometh bread: and under it is turned up as it were fire.
18_28_5.ESV: As for the earth, out of it comes bread, but underneath it is turned up as by fire.
18_28_5.NASB: 'The earth, from it comes food, And underneath it is turned up as fire.
18_28_5.NLT: Food is grown on the earth above,but down below, the earth is melted as by fire.
18_28_5.WEB: As for the earth, out of it comes bread; Underneath it is turned up as it were by fire.
18_28_5.CSB17: Food may come from the earth, but below the surface the earth is transformed as by fire.
18_28_5.AMP: "[As for] the earth, out of it comes food, But underneath [its surface, down deep] it is turned over as fire.
