18_30_1.ESV: “But now they laugh at me, men who are younger than I, whose fathers I would have disdained to set with the dogs of my flock.
18_30_1.NASB: 'But now those younger than I mock me, Whose fathers I disdained to put with the dogs of my flock.
18_30_1.NLT: “But now I am mocked by people younger than I,by young men whose fathers are not worthy to run with my sheepdogs.
18_30_1.WEB: “But now those who are younger than I have me in derision, whose fathers I would have disdained to put with my sheep dogs.
18_30_1.CSB17: But now they mock me, men younger than I am, whose fathers I would have refused to put with my sheep dogs.
18_30_1.AMP: "But now those younger than I mock and laugh at me, Whose fathers I refused to put with the sheepdogs of my flock.
18_30_1.MSG: "But no longer. Now I'm the butt of their jokes—Young ruffians! whippersnappers!
18_30_1.NIV: “But now they mock me,men younger than I,whose fathers I would have disdainedto put with my sheep dogs.
18_30_1.NRSVCE: “But now they make sport of me, those who are younger than I, whose fathers I would have disdained to set with the dogs of my flock.

18_30_2.NKJV: Indeed, what profit is the strength of their hands to me? Their vigor has perished.
18_30_2.KJV: Yea, whereto might the strength of their hands profit me, in whom old age was perished?
18_30_2.ESV: What could I gain from the strength of their hands, men whose vigor is gone?
18_30_2.NASB: 'Indeed, what [good was] the strength of their hands to me? Vigor had perished from them.
18_30_2.NLT: A lot of good they are to me —those worn-out wretches!
18_30_2.WEB: Of what use is the strength of their hands to me, men in whom ripe age has perished?
18_30_2.CSB17: What use to me was the strength of their hands? Their vigor had left them.
18_30_2.AMP: "Indeed, how could the strength of their hands profit me? Vigor had perished from them.
18_30_2.MSG: Why, I considered their fathersmere inexperienced pups.But they are worse than dogs—good for nothing,stray, mangy animals,
18_30_2.NIV: Of what use was the strength of their hands to me,since their vigor had gone from them?
18_30_2.NRSVCE: What could I gain from the strength of their hands? All their vigor is gone.

18_30_3.NKJV: They are gaunt from want and famine, Fleeing late to the wilderness, desolate and waste,
18_30_3.KJV: For want and famine they were solitary; fleeing into the wilderness in former time desolate and waste. solitary: or, dark as the night in: Heb. yesternight
18_30_3.ESV: Through want and hard hunger they gnaw the dry ground by night in waste and desolation;
18_30_3.NASB: 'From want and famine they are gaunt Who gnaw the dry ground by night in waste and desolation,
18_30_3.NLT: They are gaunt from poverty and hunger.They claw the dry ground in desolate wastelands.
18_30_3.WEB: They are gaunt from lack and famine. They gnaw the dry ground, in the gloom of waste and desolation.
18_30_3.CSB17: Emaciated from poverty and hunger, they gnawed the dry land, the desolate wasteland by night.
18_30_3.AMP: "They are gaunt with want and famine; They gnaw the dry and barren ground by night in [the gloom of] waste and desolation.
18_30_3.MSG: Half-starved, scavenging the back alleys,howling at the moon;
18_30_3.NIV: Haggard from want and hunger,they roamed the parched landin desolate wastelands at night.
18_30_3.NRSVCE: Through want and hard hunger they gnaw the dry and desolate ground,

18_30_4.NKJV: Who pluck mallow by the bushes, And broom tree roots for their food.
18_30_4.KJV: Who cut up mallows by the bushes, and juniper roots for their meat.
18_30_4.ESV: they pick saltwort and the leaves of bushes, and the roots of the broom tree for their food.
18_30_4.NASB: Who pluck mallow by the bushes, And whose food is the root of the broom shrub.
18_30_4.NLT: They pluck wild greens from among the bushesand eat from the roots of broom trees.
18_30_4.WEB: They pluck salt herbs by the bushes. The roots of the broom are their food.
18_30_4.CSB17: They plucked mallow among the shrubs, and the roots of the broom tree were their food.
18_30_4.AMP: "They pluck [and eat] saltwort (mallows) among the bushes, And their food is the root of the broom shrub.
18_30_4.MSG: Homeless guttersnipeschewing on old bones and licking old tin cans;
18_30_4.NIV: In the brush they gathered salt herbs,and their food was the root of the broom tree.
18_30_4.NRSVCE: they pick mallow and the leaves of bushes, and to warm themselves the roots of broom.

18_30_5.NKJV: They were driven out from among men, They shouted at them as at a thief.
18_30_5.KJV: They were driven forth from among men, (they cried after them as after a thief;)
18_30_5.ESV: They are driven out from human company; they shout after them as after a thief.
18_30_5.NASB: 'They are driven from the community; They shout against them as [against] a thief,
18_30_5.NLT: They are driven from human society,and people shout at them as if they were thieves.
18_30_5.WEB: They are driven out from among men. They cry after them as after a thief;
18_30_5.CSB17: They were banished from human society; people shouted at them as if they were thieves.
18_30_5.AMP: "They are driven from the community; They shout after them as after a thief.
18_30_5.MSG: Outcasts from the community,cursed as dangerous delinquents.
18_30_5.NIV: They were banished from their fellow men,shouted at as if they were thieves.
18_30_5.NRSVCE: They are driven out from society; people shout after them as after a thief.

18_30_6.NKJV: They had to live in the clefts of the valleys, In caves of the earth and the rocks.
18_30_6.KJV: To dwell in the clifts of the valleys, in caves of the earth, and in the rocks. caves: Heb. holes
18_30_6.ESV: In the gullies of the torrents they must dwell, in holes of the earth and of the rocks.
18_30_6.NASB: So that they dwell in dreadful valleys, In holes of the earth and of the rocks.
18_30_6.NLT: So now they live in frightening ravines,in caves and among the rocks.
18_30_6.WEB: So that they dwell in frightful valleys, and in holes of the earth and of the rocks.
18_30_6.CSB17: They are living on the slopes of the wadis, among the rocks and in holes in the ground.
18_30_6.AMP: "They must dwell on the slopes of wadis And in holes in the ground and in rocks.
18_30_6.MSG: Nobody would put up with them;they were driven from the neighborhood.
18_30_6.NIV: They were forced to live in the dry stream beds,among the rocks and in holes in the ground.
18_30_6.NRSVCE: In the gullies of wadis they must live, in holes in the ground, and in the rocks.

18_30_7.NKJV: Among the bushes they brayed, Under the nettles they nestled.
18_30_7.KJV: Among the bushes they brayed; under the nettles they were gathered together.
18_30_7.ESV: Among the bushes they bray; under the nettles they huddle together.
18_30_7.NASB: 'Among the bushes they cry out; Under the nettles they are gathered together.
18_30_7.NLT: They sound like animals howling among the bushes,huddled together beneath the nettles.
18_30_7.WEB: Among the bushes they bray; and under the nettles they are gathered together.
18_30_7.CSB17: They bray among the shrubs; they huddle beneath the thistles.
18_30_7.AMP: "Among the bushes they cry out [like wild animals]; Beneath the prickly scrub they gather and huddle together.
18_30_7.MSG: You could hear them out there at the edge of town,yelping and barking, huddled in junkyards,
18_30_7.NIV: They brayed among the bushesand huddled in the undergrowth.
18_30_7.NRSVCE: Among the bushes they bray; under the nettles they huddle together.

18_30_8.NKJV: They were sons of fools, Yes, sons of vile men; They were scourged from the land.
18_30_8.KJV: They were children of fools, yea, children of base men: they were viler than the earth. base: Heb. men of no name
18_30_8.ESV: A senseless, a nameless brood, they have been whipped out of the land.
18_30_8.NASB: 'Fools, even those without a name, They were scourged from the land.
18_30_8.NLT: They are nameless fools,outcasts from society.
18_30_8.WEB: They are children of fools, yes, children of base men. They were flogged out of the land.
18_30_8.CSB17: Foolish men, without even a name. They were forced to leave the land.
18_30_8.AMP: "They are the sons of [worthless and nameless] fools, They have been driven out of the land.
18_30_8.MSG: A gang of beggars and no-names,thrown out on their ears.
18_30_8.NIV: A base and nameless brood,they were driven out of the land.
18_30_8.NRSVCE: A senseless, disreputable brood, they have been whipped out of the land.

18_30_9.NKJV: “And now I am their taunting song; Yes, I am their byword.
18_30_9.KJV: And now am I their song, yea, I am their byword.
18_30_9.ESV: “And now I have become their song; I am a byword to them.
18_30_9.NASB: 'And now I have become their taunt, I have even become a byword to them.
18_30_9.NLT: “And now they mock me with vulgar songs!They taunt me!
18_30_9.WEB: “Now I have become their song. Yes, I am a byword to them.
18_30_9.CSB17: Now I am mocked by their songs; I have become an object of scorn to them.
18_30_9.AMP: "And now I have become [the subject of] their taunting; Yes, I am a byword and a laughingstock to them.
18_30_9.MSG: "But now I'm the one they're after,mistreating me, taunting and mocking.
18_30_9.NIV: “And now their sons mock me in song;I have become a byword among them.
18_30_9.NRSVCE: “And now they mock me in song; I am a byword to them.

18_30_10.NKJV: They abhor me, they keep far from me; They do not hesitate to spit in my face.
18_30_10.KJV: They abhor me, they flee far from me, and spare not to spit in my face. and: Heb. and withhold not spittle from
18_30_10.ESV: They abhor me; they keep aloof from me; they do not hesitate to spit at the sight of me.
18_30_10.NASB: 'They abhor me [and] stand aloof from me, And they do not refrain from spitting at my face.
18_30_10.NLT: They despise me and won’t come near me,except to spit in my face.
18_30_10.WEB: They abhor me, they stand aloof from me, and don’t hesitate to spit in my face.
18_30_10.CSB17: They despise me and keep their distance from me; they do not hesitate to spit in my face.
18_30_10.AMP: "They hate me, they stand aloof from me, And do not refrain from spitting in my face.
18_30_10.MSG: They abhor me, they abuse me.How dare those scoundrels—they spit in my face!
18_30_10.NIV: They detest me and keep their distance;they do not hesitate to spit in my face.
18_30_10.NRSVCE: They abhor me, they keep aloof from me; they do not hesitate to spit at the sight of me.

18_30_11.NKJV: Because He has loosed my bowstring and afflicted me, They have cast off restraint before me.
18_30_11.KJV: Because he hath loosed my cord, and afflicted me, they have also let loose the bridle before me.
18_30_11.ESV: Because God has loosed my cord and humbled me, they have cast off restraint in my presence.
18_30_11.NASB: 'Because He has loosed His bowstring and afflicted me, They have cast off the bridle before me.
18_30_11.NLT: For God has cut my bowstring.He has humbled me,so they have thrown off all restraint.
18_30_11.WEB: For he has untied his cord, and afflicted me; and they have thrown off restraint before me.
18_30_11.CSB17: Because God has loosened my bowstring and oppressed me, they have cast off restraint in my presence.
18_30_11.AMP: "For God has loosed His bowstring [attacking me] and [He has] afflicted and humbled me; They have cast off the bridle [of restraint] before me.
18_30_11.MSG: Now that God has undone me and left me in a heap,they hold nothing back. Anything goes.
18_30_11.NIV: Now that God has unstrung my bow and afflicted me,they throw off restraint in my presence.
18_30_11.NRSVCE: Because God has loosed my bowstring and humbled me, they have cast off restraint in my presence.

18_30_12.NKJV: At my right hand the rabble arises; They push away my feet, And they raise against me their ways of destruction.
18_30_12.KJV: Upon my right hand rise the youth; they push away my feet, and they raise up against me the ways of their destruction.
18_30_12.ESV: On my right hand the rabble rise; they push away my feet; they cast up against me their ways of destruction.
18_30_12.NASB: 'On the right hand their brood arises; They thrust aside my feet and build up against me their ways of destruction.
18_30_12.NLT: These outcasts oppose me to my face.They send me sprawlingand lay traps in my path.
18_30_12.WEB: On my right hand rise the rabble. They thrust aside my feet, They cast up against me their ways of destruction.
18_30_12.CSB17: The rabble rise up at my right; they trap my feet and construct their siege ramp against me.
18_30_12.AMP: "On my right the [rabble] brood rises; They push my feet away, and they build up their ways of destruction against me [like an advancing army].
18_30_12.MSG: They come at me from my blind side,trip me up, then jump on me while I'm down.
18_30_12.NIV: On my right the tribe attacks;they lay snares for my feet,they build their siege ramps against me.
18_30_12.NRSVCE: On my right hand the rabble rise up; they send me sprawling, and build roads for my ruin.

18_30_13.NKJV: They break up my path, They promote my calamity; They have no helper.
18_30_13.KJV: They mar my path, they set forward my calamity , they have no helper.
18_30_13.ESV: They break up my path; they promote my calamity; they need no one to help them.
18_30_13.NASB: 'They break up my path, They profit from my destruction; No one restrains them.
18_30_13.NLT: They block my roadand do everything they can to destroy me.They know I have no one to help me.
18_30_13.WEB: They mar my path, They set forward my calamity, without anyone’s help.
18_30_13.CSB17: They tear up my path; they contribute to my destruction, without anyone to help them.
18_30_13.AMP: "They break up and clutter my path [upsetting my plans], They profit from my destruction; No one restrains them.
18_30_13.MSG: They throw every kind of obstacle in my path,determined to ruin me—and no one lifts a finger to help me!
18_30_13.NIV: They break up my road;they succeed in destroying me—without anyone’s helping them.
18_30_13.NRSVCE: They break up my path, they promote my calamity; no one restrains them.

18_30_14.NKJV: They come as broad breakers; Under the ruinous storm they roll along.
18_30_14.KJV: They came upon me as a wide breaking in of waters: in the desolation they rolled themselves upon me.
18_30_14.ESV: As through a wide breach they come; amid the crash they roll on.
18_30_14.NASB: 'As [through] a wide breach they come, Amid the tempest they roll on.
18_30_14.NLT: They come at me from all directions.They jump on me when I am down.
18_30_14.WEB: As through a wide breach they come, in the middle of the ruin they roll themselves in.
18_30_14.CSB17: They advance as through a gaping breach; they keep rolling in through the ruins.
18_30_14.AMP: "As through a wide breach they come, Amid the crash [of falling walls] they roll on [over me].
18_30_14.MSG: They violate my broken body,trample through the rubble of my ruined life.
18_30_14.NIV: They advance as through a gaping breach;amid the ruins they come rolling in.
18_30_14.NRSVCE: As through a wide breach they come; amid the crash they roll on.

18_30_15.NKJV: Terrors are turned upon me; They pursue my honor as the wind, And my prosperity has passed like a cloud.
18_30_15.KJV: Terrors are turned upon me: they pursue my soul as the wind: and my welfare passeth away as a cloud. my soul: Heb. my principal one
18_30_15.ESV: Terrors are turned upon me; my honor is pursued as by the wind, and my prosperity has passed away like a cloud.
18_30_15.NASB: 'Terrors are turned against me; They pursue my honor as the wind, And my prosperity has passed away like a cloud.
18_30_15.NLT: I live in terror now.My honor has blown away in the wind,and my prosperity has vanished like a cloud.
18_30_15.WEB: Terrors have turned on me. They chase my honor as the wind. My welfare has passed away as a cloud.
18_30_15.CSB17: Terrors are turned loose against me; they chase my dignity away like the wind, and my prosperity has passed by like a cloud.
18_30_15.AMP: "Terrors are turned upon me; They chase away my honor and reputation like the wind, And my prosperity has passed away like a cloud.
18_30_15.MSG: Terrors assault me—my dignity in shreds,salvation up in smoke.
18_30_15.NIV: Terrors overwhelm me;my dignity is driven away as by the wind,my safety vanishes like a cloud.
18_30_15.NRSVCE: Terrors are turned upon me; my honor is pursued as by the wind, and my prosperity has passed away like a cloud.

18_30_16.NKJV: “And now my soul is poured out because of my plight; The days of affliction take hold of me.
18_30_16.KJV: And now my soul is poured out upon me; the days of affliction have taken hold upon me.
18_30_16.ESV: “And now my soul is poured out within me; days of affliction have taken hold of me.
18_30_16.NASB: 'And now my soul is poured out within me; Days of affliction have seized me.
18_30_16.NLT: “And now my life seeps away.Depression haunts my days.
18_30_16.WEB: “Now my soul is poured out within me. Days of affliction have taken hold on me.
18_30_16.CSB17: Now my life is poured out before me, and days of suffering have seized me.
18_30_16.AMP: "And now my soul is poured out within me; The days of affliction have seized me.
18_30_16.MSG: "And now my life drains out,as suffering seizes and grips me hard.
18_30_16.NIV: “And now my life ebbs away;days of suffering grip me.
18_30_16.NRSVCE: “And now my soul is poured out within me; days of affliction have taken hold of me.

18_30_17.NKJV: My bones are pierced in me at night, And my gnawing pains take no rest.
18_30_17.KJV: My bones are pierced in me in the night season: and my sinews take no rest.
18_30_17.ESV: The night racks my bones, and the pain that gnaws me takes no rest.
18_30_17.NASB: 'At night it pierces my bones within me, And my gnawing [pains] take no rest.
18_30_17.NLT: At night my bones are filled with pain,which gnaws at me relentlessly.
18_30_17.WEB: In the night season my bones are pierced in me, and the pains that gnaw me take no rest.
18_30_17.CSB17: Night pierces my bones, but my gnawing pains never rest.
18_30_17.AMP: "My bones are pierced [with aching] in the night season, And the pains that gnaw me take no rest.
18_30_17.MSG: Night gnaws at my bones;the pain never lets up.
18_30_17.NIV: Night pierces my bones;my gnawing pains never rest.
18_30_17.NRSVCE: The night racks my bones, and the pain that gnaws me takes no rest.

18_30_18.NKJV: By great force my garment is disfigured; It binds me about as the collar of my coat.
18_30_18.KJV: By the great force of my disease is my garment changed: it bindeth me about as the collar of my coat.
18_30_18.ESV: With great force my garment is disfigured; it binds me about like the collar of my tunic.
18_30_18.NASB: 'By a great force my garment is distorted; It binds me about as the collar of my coat.
18_30_18.NLT: With a strong hand, God grabs my shirt.He grips me by the collar of my coat.
18_30_18.WEB: By great force is my garment disfigured. It binds me about as the collar of my coat.
18_30_18.CSB17: My clothing is distorted with great force; he chokes me by the neck of my garment.
18_30_18.AMP: "By the great force [of my disease] my garment (skin) is disfigured and blemished; It binds about me [choking me] like the collar of my coat.
18_30_18.MSG: I am tied hand and foot, my neck in a noose.I twist and turn.
18_30_18.NIV: In his great power God becomes like clothing to me;he binds me like the neck of my garment.
18_30_18.NRSVCE: With violence he seizes my garment; he grasps me by the collar of my tunic.

18_30_19.NKJV: He has cast me into the mire, And I have become like dust and ashes.
18_30_19.KJV: He hath cast me into the mire, and I am become like dust and ashes.
18_30_19.ESV: God has cast me into the mire, and I have become like dust and ashes.
18_30_19.NASB: 'He has cast me into the mire, And I have become like dust and ashes.
18_30_19.NLT: He has thrown me into the mud.I’m nothing more than dust and ashes.
18_30_19.WEB: He has cast me into the mire. I have become like dust and ashes.
18_30_19.CSB17: He throws me into the mud, and I have become like dust and ashes.
18_30_19.AMP: "God has cast me into the mire [a swampland of crisis], And I have become [worthless] like dust and ashes.
18_30_19.MSG: Thrown facedown in the muck,I'm a muddy mess, inside and out.
18_30_19.NIV: He throws me into the mud,and I am reduced to dust and ashes.
18_30_19.NRSVCE: He has cast me into the mire, and I have become like dust and ashes.

18_30_20.NKJV: “I cry out to You, but You do not answer me; I stand up, and You regard me.
18_30_20.KJV: I cry unto thee, and thou dost not hear me: I stand up, and thou regardest me not.
18_30_20.ESV: I cry to you for help and you do not answer me; I stand, and you only look at me.
18_30_20.NASB: 'I cry out to You for help, but You do not answer me; I stand up, and You turn Your attention against me.
18_30_20.NLT: “I cry to you, O God, but you don’t answer.I stand before you, but you don’t even look.
18_30_20.WEB: I cry to you, and you do not answer me. I stand up, and you gaze at me.
18_30_20.CSB17: I cry out to you for help, but you do not answer me; when I stand up, you merely look at me.
18_30_20.AMP: "I cry to You for help, [Lord,] but You do not answer me; I stand up, but You [only] gaze [indifferently] at me.
18_30_20.MSG: "I shout for help, God, and get nothing, no answer!I stand to face you in protest, and you give me a blank stare!
18_30_20.NIV: “I cry out to you, O God, but you do not answer;I stand up, but you merely look at me.
18_30_20.NRSVCE: I cry to you and you do not answer me; I stand, and you merely look at me.

18_30_21.NKJV: But You have become cruel to me; With the strength of Your hand You oppose me.
18_30_21.KJV: Thou art become cruel to me: with thy strong hand thou opposest thyself against me. become: Heb. turned to be cruel thy: Heb. the strength of thy hand
18_30_21.ESV: You have turned cruel to me; with the might of your hand you persecute me.
18_30_21.NASB: 'You have become cruel to me; With the might of Your hand You persecute me.
18_30_21.NLT: You have become cruel toward me.You use your power to persecute me.
18_30_21.WEB: You have turned to be cruel to me. With the might of your hand you persecute me.
18_30_21.CSB17: You have turned against me with cruelty; you harass me with your strong hand.
18_30_21.AMP: "You have become harsh and cruel to me; With the might of Your hand You [keep me alive only to] persecute me.
18_30_21.MSG: You've turned into my tormenter—you slap me around, knock me about.
18_30_21.NIV: You turn on me ruthlessly;with the might of your hand you attack me.
18_30_21.NRSVCE: You have turned cruel to me; with the might of your hand you persecute me.

18_30_22.NKJV: You lift me up to the wind and cause me to ride on it; You spoil my success.
18_30_22.KJV: Thou liftest me up to the wind; thou causest me to ride upon it, and dissolvest my substance . substance: or, wisdom
18_30_22.ESV: You lift me up on the wind; you make me ride on it, and you toss me about in the roar of the storm.
18_30_22.NASB: 'You lift me up to the wind [and] cause me to ride; And You dissolve me in a storm.
18_30_22.NLT: You throw me into the whirlwindand destroy me in the storm.
18_30_22.WEB: You lift me up to the wind, and drive me with it. You dissolve me in the storm.
18_30_22.CSB17: You lift me up on the wind and make me ride it; you scatter me in the storm.
18_30_22.AMP: "You lift me up on the wind and cause me to ride [upon it]; And You toss me about in the tempest and dissolve me in the storm.
18_30_22.MSG: You raised me up so I was riding highand then dropped me, and I crashed.
18_30_22.NIV: You snatch me up and drive me before the wind;you toss me about in the storm.
18_30_22.NRSVCE: You lift me up on the wind, you make me ride on it, and you toss me about in the roar of the storm.

18_30_23.NKJV: For I know that You will bring me to death, And to the house appointed for all living.
18_30_23.KJV: For I know that thou wilt bring me to death, and to the house appointed for all living.
18_30_23.ESV: For I know that you will bring me to death and to the house appointed for all living.
18_30_23.NASB: 'For I know that You will bring me to death And to the house of meeting for all living.
18_30_23.NLT: And I know you are sending me to my death —the destination of all who live.
18_30_23.WEB: For I know that you will bring me to death, To the house appointed for all living.
18_30_23.CSB17: Yes, I know that you will lead me to death — the place appointed for all who live.
18_30_23.AMP: "For I know that You will bring me to death And to the house of meeting [appointed] for all the living.
18_30_23.MSG: I know you're determined to kill me,to put me six feet under.
18_30_23.NIV: I know you will bring me down to death,to the place appointed for all the living.

18_30_24.NKJV: “Surely He would not stretch out His hand against a heap of ruins, If they cry out when He destroys it.

18_30_24.ESV: “Yet does not one in a heap of ruins stretch out his hand, and in his disaster cry for help?
18_30_24.NASB: 'Yet does not one in a heap of ruins stretch out [his] hand, Or in his disaster therefore cry out for help?
18_30_24.NLT: “Surely no one would turn against the needywhen they cry for help in their trouble.
18_30_24.WEB: “However doesn’t one stretch out a hand in his fall? Or in his calamity therefore cry for help?

18_30_24.MSG: "What did I do to deserve this?Did I ever hit anyone who was calling for help?
18_30_24.NIV: “Surely no one lays a hand on a broken manwhen he cries for help in his distress.
18_30_24.NRSVCE: “Surely one does not turn against the needy, when in disaster they cry for help.

18_30_25.NKJV: Have I not wept for him who was in trouble? Has not my soul grieved for the poor?

18_30_25.NASB: 'Have I not wept for the one whose life is hard? Was not my soul grieved for the needy?

18_30_26.NKJV: But when I looked for good, evil came to me; And when I waited for light, then came darkness.

18_30_28.NKJV: I go about mourning, but not in the sun; I stand up in the assembly and cry out for help.

18_32_1.NKJV: So these three men ceased answering Job, because he was righteous in his own eyes.
18_32_1.KJV: So these three men ceased to answer Job, because he was righteous in his own eyes. to: Heb. from answering
18_32_1.ESV: So these three men ceased to answer Job, because he was righteous in his own eyes.
18_32_1.NASB: Then these three men ceased answering Job, because he was righteous in his own eyes.
18_32_1.NLT: Job’s three friends refused to reply further to him because he kept insisting on his innocence.
18_32_1.WEB: So these three men ceased to answer Job, because he was righteous in his own eyes.
18_32_1.CSB17: So these three men quit answering Job, because he was righteous in his own eyes.
18_32_1.AMP: So these three men ceased answering Job, because he was righteous in his own eyes [and could not be persuaded otherwise by them].
18_32_1.MSG: Job's three friends now fell silent. They were talked out, stymied because Job wouldn't budge an inch—wouldn't admit to an ounce of guilt.
18_32_1.NIV: ElihuSo these three men stopped answering Job, because he was righteous in his own eyes.
18_32_1.NRSVCE: So these three men ceased to answer Job, because he was righteous in his own eyes.

18_32_2.NKJV: Then the wrath of Elihu, the son of Barachel the Buzite, of the family of Ram, was aroused against Job; his wrath was aroused because he justified himself rather than God.
18_32_2.KJV: Then was kindled the wrath of Elihu the son of Barachel the Buzite, of the kindred of Ram: against Job was his wrath kindled, because he justified himself rather than God. himself: Heb. his soul
18_32_2.ESV: Then Elihu the son of Barachel the Buzite, of the family of Ram, burned with anger. He burned with anger at Job because he justified himself rather than God.
18_32_2.NASB: But the anger of Elihu the son of Barachel the Buzite, of the family of Ram burned; against Job his anger burned because he justified himself before God.
18_32_2.NLT: Then Elihu son of Barakel the Buzite, of the clan of Ram, became angry. He was angry because Job refused to admit that he had sinned and that God was right in punishing him.
18_32_2.WEB: Then the wrath of Elihu the son of Barachel, the Buzite, of the family of Ram, was kindled against Job. His wrath was kindled because he justified himself rather than God.
18_32_2.CSB17: Then Elihu son of Barachel the Buzite from the family of Ram became angry. He was angry at Job because he had justified himself rather than God.
18_32_2.AMP: But Elihu the son of Barachel the Buzite, of the family of Ram, became indignant. His indignation was kindled and burned and he became upset with Job because he justified himself rather than God [and even expressed doubts about God's character].
18_32_2.MSG: Then Elihu lost his temper. (Elihu was the son of Barakel the Buzite from the clan of Ram.) He blazed out in anger against Job for pitting his righteousness against God's.
18_32_2.NIV: But Elihu son of Barakel the Buzite, of the family of Ram, became very angry with Job for justifying himself rather than God.
18_32_2.NRSVCE: Then Elihu son of Barachel the Buzite, of the family of Ram, became angry. He was angry at Job because he justified himself rather than God;

18_32_3.NKJV: Also against his three friends his wrath was aroused, because they had found no answer, and yet had condemned Job.
18_32_3.KJV: Also against his three friends was his wrath kindled, because they had found no answer, and yet had condemned Job.
18_32_3.ESV: He burned with anger also at Job's three friends because they had found no answer, although they had declared Job to be in the wrong.
18_32_3.NASB: And his anger burned against his three friends because they had found no answer, and yet had condemned Job.
18_32_3.NLT: He was also angry with Job’s three friends, for they made God appear to be wrong by their inability to answer Job’s arguments.
18_32_3.WEB: Also his wrath was kindled against his three friends, because they had found no answer, and yet had condemned Job.
18_32_3.CSB17: He was also angry at Job’s three friends because they had failed to refute him and yet had condemned him.
18_32_3.AMP: Elihu's anger burned against Job's three friends because they had found no answer [and were unable to determine Job's error], and yet they had condemned Job and declared him to be in the wrong [and responsible for his own afflictions].
18_32_3.MSG: He was also angry with the three friends because they had neither come up with an answer nor proved Job wrong.
18_32_3.NIV: He was also angry with the three friends, because they had found no way to refute Job, and yet had condemned him.
18_32_3.NRSVCE: he was angry also at Job’s three friends because they had found no answer, though they had declared Job to be in the wrong.

18_32_4.NKJV: Now because they were years older than he, Elihu had waited to speak to Job.

18_32_5.NKJV: When Elihu saw that there was no answer in the mouth of these three men, his wrath was aroused.

18_32_19.MSG: The pressure has built up, like lava beneath the earth.I'm a volcano ready to blow.
18_32_19.NIV: inside I am like bottled-up wine,like new wineskins ready to burst.
18_32_19.NRSVCE: My heart is indeed like wine that has no vent; like new wineskins, it is ready to burst.

18_32_20.NKJV: I will speak, that I may find relief; I must open my lips and answer.
18_32_20.KJV: I will speak, that I may be refreshed: I will open my lips and answer. be: Heb. breathe
18_32_20.ESV: I must speak, that I may find relief; I must open my lips and answer.
18_32_20.NASB: 'Let me speak that I may get relief; Let me open my lips and answer.
18_32_20.NLT: I must speak to find relief,so let me give my answers.
18_32_20.WEB: I will speak, that I may be refreshed. I will open my lips and answer.
18_32_20.CSB17: I must speak so that I can find relief; I must open my lips and respond.
18_32_20.AMP: "I must speak so that I may get relief; I will open my lips and answer.
18_32_20.MSG: I have to speak—I have no choice.I have to say what's on my heart,
18_32_20.NIV: I must speak and find relief;I must open my lips and reply.
18_32_20.NRSVCE: I must speak, so that I may find relief; I must open my lips and answer.

18_32_21.NKJV: Let me not, I pray, show partiality to anyone; Nor let me flatter any man.
18_32_21.KJV: Let me not, I pray you, accept any man's person, neither let me give flattering titles unto man.
18_32_21.ESV: I will not show partiality to any man or use flattery toward any person.
18_32_21.NASB: 'Let me now be partial to no one, Nor flatter [any] man.
18_32_21.NLT: I won’t play favoritesor try to flatter anyone.
18_32_21.WEB: Please don’t let me respect any man’s person, neither will I give flattering titles to any man.
18_32_21.CSB17: I will be partial to no one, and I will not give anyone an undeserved title.
18_32_21.AMP: "I will not [I warn you] be partial to any man [that is, let my respect for you mitigate what I say]; Nor flatter any man.
18_32_21.MSG: And I'm going to say it straight—the truth, the whole truth, and nothing but the truth.
18_32_21.NIV: I will show partiality to no one,nor will I flatter any man;
18_32_21.NRSVCE: I will not show partiality to any person or use flattery toward anyone.

18_32_22.NKJV: For I do not know how to flatter, Else my Maker would soon take me away.
18_32_22.KJV: For I know not to give flattering titles; in so doing my maker would soon take me away.
18_32_22.ESV: For I do not know how to flatter, else my Maker would soon take me away.
18_32_22.NASB: 'For I do not know how to flatter, [Else] my Maker would soon take me away.
18_32_22.NLT: For if I tried flattery,my Creator would soon destroy me.
18_32_22.WEB: For I don’t know how to give flattering titles; or else my Maker would soon take me away.
18_32_22.CSB17: For I do not know how to give such titles; otherwise, my Maker would remove me in an instant.
18_32_22.AMP: "For I do not know how to flatter, [in an appropriate way, and I fear that], My Maker would soon take me away.
18_32_22.MSG: I was never any good at bootlicking;my Maker would make short work of me if I started in now!
18_32_22.NIV: for if I were skilled in flattery,my Maker would soon take me away.
18_32_22.NRSVCE: For I do not know how to flatter — or my Maker would soon put an end to me!

18_33_1.NKJV: “But please, Job, hear my speech, And listen to all my words.
18_33_1.KJV: Wherefore, Job, I pray thee, hear my speeches, and hearken to all my words.
18_33_1.ESV: “But now, hear my speech, O Job, and listen to all my words.
18_33_1.NASB: 'However now, Job, please hear my speech, And listen to all my words.
18_33_1.NLT: “Listen to my words, Job;pay attention to what I have to say.
18_33_1.WEB: “However, Job, please hear my speech, and listen to all my words.
18_33_1.CSB17: But now, Job, pay attention to my speech, and listen to all my words.
18_33_1.AMP: "However, Job, please listen to my words, And pay attention to everything I say.
18_33_1.MSG: "So please, Job, hear me out,honor me by listening to me.
18_33_1.NIV: “But now, Job, listen to my words;pay attention to everything I say.
18_33_1.NRSVCE: “But now, hear my speech, O Job, and listen to all my words.

18_33_2.NKJV: Now, I open my mouth; My tongue speaks in my mouth.
18_33_2.KJV: Behold, now I have opened my mouth, my tongue hath spoken in my mouth. in my mouth: Heb. in my palate
18_33_2.ESV: Behold, I open my mouth; the tongue in my mouth speaks.
18_33_2.NASB: 'Behold now, I open my mouth, My tongue in my mouth speaks.
18_33_2.NLT: Now that I have begun to speak,let me continue.
18_33_2.WEB: See now, I have opened my mouth. My tongue has spoken in my mouth.
18_33_2.CSB17: I am going to open my mouth; my tongue will form words on my palate.
18_33_2.AMP: "Behold, I have opened my mouth [to begin my speech]; My tongue in my mouth is going to speak.
18_33_2.MSG: What I'm about to sayhas been carefully thought out.
18_33_2.NIV: I am about to open my mouth;my words are on the tip of my tongue.
18_33_2.NRSVCE: See, I open my mouth; the tongue in my mouth speaks.

18_33_3.NKJV: My words come from my upright heart; My lips utter pure knowledge.
18_33_3.KJV: My words shall be of the uprightness of my heart: and my lips shall utter knowledge clearly.
18_33_3.ESV: My words declare the uprightness of my heart, and what my lips know they speak sincerely.
18_33_3.NASB: 'My words are [from] the uprightness of my heart, And my lips speak knowledge sincerely.
18_33_3.NLT: I speak with all sincerity;I speak the truth.
18_33_3.WEB: My words shall utter the uprightness of my heart. That which my lips know they shall speak sincerely.
18_33_3.CSB17: My words come from my upright heart, and my lips speak with sincerity what they know.
18_33_3.AMP: "My words will express the uprightness of my heart, And my lips will speak what they know with utter sincerity.
18_33_3.MSG: I have no ulterior motives in this;I'm speaking honestly from my heart.
18_33_3.NIV: My words come from an upright heart;my lips sincerely speak what I know.
18_33_3.NRSVCE: My words declare the uprightness of my heart, and what my lips know they speak sincerely.

18_33_4.NKJV: The Spirit of God has made me, And the breath of the Almighty gives me life.
18_33_4.KJV: The Spirit of God hath made me, and the breath of the Almighty hath given me life.
18_33_4.ESV: The Spirit of God has made me, and the breath of the Almighty gives me life.
18_33_4.NASB: 'The Spirit of God has made me, And the breath of the Almighty gives me life.
18_33_4.NLT: For the Spirit of God has made me,and the breath of the Almighty gives me life.
18_33_4.WEB: The Spirit of God has made me, and the breath of the Almighty gives me life.
18_33_4.CSB17: The Spirit of God has made me, and the breath of the Almighty gives me life.
18_33_4.AMP: "The Spirit of God has made me, And the breath of the Almighty gives me life [which inspires me].
18_33_4.MSG: The Spirit of God made me what I am,the breath of God Almighty gave me life!
18_33_4.NIV: The Spirit of God has made me;the breath of the Almighty gives me life.
18_33_4.NRSVCE: The spirit of God has made me, and the breath of the Almighty gives me life.

18_33_5.NKJV: If you can answer me, Set your words in order before me; Take your stand.
18_33_5.KJV: If thou canst answer me, set thy words in order before me, stand up.
18_33_5.ESV: Answer me, if you can; set your words in order before me; take your stand.
18_33_5.NASB: 'Refute me if you can; Array yourselves before me, take your stand.
18_33_5.NLT: Answer me, if you can;make your case and take your stand.
18_33_5.WEB: If you can, answer me. Set your words in order before me, and stand up.
18_33_5.CSB17: Refute me if you can. Prepare your case against me; take your stand.
18_33_5.AMP: "Answer me, if you can; Set yourselves before me, take your stand.
18_33_5.MSG: "And if you think you can prove me wrong, do it.Lay out your arguments. Stand up for yourself!
18_33_5.NIV: Answer me then, if you can;prepare yourself and confront me.
18_33_5.NRSVCE: Answer me, if you can; set your words in order before me; take your stand.

18_33_6.NKJV: Truly I am as your spokesman before God; I also have been formed out of clay.
18_33_6.KJV: Behold, I am according to thy wish in God's stead: I also am formed out of the clay. wish: Heb. mouth formed: Heb. cut
18_33_6.ESV: Behold, I am toward God as you are; I too was pinched off from a piece of clay.
18_33_6.NASB: 'Behold, I belong to God like you; I too have been formed out of the clay.
18_33_6.NLT: Look, you and I both belong to God.I, too, was formed from clay.
18_33_6.WEB: Behold, I am toward God even as you are. I am also formed out of the clay.
18_33_6.CSB17: I am just like you before God; I was also pinched off from a piece of clay.
18_33_6.AMP: "Behold, I belong to God like you; I too was formed out of the clay.
18_33_6.MSG: Look, I'm human—no better than you;we're both made of the same kind of mud.
18_33_6.NIV: I am just like you before God;I too have been taken from clay.
18_33_6.NRSVCE: See, before God I am as you are; I too was formed from a piece of clay.

18_33_7.NKJV: Surely no fear of me will terrify you, Nor will my hand be heavy on you.
18_33_7.KJV: Behold, my terror shall not make thee afraid, neither shall my hand be heavy upon thee.
18_33_7.ESV: Behold, no fear of me need terrify you; my pressure will not be heavy upon you.
18_33_7.NASB: 'Behold, no fear of me should terrify you, Nor should my pressure weigh heavily on you.
18_33_7.NLT: So you don’t need to be afraid of me.I won’t come down hard on you.
18_33_7.WEB: Behold, my terror shall not make you afraid, neither shall my pressure be heavy on you.
18_33_7.CSB17: Fear of me should not terrify you; no pressure from me should weigh you down.
18_33_7.AMP: "Behold, I will not make you afraid or terrified of me [for I am only mortal and not God], Nor should any pressure from me weigh heavily upon you.
18_33_7.MSG: So let's work this through together;don't let my aggressiveness overwhelm you.
18_33_7.NIV: No fear of me should alarm you,nor should my hand be heavy upon you.
18_33_7.NRSVCE: No fear of me need terrify you; my pressure will not be heavy on you.

18_33_8.NKJV: “Surely you have spoken in my hearing, And I have heard the sound of your words, saying,
18_33_8.KJV: Surely thou hast spoken in mine hearing, and I have heard the voice of thy words, saying, hearing: Heb. ears
18_33_8.ESV: “Surely you have spoken in my ears, and I have heard the sound of your words.
18_33_8.NASB: 'Surely you have spoken in my hearing, And I have heard the sound of [your] words:
18_33_8.NLT: “You have spoken in my hearing,and I have heard your very words.
18_33_8.WEB: “Surely you have spoken in my hearing, I have heard the voice of your words, saying,
18_33_8.CSB17: Surely you have spoken in my hearing, and I have heard these very words:
18_33_8.AMP: "Surely you have spoken in my hearing, And I have heard the sound of your words, saying:
18_33_8.MSG: "Here's what you said.I heard you say it with my own ears.
18_33_8.NIV: “But you have said in my hearing—I heard the very words—
18_33_8.NRSVCE: “Surely, you have spoken in my hearing, and I have heard the sound of your words.

18_33_9.NKJV: ‘I am pure, without transgression; I am innocent, and there is no iniquity in me.
18_33_9.KJV: I am clean without transgression, I am innocent; neither is there iniquity in me.
18_33_9.ESV: You say, ‘I am pure, without transgression; I am clean, and there is no iniquity in me.
18_33_9.NASB: 'I am pure, without transgression; I am innocent and there is no guilt in me.
18_33_9.NLT: You said, ‘I am pure; I am without sin;I am innocent; I have no guilt.
18_33_9.WEB: ‘I am clean, without disobedience. I am innocent, neither is there iniquity in me.
18_33_9.CSB17: “I am pure, without transgression; I am clean and have no iniquity.
18_33_9.AMP: 'I am pure, without transgression; I am innocent and there is no guilt in me.
18_33_9.MSG: You said, 'I'm pure—I've done nothing wrong.Believe me, I'm clean—my conscience is clear.
18_33_9.NIV: ‘I am pure and without sin;I am clean and free from guilt.
18_33_9.NRSVCE: You say, ‘I am clean, without transgression; I am pure, and there is no iniquity in me.

18_33_10.NKJV: Yet He finds occasions against me, He counts me as His enemy;
18_33_10.KJV: Behold, he findeth occasions against me, he counteth me for his enemy,
18_33_10.ESV: Behold, he finds occasions against me, he counts me as his enemy,
18_33_10.NASB: 'Behold, He invents pretexts against me; He counts me as His enemy.
18_33_10.NLT: God is picking a quarrel with me,and he considers me his enemy.
18_33_10.WEB: Behold, he finds occasions against me. He counts me for his enemy.
18_33_10.CSB17: But he finds reasons to oppose me; he regards me as his enemy.
18_33_10.AMP: 'Behold, God finds pretexts against me; He counts me as His enemy.
18_33_10.MSG: But God keeps picking on me;he treats me like I'm his enemy.
18_33_10.NIV: Yet God has found fault with me;he considers me his enemy.
18_33_10.NRSVCE: Look, he finds occasions against me, he counts me as his enemy;

18_33_11.NKJV: He puts my feet in the stocks, He watches all my paths.’
18_33_11.KJV: He putteth my feet in the stocks, he marketh all my paths.
18_33_11.ESV: he puts my feet in the stocks and watches all my paths.’
18_33_11.NASB: 'He puts my feet in the stocks; He watches all my paths.'
18_33_11.NLT: He puts my feet in the stocksand watches my every move.’
18_33_11.WEB: He puts my feet in the stocks. He marks all my paths.’
18_33_11.CSB17: He puts my feet in the stocks; he stands watch over all my paths.”
18_33_11.AMP: 'He puts my feet in the stocks [to hinder and humiliate me]; He [suspiciously] watches all my paths,' [you say].
18_33_11.MSG: He's thrown me in jail;he keeps me under constant surveillance.'
18_33_11.NIV: He fastens my feet in shackles;he keeps close watch on all my paths.’
18_33_11.NRSVCE: he puts my feet in the stocks, and watches all my paths.’

18_33_12.NKJV: “Look, in this you are not righteous. I will answer you, For God is greater than man.
18_33_12.KJV: Behold, in this thou art not just: I will answer thee, that God is greater than man.
18_33_12.ESV: “Behold, in this you are not right. I will answer you, for God is greater than man.
18_33_12.NASB: 'Behold, let me tell you, you are not right in this, For God is greater than man.
18_33_12.NLT: “But you are wrong, and I will show you why.For God is greater than any human being.
18_33_12.WEB: “Behold, I will answer you. In this you are not just, for God is greater than man.
18_33_12.CSB17: But I tell you that you are wrong in this matter, since God is greater than man.
18_33_12.AMP: "Look, let me answer you, in this you are not right or just; For God is greater and far superior to man.
18_33_12.MSG: "But let me tell you, Job, you're wrong, dead wrong!God is far greater than any human.
18_33_12.NIV: “But I tell you, in this you are not right,for God is greater than man.
18_33_12.NRSVCE: “But in this you are not right. I will answer you: God is greater than any mortal.

18_33_13.NKJV: Why do you contend with Him? For He does not give an accounting of any of His words.
18_33_13.KJV: Why dost thou strive against him? for he giveth not account of any of his matters. he giveth: Heb. he answereth not
18_33_13.ESV: Why do you contend against him, saying, ‘He will answer none of man's words’?
18_33_13.NASB: 'Why do you complain against Him That He does not give an account of all His doings?
18_33_13.NLT: So why are you bringing a charge against him?Why say he does not respond to people’s complaints?
18_33_13.WEB: Why do you strive against him, because he doesn’t give account of any of his matters?
18_33_13.CSB17: Why do you take him to court for not answering anything a person asks?
18_33_13.AMP: "Why do you complain against Him? That He does not answer [you with] all His doings.
18_33_13.MSG: So how dare you haul him into court,and then complain that he won't answer your charges?
18_33_13.NIV: Why do you complain to himthat he answers none of man’s words?
18_33_13.NRSVCE: Why do you contend against him, saying, ‘He will answer none of my words’?

18_33_14.NKJV: For God may speak in one way, or in another, Yet man does not perceive it.
18_33_14.KJV: For God speaketh once, yea twice, yet man perceiveth it not.
18_33_14.ESV: For God speaks in one way, and in two, though man does not perceive it.
18_33_14.NASB: 'Indeed God speaks once, Or twice, [yet] no one notices it.
18_33_14.NLT: For God speaks again and again,though people do not recognize it.
18_33_14.WEB: For God speaks once, yes twice, though man pays no attention.
18_33_14.CSB17: For God speaks time and again, but a person may not notice it.
18_33_14.AMP: "For God speaks once, And even twice, yet no one notices it [including you, Job].
18_33_14.MSG: God always answers, one way or another,even when people don't recognize his presence.
18_33_14.NIV: For God does speak—now one way, now another—though man may not perceive it.
18_33_14.NRSVCE: For God speaks in one way, and in two, though people do not perceive it.

18_33_15.NKJV: In a dream, in a vision of the night, When deep sleep falls upon men, While slumbering on their beds,
18_33_15.KJV: In a dream, in a vision of the night, when deep sleep falleth upon men, in slumberings upon the bed;
18_33_15.ESV: In a dream, in a vision of the night, when deep sleep falls on men, while they slumber on their beds,
18_33_15.NASB: 'In a dream, a vision of the night, When sound sleep falls on men, While they slumber in their beds,
18_33_15.NLT: He speaks in dreams, in visions of the night,when deep sleep falls on peopleas they lie in their beds.
18_33_15.WEB: In a dream, in a vision of the night, when deep sleep falls on men, in slumbering on the bed;
18_33_15.CSB17: In a dream, a vision in the night, when deep sleep comes over people as they slumber on their beds,
18_33_15.AMP: "In a dream, a vision of the night [one may hear God's voice], When deep sleep falls on men While slumbering upon the bed,
18_33_15.MSG: "In a dream, for instance, a vision at night,when men and women are deep in sleep,fast asleep in their beds—
18_33_15.NIV: In a dream, in a vision of the night,when deep sleep falls on menas they slumber in their beds,
18_33_15.NRSVCE: In a dream, in a vision of the night, when deep sleep falls on mortals, while they slumber on their beds,

18_33_16.NKJV: Then He opens the ears of men, And seals their instruction.
18_33_16.KJV: Then he openeth the ears of men, and sealeth their instruction, he: Heb. he revealeth, or, uncovereth
18_33_16.ESV: then he opens the ears of men and terrifies them with warnings,
18_33_16.NASB: Then He opens the ears of men, And seals their instruction,
18_33_16.NLT: He whispers in their earsand terrifies them with warnings.
18_33_16.WEB: Then he opens the ears of men, and seals their instruction,
18_33_16.CSB17: he uncovers their ears and terrifies them with warnings,
18_33_16.AMP: Then He opens the ears of men And seals their instruction,
18_33_16.MSG: God opens their earsand impresses them with warnings
18_33_16.NIV: he may speak in their earsand terrify them with warnings,
18_33_16.NRSVCE: then he opens their ears, and terrifies them with warnings,

18_33_17.NKJV: In order to turn man from his deed, And conceal pride from man,
18_33_17.KJV: That he may withdraw man from his purpose, and hide pride from man. purpose: Heb. work
18_33_17.ESV: that he may turn man aside from his deed and conceal pride from a man;
18_33_17.NASB: That He may turn man aside [from his] conduct, And keep man from pride;
18_33_17.NLT: He makes them turn from doing wrong;he keeps them from pride.
18_33_17.WEB: That he may withdraw man from his purpose, and hide pride from man.
18_33_17.CSB17: in order to turn a person from his actions and suppress the pride of a person.
18_33_17.AMP: That He may turn man aside from his conduct, And keep him from pride;
18_33_17.MSG: To turn them back from something bad they're planning,from some reckless choice,
18_33_17.NIV: to turn man from wrongdoingand keep him from pride,
18_33_17.NRSVCE: that he may turn them aside from their deeds, and keep them from pride,

18_33_18.NKJV: He keeps back his soul from the Pit, And his life from perishing by the sword.
18_33_18.KJV: He keepeth back his soul from the pit, and his life from perishing by the sword. from perishing: Heb. from passing
18_33_18.ESV: he keeps back his soul from the pit, his life from perishing by the sword.
18_33_18.NASB: He keeps back his soul from the pit, And his life from passing over into Sheol.
18_33_18.NLT: He protects them from the grave,from crossing over the river of death.
18_33_18.WEB: He keeps back his soul from the pit, and his life from perishing by the sword.
18_33_18.CSB17: God spares his soul from the Pit, his life from crossing the river of death.
18_33_18.AMP: He holds back his soul from the pit [of destruction], And his life from passing over into Sheol (the nether world, the place of the dead).
18_33_18.MSG: And keep them from an early grave,from the river of no return.
18_33_18.NIV: to preserve his soul from the pit,his life from perishing by the sword.
18_33_18.NRSVCE: to spare their souls from the Pit, their lives from traversing the River.

18_33_19.NKJV: “Man is also chastened with pain on his bed, And with strong pain in many of his bones,
18_33_19.KJV: He is chastened also with pain upon his bed, and the multitude of his bones with strong pain:
18_33_19.ESV: “Man is also rebuked with pain on his bed and with continual strife in his bones,
18_33_19.NASB: 'Man is also chastened with pain on his bed, And with unceasing complaint in his bones;
18_33_19.NLT: “Or God disciplines people with pain on their sickbeds,with ceaseless aching in their bones.
18_33_19.WEB: He is chastened also with pain on his bed, with continual strife in his bones;
18_33_19.CSB17: A person may be disciplined on his bed with pain and constant distress in his bones,
18_33_19.AMP: "Man is also disciplined with pain on his bed, And with unceasing complaint in his bones,
18_33_19.MSG: "Or, God might get their attention through pain,by throwing them on a bed of suffering,
18_33_19.NIV: Or a man may be chastened on a bed of painwith constant distress in his bones,
18_33_19.NRSVCE: They are also chastened with pain upon their beds, and with continual strife in their bones,

18_33_20.NKJV: So that his life abhors bread, And his soul succulent food.
18_33_20.KJV: So that his life abhorreth bread, and his soul dainty meat. dainty: Heb. meat of desire
18_33_20.ESV: so that his life loathes bread, and his appetite the choicest food.
18_33_20.NASB: So that his life loathes bread, And his soul favorite food.
18_33_20.NLT: They lose their appetitefor even the most delicious food.
18_33_20.WEB: So that his life abhors bread, and his soul dainty food.
18_33_20.CSB17: so that he detests bread, and his soul despises his favorite food.
18_33_20.AMP: So that his life makes him loathe food, And his soul [loathe] even his favorite dishes.
18_33_20.MSG: So they can't stand the sight of food,have no appetite for their favorite treats.
18_33_20.NIV: so that his very being finds food repulsiveand his soul loathes the choicest meal.
18_33_20.NRSVCE: so that their lives loathe bread, and their appetites dainty food.

18_33_21.NKJV: His flesh wastes away from sight, And his bones stick out which once were not seen.
18_33_21.KJV: His flesh is consumed away, that it cannot be seen; and his bones that were not seen stick out .
18_33_21.ESV: His flesh is so wasted away that it cannot be seen, and his bones that were not seen stick out.
18_33_21.NASB: 'His flesh wastes away from sight, And his bones which were not seen stick out.
18_33_21.NLT: Their flesh wastes away,and their bones stick out.
18_33_21.WEB: His flesh is so consumed away, that it can’t be seen. His bones that were not seen stick out.
18_33_21.CSB17: His flesh wastes away to nothing, and his unseen bones stick out.
18_33_21.AMP: "His flesh is so wasted away that it cannot be seen, And his bones which were not seen now stick out.
18_33_21.MSG: They lose weight, wasting away to nothing,reduced to a bag of bones.
18_33_21.NIV: His flesh wastes away to nothing,and his bones, once hidden, now stick out.

18_34_20.NKJV: In a moment they die, in the middle of the night; The people are shaken and pass away; The mighty are taken away without a hand.

18_34_29.NKJV: When He gives quietness, who then can make trouble? And when He hides His face, who then can see Him, Whether it is against a nation or a man alone?—
